Table vehicle.vin_registry {
  vin_registry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VIN registry record. Primary key for the VIN registry data product.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_configuration. Business justification: Each VIN corresponds to a single vehicle configuration; linking VIN to its configuration eliminates duplicated spec columns.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: VIN decoding determines nameplate for warranty, recall and regulatory reporting; linking enables automated recall notices per nameplate.']
  telematics_device_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the embedded telematics control unit (TCU) or connectivity module installed in this vehicle. Used for V2X communication and OTA updates.']
  battery_capacity_kwh decimal(8,2) [note: 'The total energy storage capacity of the high-voltage traction battery in kilowatt-hours. Applicable to HEV, PHEV, BEV, and FCEV vehicles. Null for pure ICE vehicles.']
  build_date date [note: 'The calendar date when the vehicle completed final assembly and rolled off the production line. Captured from Manufacturing Execution System (MES).']
  check_digit varchar [note: 'The 9th character of the VIN used to validate VIN authenticity through a mathematical algorithm. Required in North American markets. Value is 0-9 or X (representing 10).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this VIN registry record was first created in the enterprise data system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  curb_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The weight of the vehicle with standard equipment, full fluids, and a full tank of fuel, but without passengers or cargo. Measured in kilograms.']
  destination_market varchar [note: 'The intended sales and distribution market for this vehicle unit. May differ from homologation market for export vehicles. Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  emission_standard varchar [note: 'The regulatory emissions certification standard this vehicle complies with (e.g., EPA Tier 3, Euro 6d, China 6, BS VI). Defines allowable pollutant limits.']
  eop_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date when series production ceased for this vehicle\'s model and platform configuration. Null if still in production.']
  epa_combined_mpg decimal(6,2) [note: 'The EPA-certified combined city/highway fuel economy rating in miles per gallon. Applicable to ICE, HEV, and PHEV vehicles sold in the United States.']
  fuel_tank_capacity_liters decimal(8,2) [note: 'The total volume capacity of the primary fuel tank in liters. For electric vehicles, this represents the battery capacity equivalent or is null.']
  gvwr_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable total weight of the vehicle including chassis, body, engine, fluids, passengers, and cargo, as certified by the manufacturer. Measured in kilograms.']
  homologation_market varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory market for which this vehicle was homologated and certified (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN, JPN). Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this VIN registry record was most recently updated. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  line_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the vehicle exited the final assembly line and passed end-of-line quality inspection. Captured from MES.']
  model_year_decoded varchar [note: 'The decoded four-digit calendar year representing the model year (e.g., 2023, 2024). Derived from the 10th VIN character.']
  msrp_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the MSRP amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY, JPY).']
  obd_protocol varchar [note: 'The diagnostic communication protocol standard implemented in this vehicle\'s Electronic Control Units (ECUs) for emissions and diagnostics monitoring.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The 11th character of the VIN identifying the specific assembly plant where the vehicle was manufactured. Manufacturer-specific encoding.']
  production_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Characters 12-17 of the VIN representing the sequential production number assigned to this vehicle unit at the assembly plant. Resets annually per plant.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vehicle unit is currently subject to one or more open safety or compliance recalls issued by the manufacturer or regulatory authority.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'The overall safety assessment rating from regulatory crash testing programs (e.g., NHTSA 5-star, Euro NCAP 5-star). Reflects occupant protection performance.']
  sop_date date [note: 'The date when series production began for this vehicle\'s model and platform configuration. Indicates transition from pre-production to volume manufacturing.']
  telematics_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vehicle is equipped with connected vehicle telematics hardware and has an active data subscription for remote diagnostics, OTA updates, and mobility services.']
  vds varchar [note: 'Characters 4-9 of the VIN that describe vehicle attributes such as model, body style, engine type, and restraint system. Manufacturer-specific encoding.']
  vehicle_lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of this vehicle unit from manufacturing through end-of-life. Tracks the vehicle\'s journey from plant to customer to disposal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_production|in_production|quality_hold|in_transit|dealer_inventory|retail_sold|in_service|recalled|scrapped|exported|end_of_life — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vin varchar [note: 'The 17-character alphanumeric Vehicle Identification Number that uniquely identifies this physical vehicle unit worldwide. Excludes letters I, O, Q to avoid confusion with numbers.']
  vis varchar [note: 'Characters 10-17 of the VIN that include model year, plant code, and production sequence number. Uniquely identifies this specific vehicle unit.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'The date when the manufacturer\'s base warranty coverage expires for this vehicle unit. Calculated from warranty start date plus warranty duration.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'The date when the manufacturer\'s warranty coverage begins for this vehicle unit. Typically the retail delivery date or first registration date.']
  wltp_combined_consumption decimal(6,2) [note: 'The WLTP-certified combined fuel consumption in liters per 100 kilometers. Applicable to vehicles sold in markets requiring WLTP certification (EU, UK, etc.).']
  wmi varchar [note: 'The first three characters of the VIN that identify the manufacturer and country of origin. Assigned by SAE International.']

  Note: 'SSOT for every physical vehicle instance identified by its 17-character VIN per ISO 3779. Captures decoded VIN structure (WMI, VDS, VIS), manufacturing plant code, production sequence number, build date, model year (MY), line-off timestamp, homologation market, and current lifecycle status (pre-production, in-production, in-transit, in-service, end-of-life). This is the enterprise anchor record — the single identity key — that every downstream domain (sales, aftersales, logistics, mobility, finance) references to identify a specific vehicle unit.'
}

Table vehicle.model {
  model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the vehicle model record.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Model-to-nameplate mapping is required for global product catalog, pricing strategy and sales reporting across regions.']
  platform_id varchar [note: 'FK to vehicle.platform']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Required for Program Management report linking each vehicle model to its engineering program for budgeting, target metrics, and compliance.']
  adas_features varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of Advanced Driver Assistance Systems features.']
  battery_capacity_kwh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Usable energy storage capacity of the battery pack.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'Physical body configuration of the model. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sedan|hatchback|crossover|pickup|coupe|convertible|wagon|suv|van|truck — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Manufacturer brand or marque associated with the model (e.g., Chevrolet, Toyota).']
  cargo_capacity_liters decimal(7,1) [note: 'Maximum cargo volume.']
  co2_emissions_g_per_km decimal(6,1) [note: 'Regulated CO₂ emissions per kilometer.']
  connectivity_features varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of telematics and OTA capabilities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model record was first created.']
  curb_weight_kg decimal(7,1) [note: 'Weight of the vehicle with standard equipment, no passengers or cargo.']
  drive_configuration varchar [note: 'Primary drivetrain layout of the model.']
  electric_range_miles decimal(6,1) [note: 'EPA‑rated electric driving range.']
  emissions_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory emissions standard applicable to the model.']
  end_of_production_model_year varchar [note: 'Model year in which production of the model ends.']
  engine_code varchar [note: 'Factory‑assigned engine identifier for ICE/HEV models.']
  eop_date date [note: 'Date when production of the model ceased.']
  fuel_economy_city_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'EPA city fuel consumption rating.']
  fuel_economy_hwy_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'EPA highway fuel consumption rating.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary fuel used by the model.']
  height_mm decimal(7,1) [note: 'Overall vehicle height.']
  homologation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory homologation.']
  launch_model_year varchar [note: 'Model year in which the model entered production (Start of Production).']
  length_mm decimal(7,1) [note: 'Overall vehicle length.']
  market_regions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3 country codes where the model is sold.']
  model_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the model\'s positioning and key attributes.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the vehicle model (e.g., Model 3, F‑150).']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the model.']
  msrp_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base MSRP in US dollars.']
  ncap_safety_rating varchar [note: 'Euro NCAP safety rating.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain architecture category.']
  primary_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market where the model is primarily sold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: north_america|europe|asia|latin_america|middle_east|africa|australia — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Internal program identifier linking the model to development projects.']
  safety_standard varchar [note: 'Safety regulatory framework governing the model.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of occupants.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification of the model.']
  sop_date date [note: 'Date when production of the model commenced.']
  transmission_gears varchar [note: 'Count of forward gears in the transmission.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of drivetrain transmission.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the model record.']
  variant_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct trim/option configurations derived from this model.']
  vehicle_class varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the vehicle purpose.']
  wheelbase_mm decimal(7,1) [note: 'Distance between front and rear axles.']
  width_mm decimal(7,1) [note: 'Overall vehicle width.']

  Note: 'Defines the commercial vehicle model (nameplate) as a business entity — e.g., F-150, Camry, Model 3. Captures model name, brand/marque, vehicle segment (sedan, SUV, pickup, commercial), body style, drive configuration (FWD, RWD, AWD, 4WD), primary market, launch MY, EOP MY, and program code. Serves as the top-level classification anchor for all vehicle configurations and trim hierarchies.'
}

Table vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program {
  vehicle_model_year_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle_model_year_program data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Each model year program belongs to a specific vehicle model; adding model_id FK enables proper hierarchy and eliminates the siloed vehicle_model_year_program table.']

  Note: 'Governs the MY (Model Year) lifecycle for each model, from SOP (Start of Production) through EOP (End of Production). Tracks planned SOP date, actual SOP date, planned EOP date, actual EOP date, regulatory MY designation, CAFE compliance year, homologation status per market, and program phase (concept, development, launch, production, wind-down). Links model to its annual production program and drives downstream planning in manufacturing and supply chain.'
}

Table vehicle.vehicle_trim_level {
  vehicle_trim_level_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle_trim_level data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_trim_level_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.trim_level. Business justification: Trim level definitions in vehicle data must map to product trim records for pricing, options and market availability.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Trim levels are defined per model; adding model_id FK removes duplicated model attributes and ties trim to its parent model.']

  Note: 'Defines the ordered hierarchy of trim grades within a model-MY combination (e.g., Base, XL, XLT, Lariat, Platinum, Limited). Captures trim code, trim name, market positioning tier, MSRP base price, standard feature set description, target market region, and active/inactive status. Trim levels are the primary commercial differentiation unit used in sales, dealer ordering, and MSRP pricing.'
}

  powertrain_variant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each powertrain variant record.']

  powertrain_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.powertrain_spec. Business justification: Needed for Powertrain Engineering Spec compliance tracking; each variant must reference the spec defining performance, emissions, and dimensions.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: WARRANTY & REGULATORY: Powertrain variant must be linked to its engine/motor supplier for warranty and emissions compliance.']
  battery_capacity_kwh decimal(5,2) [note: 'Usable energy storage capacity of the battery pack.']
  charging_standard varchar [note: 'Supported fast‑charging protocol.']
  co2_emissions_g_per_km varchar [note: 'Tailpipe carbon dioxide emissions per kilometer.']

  cylinder_count varchar [note: 'Number of cylinders in the internal combustion engine.']
  drive_type varchar [note: 'Configuration of power delivery to wheels.']
  electric_motor_power_kw decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum electric motor output in kilowatts.']
  end_of_production_date date [note: 'Date when the variant ceased production (null if still in production).']
  engine_displacement_cc varchar [note: 'Engine cylinder volume in cubic centimeters for internal combustion engines.']
  epa_fuel_economy_combined_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'EPA combined fuel economy for regulatory reporting.']
  epa_range_miles varchar [note: 'EPA certified electric range in miles.']
  fuel_economy_city_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'EPA city fuel economy in miles per gallon.']
  fuel_economy_combined_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'EPA combined city/highway fuel economy in miles per gallon.']
  fuel_economy_highway_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'EPA highway fuel economy in miles per gallon.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary fuel used by the powertrain.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year associated with the variant.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the powertrain technology.']
  powertrain_variant_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the variant, including marketing notes.']
  powertrain_variant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the variant.']
  range_km varchar [note: 'Estimated driving range on a full charge for electric variants.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  start_of_production_date date [note: 'Date when the variant entered series production.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission used with the powertrain.']
  variant_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used by engineering and manufacturing to reference the variant.']
  variant_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the powertrain variant.']
  vehicle_platform varchar [note: 'Underlying vehicle architecture/platform shared across models.']
  wltp_fuel_economy_combined_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'Combined fuel economy measured under WLTP test cycle.']
  wltp_range_km varchar [note: 'Estimated driving range under WLTP testing.']

  Note: 'Authoritative catalog of all powertrain configurations available across the vehicle lineup. Captures powertrain type (ICE, HEV, PHEV, BEV, FCEV), engine displacement, cylinder count, fuel type, electric motor peak power (kW), combined system power, transmission type, drive type, EPA/WLTP fuel economy or range rating, CO2 emissions (g/km), battery capacity (kWh for EV/PHEV), and charging standard (CCS, CHAdeMO, NACS). Used for homologation, CAFE compliance, and consumer-facing specifications.'
}

Table vehicle.platform {
  platform_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the vehicle platform record.']
  adaptive_cruise_control boolean [note: 'True if the platform supports adaptive cruise control as a standard feature.']
  adas_features varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of Advanced Driver Assistance System features available on the platform. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lane_keep|blind_spot|auto_brake|traffic_sign_recognition|parking_assist|night_vision|driver_monitor|highway_assist|traffic_jam_assist|remote_parking|collision_warning|pedestrian_detection — promote to reference product]']
  architecture varchar [note: 'Technical architecture family (e.g., front‑engine, rear‑engine, modular).']
  compatible_powertrain_families varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of powertrain families that can be mounted on the platform.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the platform record was first created in the system.']
  development_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total engineering and tooling cost incurred to develop the platform, expressed in US dollars.']
  emissions_class varchar [note: 'Regulatory emissions classification applicable to the platform.']
  end_of_production_date date [note: 'Date when the platform ceased series production; null if still active.']
  family varchar [note: 'Family grouping used internally to cluster related platforms.']
  generation varchar [note: 'Generation identifier indicating the evolutionary version of the platform (e.g., Gen1, Gen2).']
  height_max_mm varchar [note: 'Longest overall height (mm) that the platform can accommodate.']
  height_min_mm varchar [note: 'Shortest overall height (mm) that the platform can accommodate.']
  length_max_mm varchar [note: 'Longest overall length (mm) that the platform can accommodate.']
  length_min_mm varchar [note: 'Shortest overall length (mm) that the platform can accommodate.']
  material_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary structural material approach used for the platform.']
  max_gvw_kg varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable gross vehicle weight for vehicles built on this platform, expressed in kilograms.']
  modular_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1‑10) indicating the degree of modularity and parts commonality.']
  ota_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicles on this platform support OTA software updates.']
  owner_business_unit varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the platform\'s development and lifecycle management.']
  platform_code varchar [note: 'External, human‑readable code that uniquely identifies the platform across the enterprise.']
  platform_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the platform\'s purpose and key characteristics.']
  platform_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the platform used in engineering and marketing materials.']
  platform_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the platform.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification such as \'unibody\' or \'body‑on‑frame\'.']
  regulatory_compliance varchar [note: 'Textual statement summarizing compliance with NHTSA, EPA, IATF 16949, and other applicable standards.']
  release_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year when the platform was first released for production.']
  start_of_production_date date [note: 'Date when the platform entered series production.']
  track_width_max_mm varchar [note: 'Longest track width (in millimetres) accommodated by the platform.']
  track_width_min_mm varchar [note: 'Shortest track width (in millimetres) accommodated by the platform.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the platform record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for engineering revisions of the platform.']
  weight_kg varchar [note: 'Typical curb weight of the platform without powertrain or body equipment, in kilograms.']
  wheelbase_max_mm varchar [note: 'Longest wheelbase dimension (in millimetres) supported by the platform.']
  wheelbase_min_mm varchar [note: 'Shortest wheelbase dimension (in millimetres) that a model can be built on this platform.']
  width_max_mm varchar [note: 'Longest overall width (mm) that the platform can accommodate.']
  width_min_mm varchar [note: 'Shortest overall width (mm) that the platform can accommodate.']

  Note: 'Defines the vehicle platform (architecture) that underpins one or more models. Captures platform code, platform name, architecture generation, wheelbase range, track width range, structural material strategy (steel, aluminum, mixed), compatible powertrain families, maximum GVW rating, and platform owner business unit. Platforms are shared across multiple nameplates and model years, making this a critical cross-model reference for engineering, manufacturing, and supply chain.'
}

Table vehicle.vehicle_adas_feature {
  vehicle_adas_feature_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle_adas_feature data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_configuration. Business justification: An ADAS feature is part of a specific vehicle configuration; FK provides context and removes duplicated configuration fields.']

  Note: 'Catalog of all ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) and autonomous driving features offered across the vehicle lineup. Captures feature code, feature name, SAE automation level (L0–L5), sensor dependencies (camera, radar, lidar, ultrasonic), regulatory approval status per market, OTA-updatable flag, ECU host system, software version baseline, and availability by trim/market. Supports homologation, NCAP scoring, and connected mobility feature management.'
}

Table vehicle.configuration {
  configuration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each vehicle configuration record.']
  aftersales_trim_level_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.trim_level']
  body_style_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.body_style']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_change. Business justification: Configuration traceability to the engineering change that introduced it is required for change impact analysis and audit.']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Each vehicle configuration follows a control plan that defines inspection and test requirements; FK links configuration to its quality control plan.']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: Each vehicle configuration must reference the design specification governing component requirements for compliance and validation.']
  ecu_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.ecu_catalog. Business justification: Vehicle configuration includes a set of ECUs; adding ECU catalog FK provides direct reference.']
  engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Configuration approval process mandates an engineer sign‑off; linking to employee enables approval workflow and regulatory audit of engineering responsibility.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.functional_location. Business justification: Needed for Production Planning Report that assigns each vehicle configuration to the assembly line functional location where it is built.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'FK to vehicle.model']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Configuration records are grouped under a nameplate to support global pricing, compliance and reporting across plants.']
  platform_id varchar [note: 'FK to vehicle.platform']
  powertrain_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: Vehicle configuration selects a powertrain variant; FK to powertrain_variant eliminates duplicated variant attributes.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT PLANNING: Primary supplier for a vehicle configuration is needed for BOM cost allocation and supply‑chain scheduling.']
  production_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant assigned to build the configuration.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: COMPLIANCE REPORTING: Configuration references the contract governing component supply, required for audit and cost tracking.']
  adas_features varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ADAS features included.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'Physical body style of the vehicle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sedan|hatchback|SUV|truck|coupe|convertible|wagon — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  build_feasibility_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the configuration can be built at the plant.']
  co2_emissions_g_per_km varchar [note: 'Tailpipe CO2 emissions per kilometer.']
  configuration_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the vehicle configuration within the enterprise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration record was created.']
  destination_charge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard charge for delivery to dealer.']
  drivetrain varchar [note: 'Drive configuration.']
  emissions_cert_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory emissions certification status.']
  end_of_production_date date [note: 'Date when production of this configuration ends.']
  ev_range_miles varchar [note: 'Estimated electric driving range in miles.']
  exterior_color varchar [note: 'Standard exterior paint color code.']
  fuel_economy_city_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'City fuel economy in miles per gallon.']
  fuel_economy_hwy_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highway fuel economy in miles per gallon.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary fuel used by the powertrain.']
  interior_material varchar [note: 'Material used for interior trim (e.g., leather, fabric).']
  market_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the target market.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market region for which the configuration is built.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the model is marketed.']
  msrp_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base MSRP before options and destination charges.']
  optional_package_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of optional package identifiers.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the configuration record.']
  start_of_production_date date [note: 'Date when production of this configuration begins.']
  total_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Combined MSRP and destination charge.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Transmission technology.']
  trim_level varchar [note: 'Specific trim level designation (e.g., Sport, Luxury).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration record.']
  vehicle_platform varchar [note: 'Underlying platform architecture shared across models.']
  warranty_miles varchar [note: 'Maximum mileage covered under the basic warranty.']
  warranty_years varchar [note: 'Number of years covered under the basic warranty.']
  wheel_size_inch varchar [note: 'Diameter of wheels in inches.']

  Note: 'The fully specified, buildable vehicle configuration combining model, MY, trim, powertrain, and market. Each record represents a unique orderable specification (analogous to a manufacturing SKU or dealer order code). Captures configuration code, market region, build feasibility status, MSRP, destination charge, fuel economy label values, emissions certification status, and production plant assignment. This is the master configuration record that bridges product planning to manufacturing BOM explosion and dealer order management.'
}

Table vehicle.vehicle_option_package {
  vehicle_option_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle_option_package data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: Option package definition relies on an engineering BOM that lists parts and quantities; linking enables traceability from package to BOM.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_configuration. Business justification: Option packages are assigned to specific vehicle configurations; FK creates the relationship.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: INVENTORY & COSTING: Option packages are sourced from specific suppliers; linking supports inventory allocation and cost analysis.']

  Note: 'Defines factory-installed option packages, standalone options, exterior colors, and interior trim selections available for vehicle configurations. Captures option code, option name, option type (package, standalone, accessory, exterior color, interior trim), MSRP uplift, content description, availability constraints (trim/market/powertrain restrictions), mutually exclusive options, and required prerequisite options. Drives dealer order configuration, window sticker (Monroney label) generation, and BOM explosion in manufacturing.'
}

Table vehicle.build_spec {
  build_spec_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the as-built vehicle specification record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Build spec approval is signed off by a designated employee; linking provides audit trail for quality control and change‑management reports.']
  bom_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bom_header. Business justification: Production planning uses the build spec to reference the exact BOM header that defines component list and costs.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_configuration. Business justification: Build spec records represent the as‑built configuration; linking to vehicle_configuration removes duplicated spec attributes.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Required for Build Traceability Report linking each built vehicle to the primary assembly equipment for warranty and quality investigations.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: PRODUCTION SCHEDULING: Build spec ties to the PO that supplies parts, enabling financial reconciliation and line‑balancing.']
  build_date date [note: 'Calendar date the vehicle left the production line.']
  build_spec_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the build specification record.']
  co2_emissions_g_per_km varchar [note: 'Measured carbon dioxide emissions per kilometer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the build specification record was created in the data lake.']
  diagnostic_trouble_code_initial varchar [note: 'First DTC recorded at the end of production, if any.']
  emissions_rating varchar [note: 'Regulatory emissions classification applicable to the vehicle.']
  fleet_spec_flag boolean [note: 'True if the vehicle was built to a fleet or corporate specification.']
  fuel_efficiency_mpg decimal(4,2) [note: 'Manufacturer‑reported fuel economy in miles per gallon (city/highway combined).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the build specification record.']
  ncap_rating varchar [note: 'New Car Assessment Programme safety rating.']
  ota_updatable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle supports over‑the‑air updates.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the vehicle was assembled.']
  production_line varchar [note: 'Specific assembly line or bay within the plant.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approvals for the vehicle configuration.']
  safety_feature_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of safety system identifiers (e.g., ADAS, ESC).']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to the vehicle on the production day.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Baseline software version loaded on vehicle ECUs at build.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., MES, PLM) that supplied the as‑built data.']
  special_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was built to a special customer order.']
  v2x_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'True if vehicle‑to‑everything communication hardware is installed.']
  vehicle_weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Curb weight of the vehicle as built, in kilograms.']
  vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique 17‑character identifier assigned to each vehicle at manufacture.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty period expires.']
  warranty_mileage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum mileage covered under the warranty.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty period begins.']

  Note: 'The as-built specification record for a specific VIN, capturing the exact combination of configuration, options, colors, powertrain, and features installed on that vehicle unit as it rolled off the production line. Captures VIN reference, configuration code, exterior color, interior trim selection, all installed option codes, actual build date, plant code, production sequence number, and any deviation from standard specification (e.g., special order, fleet spec, running change). This is the definitive as-built record used by aftersales, warranty, recall population identification, and residual value analytics.'
}

Table vehicle.homologation {
  homologation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each homologation record.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Homologation certifications require a compliance officer; FK records the responsible employee for regulatory reporting and traceability.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_configuration. Business justification: Homologation is tied to a specific vehicle configuration; FK eliminates duplicated regulatory fields.']
  homologation_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.homologation_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory compliance audit requires each homologation record to reference the specific requirement it satisfies.']
  homologation_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.homologation_variant. Business justification: Regulatory filing links each vehicle homologation record to the specific product homologation variant for audit trails.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the homologation was officially granted.']
  approval_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the approval applies to the entire vehicle or a specific component.']
  authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the government or standards authority that issued the approval.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official certificate or approval number assigned by the authority.']
  certification_status_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance status was last updated.']
  compliance_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital copy of the approval certificate or related documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the homologation record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the homologation becomes effective for production.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the homologation ceases to be effective (if different from expiry).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the homologation expires and must be renewed or re‑validated.']
  homologation_category varchar [note: 'Primary focus of the approval: safety, emissions, or both.']
  homologation_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the record represents a whole‑vehicle type approval or a component‑level approval.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'True if the homologation is currently active and usable.']
  is_ota_updatable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved component can receive Over‑The‑Air updates.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance inspection associated with this homologation.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the homologation record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the homologation record.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next renewal or re‑certification of the homologation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations related to the homologation.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for when the record was initially loaded.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for the latest modification of the record.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory regime under which the homologation was granted (e.g., FMVSS, ECE, NCAP, CARB, EPA).']
  regulatory_version varchar [note: 'Specific version or amendment of the regulatory framework applied (e.g., FMVSS 123‑2022).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the homologation data (e.g., SAP PLM, Teamcenter).']
  vehicle_identification_number varchar [note: '17‑character VIN of the vehicle instance covered by the homologation.']

  Note: 'Tracks the regulatory type-approval and homologation status of vehicle configurations for each target market. Captures homologation type (whole vehicle type approval, component approval), regulatory framework (FMVSS, ECE, NCAP, CARB, EPA), approval authority, certificate number, approval date, expiry date, applicable model/MY/market, emissions standard tier (e.g., EPA Tier 3, Euro 6d), and compliance status. Critical for market launch readiness and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table vehicle.ecu_catalog {
  ecu_catalog_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each ECU catalog entry.']
  ecu_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecu_specification. Business justification: ECU catalog entries must link to their technical specification for validation, OTA updates, and safety certification.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: QUALITY & COMPLIANCE: ECU entries must reference their supplier for traceability, safety certification, and PPAP tracking.']
  applicable_trim_levels varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of trim level identifiers where the ECU is available.']
  applicable_vehicle_models varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of vehicle model codes that can be equipped with this ECU.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Primary in‑vehicle communication protocol used by the ECU.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ECU catalog record was first created.']
  cybersecurity_certification varchar [note: 'Certification identifier (e.g., SAE J3061) confirming the ECU meets cybersecurity requirements.']
  ecu_catalog_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the ECU, including key capabilities and notes.']
  ecu_code varchar [note: 'Unique OEM‑assigned code that identifies the ECU across the enterprise.']
  ecu_function varchar [note: 'Primary functional domain of the ECU (e.g., engine control, transmission control, ADAS, body control, infotainment, OBD gateway).']
  ecu_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the electronic control unit.']
  end_of_life_date date [note: 'Planned date after which the ECU will no longer be produced or supported.']
  functional_safety_asil varchar [note: 'Automotive Safety Integrity Level assigned to the ECU (A‑D).']
  hardware_part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number for the ECU hardware module.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ECU entry is deprecated and should not be used for new vehicle builds.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ECU within the product portfolio.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ECU catalog record.']
  ota_updatable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ECU firmware can be updated over‑the‑air.']
  power_consumption_watts decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical steady‑state power draw of the ECU in watts.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the ECU received regulatory approval.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the ECU.']
  software_baseline_version varchar [note: 'Baseline software version shipped with the ECU at start of production.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that supplied the ECU data (e.g., Teamcenter PLM, SAP, MES).']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external supplier that provides the ECU hardware.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Supplier‑assigned part number for the ECU hardware.']
  v2x_communication_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the ECU supports vehicle‑to‑everything communication.']
  voltage_volts decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage supplied to the ECU.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Standard warranty duration provided for the ECU.']
  weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Physical weight of the ECU unit.']

  Note: 'Catalog of all ECU (Electronic Control Unit) and embedded software modules installed across vehicle configurations. Captures ECU code, ECU name, ECU function (engine control, transmission control, ADAS domain controller, body control, infotainment, OBD gateway), supplier, hardware part number, software baseline version, OTA-updatable flag, communication protocol (CAN, LIN, Ethernet, AUTOSAR), and applicable vehicle configurations. Supports OTA update management, cybersecurity compliance, and aftersales diagnostics.'
}

Table vehicle.msrp_pricing {
  msrp_pricing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each MSRP pricing record.']
  aftersales_option_package_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.option_package']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle configuration that this MSRP applies to.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'FK to vehicle.model']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price amount.']
  destination_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard charge for vehicle delivery to the dealer.']
  destination_charge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a destination charge is included.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the MSRP expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the MSRP becomes effective.']
  ev_tax_credit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the eligible EV tax credit.']
  ev_tax_credit_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'True if the vehicle qualifies for the federal EV tax credit.']
  gas_guzzler_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional tax amount for low‑efficiency vehicles, if applicable.']
  gas_guzzler_tax_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gas guzzler tax applies to this configuration.']
  market_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the market.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region for which the MSRP applies.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year for which the MSRP is defined.']
  msrp_pricing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MSRP record.']
  option_package_code varchar [note: 'Code of the option package associated with the MSRP.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain technology of the vehicle (e.g., ICE, HEV, PHEV, EV).']
  price_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount for the price component, expressed in the specified currency.']
  price_key varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the MSRP entry, unique within market and model.']
  price_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label describing the MSRP entry (e.g., "2024 Model X Base").']
  price_notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or special conditions for the MSRP entry.']
  price_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the MSRP data.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price component (base MSRP, destination charge, tax, total).']
  price_uplift_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary increase applied due to selected option packages or market adjustments.']
  price_uplift_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the price uplift (e.g., "Premium Paint", "Advanced Safety Package").']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the MSRP record for change tracking.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MSRP record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the MSRP record.']
  trim_level varchar [note: 'Trim level designation for the vehicle configuration.']

  Note: 'Manages the MSRP (Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price) schedule for vehicle configurations, trim levels, and option packages by market and MY. Captures base MSRP, destination and delivery charge, gas guzzler tax (if applicable), federal EV tax credit eligibility, effective date, expiry date, currency, and market region. Provides the authoritative pricing reference for dealer ordering, window sticker generation, and sales analytics. Distinct from dealer invoice pricing (owned by billing domain).'
}

Table vehicle.lifecycle_event {
  lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each vehicle lifecycle event record.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Vehicle lifecycle events occurring at a dealership need a dealership_id FK to attribute service, warranty, and recall actions to the correct dealer.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for compliance reports: each lifecycle event must record the employee who logged/handled it, ensuring traceability and auditability.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Vehicle lifecycle events reference a VIN; linking to vin_registry provides a stable surrogate and removes the raw VIN column.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the event (e.g., production, logistics, sales, post‑sale, disposal). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production|logistics|sales|post_sale|disposal — promote to reference product]']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative describing the event details and context.']
  event_location_city varchar [note: 'City name where the event was recorded.']
  event_location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the event took place.']
  event_location_latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the event location in decimal degrees.']
  event_location_longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the event location in decimal degrees.']
  event_location_state varchar [note: 'State or province abbreviation for the event location, if applicable.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or internal notes attached to the event.']
  event_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of events for the same VIN, starting at 1.']
  event_source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or component within the source system that emitted the event.']
  event_source_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the source system instance that emitted the event (e.g., message ID, log ID).']
  event_source_system varchar [note: 'Higher‑level system or application that originated the event record.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Processing status of the event record within the data pipeline.']
  event_subtype varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the event category.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the lifecycle event actually occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of lifecycle transition (e.g., manufactured, pdi_completed, shipped_to_dealer, sold_retail, sold_fleet, exported, titled, re_acquired, scrapped, stolen, total_loss). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufactured|pdi_completed|shipped_to_dealer|sold_retail|sold_fleet|exported|titled|re_acquired|scrapped|stolen|total_loss — promote to reference product]']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event is considered critical for compliance or safety reporting.']
  odometer_reading_km varchar [note: 'Vehicle mileage at the time of the event, expressed in kilometers.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record.']
  triggering_system varchar [note: 'Name of the IT/OT system that generated the event (e.g., MES, DMS, Telematics Platform).']

  Note: 'Transactional log of significant lifecycle state transitions for a specific VIN throughout its life. Captures event type (manufactured, PDI-completed, shipped-to-dealer, sold-retail, sold-fleet, exported, titled, re-acquired, scrapped, stolen, total-loss), event timestamp, event location, responsible party, odometer reading at event, and triggering system. Provides the complete lifecycle audit trail for a vehicle unit from production to end-of-life, supporting title history, recall tracking, and residual value analytics.'
}

Table vehicle.vehicle_emissions_certification {
  vehicle_emissions_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle_emissions_certification data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.configuration. Business justification: Vehicle emissions certification pertains to a specific vehicle configuration; linking to configuration enables traceability and eliminates redundancy.']

  Note: 'Stores the official emissions test results and certification records for vehicle configurations submitted to regulatory bodies (EPA, CARB, Euro 6, WLTP). Captures test cycle type (FTP-75, HWFET, WLTP, RDE), CO2 g/km, NOx mg/km, PM mg/km, fuel economy (MPG/L/100km), electric range (km for EV/PHEV), combined CAFE contribution value, certification authority, certificate number, test date, and certification status. Directly supports CAFE compliance reporting and homologation.'
}

Table vehicle.vehicle_ota_deployment {
  vehicle_ota_deployment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ota_deployment association']
  ota_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the OTA campaign']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vehicle VIN registry']
  consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle owner consented to the OTA update']
  deployment_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deployment was initiated for this vehicle']
  download_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle started downloading the OTA package']
  install_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle began installing the OTA package']
  post_software_version varchar [note: 'Software version after successful OTA update']
  pre_software_version varchar [note: 'Software version installed on the vehicle before the OTA update']
  vehicle_ota_deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current deployment status (e.g., pending, in_progress, completed, failed)']

  Note: 'Association product representing the deployment of an OTA campaign to a specific vehicle. Each record captures the operational details of that deployment, linking one vin_registry record to one ota_campaign record.. Existence Justification: Each OTA campaign can be applied to many individual vehicles, and a single vehicle can receive multiple OTA campaigns over its lifecycle. The deployment of a campaign to a vehicle is recorded as a distinct operational event with its own status, timestamps, and version information, which is actively created and managed by the OTA management team.'
}

Table vehicle.ownership {
  ownership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the vehicle_ownership association']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to party']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vin_registry']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the party acquired the vehicle']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date the party disposed of the vehicle']
  ownership_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the ownership contract or agreement']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Type of ownership (e.g., lease, purchase, fleet)']

  Note: 'This association product represents the ownership relationship between a vehicle (vin_registry) and a party (customer). It captures acquisition and disposition dates, ownership type, and ownership number, tracking the history of which parties own which vehicles.. Existence Justification: A vehicle can be owned by multiple parties over its lifecycle (e.g., resale), and a party can own multiple vehicles simultaneously. The business actively records each ownership event with dates, type, and ownership number, and users query ownership history for warranty, service, recall, and resale analytics.'
}

Table vehicle.campaign_enrollment {
  campaign_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the vehicle_campaign_enrollment association']
  service_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service_campaign']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vin_registry']
  labor_time_hours decimal [note: 'Labor hours spent on the campaign for the vehicle']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle owner was notified about the campaign']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the notification for the vehicle regarding the campaign (e.g., pending, sent, acknowledged)']
  parts_consumed varchar [note: 'Number of parts used to fulfill the campaign for the vehicle']
  remedy_completion_date date [note: 'Date the remedial action was completed for the vehicle']
  scheduled_service_date date [note: 'Date the service for the campaign is scheduled for the vehicle']
  total_labor_cost_usd decimal [note: 'Total labor cost for the vehicle in the campaign']
  total_parts_cost_usd decimal [note: 'Total cost of parts used for the vehicle in the campaign']
  total_remedy_cost_usd decimal [note: 'Aggregate cost (parts + labor) for the vehicle\'s remedy']
  warranty_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates if the remedy is covered under warranty for the vehicle']

  Note: 'Association representing the enrollment of a vehicle (identified by VIN) into a service campaign. Captures enrollment status, dates, remedy completion, parts and labor costs, and warranty coverage for each vehicle‑campaign pair.. Existence Justification: Vehicles are enrolled in service campaigns; each campaign can affect many vehicles and a single vehicle can participate in multiple campaigns over its lifecycle. The enrollment is actively managed by aftersales operations and carries its own data such as notification dates, status, remedy completion, parts and labor costs, and warranty coverage.'
}

Table vehicle.regulatory_compliance_assignment {
  regulatory_compliance_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the RegulatoryComplianceAssignment association']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vehicle model']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory requirement']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the model for this requirement']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory requirement became effective for the model']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments about the compliance relationship']

  Note: 'This association captures the compliance relationship between a vehicle model and a regulatory requirement. It records the compliance status, the date the requirement became effective for the model, and any notes specific to that pairing.. Existence Justification: Each vehicle model must satisfy multiple regulatory requirements, and each regulatory requirement applies to many vehicle models. The compliance team actively maintains a matrix that records the compliance status, effective dates, and notes for every model‑requirement pair.'
}

Table product.bom_header {
  bom_header_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the BOM header record. Primary key for the commercial product-level BOM structure.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: Product BOM generation uses the Engineering BOM version; needed for the BOM Release Report and change control.']
  adas_level varchar [note: 'The SAE autonomy level supported by this BOM configuration. L0 = no automation; L1 = driver assistance; L2 = partial automation; L3 = conditional automation; L4 = high automation; L5 = full automation. Drives Electronic Control Unit (ECU) content and software Over-the-Air (OTA) update requirements.']
  alternative_bom_group varchar [note: 'Grouping identifier for alternative BOM configurations that serve the same functional purpose but differ in sourcing, supplier, or regional requirements. Supports supply chain flexibility and dual-sourcing strategies.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the product manager, engineering lead, or quality authority who approved this BOM for production release. Supports accountability and PPAP documentation requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this BOM was formally approved for production use. Milestone timestamp for traceability and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the BOM header quantity is expressed. Typically EA (each) for complete vehicle assemblies or KIT for bundled option packages.']
  bom_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the BOM header. Active BOMs are in production use; superseded BOMs have been replaced by newer revisions; obsolete BOMs are end-of-life; frozen BOMs are locked for regulatory or homologation purposes.']
  bom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM purpose. Commercial BOMs are product-domain owned structures for SKU definition and pricing; engineering BOMs are design structures owned by engineering domain; service BOMs support after-sales parts; manufacturing BOMs are plant-specific production structures.']
  bom_usage varchar [note: 'The intended application context for this BOM. Production BOMs are for series manufacturing; prototype BOMs support R&D; CKD (Completely Knocked Down) and SKD (Semi Knocked Down) BOMs are for export assembly operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production|prototype|pre_series|pilot|service|CKD|SKD — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_number varchar [note: 'The most recent engineering change order identifier that modified this BOM. Links to PLM change management workflows and supports traceability for Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) and corrective actions.']
  configuration_profile varchar [note: 'A coded representation of the option and feature set included in this BOM. Used for variant configuration in SAP SD, dealer ordering systems (DMS), and customer configurators.']
  connectivity_package varchar [note: 'The level of connected vehicle and telematics capability included in this BOM. Basic = 4G LTE; Premium = 5G with cloud services; V2X = Vehicle-to-Everything communication for smart city integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this BOM header record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance with ISO 9001 quality management documentation requirements.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this BOM version becomes valid and applicable for production, sales configuration, and order fulfillment. Aligns with model year start or mid-year product refresh timing.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this BOM version remains valid. Nullable for open-ended BOMs. Used to manage model year transitions, End of Production (EOP), and product phase-out scenarios.']
  emissions_standard varchar [note: 'The regulatory emissions certification standard that this BOM configuration meets. Determines legal salability in jurisdictions with emissions regulations (EPA, CARB, UNECE).']
  engineering_release_date date [note: 'The date on which the engineering design for this BOM was formally released from the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system (Teamcenter, Windchill) to manufacturing. Marks the transition from design to production readiness.']
  eop_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date when series production ceases for this BOM configuration. Triggers service parts planning, warranty reserve calculations, and dealer inventory clearance programs.']
  homologation_status varchar [note: 'The regulatory approval state for this BOM configuration. Certified BOMs have passed all required safety, emissions, and compliance testing (NCAP, FMVSS, WLTP). Required for legal sale and registration in target markets.']
  is_configurable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM supports customer-selectable options and variant configuration. True for BOMs with option packages; False for fixed-configuration BOMs. Drives dealer ordering system behavior and customer configurator logic.']
  lot_size_minimum varchar [note: 'The minimum production batch quantity for which this BOM is economically viable. Drives production scheduling, changeover planning, and capacity utilization in Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES).']
  market_region varchar [note: 'The geographic market or regulatory region for which this BOM is configured. Drives homologation requirements (UNECE, Euro NCAP, NHTSA), emissions standards (EPA, CARB, WLTP), and localized feature content. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAM|EUR|CHN|JPN|MEA|LATAM|APAC — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year designation for which this BOM is applicable. Critical for automotive product lifecycle management, regulatory compliance (CAFE, EPA ratings), and dealer ordering systems.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this BOM header record was last updated. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and synchronization with PLM and ERP systems.']
  msrp_base_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The baseline MSRP for this BOM configuration before options, packages, and destination charges. Used for dealer pricing, customer quotations, and competitive positioning. Currency is USD unless otherwise specified in market-specific price books.']
  nameplate_code varchar [note: 'The brand or model nameplate identifier (e.g., F-150, Camry, Model_S) that this BOM supports. Links to the commercial product catalog and marketing nomenclature.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or cross-references related to this BOM. May include links to Technical Service Bulletins (TSB), engineering memos, or supplier coordination notes.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The production plant or assembly facility where this BOM is applicable. Supports multi-plant manufacturing strategies, regional sourcing variations, and Just-in-Time (JIT) / Just-in-Sequence (JIS) supply chain execution.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'The propulsion system category for this BOM. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine; HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle; PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle; BEV = Battery Electric Vehicle; FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Drives component selection, regulatory compliance (EPA, CARB), and dealer training requirements.']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this BOM iteration. Incremented with each engineering change order (ECO) or product configuration update. Supports traceability for Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) and Advanced Product Quality Planning (APQP).']
  safety_certification_level varchar [note: 'The crash test rating achieved by this BOM configuration from New Car Assessment Programme (NCAP) testing. Influences marketing claims, insurance premiums, and regulatory compliance in target markets.']
  sop_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date when series production begins for this BOM configuration. Critical milestone for supply chain activation, dealer allocation planning, and launch readiness.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system of record for this BOM header. Identifies whether the BOM was created in SAP, PLM (Teamcenter, Windchill, ENOVIA), or manually entered. Supports data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  standard_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The planned manufacturing cost for this BOM at standard production volumes. Includes material, labor, and overhead. Used for margin analysis, make-vs-buy decisions, and financial planning. Currency is USD unless otherwise specified.']
  total_assembly_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The cumulative weight of all components in this BOM, expressed in kilograms. Critical for vehicle curb weight calculation, Corporate Average Fuel Economy (CAFE) compliance, and freight logistics planning.']
  total_component_count varchar [note: 'The total number of distinct component line items (BOM components) included in this BOM structure. Used for complexity assessment, supply chain planning, and Material Requirements Planning (MRP).']
  variant_code varchar [note: 'Product variant or trim level identifier (e.g., LX, Sport, Premium, AWD). Differentiates configurations within a nameplate for option package and feature matrix management.']
  vin_pattern varchar [note: 'The VIN prefix or pattern template associated with this BOM configuration. Positions 1-11 of the VIN encode manufacturer, plant, model, and body style. Supports VIN decoding and vehicle domain linkage.']
  warranty_program_code varchar [note: 'The standard warranty coverage program applicable to this BOM configuration. Links to warranty claim processing, service bulletin distribution, and Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) calculations.']

  Note: 'Product-level BOM (Bill of Materials) header record representing the commercial product structure for a given SKU or model year program. Captures BOM number, BOM type (commercial/engineering/service), effective date range, revision level, plant applicability, BOM status (active/superseded/obsolete), and total component count. This is the commercial BOM owned by the product domain — distinct from the engineering BOM managed in Teamcenter/Windchill, which is owned by the engineering domain.'
}

Table product.product_bom_line {
  product_bom_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product_bom_line data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  bom_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bom_header. Business justification: Product BOM line items belong to a BOM header; the FK creates the required parent‑child relationship.']

  Note: 'Individual component line items within a product BOM (Bill of Materials). Captures BOM line number, part number, part description, part type (raw material/sub-assembly/purchased component/fastener), quantity per vehicle, unit of measure, effective date range, plant applicability, alternative item group, scrap factor percentage, and PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) status. Enables product cost rollup, MRP (Material Requirements Planning) explosion, and supply chain sourcing alignment.'
}

Table product.product_segment {
  product_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle market segment. Primary key.']
  parent_segment_product_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent segment in the hierarchy. Null for top-level segments.']
  adas_level_range varchar [note: 'Range of SAE automation levels typically offered in this segment (e.g., L0-L2, L2-L3), following SAE J3016 standard.']
  annual_sales_volume_target varchar [note: 'Target annual sales volume across all nameplates in this segment, used for capacity planning and market share analysis.']
  body_style_category varchar [note: 'Primary body style category associated with this segment (e.g., sedan, SUV, pickup, van, coupe). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sedan|coupe|hatchback|suv|crossover|pickup|van|wagon|convertible|roadster|minivan|commercial — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cafe_fleet_category varchar [note: 'CAFE fleet category for fuel economy averaging and compliance (e.g., passenger car, light truck, medium-duty, heavy-duty, exempt).']
  cargo_volume_range_cu_ft varchar [note: 'Typical cargo volume range for vehicles in this segment, measured in cubic feet (e.g., 10-20, 30-50).']
  competitive_set_definition varchar [note: 'Description of the competitive set for this segment, listing key competitor nameplates and brands used for benchmarking and market share analysis.']
  connectivity_capability varchar [note: 'Typical connectivity capability level for vehicles in this segment (e.g., none, basic telematics, advanced connected services, V2X, full connected mobility).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this segment record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active segments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition became effective for market planning and reporting purposes.']
  emissions_standard_target varchar [note: 'Target emissions standard for vehicles in this segment (e.g., EPA Tier 3, Euro 6d, CARB LEV III, WLTP Class 3).']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the segment hierarchy tree (e.g., 1 for top-level categories like Passenger Vehicle, 2 for sub-categories like SUV, 3 for granular segments like Compact SUV).']
  homologation_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regions where this segment is homologated and available for sale (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, EUR, CHN, JPN, AUS).']
  industry_classification_code varchar [note: 'Additional industry-standard classification code from bodies such as UNECE, EPA, or regional automotive authorities.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is currently active and used in market planning, competitive analysis, and sales reporting.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the market segment (e.g., emerging, growth, mature, declining, discontinued).']
  market_positioning_tier varchar [note: 'Market positioning tier for competitive analysis (e.g., economy, mainstream, premium, luxury, performance, commercial).']
  market_share_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target market share percentage for this segment within the total addressable market.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this segment record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was last modified.']
  ncap_rating_applicability varchar [note: 'Applicable NCAP rating program for safety assessment in this segment (e.g., Euro NCAP, US NCAP, China NCAP, Latin NCAP, ASEAN NCAP).']
  ota_update_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicles in this segment typically support OTA software updates.']
  powertrain_category varchar [note: 'Primary powertrain type associated with this segment: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle), or Hybrid.']
  price_range_max_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for vehicles in this segment, in USD.']
  price_range_min_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for vehicles in this segment, in USD.']
  primary_use_case varchar [note: 'Primary intended use case for vehicles in this segment (e.g., personal commuting, family transportation, commercial delivery, fleet operations, performance driving, luxury travel, utility work). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: personal|family|commercial|fleet|performance|luxury|utility — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification category used for compliance reporting (e.g., passenger car, light truck, medium-duty vehicle, heavy-duty vehicle) as defined by NHTSA, EPA, or CARB.']
  sae_classification_code varchar [note: 'SAE International standard classification code for vehicle type and segment (e.g., J1979, J2735 vehicle class codes).']
  seating_capacity_range varchar [note: 'Typical seating capacity range for vehicles in this segment (e.g., 2-4, 5-7, 8-12).']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the market segment (e.g., COMP-CAR, MID-SUV, FULL-PKP).']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the market segment, including typical characteristics, target customer profile, and competitive positioning.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the vehicle market segment (e.g., Compact Car, Midsize SUV, Full-Size Pickup, Commercial Van, Luxury Sedan, Electric Crossover).']
  strategic_priority varchar [note: 'Strategic priority level assigned to this segment for investment and resource allocation decisions.']
  target_customer_profile varchar [note: 'Description of the primary target customer demographic and psychographic profile for this segment (e.g., young families, urban professionals, fleet operators).']

  Note: 'Reference classification of vehicle market segments used to position nameplates competitively (e.g., Compact Car, Midsize SUV, Full-Size Pickup, Commercial Van, Luxury Sedan, Electric Crossover). Captures segment code, segment name, segment hierarchy level, parent segment, SAE/industry classification code, competitive set definition, and applicable regulatory category. Used in market planning, competitive benchmarking, CAFE fleet averaging, and sales reporting.'
}

Table product.catalog_publication {
  catalog_publication_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the catalog publication event. Primary key.']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the catalog for publication. Supports governance and compliance requirements for catalog release authorization.']
  catalog_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific catalog version being published. Links to the catalog version master.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that executed the publication event. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  primary_catalog_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that executed the publication event. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  superseded_by_publication_catalog_publication_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer publication that replaces this one, if applicable. Supports publication version chain tracking.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the catalog publication was formally approved for release. Part of the publication lifecycle audit trail.']
  approved_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the approver for human-readable audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the publication record was first created in the system. Represents the start of the publication preparation process.']
  distribution_confirmation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target system has confirmed successful receipt and processing of the published catalog. True if confirmed, False if pending or failed.']
  distribution_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the target system confirmed successful receipt of the catalog publication. Nullable if confirmation is pending or not received.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'Technical method used to distribute the catalog: push (system-initiated send), pull (consumer-initiated retrieval), batch (scheduled bulk transfer), real-time (immediate synchronous), or scheduled (time-based automated).']
  distribution_retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the system attempted to distribute this catalog publication to the target system. Used for monitoring distribution reliability and troubleshooting failures.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the published catalog expires and is no longer valid for new orders or configurations. Nullable for open-ended publications.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the published catalog becomes active and valid for use in ordering, configuration, and sales processes. May differ from publication date to allow advance distribution.']
  file_checksum varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., MD5, SHA-256) of the published catalog file to ensure data integrity during transmission and detect corruption.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the file or data package containing the published catalog, if applicable. Used for file-based distribution tracking.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the published catalog file in bytes. Used for distribution monitoring and capacity planning.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code for the catalog content (e.g., en, fr, de, es, ja). Supports multilingual catalog distribution.']
  last_distribution_error varchar [note: 'Error message or code from the most recent failed distribution attempt, if applicable. Supports troubleshooting and root cause analysis.']
