  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the publication record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to publication metadata or status.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year (MY) of the vehicle catalog being published, representing the production year designation for the vehicles in this catalog (e.g., 2024, 2025).']
  nameplate_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct nameplates (vehicle model lines) included in this catalog publication. Supports publication scope tracking.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority of the publication: critical (urgent regulatory or safety update), high (important product launch), normal (routine catalog update), or low (minor correction).']
  publication_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the catalog was formally published and released to the target system or channel. Represents the actual distribution event timestamp.']
  publication_format varchar [note: 'Technical file or data format of the published catalog: Extensible Markup Language (XML), JavaScript Object Notation (JSON), Comma-Separated Values (CSV), Portable Document Format (PDF), Excel spreadsheet, Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) X12, EDIFACT, or proprietary format. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: xml|json|csv|pdf|excel|edi_x12|edifact|proprietary — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  publication_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the publication event, including special instructions, known issues, or distribution details.']
  publication_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the publication event, formatted as PUB-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX for external reference and audit trail.']
  publication_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the publication: draft (in preparation), approved (ready for release), published (actively distributed), recalled (withdrawn due to error), superseded (replaced by newer version), or archived (historical record).']
  publication_type varchar [note: 'Category of publication indicating the target audience: dealer ordering systems (DMS), consumer configurators, regulatory filings (NHTSA/EPA), internal sales tools, supplier portals, or fleet management systems.']
  published_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who published the catalog, for human-readable audit trail and reporting.']
  recall_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the catalog publication was recalled or withdrawn, if applicable. Captures business justification for publication reversal.']
  recall_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the catalog publication was recalled or withdrawn. Part of the publication lifecycle audit trail.']
  record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of catalog records (e.g., Stock Keeping Units (SKUs), configurations, options) included in this publication. Used for validation and reconciliation.']
  regulatory_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the catalog was submitted to regulatory authorities, if applicable. Supports compliance audit trail.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the associated regulatory filing submission, if applicable. Links catalog publication to compliance documentation.']
  regulatory_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this catalog publication requires submission to regulatory authorities (e.g., National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), California Air Resources Board (CARB)). True if regulatory filing is required.']
  target_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel or interface through which the catalog is published: Dealer Management System (DMS), web configurator, mobile application, Application Programming Interface (API), File Transfer Protocol (FTP), Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), regulatory portal, or dealer portal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dms|web_configurator|mobile_app|api|ftp|edi|regulatory_portal|dealer_portal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the specific country market of this publication (e.g., USA, CAN, DEU, JPN). Supports country-specific catalog requirements and regulatory compliance.']
  target_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market for which this catalog publication is intended (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific). Supports regional catalog variations and compliance requirements.']
  target_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the downstream system receiving this publication (e.g., CDK Global DMS, Salesforce Automotive Cloud, dealer portal, consumer configurator web application, NHTSA filing system).']
  trim_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct trim levels included in this catalog publication. Supports publication scope tracking and validation.']
  validation_error_count varchar [note: 'Number of validation errors detected during pre-publication quality checks. Zero indicates clean publication.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of pre-publication data quality validation checks: passed (all validations successful), failed (critical errors detected), warning (non-critical issues found), or not_validated (validation skipped).']
  validation_warning_count varchar [note: 'Number of validation warnings (non-critical issues) detected during pre-publication quality checks.']

  Note: 'Tracks the formal publication events of the commercial product catalog to downstream consumers — dealer ordering systems (DMS), consumer configurators, sales portals, and regulatory filings. Captures publication ID, catalog version, publication type (dealer/consumer/regulatory/internal), target system/channel, publication date, effective date range, published-by user, publication status (draft/approved/published/recalled), and distribution confirmation. Provides an audit trail of when and to whom catalog versions were released.'
}

Table product.package_availability {
  package_availability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the PackageAvailability association']
  aftersales_option_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the option package']
  catalog_publication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_publication. Business justification: Package availability records belong to a specific catalog publication; linking them enables traceability of which publication defines the availability status and launch date.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the dealership']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the package at the dealer (e.g., Available, Discontinued, Pending)']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the option package becomes available for sale at the dealer']

  Note: 'Associates an option package with a dealership, capturing the availability status and launch date for that package at the dealer. Each record links one option package to one dealership and stores attributes that belong only to this relationship.. Existence Justification: Dealerships actively manage which option packages they carry, setting an availability status and launch date for each package. Each option package can be offered by many dealerships, and each dealership can offer many option packages, creating a many‑to‑many relationship that carries its own attributes.'
}

Table product.pricing_condition_assignment {
  pricing_condition_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the PricingConditionAssignment association']
  billing_price_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the pricing condition']
  catalog_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_version. Business justification: Pricing condition assignments are defined per catalog version; linking them provides versioned pricing context for each assignment.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vehicle SKU']
  condition_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the condition assignment (e.g., active, expired)']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'Type of pricing condition (e.g., discount, tax, freight)']
  condition_value decimal [note: 'Monetary or percentage value of the pricing condition for this SKU']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the condition no longer applies to the SKU']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the condition becomes applicable to the SKU']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of a pricing condition to a specific vehicle SKU. Each record links one SKU to one pricing condition and captures attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment, such as the condition value and its validity period.. Existence Justification: A pricing condition (e.g., discount, tax, freight) can be applied to many vehicle SKUs, and each SKU can have multiple pricing conditions such as MSRP, discounts, taxes, and fees. The pricing team actively creates, updates, and deletes these assignments, and each assignment carries attributes like value, validity dates, type, and status. This operational management makes the relationship a true many‑to‑many business entity.'
}

Table product.catalog_version {
  catalog_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for catalog_version']
  previous_catalog_version_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on catalog_version (previous_catalog_version_id)']
  price_book_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price book associated with this catalog version.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the catalog version.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the catalog version.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the catalog version was approved.']
  catalog_type varchar [note: 'Category of catalog version indicating its purpose.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of major changes compared to the previous catalog version.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the catalog version record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for pricing in this catalog version.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the catalog version is superseded or expires.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the catalog version becomes effective for sales.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this version is the currently active catalog.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Primary market segment targeted by this catalog version.']
  region_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic regions where this catalog version is offered.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing changes, new features, or fixes in this version.']
  sku_structure_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU structure definition used for this catalog version.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the catalog version.']
  total_models varchar [note: 'Number of distinct vehicle models included in this catalog version.']
  total_options varchar [note: 'Number of option packages defined in this catalog version.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update to the catalog version record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the catalog version record.']
  version_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the catalog version.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable version identifier (e.g., \'2024Q1\', \'v2.3\').']
  version_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the catalog version data (e.g., PLM, ERP).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the catalog version record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for catalog_version. Referenced by catalog_version_id.'
}

Table engineering.vehicle_program {
  vehicle_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vehicle_program']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Program budgeting uses a Cost Center; finance cost center reports expenses per vehicle program.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the manager overseeing day‑to‑day execution.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Vehicle Program Management Report requires linking each engineering program to its product nameplate for schedule, cost, and compliance tracking.']
  ota_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.ota_campaign. Business justification: Enables OTA Campaign Planning reports linking each program to its scheduled OTA campaign for targeted firmware deployment.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Program profitability analysis assigns each vehicle program to a Profit Center for revenue and margin reporting.']
  bom_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the engineering Bill of Materials.']
  budget_allocation decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated to the program (currency defined by currency_code).']
  cad_release_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CAD data set released for the program.']
  cae_release_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CAE simulation package released for the program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle program record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for monetary values.']
  digital_twin_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a digital twin is maintained for the vehicle program.']
  drivetrain varchar [note: 'Drive configuration of the vehicle.']
  emission_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory emissions standard the vehicle must meet.']
  end_date date [note: 'Target date for End of Production (EOP) of the program.']
  engineering_change_order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of ECOs/ECNs issued for the program.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for market launch of the vehicle.']
  model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year covered by the program.']
  model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year covered by the program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, observations, or comments captured by engineering.']
  ota_update_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle will support Over‑The‑Air software updates.']
  platform_architecture varchar [note: 'Technical platform on which the vehicle is built (e.g., "Modular Electric Architecture").']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Primary propulsion technology of the vehicle.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used across systems to reference the program (e.g., "P1234").']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the vehicle program (e.g., "All‑New X5").']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the program as a nameplate, platform, or concept.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approvals for the program.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Market segment the vehicle belongs to (e.g., SUV, sedan).']
  start_date date [note: 'Target date for Start of Production (SOP) of the program.']
  target_cost_per_vehicle decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target average manufacturing cost per vehicle (currency defined by currency_code).']
  target_emissions_g_per_km decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target CO₂ emissions in grams per kilometer.']
  target_fuel_efficiency_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target fuel economy in miles per gallon for ICE/HEV variants.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Primary geographic market(s) for the vehicle (e.g., "North America").']
  target_production_volume varchar [note: 'Planned total number of vehicles to be produced for the program.']
  target_range_km varchar [note: 'Target all‑electric driving range in kilometers for EV variants.']
  target_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target curb weight of the vehicle in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the vehicle program record.']
  vehicle_class varchar [note: 'Regulatory vehicle class (e.g., "Passenger", "Light Commercial").']
  vehicle_program_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the program objectives and scope.']
  vehicle_program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the program.']

  Note: 'Master record for a vehicle development program (nameplate/platform program), capturing program code, program name, vehicle segment, platform architecture, SOP (Start of Production) target date, EOP (End of Production) date, MY (Model Year) scope, program phase (concept, development, validation, launch), program director, budget allocation, and program status. Serves as the top-level anchor for all engineering activities within a development cycle. Owned by Siemens Teamcenter PLM.'
}

Table engineering.bom {
  bom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the engineering bill of materials record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: BOM approval requires tracking approving employee for audit compliance.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change order linked to this BOM revision.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: BOM cost allocation is performed via a Cost Center; replace plain cost_center attribute with FK for accurate finance reporting.']
  engineering_change_order_change_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change order linked to this BOM revision.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Needed for BOM‑to‑inventory allocation process, enabling the production scheduling system to reserve SKUs for each BOM.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the BOM revision was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the BOM revision.']
  bom_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier that uniquely identifies the BOM within a program.']
  bom_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the BOM purpose and scope.']
  bom_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the engineering BOM.']
  bom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM: engineering (eBOM), manufacturing (mBOM), or service (sBOM).']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason documented for the latest BOM revision.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard to which the BOM complies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOM record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the BOM expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the BOM becomes effective for production.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the BOM is locked for further changes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent engineering review of the BOM.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the BOM record.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle for which the BOM is defined.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Engineering department responsible for the BOM.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the BOM will be used.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vehicle program (e.g., Model X) to which the BOM belongs.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Current release state of the BOM.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the BOM (e.g., A, B, C).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'PLM system where the BOM originates.']
  total_parts_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct part numbers referenced in the BOM.']
  total_quantity varchar [note: 'Sum of all component quantities across the BOM.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BOM record.']
  vehicle_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific vehicle variant (e.g., Sport, Luxury).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Internal version counter for the BOM record.']

  Note: 'Engineering Bill of Materials (eBOM) header record representing the complete structured parts list for a vehicle program or variant at a specific revision level. Captures BOM type (eBOM, mBOM, sBOM), program reference, model year, configuration context, effectivity dates, release status, BOM owner, and PLM system source. The eBOM is the authoritative design-intent parts structure managed in Siemens Teamcenter and ENOVIA before handoff to manufacturing BOM.'
}

Table engineering.engineering_bom_line {
  engineering_bom_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the engineering_bom_line data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: A line item belongs to a single engineering BOM; adding engineering_bom_id creates the required parent link.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: BOM line execution tracks which equipment assembles the part; needed for production scheduling and OEE reporting.']
  parent_assembly_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Parent assembly reference is a part_master; replace with descriptive FK to part_master.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: BOM line sourcing assignment; procurement uses this FK to generate purchase orders for each part line.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within an engineering BOM, representing a single part-to-parent relationship in the BOM hierarchy. Captures parent assembly reference, child part number, find number, quantity, unit of measure, effectivity start/end dates, variant applicability, substitution flags, BOM level, and engineering change reference. Supports multi-level BOM explosion and where-used analysis. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter BOM Management.'
}

Table engineering.part_master {
  part_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for part_master']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Part cost accounting charges material cost to a Cost Center; finance tracks part expenses per cost center.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer responsible for the part definition.']
  owning_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer responsible for the part definition.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Parts must be linked to applicable regulatory requirements for compliance tracking during part engineering.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Supplier Management Process: engineering part master must be linked to the external supplier record for PPAP, cost, and lead‑time tracking.']
  cad_model_reference varchar [note: 'Path or identifier of the CAD 3D model stored in the PLM system.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of the part in US dollars as defined in the ERP system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the part master record was first created in the system.']
  criticality varchar [note: 'Criticality of the part to vehicle safety or performance.']
  drawing_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the engineering drawing document for the part.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current part version became effective.']
  eol_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for part end‑of‑life (e.g., technology superseded, supplier discontinued).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the part is scheduled to be retired or become obsolete, if applicable.']
  height_mm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical height of the part in millimetres.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is currently active for use in new designs.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality inspection of the part.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the part master record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average supplier lead time in days for this part.']
  length_mm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical length of the part in millimetres.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the part within PLM.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the part (e.g., steel, aluminum, plastic).']
  obsolescence_notice boolean [note: 'True if an official obsolescence notice has been issued for the part.']
  part_classification varchar [note: 'High‑level functional classification of the part.']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the part\'s function, features, and application.']
  part_family varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of related parts sharing common characteristics.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Unique part number assigned by the organization, used to identify the part across systems.']
  part_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the part by its role in the product structure.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Quality rating based on internal quality metrics (e.g., PPAP status).']
  reach_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part complies with REACH chemical regulations (True=Compliant).']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Current revision identifier of the part. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|B|C|D|E|F|G|H|I|J — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rohs_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part complies with RoHS restrictions (True=Compliant).']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number used by the supplier for this component.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Numeric version of the part master record for internal version control.']
  volume_cm3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Calculated volume of the part in cubic centimetres.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the part in kilograms.']
  width_mm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical width of the part in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Engineering part master record representing a unique part or component managed within the PLM system. Captures part number, part name, part classification, material type, weight, dimensions, drawing number, CAD model reference, lifecycle state (in-work, released, obsolete), revision level, owning engineer, supplier part number, REACH/RoHS compliance flag, and part family. Authoritative source for engineering part identity in Siemens Teamcenter / PTC Windchill.'
}

Table engineering.design_specification {
  design_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the design specification record.']
  author_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Specification author must be recorded for traceability and regulatory audits.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory Requirement Mapping required for design approval; each design spec must reference the regulatory requirement it satisfies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the specification was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the specification.']
  approver varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the specification.']
  author varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or team that authored the specification.']
  change_order_date date [note: 'Date the change order was issued.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change order associated with this spec.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the specification.']
  component_name varchar [note: 'Specific component the specification describes.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Internal classification indicating data sensitivity.']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to implement the specification, in US dollars.']
  design_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the design lifecycle.']
  design_specification_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the design intent and scope.']
  dimensions_mm varchar [note: 'Physical dimensions expressed as LxWxH in millimetres.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the specification document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the specification becomes effective for design work.']
  engineering_department varchar [note: 'Department responsible for the specification (e.g., Powertrain, Chassis).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the specification is no longer valid.']
  interface_name varchar [note: 'Interface name when the spec defines an interaction point between components.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specification is currently active.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Stage of the product lifecycle where the spec is applied.']
  linked_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of requirement IDs that this specification satisfies.']
  material_specification varchar [note: 'Material(s) and grades specified for the component.']
  obsolescence_date date [note: 'Planned date when the specification will be retired.']
  program varchar [note: 'Name of the vehicle program (e.g., Model X, MY2024) to which the specification belongs.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory standards referenced (e.g., FMVSS 123, UNECE R123).']
  related_documents varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of other design specification IDs related to this one.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date the specification was released for use.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date the current revision was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision identifier (e.g., A, B, C).']
  spec_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the specification, e.g., DS-2023-001.']
  spec_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the specification scope.']
  subsystem_name varchar [note: 'Name of the subsystem within the system, if applicable.']
  system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vehicle system covered (e.g., Powertrain, Chassis).']
  target_performance_units varchar [note: 'Units for the target performance value (e.g., Nm, kW).']
  target_performance_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric target performance metric (e.g., torque, efficiency).']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Primary analysis or test method used to validate the spec.']
  test_result_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of test outcomes.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title describing the purpose of the specification.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version string for the specification (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target weight of the component or system in kilograms.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the record.']

  Note: 'Engineering design specification document record capturing the technical requirements and design intent for a vehicle system, subsystem, or component. Includes specification number, title, specification type (system, subsystem, component, interface), applicable program, revision history, author, approval status, linked requirements, target performance values, and regulatory references (FMVSS, UNECE, NCAP). Managed in Teamcenter or ENOVIA document management.'
}

Table engineering.cad_model {
  cad_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each CAD model record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the engineer responsible for the model.']
  primary_cad_employee_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the engineer responsible for the model.']
  assembly_name varchar [note: 'Name of the parent assembly if the model is a sub‑component.']
  cad_model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the CAD model as shown in the engineering portal.']
  cad_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the model within the PLM system.']
  compliance_iso26262_level varchar [note: 'Functional safety classification of the component according to ISO 26262.']
  configuration_context varchar [note: 'Contextual configuration (e.g., default, sport, luxury) for which the model variant is defined.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Reference coordinate system (e.g., right‑hand, left‑hand) applied to the model.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the CAD model record was first created in the system.']
  design_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the design.']
  design_change_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent engineering change was applied.']
  design_change_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change order (ECO/ECN) that last modified the model.']
  design_release_date date [note: 'Official date when the design was released for manufacturing.']
  digital_twin_ready boolean [note: 'True if the model meets criteria for inclusion in a digital twin.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'File name of the CAD geometry (including extension).']
  file_path varchar [note: 'Repository location or network path where the CAD file is stored.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the CAD file in bytes; used for storage planning and transfer cost estimation.']
  geometry_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for the model\'s geometry coordinates.']
  is_digital_mockup_included boolean [note: 'True if the model is part of a Digital Mock‑Up (DMU) package.']
  last_published_date date [note: 'Date when the model was last released to downstream teams or systems.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Detailed stage of the model from creation to retirement.']
  maturity_level varchar [note: 'Indicates how mature the model is within the product development lifecycle.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the CAD model; often mirrors the part number used in downstream processes.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CAD geometry type.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks entered by the designer.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'OEM part number that the CAD model represents; links the digital geometry to the physical component.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory sign‑off (e.g., NHTSA, EPA) for the model.']
  related_vehicle_variant varchar [note: 'Specific vehicle variant (e.g., sport, hybrid) that the model supports.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier (e.g., A, B, C) used for minor changes within a version.']
  simulation_ready boolean [note: 'True if the model is prepared for CAE simulations (e.g., meshed, cleaned).']
  tool_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CAD application (e.g., CATIA V5R30) used to author the model.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Full name of the engineer who performed the latest edit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the CAD model record.']
  vehicle_model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle platform that uses this CAD model.']
  vehicle_platform varchar [note: 'Platform identifier (e.g., MQB, CMF) associated with the model.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label of the CAD model (e.g., V1.0, V2.1).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Full name of the engineer who initially created the CAD model.']

  Note: 'CAD (Computer-Aided Design) model record representing a 3D digital geometry asset created in CATIA or equivalent tool. Captures model file reference, part or assembly association, CAD tool version, coordinate system, model maturity level, digital mock-up (DMU) inclusion flag, last published date, file size, configuration context, and owning designer. Supports digital twin construction and downstream CAE simulation setup. Sourced from Dassault Systèmes CATIA and ENOVIA.'
}

Table engineering.change {
  change_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the engineering change record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or group that approved the change.']
  approver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or group that approved the change.']
  change_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or group that originated the change request.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or group that originated the change request.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: ECO process requires identifying which production equipment must be reconfigured; linking change to equipment enables change impact analysis report.']
  tooling_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.tooling_registry. Business justification: Tooling impact of an engineering change must be tracked for re‑tooling schedules and cost estimation.']
  affected_parts varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers (e.g., VIN, SKU) impacted by the change.']
  affected_programs varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of vehicle programs impacted by the change.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change was formally approved.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the purpose and scope of the change.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the change request (e.g., EC2023001234).']
  change_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the change affecting a part, assembly, specification, or process.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the engineering change.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change request: Engineering Change Request (ECR), Engineering Change Order (ECO), or Engineering Change Notice (ECN).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the change record was closed after implementation verification.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change is driven by regulatory or standards compliance.']
  cost_adjustments decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost adjustments (e.g., tooling, re‑work) associated with the change.']
  cost_estimate_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial estimated total cost of the change before any adjustments.']
  cost_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final net cost after applying adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the engineering change record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost estimates.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the change becomes effective in production or documentation.']
  impact_analysis varchar [note: 'Summary of technical and cost impact analysis performed for the change.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Target calendar date for implementing the approved change on the product.']
  origin varchar [note: 'Source of the change request: internal, supplier, or customer.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the change (high, medium, low).']
  reason_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing why the change is initiated.']
  reason_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional context for the change reason.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change request was initially submitted.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for the engineering change document.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Detailed risk assessment narrative for the change.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level of the change to product quality, schedule, or cost.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the engineering change.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the engineering change record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the change record, incremented on each revision.']

  Note: 'Engineering Change Order/Notice (ECO/ECN) record capturing a formal request to modify a part, assembly, design specification, or BOM. Includes change number, change type (ECR/ECO/ECN), title, reason for change (cost reduction, quality, regulatory, customer request), affected parts list, affected programs, initiator, approver chain, priority, implementation date, cost impact estimate, and change status (draft, under review, approved, implemented, rejected). Managed in Siemens Teamcenter Change Management.'
}

Table engineering.cae_simulation {
  cae_simulation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the CAE simulation record.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering analyst who initiated or owned the simulation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering analyst who initiated or owned the simulation.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part or assembly that the simulation analyzes.']
  part_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part or assembly that the simulation analyzes.']
  analyst_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the analyst responsible for the simulation.']
  boundary_conditions varchar [note: 'Structured description of boundary conditions (e.g., constraints, supports) used in the simulation.']
  convergence_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria used to determine simulation convergence (e.g., residual threshold).']
  cpu_time_seconds decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total CPU processing time consumed by the simulation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the simulation record was first created in the system.']
  load_case varchar [note: 'Description of the load case applied in the simulation.']
  memory_usage_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Peak memory usage of the simulation process in megabytes.']
  mesh_element_count varchar [note: 'Total number of finite elements in the simulation mesh.']
  mesh_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality rating of the mesh (higher is better).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the analyst.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the part or assembly.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Catalog or engineering part number of the simulated component.']
  result_metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary result metric produced by the simulation (e.g., Max Stress).']
  result_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the primary result metric.']
  result_metric_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value of the primary result metric.']
  result_outcome varchar [note: 'Pass/fail determination of the simulation against target criteria.']
  run_duration_seconds decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total wall‑clock time of the simulation run in seconds.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the simulation execution started.']
  simulation_number varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned to the simulation run, used for tracking across systems.']
  simulation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the simulation.']
  simulation_type varchar [note: 'Category of analysis performed (e.g., Finite Element Analysis, Computational Fluid Dynamics).']
  solver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the computational solver used for the simulation.']
  target_metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the target metric the simulation is evaluated against.']
  target_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target metric.']
  target_metric_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric target value for the primary metric.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the simulation record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the simulation setup (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'CAE (Computer-Aided Engineering) simulation record capturing a virtual analysis run performed on a vehicle component or system. Includes simulation ID, simulation type (FEA — Finite Element Analysis, CFD — Computational Fluid Dynamics, NVH — Noise Vibration Harshness, crash, thermal, fatigue), associated part or assembly, solver used, load cases, boundary conditions, mesh parameters, run date, analyst, result summary (pass/fail against targets), and linked design specification. Supports virtual validation before physical prototype build.'
}

Table engineering.prototype_build {
  prototype_build_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the prototype build record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer accountable for the build.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.functional_location. Business justification: Prototype builds are scheduled at specific plant locations; linking enables location‑based resource planning and cost allocation.']
  responsible_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer accountable for the build.']
  build_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to produce the prototype build.']
  build_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the prototype was physically built.']
  build_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the build process expressed in hours.']
  build_location varchar [note: 'Plant or facility where the prototype build took place.']
  build_number varchar [note: 'Internal sequential number assigned to each prototype build.']
  build_phase varchar [note: 'Stage of the prototype build lifecycle.']
  build_purpose varchar [note: 'Primary testing or validation purpose of the prototype build.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory and quality compliance status of the prototype build.']
  configuration_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the prototype\'s configuration and options.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the build record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the build cost.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the build data.']
  emission_rating varchar [note: 'Emission classification assigned to the prototype based on testing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments captured by engineering staff.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the vehicle program or project associated with the prototype.']
  prototype_build_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prototype build.']
  prototype_number varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the prototype unit or component.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety assessment rating for the prototype.']
  test_results_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of test outcomes associated with the prototype build.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the build record.']
  vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier assigned to the prototype vehicle.']

  Note: 'Prototype vehicle or component build record capturing the physical build event for validation purposes. Includes build number, build phase (mule, alpha, beta, pre-production), program reference, build date, build location, VIN or prototype identifier, configuration description, build purpose (durability, crash, NVH, emissions, homologation), responsible engineer, and build status. Tracks the physical instantiation of engineering designs for testing and validation activities.'
}

Table engineering.validation_test {
  validation_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the validation test record.']
  test_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Test engineer must be linked to validation test for traceability and regulatory reporting.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the test was formally approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the test.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the test engineer.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory standard evaluated by the test.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test record was created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that captured the test data (e.g., Teamcenter, MES).']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Disposition decision based on test result.']
  emission_co2_g_per_km decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured carbon dioxide emission per kilometer.']
  engineering_test_result_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the detailed test report document.']
  equipment_used varchar [note: 'List of major equipment or rigs employed during the test.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test is classified as critical for safety or compliance.']
  noise_db decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured acoustic noise level in decibels.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the test against applicable regulations.']
  target_emission_co2 decimal(12,4) [note: 'Regulatory or design target for CO2 emission.']
  target_noise_db decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target acoustic noise level for compliance.']
  target_torque_nm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target torque specification for the test.']
  test_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval state after review of test results.']
  test_batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier linking the test to a production or prototype batch.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'High‑level engineering domain of the test.']
  test_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation (e.g., test plan PDF).']
  test_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the test execution in minutes.']
  test_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer responsible for conducting the test.']
  test_facility varchar [note: 'Name of the facility or lab where the test was performed.']
  test_location varchar [note: 'Code identifying the physical location or plant where the test took place.']
  test_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the validation test.']
  test_phase varchar [note: 'Development phase during which the test was performed.']
  test_report_url varchar [note: 'Web address or path to the stored test report.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the test execution.']
  test_result_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result was entered into the system.']
  test_revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision number of the test procedure or setup.']
  test_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or document identifier for the standard governing the test.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test.']
  test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test was executed.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Category of the validation test according to engineering verification plans.']
  test_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the test procedure.']
  torque_nm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured torque value in newton‑meters.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the test record.']
  variance_percent decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between measured and target values.']

  Note: 'Engineering validation test record capturing a physical or virtual test event performed on a prototype or production-intent part/vehicle. Includes test ID, test type (DVP — Design Verification Plan, PVP — Process Validation Plan, PPAP, durability, emissions, NCAP/WLTP, FMVSS), test name, test standard reference, test facility, test date, test engineer, test result (pass/fail/conditional), measured values vs. targets, and disposition. Supports DVP&R (Design Verification Plan and Report) tracking.'
}

Table engineering.engineering_test_result {
  engineering_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the engineering_test_result data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  validation_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.validation_test. Business justification: Test results belong to a validation test; linking enables traceability.']

  Note: 'Detailed test result record capturing individual measurement data points from a validation test event. Includes result ID, parent test reference, measurement parameter name, unit of measure, measured value, target value, tolerance band (min/max), pass/fail status, test condition description, channel or sensor ID, timestamp of measurement, and data file reference. Enables granular traceability of test outcomes against engineering targets and regulatory thresholds.'
}

Table engineering.digital_twin {
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the digital twin record.']
  digital_twin_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for digital_twin']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.functional_location. Business justification: Digital twin of a component is tied to the physical plant location for synchronization and maintenance planning.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical part associated with the twin.']
  primary_digital_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical part associated with the twin.']
  prototype_build_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prototype unit used for validation of the twin.']
  prototype_prototype_build_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prototype unit used for validation of the twin.']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the engineering team.']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: OTA update process requires linking a digital twin to the specific owned vehicle record for update history and compliance tracking.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering program linked to the digital twin.']
  asset_vin varchar [note: 'VIN of the physical vehicle linked to the digital twin, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the digital twin record was first created.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall quality rating of the twin\'s data (0‑100).']
  digital_twin_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the digital twin.']
  digital_twin_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the digital twin.']
  fidelity_level varchar [note: 'Granularity of the digital twin model (e.g., high‑fidelity CFD vs. low‑fidelity schematic).']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful sync operation.']
  model_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the digital twin\'s purpose and scope.']
  model_file_path varchar [note: 'File system or object storage path to the model artifact.']
  model_storage_location varchar [note: 'Logical storage location descriptor (e.g., Data Lake, Git repo).']
  performance_metric decimal(10,4) [note: 'Key performance indicator derived from simulation (e.g., drag coefficient).']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance state with relevant automotive regulations (e.g., NHTSA, EPA).']
  simulation_model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the simulation model used for the digital twin.']
  simulation_tool varchar [note: 'Software tool used to generate the simulation model.']
  sync_status varchar [note: 'Current synchronization state between the virtual model and physical asset data.']
  twin_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the digital twin within engineering systems.']
  twin_type varchar [note: 'Category of the digital twin indicating the physical scope it represents.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User name or identifier that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the digital twin record.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest validation run against physical test data.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User name or identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Digital twin model record representing the virtual counterpart of a physical vehicle, system, or component. Captures twin ID, twin type (vehicle-level, powertrain, chassis, body), associated program and part references, simulation model version, real-world asset linkage (VIN or prototype ID), synchronization status, last updated timestamp, fidelity level, and owning engineering team. Enables continuous correlation between virtual models and physical test/field data for predictive engineering.'
}

Table engineering.milestone {
  milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the engineering milestone record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person responsible for delivering the milestone.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.functional_location. Business justification: Milestones (e.g., design freeze) occur at a plant location; linking supports location‑based milestone dashboards.']
  milestone_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  milestone_sign_off_authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authority who signed off the milestone.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  primary_milestone_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person responsible for delivering the milestone.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering program to which this milestone belongs.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date when the milestone was achieved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone was approved during gate review.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for any change to milestone dates or status.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable compliance standard(s) for the milestone (e.g., ISO 26262, IATF 16949).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was created.']
  gate_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the gate review for the milestone.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone is deemed critical for program success.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Short code representing the milestone phase.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the milestone purpose, scope, and deliverables.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the milestone (e.g., P0 Concept Freeze, P1 Design Freeze).']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Category of the milestone within the product development process.']
  open_action_items_count varchar [note: 'Number of unresolved action items after the gate review.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for milestone completion.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or site associated with the milestone.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the milestone.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the milestone record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the milestone record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Engineering program milestone record capturing key gate reviews and decision points in the vehicle development process. Includes milestone ID, milestone name (P0 concept freeze, P1 design freeze, P2 prototype release, P3 pilot build, SOP), program reference, planned date, actual date, milestone owner, gate review outcome (approved, conditional, deferred), open action items count, and sign-off authority. Supports APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) phase gate management.'
}

Table engineering.fmea_record {
  fmea_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the FMEA record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer or manager who approved the FMEA analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer or responsible person assigned to implement the recommended action.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: FMEA analysis often references the equipment where failure mode is observed; linking enables root‑cause tracking and preventive maintenance.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the part (or component) that the FMEA analyzes.']
  part_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the part (or component) that the FMEA analyzes.']
  primary_fmea_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer or responsible person assigned to implement the recommended action.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the recommended action was actually completed.']
  analysis_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the cross‑functional team that performed the FMEA.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the FMEA record received formal approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FMEA record was first created in the system.']
  current_controls varchar [note: 'Existing controls or safeguards that address the failure mode.']
  detection_rating varchar [note: 'Detection score (1‑10) representing the ability of current controls to detect the failure before it reaches the customer.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed FMEA PDF or digital twin document stored in the repository.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the FMEA analysis is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the FMEA analysis is considered active.']
  failure_effect varchar [note: 'Potential effect on the vehicle or process if the failure mode occurs.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Textual description of the way the part or process could fail.']
  fmea_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the FMEA record, often used in reports and traceability.']
  fmea_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FMEA record.']
  fmea_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the analysis is a Design FMEA (DFMEA) or Process FMEA (PFMEA).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the FMEA.']
  occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Occurrence score (1‑10) indicating how likely the failure mode is to happen.']
  process_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing process step when the FMEA is a PFMEA.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Proposed corrective or preventive action to reduce risk.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the FMEA record.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High‑level risk classification derived from the RPN.']
  rpn varchar [note: 'Calculated risk priority number (S × O × D) used to prioritize mitigation actions.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Severity score (1‑10) reflecting the seriousness of the failure effect.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source application (e.g., Teamcenter, ENOVIA) that originated the record.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the recommended action should be completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the FMEA record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric version label (e.g., v1.0, v2.1) for the FMEA record.']

  Note: 'FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) record capturing the systematic risk analysis of a design or process. Includes FMEA ID, FMEA type (DFMEA — Design FMEA, PFMEA — Process FMEA), associated part or process, failure mode description, potential effect of failure, severity rating, potential cause, occurrence rating, current controls, detection rating, RPN (Risk Priority Number), recommended actions, responsible engineer, and completion date. Managed per IATF 16949 and AIAG-VDA FMEA methodology.'
}

Table engineering.dvp_plan {
  dvp_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the Design Verification Plan.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change order that triggered the plan.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the plan record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer accountable for the plan execution.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part or component covered by the DVP.']
  part_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part or component covered by the DVP.']
  primary_dvp_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer accountable for the plan execution.']
  related_design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering specification linked to the plan.']
  related_specification_design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering specification linked to the plan.']
  tertiary_dvp_updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the latest update.']
  updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the latest update.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle program to which the plan belongs.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the last test in the plan was completed.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the plan received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the verification plan.']
  completed_test_count varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that have been completed.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of tests completed (0‑100).']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to execute the verification plan, expressed in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan record was created.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Web link or repository path to the full DVP document.']
  dvp_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the verification plan.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the plan is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes active for execution.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test procedures are automated.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is locked from further edits.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the plan.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the plan status.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle variant the plan applies to.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments about the verification plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible alphanumeric code used to reference the DVP plan.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope and objectives of the plan.']
  plan_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the verification plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plan indicating the level of the product it validates.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for completing all tests in the plan.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the plan.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory sign‑off is required for the plan.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the plan.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the verification plan.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the record (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM).']
  system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle system (e.g., powertrain, ADAS) the plan validates.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual environment where testing occurs.']
  test_phase varchar [note: 'Lifecycle phase during which the tests are performed.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the tests in the plan.']
  total_test_count varchar [note: 'Total number of test cases defined in the plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plan record.']
  vehicle_variant varchar [note: 'Specific vehicle variant or trim level associated with the plan.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for the plan (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']

  Note: 'Design Verification Plan (DVP) record defining the complete test plan for validating a vehicle system or component against its design specifications. Captures DVP ID, plan title, associated part or system, program reference, total test count, completion percentage, responsible engineer, approval status, linked specification, and planned vs. actual completion dates. Acts as the master test planning document that governs all validation_test records for a given scope.'
}

Table engineering.homologation_requirement {
  homologation_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the homologation requirement record.']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Homologation requirement must reference the homologation record that documents market approval for that requirement.']
  primary_homologation_vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle program to which this requirement belongs.']
  compliance_method varchar [note: 'How compliance is demonstrated: test, calculation, or declaration.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the requirement\'s compliance lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the homologation requirement record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the requirement becomes legally effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the requirement is no longer applicable, if applicable.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requirement is mandatory (true) or optional (false).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the requirement was last reviewed for relevance or changes.']
  linked_validation_test_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of validation test identifiers that satisfy this requirement.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country/region code where the requirement applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|DEU|JPN|CHN|AUS|GBR — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the requirement.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the requirement for planning purposes.']
  regulation_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory standard governing the requirement, e.g., FMVSS, ECE_R, CARB.']
  regulation_number varchar [note: 'Official number or identifier of the regulation (e.g., FMVSS 123).']
  requirement_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the requirement by the engineering organization.']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of what the regulation mandates.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record (e.g., SAP, Teamcenter, ENOVIA).']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Latest date by which evidence of compliance must be submitted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the requirement record.']
  vehicle_model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle to which the requirement is tied.']
  vehicle_variant varchar [note: 'Specific variant or trim level of the vehicle (e.g., sport, hybrid).']

  Note: 'Homologation and regulatory requirement record capturing the specific regulatory standards and type-approval requirements that a vehicle or component must satisfy for a target market. Includes requirement ID, regulation name (FMVSS, ECE-R, CARB, Euro NCAP, WLTP, EPA), regulation number, market/region applicability, requirement description, compliance method (test, calculation, declaration), linked validation tests, compliance status, and submission deadline. Supports regulatory compliance and homologation engineering activities.'
}

Table engineering.ecu_specification {
  ecu_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ecu_specification']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: ECU sourcing workflow requires each ECU spec to reference the approved supplier for compliance and warranty management.']
  applicable_model_years varchar [note: 'Model year range for which the ECU specification is valid (e.g., 2023‑2025).']
  applicable_vehicle_variants varchar [note: 'Vehicle variants or trims that use this ECU.']
  asw_release_date date [note: 'Date of the ASW release.']
  asw_release_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the automotive software (ASW) release.']
  calibration_dataset_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the calibration data set used by the ECU.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Primary vehicle network protocol used by the ECU.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality standard applicable to the ECU.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ECU specification record was created.']
  diagnostic_trouble_code_support varchar [note: 'List or description of DTCs supported by the ECU.']
  dimensions_mm varchar [note: 'Physical dimensions (L×W×H) in millimetres.']
  ecu_family varchar [note: 'Higher‑level family grouping for related ECUs.']
  ecu_specification_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the ECU functionality and purpose.']
  ecu_specification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the electronic control unit.']
  ecu_specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ECU specification.']
  ecu_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ECU function (e.g., engine control, ADAS).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the ECU specification is retired or superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the ECU specification becomes effective.']
  eol_date date [note: 'Planned date when the ECU will be discontinued.']
  functional_safety_asil varchar [note: 'Automotive Safety Integrity Level per ISO 26262.']
  hardware_part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number for the ECU hardware.']
  hardware_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for the ECU hardware.']
  hardware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the ECU hardware revision.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ECU is safety‑critical.']
  max_operating_temperature_c varchar [note: 'Maximum ambient temperature at which the ECU can operate safely.']
  memory_size_mb varchar [note: 'On‑board memory capacity in megabytes.']
  min_operating_temperature_c varchar [note: 'Minimum ambient temperature at which the ECU can operate safely.']
  power_consumption_w decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical power consumption in watts.']
  processing_speed_mhz varchar [note: 'CPU processing speed in megahertz.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory approval for the ECU (e.g., NHTSA, EPA).']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Current release state of the ECU specification.']
  software_release_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing changes in the software release.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the ECU software (e.g., v1.2.3).']
  supported_features varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of functional features provided by the ECU.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ECU specification.']
  vehicle_platform varchar [note: 'Vehicle platform or architecture that the ECU supports (e.g., MQB, e‑CM).']
  voltage_range_v varchar [note: 'Operating voltage range expressed as min‑max volts.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical weight of the ECU unit.']

  Note: 'ECU (Electronic Control Unit) software and hardware specification record capturing the technical definition of an automotive electronic control module. Includes ECU ID, ECU name, ECU type (engine control, transmission, ADAS, body control, battery management), hardware part number, software version, calibration dataset reference, communication protocol (CAN, LIN, Ethernet, FlexRay), functional safety level (ASIL rating per ISO 26262), supplier reference, and release status. Critical for EV, HEV, and ADAS program engineering.'
}

Table engineering.powertrain_spec {
  powertrain_spec_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the powertrain specification record.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Powertrain specifications are defined per vehicle program; linking enables program-level aggregation and reporting.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the specification received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the authority who approved the specification.']
  architecture_type varchar [note: 'Physical layout of the engine (inline, V, boxer, etc.).']
  aspiration_type varchar [note: 'Method of air induction for the engine.']
  battery_capacity_kwh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Energy storage capacity for electrified powertrains; null for ICE/HEV.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory compliance state of the specification.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the cost estimate.']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated manufacturing cost for the powertrain.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was created.']
  cylinder_count varchar [note: 'Number of cylinders for internal combustion engines.']
  dimensions_mm varchar [note: 'Physical envelope of the powertrain expressed as LxWxH in millimetres.']
  displacement_cc varchar [note: 'Engine cylinder displacement for internal combustion variants.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the specification is no longer effective.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the specification is considered effective.']
  emission_control_technology varchar [note: 'Technology used to reduce exhaust emissions (e.g., catalytic converter, DPF, SCR).']
  emissions_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory emissions standard the powertrain meets.']
  end_of_production_date date [note: 'Date when the powertrain ceased production.']
  epa_range_miles varchar [note: 'Estimated driving range under EPA test cycle.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary fuel or energy source for the powertrain.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specification is locked from further edits.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent engineering review.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year the specification applies to.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or engineering notes.']
  power_output_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Peak power rating of the powertrain.']
  powertrain_spec_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the powertrain technology.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record (e.g., Teamcenter, CATIA).']
  spec_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the specification across systems.']
  spec_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the powertrain specification.']
  start_of_production_date date [note: 'Date when the powertrain entered series production.']
  target_program_code varchar [note: 'Program identifier for which this specification is intended.']
  thermal_management varchar [note: 'Cooling/heating strategy used for the powertrain.']
