  torque_nm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Peak torque rating of the powertrain.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Transmission architecture used with the powertrain.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification.']
  vehicle_variant varchar [note: 'Specific vehicle variant (e.g., trim level) the spec supports.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the specification revision.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mass of the powertrain assembly.']
  wltp_range_km varchar [note: 'Estimated driving range under WLTP test cycle.']

  Note: 'Powertrain engineering specification record capturing the technical definition of an ICE, HEV, PHEV, or EV powertrain system. Includes spec ID, powertrain type (ICE, HEV, PHEV, BEV, FCEV), engine or motor displacement/power rating, torque output, transmission type, battery capacity (kWh) for electrified variants, fuel type, emissions standard compliance (Euro 6, EPA Tier 3, CARB LEV III), WLTP/EPA range rating, thermal management approach, and target program applicability. Supports R&D and powertrain engineering teams.'
}

Table engineering.weight_report {
  weight_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the weight report record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer accountable for the weight report.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Weight reports are generated by weighing equipment; linking allows equipment calibration tracking and compliance audit.']
  primary_weight_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer accountable for the weight report.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Weight reports are generated for a specific vehicle program; linking provides program aggregation.']
  actual_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical measurement of the component weight obtained during testing, in kilograms.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the report was approved, if applicable.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations related to the weight report.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards compliance indicator relevant to the weight data.']
  component_scope varchar [note: 'Specific component or assembly within the system that the weight pertains to.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the weight report record was first created.']
  estimated_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current engineering estimate of the component weight, in kilograms.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the report is locked from further edits.']
  reduction_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed engineering action intended to reduce the component weight.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory approval for the weight report.']
  report_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the weight report.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the weight report for external reference.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the weight report.']
  reporting_date date [note: 'Date on which the weight data was reported.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the weight data.']
  system_scope varchar [note: 'Engineering system or vehicle subsystem the weight measurement applies to (e.g., chassis, powertrain).']
  target_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target mass budget allocation for the component, expressed in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the weight report.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the report for change tracking.']
  weight_delta_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference between actual weight and target weight (actual – target), expressed in kilograms.']
  weight_delta_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weight delta expressed as a percentage of the target weight.']
  weight_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the weight data, such as simulation, prototype measurement, or supplier data.']
  weight_target_category varchar [note: 'Category of the weight target (e.g., mass budget, component limit).']

  Note: 'Vehicle or component weight tracking record capturing mass budget allocations and actual measured weights throughout the development program. Includes report ID, program reference, reporting date, system or component scope, target weight (kg), current estimated weight, actual measured weight, weight delta vs. target, weight reduction actions, responsible engineer, and report status. Supports mass management — a critical engineering discipline for fuel economy (CAFE), EV range, and performance targets.'
}

Table engineering.action {
  action_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the engineering action record.']
  assigned_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer responsible for executing the action.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer responsible for executing the action.']
  action_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the action, used for tracking and reference across systems.']
  action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the issue, corrective measure, or improvement required.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the engineering action.']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the real-world event that initiated the action.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Category of the engineering action indicating the nature of work required.']
  actual_effort_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded labor effort spent on the action.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents or files attached to the action.']
  closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the action was officially closed in the system.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks or notes.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the action was marked completed.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the action is required to meet regulatory or internal compliance standards.']
  cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost to implement the action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the engineering action record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the cost estimate.']
  due_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the action should be completed.']
  estimated_effort_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Projected labor effort required to complete the action.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the action is deemed critical to program success.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the action to indicate urgency.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of how the action was resolved or closed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk associated with the action\'s impact on the program.']
  source_event varchar [note: 'Originating event that triggered the creation of the engineering action.']
  target_milestone varchar [note: 'Program milestone or gate that the action is associated with.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the engineering action.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the action record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the engineering action record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the action record.']

  Note: 'Engineering action item record capturing a specific task or corrective action arising from a design review, test failure, FMEA finding, or milestone gate review. Includes action ID, action title, action type (design change, test rerun, analysis, supplier engagement), source event (test failure, FMEA, gate review), assigned engineer, due date, priority, completion status, resolution description, and linked parent record (validation test, FMEA, milestone). Enables systematic tracking of open engineering issues to closure.'
}

Table engineering.design_review {
  design_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the design review event.']
  chair_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Design review chair employee needed for meeting minutes, decisions, and audit logs.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle program associated with this design review.']
  attendees_count varchar [note: 'Number of participants who attended the design review.']
  chair_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the chairperson leading the design review.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reviewed design meets required compliance standards (true = compliant).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design review record was first created in the system.']
  design_review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the design review record.']
  findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of findings identified during the review.']
  minutes_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., file path or URL) to the detailed minutes of the review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured during the design review.']
  open_actions_count varchar [note: 'Number of action items that remain open after the review.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the design review: approved, conditional, or rejected.']
  review_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the design review took place.']
  review_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the design review in minutes.']
  review_identifier varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the design review (e.g., DR-2024-001).']
  review_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the design review was conducted.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Type of design review (Preliminary Design Review, Critical Design Review, System Design Review, Supplier Design Review).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment level assigned to the review outcomes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the design review record originated (e.g., Teamcenter, ENOVIA).']
  system_or_component varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system or component that was reviewed.']
  total_action_items varchar [note: 'Total number of action items generated by the review (including open and closed).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the design review record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the design review record for audit tracking.']

  Note: 'Formal design review event record capturing structured peer reviews of engineering designs at key program stages. Includes review ID, review type (PDR — Preliminary Design Review, CDR — Critical Design Review, system design review, supplier design review), program reference, review date, reviewed system or component, review chair, attendees count, findings count, open actions count, review outcome (approved, conditional, rejected), and minutes document reference. Supports APQP and PLM governance processes.'
}

Table engineering.material_specification {
  material_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the material specification record.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.material. Business justification: Material specifications describe a concrete material entity; linking removes redundancy and centralizes material attributes.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Material specifications describe a part; linking to part_master removes redundant part identifiers.']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to external standards governing the material (e.g., SAE, ASTM, DIN).']
  application_restriction varchar [note: 'Business rules limiting where the material may be used (e.g., high‑temperature zones).']
  approved_supplier varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of suppliers approved to provide this material.']
  compliance_reach varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the material under EU REACH.']
  compliance_rohs varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the material under RoHS directives.']
  cost_per_kg_usd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Current purchase cost of the material per kilogram in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created.']
  density_kg_per_m3 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mass per unit volume of the material.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the specification is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the specification is valid.']
  elongation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent increase in length at fracture, indicating ductility.']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the material\'s environmental footprint.']
  grade_or_alloy varchar [note: 'Specific grade or alloy designation (e.g., 6061‑T6, 304L).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is critical for safety or performance.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the specification was last reviewed for relevance or compliance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical supplier lead time for the material, in calendar days.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle phase of the material specification.']
  material_class varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the material type.']
  material_specification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the material specification.']
  material_specification_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the specification.']
  material_specification_type varchar [note: 'Primary functional category of the material.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the material specification.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the material according to internal or regulatory criteria.']
  specific_heat_j_per_kgk decimal(10,2) [note: 'Energy required to raise the temperature of 1 kg of material by 1 K.']
  surface_treatment varchar [note: 'Required surface finishing process for the material.']
  tensile_strength_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum stress the material can withstand while being stretched before breaking.']
  thermal_conductivity_w_per_mk decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rate at which heat passes through the material.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the specification for change tracking.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Manufacturer warranty period applicable to the material.']
  yield_strength_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Stress at which material begins to deform plastically.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Engineering material specification record defining the approved materials for use in vehicle components. Captures spec ID, material name, material class (steel, aluminum, polymer, composite, glass), grade or alloy designation, mechanical properties (tensile strength, yield strength, elongation), thermal properties, density, surface treatment requirements, applicable standards (SAE, ASTM, DIN), REACH/RoHS compliance status, approved supplier list, and application restrictions. Supports part design and supplier qualification.'
}

Table engineering.configuration_rule {
  configuration_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vehicle configuration rule.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Rule creator employee tracked for change management and compliance documentation.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Configuration rules are defined per vehicle program; adding program FK groups rules correctly.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the rule within engineering governance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authority who approved the rule.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the rule was created or modified (e.g., new regulation, market demand).']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory or internal standard the rule satisfies (e.g., ISO 26262, FMVSS).']
  configuration_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the rule logic and intent.']
  configuration_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the rule becomes active.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the rule is no longer valid (null if indefinite).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule must be enforced for all builds (True) or is advisory (False).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the rule was last reviewed for relevance or compliance.']
  market varchar [note: 'Geographic market(s) where the rule is applicable (e.g., US, EU, CN).']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year for which the rule is valid.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the rule.']
  option_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the options involved (e.g., powertrain, interior, safety).']
  option_code_a varchar [note: 'Identifier of the first option involved in the rule (e.g., engine, trim).']
  option_code_b varchar [note: 'Identifier of the second option involved in the rule.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vehicle program (e.g., SUV‑X, EV‑Y) to which the rule applies.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the rule in engineering and ordering systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the rule.']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when multiple rules conflict; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rule_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the rule: commercial policy, engineering requirement, or regulatory mandate.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule logic: include, exclude, requires, or incompatible.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the rule data (e.g., ENOVIA, CATIA).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule record.']
  vehicle_platform varchar [note: 'Platform architecture (e.g., MQB, CMF) to which the rule applies.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the rule for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the rule.']

  Note: 'Vehicle configuration rule record defining the valid combinations of options, packages, and features for a vehicle program. Captures rule ID, rule type (include, exclude, requires, incompatible), applicable program and model year, option code A, option code B, rule description, market applicability, effective date, and rule source (commercial, engineering, regulatory). Managed in ENOVIA Configuration Management and supports order-to-build feasibility validation and BOM variant management.'
}

Table engineering.ota_release {
  ota_release_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the OTA software release record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: OTA release approval must be signed off by an employee for regulatory compliance.']
  ecu_specification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the electronic control unit that the OTA package targets.']
  engineering_team_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering team responsible for creating the OTA package.']
  ota_compliance_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ota_compliance_approval. Business justification: Each OTA release needs a compliance approval record linking the software update to its regulatory approval.']
  target_ecu_ecu_specification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the electronic control unit that the OTA package targets.']
  validation_test_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the validation test suite that approved the OTA release.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: OTA releases are scoped to a vehicle program; linking provides program ownership and traceability.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the approval action was recorded.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authority that approved the OTA release.']
  compatible_vin_range varchar [note: 'VIN prefix range (e.g., 1HGCM*) that identifies vehicles eligible for the update.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or industry standard satisfied (e.g., ISO 26262).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA release record was first created in the system.']
  cybersecurity_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Result of the security assessment for the OTA package.']
  cybersecurity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score reflecting the security posture of the OTA package.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the OTA update is delivered to vehicles.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the OTA release becomes available for deployment.']
  estimated_download_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected size of the OTA package for vehicle download, in megabytes.']
  estimated_install_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Projected time required for the vehicle to install the OTA update.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the OTA release should no longer be offered.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the update addresses critical safety or security issues.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA update is required for compliance or safety.']
  ota_release_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OTA release.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA release meets applicable regulatory requirements.']
  release_approval_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the OTA release was formally approved.']
  release_build_number varchar [note: 'Technical build identifier generated by the build system.']
  release_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the OTA package for integrity verification.']
  release_signature varchar [note: 'Digital signature used to authenticate the OTA package.']
  release_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the OTA package content.']
  release_type varchar [note: 'Category of the OTA update indicating its purpose.']
  release_version varchar [note: 'Human‑readable version string of the OTA package (e.g., 2024.03.01).']
  rollback_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle can revert to a prior software version if needed.']
  rollback_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after deployment during which a rollback is permitted.']
  rollout_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned deployment approach (e.g., staged, immediate, region‑based).']
  software_delta_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of code changes, new features, or bug fixes included in the OTA package.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Market segment (e.g., North America, EU) for which the OTA release is applicable.']
  target_model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year (inclusive) for which the OTA release is valid.']
  target_model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year (inclusive) for which the OTA release is valid.']
  target_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) where the OTA release is intended to be deployed.']
  target_vehicle_model varchar [note: 'Vehicle model name or code that the OTA release applies to.']
  test_result_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of key test outcomes supporting the release.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the OTA release record.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Overall result of all validation activities for the OTA release.']

  Note: 'OTA (Over-the-Air) software release engineering record capturing the definition and approval of a software update package for connected vehicle ECUs. Includes release ID, release version, target ECU or system, software delta description, release type (feature, security patch, calibration, regulatory), compatible vehicle configurations (VIN range or model/MY), validation test references, cybersecurity assessment status, rollout strategy, and release approval date. Bridges engineering and connected mobility domains for software-defined vehicle programs.'
}

  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the engineering document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drawing|specification|test_report|analysis_report|fmea|dvp|meeting_minutes — promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the document content is no longer valid (if applicable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the document content is considered valid.']

  release_date date [note: 'Effective date when the document became officially released.']
  retention_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how long the document must be retained.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier indicating the version of the document (e.g., A, B, C, 1.0, 2.1).']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the engineering document.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the document record.']

  Note: 'Engineering document record serving as the metadata catalog for all controlled engineering documents managed in the PLM system. Includes document ID, document number, title, document type (drawing, specification, test report, analysis report, FMEA, DVP, meeting minutes), revision level, author, approval status, release date, associated program and part references, document format, storage location reference, and retention policy. Supports document control per IATF 16949 and ISO 9001 requirements.'
}

Table engineering.engineering_adas_feature {
  engineering_adas_feature_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the engineering_adas_feature data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: ADAS feature definitions are scoped to a vehicle program; linking enables program‑level reporting.']

  Note: 'ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) feature engineering record capturing the definition, requirements, and validation status of an autonomous or assisted driving feature. Includes feature ID, feature name, SAE automation level (L1–L5), feature category (ACC, AEB, LKA, parking assist, highway pilot), applicable program, sensor suite requirements (camera, radar, lidar, ultrasonic), ODD (Operational Design Domain) definition, functional safety ASIL rating, regulatory approval status, and SOP readiness. Supports autonomous driving R&D programs.'
}

Table engineering.cost_target {
  cost_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost target record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer responsible for the cost target.']
  primary_cost_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer responsible for the cost target.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle program to which the cost target belongs.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the cost target was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the cost target.']
  component_code varchar [note: 'Standard code for the component (e.g., BOM part number).']
  cost_basis varchar [note: 'Basis for the cost target calculation.']
  cost_gap_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage gap between target and current total cost.']
  cost_gap_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between target total cost and current estimated total cost.']
  cost_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to define the cost target.']
  cost_reduction_ideas_count varchar [note: 'Number of cost reduction ideas recorded for this target.']
  cost_target_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the cost target record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost target record was created.']
  current_estimated_cost_manufacturing decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current estimated manufacturing cost.']
  current_estimated_cost_material decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current estimated material cost.']
  current_estimated_cost_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total estimated cost.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the cost target expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the cost target becomes effective.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost target is locked from further changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the cost target.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the cost target originated (e.g., SAP, Teamcenter).']
  system_or_component varchar [note: 'Name of the vehicle system or component the cost target applies to.']
  target_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the cost target.']
  target_cost_manufacturing decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target manufacturing cost amount.']
  target_cost_material decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target material cost amount.']
  target_cost_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Overall target cost amount.']
  target_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for cost values.']
  target_freeze_date date [note: 'Date when the cost target is frozen and no longer editable.']
  target_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the cost target, e.g., \'Powertrain System Cost Target\'.']
  target_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost target (design, manufacturing, total, etc.).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost target record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the cost target record.']

  Note: 'Engineering cost target record capturing the design-to-cost objectives assigned to vehicle systems, subsystems, or components during the development program. Includes target ID, program reference, system or component scope, target cost (material cost, manufacturing cost, total cost), current estimated cost, cost gap vs. target, cost reduction ideas count, responsible engineer, target freeze date, and approval status. Supports design-to-cost (DTC) and value engineering disciplines critical to program profitability.'
}

Table engineering.engineering_bom_component {
  engineering_bom_component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the bom_component association']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the part master record']
  production_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the production BOM record']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the part within the BOM (e.g., standard, optional, service)']
  installation_sequence varchar [note: 'Order in which the part is installed during assembly']
  quantity_per_vehicle decimal [note: 'Number of units of the part required for one vehicle']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is expressed']

  Note: 'This association product represents the BOM Component relationship between part_master and production_bom. It captures the quantity of the part per vehicle, the unit of measure, the type of component, and the installation sequence within the manufacturing BOM.. Existence Justification: A part_master can be used in many production_bom records (different vehicle models, configurations) and each production_bom contains many part_master entries. The link is managed as BOM component lines that capture quantity, unit of measure, component type, and installation sequence. This relationship is actively created, updated, and deleted by engineering/manufacturing teams as part of the BOM management process.'
}

Table engineering.dealer_part_inventory {
  dealer_part_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the dealer_part_inventory association']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the dealership']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the engineering part master']
  dealer_cost_price decimal [note: 'Cost price of the part for the dealership']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected lead time in days for the part to be delivered to the dealership']
  list_price decimal [note: 'Manufacturer list price for the part']
  quantity_on_hand varchar [note: 'Current stock quantity of the part at the dealership']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a reorder for the part at the dealership']
  reorder_quantity varchar [note: 'Standard quantity to reorder when the reorder point is reached']
  retail_price decimal [note: 'Retail price offered to end customers by the dealership']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the part quantity (e.g., pieces, sets)']

  Note: 'This association product represents the stocking relationship between engineering part_master records and dealer locations. It captures per‑dealer part quantities, pricing, reorder thresholds, and lead‑time information that are specific to each part‑dealership pairing.. Existence Justification: Dealerships maintain inventory of engineering parts; a single part can be stocked at many dealership locations and each dealership stocks many different parts. The stocking relationship is actively managed through inventory records that capture quantities, pricing, and lead‑time per dealer‑part combination.'
}

Table engineering.engineering_team {
  engineering_team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for engineering_team']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who manages the team.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  parent_engineering_team_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on engineering_team (parent_engineering_team_id)']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Engineering teams are organized around vehicle programs; linking enables program‑level resource planning.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated to the engineering team (currency assumed corporate default).']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to reference the team across systems.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the team with internal and external regulations.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for team communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the team.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost‑center identifier to which the team\'s expenses are charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the team record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level applied to data owned by the team.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the team\'s purpose, scope, and responsibilities.']
  digital_twin_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the team actively creates and maintains digital twin models.']
  domain varchar [note: 'Functional domain the team primarily supports.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the team became operational or was officially established.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the team was dissolved or ceased operations (null if still active).']
  engineering_toolset varchar [note: 'Primary CAD/CAE software suite used by the team.']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'True if the team operates primarily in a virtual/remote mode.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent governance or performance review of the team.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the last review.']
  location varchar [note: 'Primary physical site or plant where the team is based (e.g., plant code).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the engineering team.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or observations about the team.']
  number_of_members varchar [note: 'Current headcount of engineers and staff assigned to the team.']
  primary_skill varchar [note: 'Dominant engineering discipline of the team.']
  safety_certification_level varchar [note: 'Functional safety classification of the team\'s deliverables per ISO 26262.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the team.']
  team_level varchar [note: 'Scope level of the team within the organization.']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Category of engineering work performed by the team.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the team record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for engineering_team. Referenced by team_id.'
}

Table engineering.material {
  material_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for material']
  parent_material_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on material (parent_material_id)']
  boiling_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the material transitions from liquid to gas.']
  certification varchar [note: 'Quality or compliance certifications associated with the material.']
  corrosion_resistance varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the material\'s resistance to corrosion in typical automotive environments.']
  cost_per_kg_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Current purchase cost of the material per kilogram in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the material record was first created in the system.']
  customs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'Eight‑digit HS code used for import/export classification.']
  density_kg_per_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Mass per unit volume of the material.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the material becomes valid for use in engineering projects.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the material is retired or no longer approved for new designs (nullable).']
  electrical_resistivity_ohm_m decimal(12,4) [note: 'Resistance to electrical current flow.']
  elongation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage increase in length at fracture, indicating ductility.']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the material\'s overall environmental footprint.']
  grade varchar [note: 'Specific grade or standard within the material type (e.g., 6061‑T6 for aluminum).']
  hardness_hb decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hardness measurement using the Brinell scale.']
  hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous under safety regulations.']
  hazardous_class varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the hazardous material.']
  material_application varchar [note: 'Typical engineering subsystem where the material is used.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'External catalog or part number assigned to the material by the organization.']
  material_color varchar [note: 'Standard color designation of the material (e.g., black, silver).']
  material_family varchar [note: 'Secondary classification grouping materials by typical engineering use.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping used for cost and inventory planning.']
  material_origin_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the country where the material is sourced.']
  material_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the material is produced in‑house or procured from external suppliers.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the material based on its composition.']
  melting_point_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the material transitions from solid to liquid.']
  msds_url varchar [note: 'Link to the online Material Safety Data Sheet for the material.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the material as used in engineering documentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or engineering comments.']
  reach_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the material complies with EU REACH chemical safety requirements.']
  recyclable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material can be recycled at end‑of‑life.']
  recycle_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the material that can be recovered and reused.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Minor revision identifier for changes to the material record.']
  rohs_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the material meets RoHS restrictions on hazardous substances.']
  specification varchar [note: 'Detailed technical specification document reference for the material.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material in the engineering catalog.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary supplier providing the material.']
  surface_finish varchar [note: 'Description of the material\'s surface treatment (e.g., polished, anodized).']
  tensile_strength_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum stress the material can withstand while being stretched before failure.']
  thermal_conductivity_w_per_mk decimal(10,3) [note: 'Rate at which heat passes through the material.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for material quantity calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the material record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Major version of the material definition.']
  yield_strength_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Stress at which material begins to deform plastically.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for material. Referenced by material_id.'
}

Table engineering.packaging_specification {
  packaging_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for packaging_specification']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Packaging specifications are defined for specific parts; linking enables part‑level packaging management.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that the packaging must meet (e.g., ISO 14001). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ISO_9001|ISO_14001|ISO_45001|SAE_J1739|REACH|RoHS — promote to reference product]']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the specification becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the specification expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the environmental footprint of the packaging (higher = greater impact).']
  hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the packaging contains or is used for hazardous materials.']
  height_mm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Internal height dimension of the packaging in millimetres.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specification is the default choice for its product line.']
  length_mm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Internal length dimension of the packaging in millimetres.']
  material_grade varchar [note: 'Specific grade or alloy designation of the material.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Primary material from which the packaging is constructed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or special handling instructions.']
  packaging_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of packaging (e.g., container, pallet, crate).']
  packaging_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of the packaging unit in US dollars.']
  packaging_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the packaging category.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification record.']
  recyclable boolean [note: 'True if the packaging material is recyclable under standard industry programs.']
  spec_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the specification across systems.']
  spec_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the packaging specification.']
  spec_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the purpose or nature of the packaging specification.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the supplier that provides the packaging material.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the specification, incremented on changes.']
  volume_l decimal(12,3) [note: 'Internal volume capacity of the packaging in litres.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the packaging in kilograms.']
  width_mm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Internal width dimension of the packaging in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for packaging_specification. Referenced by packaging_specification_id.'
}

  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment for the project.']

  sustainability_score varchar [note: 'Internal score (0-100) assessing the project\'s environmental impact.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of engineering work the project represents.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the project record.']
  vehicle_platform varchar [note: 'Vehicle platform or model family associated with the project.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the project\'s documentation set.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for project. Referenced by project_id.'
}

Table manufacturing.plant {
  plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each manufacturing plant or assembly facility. Primary key for the plant master record, referenced across MES, SAP PP, and logistics domains.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Plant regulatory reporting requires knowing the jurisdiction (country/region) governing emissions and safety permits.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the plant facility. Used for logistics, regulatory filings, and official correspondence. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the plant facility (e.g., building number, gate, or suite). Supplements address_line1 for complete facility location identification.']
  agv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant is equipped with Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) systems for material transport on the shop floor. Used for automation capability classification and MES integration configuration.']
  annual_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicles or assemblies the plant is designed to produce per year under normal two-shift operations. Used for capacity planning, CapEx justification, and production scheduling in SAP PP.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the plant is physically located. Used for logistics planning, workforce commute analysis, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'The date on which the plant was officially commissioned and handed over for production operations. Distinct from SOP date; represents the physical readiness milestone. Used for asset lifecycle management and depreciation calculations in SAP AM.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA company code to which this plant is assigned for financial accounting and controlling purposes. Links plant operations to the FI/CO module for cost center allocation and EBITDA reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling (CO) cost center code assigned to the plant for financial cost allocation, CapEx tracking, and R&D expenditure reporting. Used in management accounting and EBITDA analysis.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code indicating the country where the plant is physically located (e.g., \'USA\', \'DEU\', \'CHN\'). Used for regulatory compliance, CAFE reporting, and logistics routing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plant master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency used in plant-level financial reporting and cost accounting (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'CNY\'). Used in SAP FI/CO for cost center reporting and CapEx tracking.']
  daily_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicles or assemblies the plant can produce per day (Jobs Per Day - JPD). Derived from takt time and shift configuration. Used for daily production scheduling and MES throughput planning.']
  eop_date date [note: 'The official End of Production (EOP) date when the plant is scheduled to cease production of the current model or permanently close. Used for capacity planning, workforce transition, and asset disposal planning.']
  epa_facility_code varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned facility identifier for emissions and environmental compliance reporting. Required for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) reporting and Clean Air Act compliance.']
  floor_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total manufacturing floor area of the plant in square meters. Used for facility planning, CapEx allocation, insurance valuation, and ISO 14001 environmental footprint reporting.']
  iatf_16949_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant holds a current IATF 16949 automotive quality management system certification. Mandatory for OEM supplier qualification and customer-specific requirements compliance.']
  iso_14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant holds a current ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. Required for EPA compliance reporting and sustainability disclosures.']
  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant holds a current ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Required for supplier qualification and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the plant in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for geospatial analytics, logistics network optimization, and supply chain mapping.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the plant in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for geospatial analytics, logistics network optimization, and supply chain mapping.']
  manager varchar [note: 'Full name of the plant manager or general manager responsible for overall plant operations. Used for escalation routing, regulatory correspondence, and organizational reporting. Not classified as PII as this is a business role reference.']
  mes_plant_code varchar [note: 'The plant identifier as registered in the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between SAP PP production orders and MES shop floor execution records. Enables traceability from ERP to shop floor.']
  model_year_current varchar [note: 'The current Model Year (MY) of vehicles being produced at the plant (e.g., \'2025\'). Used for production scheduling, regulatory compliance submissions, and CAFE reporting alignment.']
  nhtsa_facility_code varchar [note: 'NHTSA-assigned facility identifier for the plant. Required for vehicle recall management, safety defect reporting, and Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards (FMVSS) compliance submissions.']
  number_of_assembly_lines boolean [note: 'Total count of active production or assembly lines within the plant. Used for capacity planning, MES line configuration, and production scheduling.']
  number_of_shifts boolean [note: 'Number of production shifts operated per day at the plant (typically 1, 2, or 3). Drives capacity calculations, workforce planning in SuccessFactors, and MES scheduling.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the plant. \'active\' indicates full production; \'idle\' indicates temporary suspension; \'ramp_up\' indicates pre-SOP commissioning phase; \'ramp_down\' indicates post-EOP wind-down; \'shutdown\' indicates planned or unplanned stoppage; \'decommissioned\' indicates permanently closed.']
  ota_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant has the infrastructure to perform OTA (Over-the-Air) software updates on vehicles during the final assembly or PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection) process. Relevant for connected vehicle production.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the plant facility. Used for operational communications, emergency contact, and supplier/logistics coordination. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the plant within SAP S/4HANA and MES systems (e.g., \'DETF01\', \'USNA02\'). Used as the business key across ERP, MES, and logistics integrations. Maps to SAP Organizational Unit - Plant Code.']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the manufacturing plant or assembly facility (e.g., \'Detroit Final Assembly Plant No. 1\'). Used in reporting, regulatory submissions, and communications.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the plant\'s primary manufacturing function. Values include stamping (metal forming), body_shop (body-in-white welding), paint (paint application), final_assembly (vehicle assembly), ckd (Completely Knocked Down assembly), skd (Semi Knocked Down assembly), powertrain (engine/transmission), logistics (parts distribution). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint|final_assembly|ckd|skd|powertrain|logistics — promote to reference product]']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the plant location. Used for logistics routing, tax jurisdiction assignment, and regulatory reporting.']
  powertrain_types_produced varchar [note: 'Classification of powertrain technologies assembled or manufactured at this plant. ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), EV (Electric Vehicle), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle), or mixed. Used for EPA emissions compliance, CAFE reporting, and R&D investment tracking.']
  primary_vehicle_segment varchar [note: 'The primary vehicle segment produced at this plant. Values: car (passenger cars), truck (light/heavy trucks), suv (Sport Utility Vehicles), commercial_vehicle (vans/buses/heavy commercial), ev (Battery Electric Vehicle), hev (Hybrid Electric Vehicle - HEV), phev (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle - PHEV), mixed (multiple segments). Drives model year planning and regulatory compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: car|truck|suv|commercial_vehicle|ev|hev|phev|mixed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic or operational region in which the plant is located (e.g., \'North America\', \'Europe\', \'Asia Pacific\'). Used for regional production planning, capacity allocation, and management reporting.']
  scada_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant operates a SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system for real-time monitoring and control of manufacturing processes, utilities, and equipment.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used name for the plant used in dashboards, shop floor displays, and internal communications (e.g., \'Detroit FA1\').']
  sop_date date [note: 'The official Start of Production (SOP) date when the plant commenced or is scheduled to commence volume production. Critical milestone for capital planning, workforce ramp-up, and supplier readiness. Aligns with APQP phase-gate milestones.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative subdivision where the plant is located (e.g., \'Michigan\', \'Bavaria\', \'Ontario\'). Used for local regulatory compliance, tax jurisdiction, and workforce reporting.']
  takt_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The rate at which vehicles must be produced to meet customer demand, expressed in seconds per unit. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Fundamental to JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling and line balancing in MES.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the plant location (e.g., \'America/Detroit\', \'Europe/Berlin\'). Used for MES shift scheduling, production timestamp normalization, and cross-plant reporting in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  union_represented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant workforce is represented by a labor union. Impacts workforce planning, shift scheduling, overtime policies, and collective bargaining agreement compliance in SuccessFactors.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plant master record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and the Databricks lakehouse, and audit compliance.']
  workforce_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of employees (direct and indirect labor) currently employed at the plant. Used for workforce planning, capacity analysis, and regulatory headcount reporting in SuccessFactors.']

  Note: 'Master record for each manufacturing plant or assembly facility operated by Automotive. Captures plant identity, location, type (stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly, CKD/SKD), production capacity, SOP/EOP dates, plant code, and operational status. SSOT for plant-level identity referenced across MES, SAP PP, and logistics domains.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_line {
  production_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each production line record within the manufacturing data platform. Serves as the primary key for the production_line entity. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line‑level cost allocation report requires linking each production line to its finance cost center; experts expect a cost_center_id instead of the denormalized code.']
  line_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the production line manager or area manager responsible for this line\'s operations, quality, and safety performance. References the SuccessFactors HR system employee record. Used for accountability assignment, escalation routing, and workforce reporting.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production line is physically located. Links to the plant master record for capacity planning, OEE reporting, and regulatory compliance reporting by facility.']
  sap_work_center_id varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA PP (Production Planning) work center identifier corresponding to this production line. Enables direct linkage between the lakehouse production_line master and SAP production orders, capacity planning, and cost center assignments in SAP CO (Controlling).']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Line scheduling and line‑to‑program assignment use vehicle_program_id to match line capabilities with program requirements.']
  agv_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production line is integrated with an AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) system for automated material delivery, WIP (Work in Progress) transport, or carrier movement. True = AGV-integrated; False = manual or conventional conveyor-based material handling. Used for automation investment tracking and MES configuration.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the overall automation level of the production line: manual (predominantly human-operated), semi_automated (mix of manual and robotic operations), highly_automated (majority robotic with human oversight), or fully_automated (lights-out or near-lights-out operation). Used for workforce planning, CapEx investment analysis, and Industry 4.0 maturity assessments.']
  ckd_skd_capable varchar [note: 'Indicates the assembly kit type this line is configured to process: CKD (Completely Knocked Down — full kit assembly from parts), SKD (Semi Knocked Down — partial pre-assembly), CBU (Completely Built Up — no kit assembly, line-off only), or multi (capable of multiple kit types). Relevant for export assembly plants and regulatory homologation.']
  conveyor_type varchar [note: 'Type of conveyor or carrier system used to move vehicles or assemblies along this production line: overhead_conveyor (hanging carrier), floor_conveyor (ground-level chain/belt), skillet (moving platform for final assembly), agv_carrier (autonomous guided vehicle carrier), stationary (fixed-position assembly), or mixed (combination of types).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production line master record was first created in the data platform (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data governance, lineage tracking, and IATF 16949 record retention compliance. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  current_jph decimal(10,2) [note: 'The current actual or most recently measured throughput rate of the production line in Jobs Per Hour (JPH), reflecting real-world performance after accounting for constraints, model mix, and current configuration. Used to compare against designed_jph for capacity gap analysis and production scheduling.']
  designed_jph decimal(10,2) [note: 'The engineered design throughput capacity of the production line expressed in Jobs Per Hour (JPH) — the number of vehicle units or assemblies the line is designed to produce per hour under optimal conditions. This is the nameplate capacity used for capacity planning, takt time calculation, and OEE baseline. Maps to MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  energy_consumption_kwh_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average energy consumption of this production line in kilowatt-hours (kWh) per vehicle unit produced. Used for ISO 14001 environmental management reporting, carbon footprint calculation, energy efficiency benchmarking, and EPA/CARB manufacturing emissions compliance.']
  eop_date date [note: 'The planned or actual End of Production (EOP) date for this production line — the date on which series production of the current vehicle program is scheduled to cease. Used for capacity reallocation planning, tooling disposition, and supply chain wind-down management. Nullable for lines with no defined EOP.']
  floor_space_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total floor space occupied by this production line in square meters, including the line footprint, buffer zones, and material staging areas. Used for facility capacity planning, real estate cost allocation, and plant layout optimization.']
  iatf_audit_status varchar [note: 'Current IATF 16949 (International Automotive Task Force) quality management system audit status for this production line: compliant (no non-conformances), minor_nc (minor non-conformance identified), major_nc (major non-conformance — corrective action required), suspended (production suspended pending corrective action), or not_audited (not yet subject to IATF audit).']
  jis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production line operates under JIS (Just-in-Sequence) material delivery sequencing, where parts are delivered to the line in the exact sequence of vehicle build order. True = JIS-enabled; False = standard JIT or batch delivery. Critical for supply chain integration and sequencing system configuration.']
  last_iatf_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent IATF 16949 quality management system audit conducted on this production line. Used for audit cycle management, corrective action tracking, and regulatory compliance reporting to OEM customers and certification bodies.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent planned preventive maintenance activity performed on this production line. Used for maintenance scheduling in SAP PM (Plant Maintenance), OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation, and IATF 16949 preventive maintenance compliance.']
  line_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the production line within the plant and across the enterprise (e.g., FA-01, BS-03, PT-02). Used as the business key in MES, SAP PP, and Apriso/Dassault shop-floor execution systems. Maps to BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  line_length_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length of the production line in meters, measured from the first to the last station. Used for facility layout planning, AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) path configuration, and ergonomic compliance assessments per plant engineering standards.']
  line_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the production line (e.g., \'Final Assembly Line 1 – Truck Platform\', \'Body Shop Framing Line – SUV\'). Used in reporting dashboards, capacity planning, and operator interfaces. Maps to IDENTITY_LABEL category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the production line: active (running production), idle (scheduled downtime or no orders), maintenance (planned or unplanned maintenance), ramp_up (pre-SOP commissioning), ramp_down (approaching EOP), or decommissioned (permanently retired). Maps to LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the production line by manufacturing process area: stamping (metal forming), body_shop (welding/framing), paint (surface treatment), final_assembly (vehicle build), powertrain (engine/transmission), chassis (underbody), or trim (interior/exterior finishing). Maps to CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint|final_assembly|powertrain|chassis|trim|subassembly|battery_pack|quality_gate — promote to reference product]']
  max_vehicle_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum gross vehicle weight in kilograms that this production line\'s conveyor, tooling, and fixtures are rated to handle. Constrains which vehicle programs can be assigned to this line and is critical for structural safety compliance and tooling certification.']
  mes_line_code varchar [note: 'The line identifier as registered in the Apriso/Dassault MES (Manufacturing Execution System) for shop floor execution, work order dispatch, and build traceability. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational MES source system.']
  mixed_model_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production line is capable of producing multiple vehicle models or variants in a mixed sequence on the same line (mixed-model assembly). True = mixed-model capable; False = dedicated single-model line. Drives production scheduling complexity and sequencing logic in MES.']
  model_year_capability varchar [note: 'The model year (MY) or range of model years that this production line is currently tooled and certified to produce (e.g., \'2024\' or \'2024-2026\'). Drives SOP (Start of Production) and EOP (End of Production) planning, tooling investment decisions, and homologation scheduling.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity scheduled for this production line. Drives maintenance work order creation in SAP PM, production scheduling adjustments, and downtime planning to minimize impact on JPH targets.']
  number_of_stations varchar [note: 'Total count of individual work stations or assembly stations on this production line. Used for line balancing analysis, ergonomic assessments, and determining the number of operators required per shift. Key input for APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) and control plan development.']
  paint_booth_type varchar [note: 'Type of paint application system used on this line (applicable to paint shop lines): waterborne (water-based paint — lower VOC emissions), solventborne (solvent-based paint), powder_coat (electrostatic powder coating), or none (non-paint line). Drives EPA/CARB VOC (Volatile Organic Compound) emissions compliance reporting and environmental permit management.']
  plc_system_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) system or network controlling this production line\'s automated equipment (e.g., \'Siemens S7-1500 Network A\', \'Allen-Bradley ControlLogix Zone 3\'). Used for maintenance management in SAP PM, SCADA integration, and cybersecurity asset inventory.']
  powertrain_type_capability varchar [note: 'The powertrain technology types this production line is capable of assembling: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle), or multi (flexible line supporting multiple powertrain types). Critical for EV transition capacity planning and regulatory compliance with EPA/CARB emissions standards.']
  quality_gate_count varchar [note: 'Number of quality inspection gates or checkpoints integrated into this production line (e.g., in-process inspection stations, end-of-line audit points). Used for quality planning per APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), control plan documentation, and IATF 16949 compliance audits.']
  robot_count varchar [note: 'Total number of industrial robots (welding, painting, assembly, material handling) deployed on this production line. Used for maintenance planning in SAP PM, automation investment tracking, and Industry 4.0 KPI reporting.']
  safety_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the production line\'s current safety certification. Used to trigger renewal workflows, prevent operation of uncertified lines, and support regulatory audit readiness for OSHA, DOT, and IATF 16949 compliance.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the production line\'s safety certification and compliance with applicable occupational safety standards (e.g., OSHA, CE Machinery Directive, ISO 13849): certified (valid and current), expired (certification lapsed), pending_renewal (renewal in progress), or not_required (exempt from certification requirement).']
  scada_system_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system monitoring and controlling this production line (e.g., \'Ignition SCADA Plant2-Zone1\', \'Wonderware InTouch Line FA-01\'). Used for real-time production monitoring, alarm management, and integration with MES and ERP systems.']
  shift_configuration varchar [note: 'Current shift schedule configuration for the production line, indicating the number of operating shifts per day: 1_shift (single day shift), 2_shift (day and afternoon), 3_shift (continuous 24-hour), or 4_shift_rotating (rotating continental schedule). Drives capacity planning, workforce scheduling in SuccessFactors, and MRP calculations in SAP PP.']
  sop_date date [note: 'The official Start of Production (SOP) date for this production line — the date on which the line commenced or is scheduled to commence series production of the current vehicle program. Used for program milestone tracking, APQP phase gate management, and regulatory homologation scheduling.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this production line master record was sourced: SAP_PP (SAP S/4HANA Production Planning), Apriso_MES (Apriso/Dassault Manufacturing Execution System), Teamcenter (Siemens Teamcenter PLM), Manual (manually entered), or SCADA (derived from SCADA system configuration). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  takt_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'The calculated takt time for this production line in seconds — the maximum allowable time to produce one unit to meet customer demand. Derived from available production time divided by customer demand rate. Stored as a master configuration value for scheduling and line balancing in JIS (Just-in-Sequence) operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this production line master record in the data platform (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']
  vehicle_platform_code varchar [note: 'The vehicle platform or architecture code that this production line is primarily configured to build (e.g., \'F-150 Gen14\', \'GMT800\', \'MEB\'). A single line may support multiple model variants on the same platform. Used for PLM integration with Siemens Teamcenter and production scheduling.']
  weld_gun_count varchar [note: 'Total number of resistance spot weld guns (robotic and manual) deployed on this production line. Applicable primarily to body shop lines. Used for weld quality management per IATF 16949, preventive maintenance scheduling in SAP PM, and SPC (Statistical Process Control) monitoring of weld quality.']

  Note: 'Master record for each production line within a plant, including stamping lines, body shop lines, paint booths, and final assembly lines. Tracks line code, line type, designed throughput (JPH - Jobs Per Hour), current shift configuration, AGV integration flags, PLC/SCADA system references, and operational status. Owned by manufacturing as the SSOT for line-level capacity and configuration.'
}

Table manufacturing.work_center {
  work_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the work center (station) record within the manufacturing data platform. Primary key for the work_center master entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Work‑center cost tracking for OEE and labor cost reporting uses a finance cost center; replacing cost_center_code with cost_center_id is standard practice.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production supervisor or team leader responsible for this work center. Used for accountability tracking, escalation routing, and workforce management integration with SuccessFactors.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.functional_location. Business justification: Required for Work Center Maintenance Planning reports linking each work center to its functional location in the plant hierarchy.']
  responsible_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production supervisor or team leader responsible for this work center. Used for accountability tracking, escalation routing, and workforce management integration with SuccessFactors.']
  agv_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center is integrated with AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) systems for automated material delivery and WIP transport. Drives AGV routing configuration in the MES.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation at this work center: fully_automated (no human intervention), highly_automated (minimal human oversight), semi_automated (human-assisted), manual (entirely human-operated), or collaborative (human-robot collaboration). Informs CapEx planning and Industry 4.0 transformation roadmap.']
  available_capacity_hours_per_day decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total available production hours per day for this work center, accounting for planned shift schedules and scheduled breaks. Used in SAP PP capacity requirements planning (CRP) and MES scheduling.']
  capacity_category varchar [note: 'Capacity classification of the work center: bottleneck (constraining throughput of the line), standard (normal capacity), buffer (excess capacity used to absorb variation), or flexible (can be redeployed across lines). Critical for Theory of Constraints analysis and production scheduling.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which this work center was officially commissioned and made available for production use. Used for asset age tracking, depreciation calculation, and maintenance lifecycle management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work center master record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost rates (machine_rate_per_hour, labor_rate_per_hour) associated with this work center (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard cycle time in seconds for this work center to complete one unit of work. Used in production scheduling, capacity calculation, and bottleneck analysis. Represents the designed throughput rate per station.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which this work center was or is planned to be permanently decommissioned and removed from production service. Nullable for active stations. Used for asset lifecycle planning and capacity forecasting.']
  energy_consumption_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rated energy consumption of this work center in kilowatts (kW) under standard operating conditions. Used for energy management, sustainability reporting, and ISO 14001 environmental compliance tracking.']
  floor_area_sqm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical floor area occupied by this work center in square meters. Used for facility layout planning, ergonomic assessments, and plant capacity management.']
  is_bottleneck boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center has been identified as a production bottleneck constraining overall line throughput. Bottleneck stations receive priority in capacity planning, maintenance scheduling, and continuous improvement initiatives.']
  is_jis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center participates in JIS (Just-in-Sequence) production sequencing. When true, the MES enforces strict vehicle sequence delivery to this station to support mixed-model assembly.']
  is_quality_gate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center serves as a quality gate (inspection checkpoint) in the production flow. Quality gate stations require SPC (Statistical Process Control) data capture and may hold WIP pending quality approval.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard direct labor cost rate per hour (in local currency) for this work center. Used in production order costing, standard cost roll-up, and labor efficiency variance reporting.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration or measurement system verification performed on this work center\'s tooling and gauges. Required for IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.5 measurement system compliance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this work center master record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the physical location of the work center within the plant (e.g., \'Building A, Bay 3, Row 5\'). Supports maintenance routing, safety inspections, and shop floor navigation.']
  machine_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard machine cost rate per hour (in local currency) for this work center, used in production order costing, standard cost calculation, and variance analysis in SAP CO.']
