  maintenance_strategy_code varchar [note: 'Maintenance strategy applied to this work center: preventive (scheduled interval-based), predictive (condition-monitoring-based), corrective (repair after failure), or condition_based (triggered by sensor thresholds). Drives SAP PM maintenance planning.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard mean time between failures (MTBF) in hours for this work center, representing the expected average operational time between unplanned breakdowns. Used in reliability analysis, maintenance planning, and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) benchmarking.']
  mean_time_to_repair_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard mean time to repair (MTTR) in hours for this work center, representing the average time required to restore the station to operational status after a failure. Used in maintenance planning and availability calculations.']
  mes_work_center_code varchar [note: 'Native work center identifier in the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational MES for shop floor execution and WIP (Work in Progress) tracking.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next calibration or measurement system verification at this work center. Triggers maintenance work orders in SAP PM and quality alerts in the MES when approaching or overdue.']
  number_of_shifts varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts per day this work center operates (typically 1, 2, or 3). Used in daily capacity calculation and workforce planning.']
  number_of_workers varchar [note: 'Standard number of direct labor workers assigned to this work center per shift. Used for workforce planning, labor cost allocation, and ergonomic assessment.']
  overhead_rate_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Manufacturing overhead absorption rate as a percentage applied to this work center\'s direct costs for product costing and standard cost calculation in SAP CO.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where this work center is physically located. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA organizational structure for production planning.']
  plc_node_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) node associated with this work center in the shop floor control network. Used for real-time production control integration and SCADA data correlation.']
  process_category varchar [note: 'Manufacturing process category to which this work center belongs, reflecting the major shop segment (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly, powertrain). Used for shop floor segmentation, capacity analysis, and APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint|final_assembly|powertrain|chassis|trim|quality_inspection|logistics — promote to reference product]']
  production_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the production line (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly) to which this work center belongs. Supports JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling and MES line-level reporting.']
  sap_work_center_code varchar [note: 'Native work center identifier in SAP S/4HANA PP module. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse and SAP for production order routing, capacity planning, and cost center allocation.']
  scada_node_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) node associated with this work center. Enables real-time monitoring, alarm management, and production data acquisition from the shop floor.']
  setup_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard setup/changeover time in minutes required to prepare this work center for a new production run or model variant. Used in SAP PP capacity planning and MES work order scheduling.']
  shift_model_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the shift schedule model assigned to this work center (e.g., 1-shift, 2-shift, 3-shift, weekend shift). Determines available production windows for capacity planning and MES scheduling.']
  standard_value_key varchar [note: 'SAP PP standard value key (e.g., SAP1, SAP2) defining which standard values (setup, machine, labor, teardown times) are relevant for this work center in routing operations. Governs how production order times are calculated.']
  takt_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Takt time in seconds representing the rate at which vehicles must be produced to meet customer demand. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) line balancing and MES pacing.']
  teardown_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard teardown/cleanup time in minutes required after completing a production run at this work center. Included in total station time calculations for capacity planning.']
  work_center_category varchar [note: 'SAP PP work center category classifying the primary resource type: machine (machine-driven capacity), labor (labor-driven capacity), machine_labor (both machine and labor capacity), or external (subcontracted operation). Determines how capacity and costing are calculated in SAP.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the work center across MES, SAP PP, and SCADA systems. Used as the business key in cross-system integration (e.g., Apriso/Dassault MES work center identifier).']
  work_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the work center\'s function, scope of operations, and any special capabilities or constraints relevant to production planning and scheduling.']
  work_center_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the work center station (e.g., \'Body Framing Station 04\', \'Final Torque Assembly Line 2\'). Used in shop floor displays, MES dashboards, and production reporting.']
  work_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work center: active (in production use), inactive (temporarily not in use), under_maintenance (scheduled or unscheduled maintenance), decommissioned (permanently retired), or planned (not yet commissioned). Drives MES scheduling eligibility and capacity planning.']
  work_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work center by its operational mode: manual (human-operated), automated (machine-driven without robotics), robotic (robot arm/AGV-integrated), semi_automated (human-assisted automation), inspection (quality gate), or rework (defect correction station). Drives capacity planning and labor allocation.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual work centers (stations) within a production line. Captures work center code, name, type (manual, automated, robotic), cycle time standard, takt time, capacity category, shift availability, and associated PLC/SCADA node identifiers. Supports JIS sequencing and MES shop floor execution at the station level.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_order {
  production_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for production_order']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle configuration or variant being built under this production order. Links to the vehicle configuration master (model, trim, powertrain, options) managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or SAP Variant Configuration.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Order cost tracking and variance analysis require linking each production order to its finance cost center, replacing the denormalized code.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: REQUIRED: Order creation audit requires tracking planner employee; essential for traceability and compliance.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Order‑to‑customer linkage required for order fulfillment, warranty, and recall reports; manufacturers need to know which party placed each production order.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Production order cost posting uses a specific GL account; the order‑to‑GL link is required for the Production Order Cost Ledger report.']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Production Order release process checks Homologation_Record to ensure model/year has regulatory approval before manufacturing starts.']
  mobility_fleet_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.mobility_fleet_account. Business justification: Required for fleet sales processing: link production order to the purchasing fleet account for order tracking, invoicing, and delivery coordination.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Supports Opportunity‑to‑Production conversion analysis; ties each production order back to the sales opportunity that generated it.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production order is executed. Corresponds to the SAP Plant (WERKS) organizational unit. Links to the plant master data product.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Enables Production Order Supplier Impact Analysis, identifying the primary supplier for critical components.']
  production_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) version used for this production order. The BOM defines all components and sub-assemblies required to build the target material. Managed in SAP PP and Siemens Teamcenter PLM.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production line (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly) assigned to execute this order. Maps to SAP Work Center (ARBPL) or MES line definition.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Order‑level profitability analysis (Order Profitability Statement) needs a profit_center FK to allocate revenue and cost.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Provides Quote‑to‑Production traceability for financial and compliance reporting; links the quote that was accepted to the resulting production order.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production routing (task list / operation sequence) assigned to this order. The routing defines the sequence of manufacturing operations, work centers, and standard times. Maps to SAP PP Routing (PLNNR).']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift (e.g., Day, Afternoon, Night) assigned to execute this order. Used for capacity planning, labor allocation, and shift-level production reporting.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP Material Master (MATNR) representing the finished vehicle, sub-assembly, or component being produced. The primary product/material being manufactured under this order.']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: Production scheduling aligns each production order with its originating purchase order to ensure material availability and OTD compliance.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Each production order is created for a specific vehicle program; order tracking dashboards filter by vehicle_program_id.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total actual cost incurred to execute this production order, including material consumption, labor, machine, and overhead costs as settled in SAP CO. Expressed in the plant\'s local currency. Used for cost variance analysis, profitability reporting, and CapEx/OpEx tracking.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when all production activities for this order were completed and the final goods receipt was posted, as recorded by MES or SAP PP. Used for cycle time calculation, OTD (On-Time Delivery) measurement, and throughput analysis.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total actual direct labor hours consumed during execution of this production order, as confirmed through SAP PP operation confirmations or MES time tracking. Used for labor efficiency analysis, variance reporting, and cost actuals in SAP CO.']
  actual_machine_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total actual machine/equipment hours consumed during execution of this production order, as recorded by SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) systems or MES. Used for OEE analysis, machine utilization reporting, and cost variance calculation.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when production activities for this order actually commenced on the shop floor, as recorded by the MES or confirmed by the first operation confirmation in SAP PP. Used for cycle time analysis and schedule adherence measurement.']
  assembly_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The Just-in-Sequence (JIS) sequence number assigned to this production order, defining its position in the assembly line build sequence. Critical for JIS supply chain synchronization — ensures parts arrive at the line in the exact order vehicles are built. Managed by MES and communicated to tier-1 suppliers.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of vehicles or sub-assemblies that have been confirmed as successfully produced and goods-receipted against this order. Maps to SAP PP confirmed yield quantity (WEMNG). Used for production performance and yield tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this production order record was first created in the source system (SAP PP). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management. Maps to SAP order creation timestamp (ERDAT + ERZET).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard_cost and actual_cost amounts on this production order (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, MXN). Determined by the plant\'s controlling area currency in SAP CO.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production order involves the use or production of hazardous materials (e.g., battery electrolytes for EV/HEV powertrains, paint solvents, chemical treatments). True = hazardous materials involved, requiring compliance with EPA, CARB, and ISO 14001 environmental management protocols.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this production order record was most recently modified in the source system. Used for change tracking, delta load processing in the lakehouse pipeline, and audit compliance under IATF 16949.']
  mes_order_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier assigned by the Apriso/Dassault MES system for this production order. Used to correlate SAP PP orders with shop floor execution records in the MES for traceability and real-time production control.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The vehicle model year (MY) associated with the units being produced under this order. Critical for regulatory compliance (CAFE, EPA, NCAP), warranty tracking, and production program management. Typically a 4-digit year (e.g., 2025).']
  order_date date [note: 'The business date on which the production order was created and issued in SAP PP. This is the principal business event date representing when the manufacturing work order was formally raised. Maps to SAP order creation date (ERDAT).']
  order_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the production order providing additional context about the manufacturing scope, special instructions, or engineering change notes. Used by shop floor supervisors and production planners for operational clarity.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the production order as assigned by SAP PP (Production Planning) or Apriso MES. This is the business-facing reference used on the shop floor, in MES screens, and in cross-system communication. Maps to SAP PP order number (AUFNR).']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the production order within the SAP PP and MES workflow. \'created\' = order created but not yet released to shop floor; \'released\' = released for execution; \'in_progress\' = active manufacturing underway; \'partially_confirmed\' = some operations confirmed; \'confirmed\' = all operations confirmed; \'technically_completed\' = TECO status in SAP — production complete, pending settlement; \'closed\' = fully settled and archived; \'cancelled\' = order voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|in_progress|partially_confirmed|confirmed|technically_completed|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production order indicating its manufacturing purpose. \'vehicle_build\' represents a full vehicle assembly order; \'sub_assembly\' covers component or module builds (e.g., powertrain, axle); \'rework\' covers defect correction orders; \'repair\' covers warranty or field return repairs; \'prototype\' covers R&D and pre-SOP (Start of Production) builds. Maps to SAP PP order type (AUART).']
  planned_finish_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which all production activities for this order are planned to be completed. Used for delivery commitment, JIT (Just-in-Time) scheduling, and OTD (On-Time Delivery) performance tracking. Maps to SAP PP planned finish date (GLTRS).']
  planned_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total planned direct labor hours required to execute this production order, derived from the routing standard times and target quantity. Used for capacity planning, workforce scheduling via SuccessFactors, and standard cost calculation in SAP CO.']
  planned_machine_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total planned machine/equipment hours required to execute this production order, derived from routing standard machine times. Used for equipment capacity planning, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) baseline, and standard cost calculation.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which production activities for this order are planned to begin, as determined by MRP (Material Requirements Planning) or production scheduling. Maps to SAP PP planned start date (GSTRS).']
  ppap_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) approval is required before this production order can be released for full-volume production. Typically True for new part introductions, engineering changes, or supplier changes. Governed by IATF 16949 and AIAG PPAP standards.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority level assigned to this production order for shop floor scheduling and sequencing. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by MES and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) systems to determine execution order when multiple orders compete for the same resources.']
  production_stage varchar [note: 'The manufacturing stage or shop area where this production order is executed. Reflects the physical production process: \'stamping\' = metal forming; \'body_shop\' = body-in-white welding; \'paint\' = surface coating; \'final_assembly\' = vehicle assembly; \'powertrain\' = engine/transmission build; \'trim_chassis\' = trim, chassis, and final fit.']
  quality_inspection_lot varchar [note: 'The SAP QM (Quality Management) inspection lot number created for quality inspection activities associated with this production order. Links production execution to quality results, usage decisions, and non-conformance records. Maps to SAP QM inspection lot (QMNUM).']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the production order was formally released to the shop floor for execution. Release triggers material staging, capacity reservation, and MES order activation. Maps to SAP PP order release date/time.']
  rework_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units that required rework (non-conforming units corrected and returned to production flow) during execution of this order. Distinct from scrap — reworked units are salvaged. Used for quality cost analysis and APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) reporting.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units scrapped or rejected during production of this order due to quality defects or process failures. Used for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation, scrap cost analysis, and FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) reporting.']
  sop_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production order is part of the SOP (Start of Production) launch phase for a new vehicle program. True = SOP launch order subject to enhanced quality monitoring and APQP controls; False = regular series production order.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The planned standard cost to produce the target quantity under this order, calculated from the BOM (Bill of Materials) component costs and routing activity rates in SAP CO (Controlling). Expressed in the plant\'s local currency. Used for cost variance analysis and EBITDA reporting.']
  target_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The planned quantity of vehicles or sub-assemblies to be produced under this order, expressed in the base unit of measure (typically \'EA\' for each). Maps to SAP PP planned order quantity (GAMNG). Used for capacity planning and production scheduling.']
  technical_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the production order was set to Technically Completed (TECO) status in SAP PP, indicating all manufacturing operations are finished and the order is pending cost settlement. Distinct from actual_finish_timestamp which records the last physical operation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure for the target and confirmed quantities on this production order (e.g., EA = Each for vehicles, PC = Piece for components, SET for kits, KG for bulk materials). Maps to SAP base unit of measure (MEINS).']
  vin_range_end varchar [note: 'The last VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) in the sequential range assigned to units produced under this order. Together with vin_range_start, defines the complete set of vehicles traceable to this production order for recall, warranty, and homologation purposes.']
  vin_range_start varchar [note: 'The first VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) in the sequential range assigned to units produced under this order. VINs are 17-character alphanumeric identifiers conforming to ISO 3779. Used for build traceability, recall management, and regulatory reporting to NHTSA.']
  wip_posting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether WIP (Work in Progress) costs have been calculated and posted to the financial ledger for this production order in the current period. True = WIP posted; False = WIP not yet posted. Used for period-end financial closing in SAP CO.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record representing a manufacturing work order issued to produce a specific vehicle or sub-assembly. Captures order number, order type (vehicle build, sub-assembly, rework), planned and actual start/finish dates, target quantity, produced quantity, order status, priority, shift assignment, and MES order reference. Links to plant, production line, and vehicle configuration. SSOT for shop floor execution tracking in SAP PP and Apriso MES.'
}

Table manufacturing.build_sequence {
  build_sequence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Just-in-Sequence (JIS) build sequence record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key generated by the Apriso/Dassault MES sequencing module upon sequence creation.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle configuration record defining the model, trim, powertrain, and option codes for the vehicle to be assembled at this sequence position.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this build sequence is executed. Links to the plant master record in the enterprise data model.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific assembly or production line to which this build sequence is assigned. Drives AGV routing and JIS supplier call-off logic.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Rename work_order_id to production_order_id to follow FK naming convention and link each build sequence to its parent production order.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this build sequence is scheduled to execute. Determines labor allocation and JIS call-off windows.']
  vehicle_config_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle configuration record defining the model, trim, powertrain, and option codes for the vehicle to be assembled at this sequence position.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which assembly of the vehicle at this sequence position was completed on the production line, as recorded by the MES. Null until assembly is complete.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which assembly of the vehicle at this sequence position commenced on the production line, as recorded by the MES. Null until assembly begins.']
  assembly_stage varchar [note: 'Current or target assembly stage for this sequence position: \'stamping\' = metal forming; \'body_shop\' = body-in-white welding; \'paint\' = paint application; \'final_assembly\' = trim and chassis assembly; \'PDI\' = Pre-Delivery Inspection. Drives shop floor routing in the MES.']
  body_style_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the body style of the vehicle (e.g., sedan, SUV, truck, coupe, van). Influences stamping and body shop routing within the production sequence.']
  build_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle build purpose: \'retail\' = customer-ordered retail unit; \'fleet\' = fleet customer order; \'stock\' = dealer stock build; \'pilot\' = pre-production pilot build; \'homologation\' = regulatory homologation unit; \'press\' = media/press fleet unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this build sequence record was first created in the MES sequencing module and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_order_number varchar [note: 'Sales order number from SAP SD or Salesforce Automotive Cloud associated with this build sequence position, if the vehicle is built to a specific customer or dealer order. Null for stock builds.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the dealer for whom this vehicle is being built, if applicable. Sourced from the dealer master in CDK Global DMS or Salesforce Automotive Cloud. Null for unallocated stock builds.']
  destination_market_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the intended destination market for the vehicle. Determines homologation variant, regulatory compliance requirements (NHTSA, UNECE, CARB), and market-specific option content.']
  drive_configuration varchar [note: 'Drivetrain configuration of the vehicle: FWD (Front-Wheel Drive), RWD (Rear-Wheel Drive), AWD (All-Wheel Drive), or 4WD (Four-Wheel Drive). Determines axle and driveshaft JIS call-off and assembly routing.']
  exterior_color_code varchar [note: 'Paint color code for the vehicle exterior as specified in the build sequence. Drives paint shop scheduling, batch sequencing for color changeover optimization, and JIS paint material call-off.']
  freeze_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the build sequence was frozen and locked for JIS supplier call-off. After this timestamp, sequence changes are restricted and require formal change control. Critical for JIS supplier synchronization.']
  interior_color_code varchar [note: 'Interior trim and upholstery color code for the vehicle. Used for JIS seat and interior component call-off to tier-1 suppliers.']
  is_frozen boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the build sequence record has been frozen for JIS supplier call-off. When True, sequence changes are restricted and require formal change control approval. Directly linked to freeze_timestamp.']
  is_priority_build boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vehicle has been flagged as a priority build (e.g., executive order, critical dealer allocation, regulatory compliance unit). Priority builds may override standard JIS sequencing rules.']
  jis_call_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the JIS supplier call-off message was transmitted for this sequence position. Triggers sequenced part delivery from tier-1 JIS suppliers. Critical for supply chain synchronization and OTD measurement.']
  mes_sequence_reference varchar [note: 'Native sequence record identifier from the Apriso/Dassault MES sequencing module. Serves as the source system key for lineage and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the operational MES.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit model year (MY) of the vehicle to be assembled at this sequence position. Determines applicable BOM revision, regulatory homologation requirements, and CAFE/EPA compliance targets.']
  option_package_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the bundled option package (e.g., technology package, towing package, safety package) selected for the vehicle. Determines additional BOM components and JIS call-off requirements.']
  planned_build_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the vehicle at this sequence position is planned to be assembled. Used for JIS supplier call-off scheduling and production planning in SAP PP.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which assembly of the vehicle at this sequence position is planned to complete. Used for takt time validation and shift throughput planning.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which assembly of the vehicle at this sequence position is planned to begin on the production line. Drives JIS call-off timing and AGV dispatch scheduling.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle powertrain to be assembled: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), or FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives line routing, tooling selection, and regulatory compliance checks.']
  quality_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this build sequence position has been placed on a quality hold, preventing progression to the next assembly stage. Triggered by MES quality gate failures or FMEA-driven stop conditions.']
  regulatory_variant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regulatory homologation variant of the vehicle (e.g., US-NHTSA, EU-UNECE, CARB-ZEV). Ensures correct BOM variant selection for emissions, safety, and market-specific compliance requirements.']
  sequence_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for the most recent modification to this build sequence record (e.g., material shortage, quality hold, customer order change, line disruption). Required for change control audit trail under IATF 16949.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordered position number of this vehicle within the production run for the assigned line and shift. Drives JIS supplier call-off sequencing and AGV routing. Must be unique within a plant, line, shift, and planned build date combination.']
  sequence_revision_number varchar [note: 'Incremental revision counter tracking how many times this build sequence record has been modified since initial creation. Supports audit trail and change control compliance under IATF 16949.']
  sequence_set_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known identifier for the production sequence set or run to which this record belongs, as generated by the MES sequencing module. Used for JIS call-off batch identification and supplier communication.']
  sequence_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the build sequence record within the MES workflow. \'planned\' = sequence created but not yet released to shop floor; \'released\' = released for production; \'in_progress\' = vehicle currently being assembled; \'completed\' = assembly finished; \'cancelled\' = sequence removed from run; \'on_hold\' = temporarily suspended pending resolution.']
  sequence_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the build sequence record indicating whether it is the original planned sequence, a revised sequence after change control, an emergency insertion, or a resequenced position due to line disruption.']
  takt_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Planned takt time in seconds allocated for assembly of the vehicle at this sequence position. Derived from the line rate and used for production pacing, WIP tracking, and OTD performance measurement.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission to be installed in the vehicle at this sequence position. Drives drivetrain component JIS call-off and assembly station routing.']
  trim_level_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the trim or grade level of the vehicle (e.g., base, mid, premium, sport). Determines option content, BOM variant, and JIS part call-off requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to this build sequence record, as recorded in the MES or Silver Layer. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the vehicle model (e.g., platform or nameplate code) to be assembled at this sequence position. Sourced from Teamcenter PLM product structure.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) assigned to the vehicle at this sequence position, conforming to ISO 3779 and NHTSA standards. May be pre-assigned at sequence creation or stamped during body shop operations. Critical for build traceability and regulatory compliance.']

  Note: 'JIS (Just-in-Sequence) build sequence record defining the ordered sequence of vehicles to be assembled on a production line for a given shift or production run. Captures sequence number, planned build date, shift, line assignment, vehicle configuration reference (model, trim, powertrain, color), sequence status, and freeze timestamp. Critical for JIS supplier call-offs and AGV routing. Sourced from Apriso MES sequencing module.'
}

Table manufacturing.vehicle_build {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each vehicle build record in the manufacturing domain. Primary key for the vehicle_build data product.']
  vehicle_build_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vehicle_build']
  build_stage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_stage. Business justification: Vehicle build references its current build stage; replace string column with FK to build_stage for normalization.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Required for OTA update management: associate each built vehicle with its connected vehicle record for remote diagnostics, firmware deployment, and compliance reporting.']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Each built vehicle must be tied to its Homologation_Record for VIN‑level compliance verification and reporting.']
  operator_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operator team or crew responsible for executing this vehicle build. Used for workforce productivity and quality traceability.']
  owner_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Warranty registration and post‑sale service depend on linking each built vehicle to its owning party; essential for service history and recall notifications.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this vehicle was assembled. Enables plant-level production reporting and capacity analysis.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PP production order that authorized and scheduled this vehicle build. Links the build event to the manufacturing planning hierarchy.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this vehicle build was executed. Supports shift-level throughput and quality analysis.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Outbound logistics scheduling uses the vehicle build record to assign the shipment that will transport the finished vehicle.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Enables Build ↔ Delivery scheduling; associates each built vehicle with the sales order it fulfills for handover and logistics planning.']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: Recall and ownership analytics require tying the build record to the ownership record that stores acquisition date, purchase price, and ownership number.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: VIN Registration Integration needed for warranty, recall, and regulatory reporting linking build record to VIN registry.']
  assembly_line_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific production/assembly line within the plant on which this vehicle was built (e.g., LINE-A1, TRIM-LINE-3). Used for line-level throughput analysis, bottleneck identification, and SCADA/PLC integration.']
  battery_serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number of the high-voltage traction battery pack installed in this vehicle (applicable to HEV, PHEV, BEV). Null for ICE vehicles. Critical for EV battery genealogy, warranty, and end-of-life regulatory compliance.']
  body_shop_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the body shop stage was completed and the body-in-white was released to the paint shop. Null if stage not yet completed.']
  body_shop_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle body-in-white (BIW) entered the body shop for welding and assembly. Key milestone for build genealogy and stage cycle time tracking.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'The body style classification of the vehicle being built. Used for production line routing, tooling selection, and regulatory homologation grouping.']
  bom_version varchar [note: 'The specific BOM (Bill of Materials) version or engineering change level used to build this vehicle. Captured at build time for genealogy and recall traceability. Links to Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM management.']
  build_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the vehicle build was completed at the final assembly stage and signed off for PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection). Null if the build is still in progress. Used for cycle time, throughput, and SOP/EOP milestone tracking.']
  build_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The Just-in-Sequence (JIS) production sequence number assigned to this vehicle on the assembly line. Determines the order in which vehicles are built and parts are delivered to the line. Critical for JIS/JIT supply chain synchronization.']
  build_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the vehicle build process commenced at the first production stage (typically body-in-white or stamping entry). Principal business event timestamp for the build lifecycle. Used for cycle time calculation and OTD (On-Time Delivery) analysis.']
  build_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vehicle build record. Drives shop floor workflow, MES gating, and production reporting. \'on_hold\' indicates a quality or material hold; \'rework\' indicates a defect requiring corrective action before completion.']
  build_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle build type: standard (full local assembly), CKD (Completely Knocked Down – assembled from imported kits), SKD (Semi Knocked Down), pilot (pre-SOP trial build), or prototype (engineering validation). Affects BOM, duty classification, and regulatory reporting.']
  chassis_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the chassis assembly stage was completed. Null if stage not yet completed.']
  chassis_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle entered the chassis assembly stage for powertrain marriage, suspension, and drivetrain installation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle build record was first created in the MES/data platform. Represents the audit trail creation event, distinct from the build_start_timestamp which is the actual shop floor event time.']
  end_of_line_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the automated end-of-line (EOL) functional test performed on the completed vehicle, covering powertrain, brakes, ADAS, and electrical systems. Distinct from quality_gate_status which covers visual/dimensional inspection.']
  engine_serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number of the engine unit installed in this vehicle during chassis assembly. Critical for powertrain genealogy, warranty traceability, and recall management.']
  final_assembly_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the final assembly stage was completed and the vehicle was released for PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection). Null if stage not yet completed.']
  final_assembly_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle entered the final assembly stage for fluids, wheels, glass, and end-of-line checks.']
  hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vehicle build is currently under a production hold (True = on hold). A hold may be triggered by a quality non-conformance, missing parts, engineering change, or safety concern. Drives MES gating to prevent further assembly until resolved.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for the production hold applied to this vehicle build (e.g., QUALITY_DEFECT, MISSING_PART, ECN_CHANGE, SAFETY_CONCERN). Null if hold_flag is False. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: QUALITY_DEFECT|MISSING_PART|ECN_CHANGE|SAFETY_CONCERN|SUPPLIER_DEVIATION|TOOLING_FAILURE|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  homologation_market varchar [note: 'The target regulatory market for which this vehicle is homologated and built (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR, CHN, AUS). Determines applicable safety, emissions, and labeling standards. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The automotive model year (MY) designation for the vehicle being built (e.g., 2025). Determines applicable regulatory standards, BOM revision, and homologation requirements. Distinct from the calendar year of production.']
  paint_color_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturer\'s paint color code applied to this vehicle during the paint stage (e.g., G1W – Super White, 040 – Classic White). Used for paint shop scheduling, color sequencing optimization, and customer order matching.']
  paint_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the paint stage was completed and the vehicle body was released to the trim/general assembly line. Null if stage not yet completed.']
  paint_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle body entered the paint shop for priming, painting, and clear-coat application.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle powertrain technology being assembled: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), or FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives assembly line routing, tooling, and EPA/CARB emissions compliance tracking.']
  quality_gate_status varchar [note: 'Result of the end-of-line quality gate inspection for this vehicle build. \'conditional_pass\' indicates the vehicle passed with minor deviations requiring dealer rectification. Drives release-to-shipping authorization.']
  rework_count varchar [note: 'Number of rework operations performed on this vehicle build. A count greater than zero indicates quality issues encountered during assembly. Used for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation and FMEA analysis.']
  rework_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vehicle build has been flagged for rework due to a detected defect or non-conformance (True = rework required). Distinct from hold_flag; a vehicle can be in rework while not on hold.']
  scheduled_build_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which this vehicle was scheduled to be built per the production plan (MRP/MES schedule). Used to measure schedule adherence and production plan vs. actual variance.']
  sop_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vehicle build is part of the official Start of Production (SOP) launch batch (True = SOP vehicle). SOP vehicles are subject to enhanced quality scrutiny and PPAP sign-off requirements.']
  stamping_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the stamping stage was completed and the body panels were released to the body shop. Null if stage not yet completed.']
  stamping_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle body panels entered the stamping stage. Used for stage-level cycle time analysis and production flow monitoring.']
  transmission_serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number of the transmission unit installed in this vehicle. Used for drivetrain genealogy, warranty claims, and component-level recall traceability.']
  trim_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the trim assembly stage was completed. Null if stage not yet completed.']
  trim_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle entered the trim assembly stage for interior fitting, electrical harness installation, and dashboard assembly.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this vehicle build record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, audit trails, and detecting stale records in the Silver layer.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'Internal product/model code identifying the vehicle nameplate and variant being built (e.g., F150-XLT-4WD). Maps to the PLM product structure in Siemens Teamcenter. Used for BOM resolution and build sequence planning.']
  vin varchar [note: 'The globally unique 17-character Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) assigned to this vehicle unit at body-in-white or VIN stamping stage. Conforms to ISO 3779 / NHTSA VIN standard. SSOT for vehicle-level traceability across manufacturing, sales, and after-sales domains.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the actual build event for a single vehicle unit on the shop floor, from body-in-white through final assembly. Tracks VIN assignment, production order reference, actual start and completion timestamps per build stage (stamping, body shop, paint, trim, chassis, final), build status, shift, operator team, and any hold or rework flags. SSOT for vehicle-level build traceability and genealogy in the manufacturing domain.'
}

Table manufacturing.build_stage {
  build_stage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each build stage record in the vehicle manufacturing process. Primary key for the build_stage reference master. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a reference master defining standard build stages; per-role minimums for REFERENCE_LOOKUP are exempt, but all meaningful business attributes are still included for completeness.]']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Stage‑level cost capture for capacity planning and cost of quality uses a finance cost center; the FK replaces the string code.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Build stages are defined per production line; adding production_line_id FK groups stages under their line.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: REQUIRED: Each build stage is overseen by a supervisor employee; needed for accountability and shift handover reports.']
  agv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) systems are deployed at this build stage for material transport, vehicle body movement, or component sequencing. Drives AGV routing configuration and integration with SCADA and MES systems.']
  applicable_vehicle_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of vehicle body types or powertrain categories for which this build stage applies (e.g., Sedan, SUV, Truck, EV, HEV, PHEV, ICE, Commercial). Enables stage filtering in mixed-model assembly lines and supports model-specific production routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Sedan|SUV|Truck|EV|HEV|PHEV|ICE|Commercial|CKD|SKD — promote to reference product]']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the degree of automation at this build stage: manual (operator-driven), semi-automated (operator-assisted machinery), fully-automated (machine-driven with minimal operator oversight), or robotic (AGV/robot-cell driven). Informs CapEx planning, maintenance scheduling, and OEE benchmarking.']
  buffer_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicle units that can be held in the buffer/queue between this build stage and the next stage. Defines the WIP (Work in Progress) inventory limit for line balancing and is used in production simulation and bottleneck analysis.']
  co2_emission_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard CO2-equivalent emissions in kilograms per vehicle unit processed at this build stage. Supports EPA and CARB (California Air Resources Board) environmental compliance reporting, sustainability KPIs, and manufacturing carbon footprint tracking.']
  control_plan_code varchar [note: 'Reference code of the APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) control plan document governing quality control activities at this build stage. Specifies product/process characteristics, control methods, and reaction plans for out-of-control conditions per IATF 16949.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this build stage record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for master data governance and change history tracking per IATF 16949 documented information control requirements.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this build stage definition record is valid and applicable for production use. Supports temporal versioning of the build stage master, enabling historical traceability of process changes and engineering change management.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date until which this build stage definition record is valid. Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned expiry. Used for managing engineering changes, process revisions, and model-year transitions in the build stage master.']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard energy consumption in kilowatt-hours (kWh) per vehicle unit processed through this build stage. Used for manufacturing sustainability reporting, carbon footprint calculation, and ISO 14001 environmental management compliance. Particularly significant for paint ovens and E-Coat tanks.']
  eop_date date [note: 'The EOP (End of Production) date on which this build stage is scheduled to be or was decommissioned. Null if the stage is currently active with no planned end date. Used for production planning horizon management and asset retirement scheduling.']
  fmea_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this build stage to its corresponding FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) document. Ensures traceability between the process stage definition and its risk assessment per IATF 16949 and APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) requirements.']
  hazmat_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of hazardous materials present or used at this build stage per DOT and EPA regulations (e.g., paint solvents, E-Coat chemicals, welding fumes). Drives EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) compliance, PPE requirements, and ISO 14001 environmental management reporting.']
  iatf_process_category varchar [note: 'IATF 16949 process category designation for this build stage: special-characteristic (processes producing special product/process characteristics requiring enhanced control), standard-process, critical-process (safety-critical per FMVSS), or monitoring-only (observation without active control). Drives audit scope and PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) requirements.']
  is_quality_gate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this build stage is designated as a quality gate checkpoint where vehicles must pass defined quality criteria before advancing to the next stage. Quality gates are mandatory hold points per IATF 16949 and APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) requirements.']
  is_rework_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicles failing quality checks at this build stage are eligible for rework and re-entry into the production flow, as opposed to being routed to scrap or offline repair. Drives rework routing logic in MES and quality management workflows.']
  labor_headcount_standard varchar [note: 'The standard number of direct labor operators assigned to this build stage per shift under normal production conditions. Used for workforce planning, capacity balancing, and labor cost standard costing in SAP S/4HANA CO.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable ambient or process temperature in degrees Celsius for this build stage. Exceeding this threshold triggers a quality hold and SCADA alarm. Particularly relevant for paint curing ovens, E-Coat baths, and weld quality control.']
  mes_stage_code varchar [note: 'The native stage identifier as defined in the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational MES, ensuring traceability of production events back to the source system of record.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable ambient or process temperature in degrees Celsius for this build stage to operate within specification. Critical for paint shop, E-Coat, and adhesive curing stages where temperature directly affects product quality. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) monitoring.']
  model_year_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of MY (Model Year) designations for which this build stage definition is applicable (e.g., MY2024,MY2025) or ALL if applicable across all model years. Supports model-year-specific production routing and engineering change management.']
  plant_area_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific plant area or shop within the manufacturing facility where this build stage is physically located (e.g., SH01 for Stamping Hall 1, BS02 for Body Shop 2). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA work center and plant layout master data.']
  plc_program_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) program controlling the automated equipment at this build stage. Used for change management, version control of control programs, and traceability of equipment configuration changes per IATF 16949 requirements.']
  ppm_target decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target defect rate for this build stage expressed in PPM (Parts Per Million). Defines the quality performance benchmark used in IATF 16949 supplier and process quality reporting, SPC control limit setting, and continuous improvement initiatives.']
  preceding_stage_code varchar [note: 'Stage code of the immediately preceding build stage in the standard production sequence. Defines the upstream dependency for production flow control, WIP (Work in Progress) tracking, and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling. Null for the first stage in the sequence.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The physical or functional manufacturing area where this build stage is executed (e.g., Stamping Plant, Body Shop, Paint Shop, General Assembly, Chassis Line, PDI Bay). Maps to plant layout zones and is used for capacity planning and SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) integration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Stamping|Body Shop|Paint Shop|E-Coat|General Assembly|Chassis Line|Trim Line|PDI Bay|Quality Gate|Logistics — promote to reference product]']
  quality_inspection_plan_code varchar [note: 'Reference code of the SAP QM (Quality Management) inspection plan associated with this build stage. Defines the inspection characteristics, sampling procedures, and acceptance criteria applied during quality gate checks. Linked to FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) and APQP documentation.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Safety risk classification of this build stage per DOT and OSHA standards: standard (routine operations), elevated-risk (requires additional PPE or safety protocols), critical-safety (safety-critical operations with mandatory sign-off), or restricted-access (limited to certified personnel only).']
  sap_operation_code varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA PP routing operation code corresponding to this build stage. Enables direct linkage between the build stage reference master and SAP production order routing operations for cost allocation, scheduling, and confirmation.']
  sequence_order varchar [note: 'Integer defining the sequential position of this build stage within the overall vehicle assembly process flow. Used to enforce JIS (Just-in-Sequence) ordering, production line scheduling, and WIP (Work in Progress) traceability. Lower values indicate earlier stages.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern under which this build stage operates: single (one shift per day), double (two shifts), triple (three shifts), or continuous (24/7 operation). Used for capacity planning, labor scheduling in SuccessFactors, and production throughput calculations.']
  sop_date date [note: 'The SOP (Start of Production) date on which this build stage was first commissioned and became operational for regular production. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, warranty baseline calculations, and production history reporting.']
  spc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SPC (Statistical Process Control) monitoring is active for this build stage. When true, process parameters are continuously monitored against control limits and deviations trigger automated alerts in the MES and SAP QM systems per IATF 16949 requirements.']
  stage_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the build stage within the manufacturing process (e.g., STAMP, BODY, ECOAT, PAINT, TRIM1, CHAS, TRIM2, FINAL, PDI). Used as the business key in MES (Manufacturing Execution System) integrations and production reporting.']
  stage_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the manufacturing activities, operations, and objectives performed at this build stage. Provides context for production engineers, quality auditors, and training documentation. Sourced from PLM and MES configuration documentation.']
  stage_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the build stage as used in production planning and reporting (e.g., Stamping, Body Shop, E-Coat, Paint, Trim-1, Chassis, Trim-2, Final Assembly, PDI — Pre-Delivery Inspection). Displayed in MES dashboards and production schedules.']
  stage_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the build stage definition: active (in production use), inactive (temporarily not in use), pilot (under validation/trial), suspended (halted due to quality or safety issue), or decommissioned (permanently retired from the process). Controls whether the stage is available for new production orders.']
  stage_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the build stage by its primary manufacturing function: assembly (joining components), fabrication (forming/stamping metal), coating (E-Coat/paint application), inspection (quality gate checks), testing (functional/NVH testing), or logistics (material staging/sequencing).']
  standard_cycle_time_sec decimal(10,2) [note: 'The engineered standard cycle time for this build stage measured in seconds. Represents the designed takt-aligned duration for one vehicle unit to complete the stage under normal operating conditions. Used for production scheduling, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation, and bottleneck analysis.']
  succeeding_stage_code varchar [note: 'Stage code of the immediately succeeding build stage in the standard production sequence. Defines the downstream handoff point for production flow control and vehicle build traceability. Null for the final stage (PDI) in the sequence.']
  takt_time_sec decimal(10,2) [note: 'The customer-demand-driven takt time for this build stage in seconds, calculated as available production time divided by required production volume. Defines the rhythm of production and is used to balance line capacity and identify over/under-capacity stages.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this build stage record. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines, audit trail maintenance, and engineering change management workflows per IATF 16949 documented information control.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA work center identifier associated with this build stage. Links the build stage to capacity planning, cost center allocation, and production order routing in the ERP system. Enables integration between MES stage execution and SAP PP/CO modules.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the standard build stages in the vehicle manufacturing process (e.g., Stamping, Body Shop, E-Coat, Paint, Trim-1, Chassis, Trim-2, Final, PDI). Captures stage code, stage name, sequence order, process area, standard cycle time, and applicable vehicle types. Used to structure vehicle_build traceability and production reporting.'
}

Table manufacturing.shop_floor_event {
  shop_floor_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shop floor event record generated by MES, PLC, or SCADA systems during production operations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shop floor operator or technician who performed or acknowledged the event. Sourced from SuccessFactors workforce records.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific machine, robot, or automated equipment (e.g., welding robot, torque tool, paint gun, AGV unit) that generated or is associated with this event. Sourced from SAP PM equipment master.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific machine, robot, or automated equipment (e.g., welding robot, torque tool, paint gun, AGV unit) that generated or is associated with this event. Sourced from SAP PM equipment master.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shop floor operator or technician who performed or acknowledged the event. Sourced from SuccessFactors workforce records.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where the event occurred. Supports multi-plant OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PP production order under which this shop floor event was generated. Links the event to the scheduled manufacturing work order.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this event was recorded. Enables shift-level OEE and throughput analysis.']
  tool_tooling_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific hand tool or automated tool (e.g., torque wrench, weld gun tip, drill bit) used during this event. Enables tool traceability for PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) and tool wear monitoring.']
  tooling_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific hand tool or automated tool (e.g., torque wrench, weld gun tip, drill bit) used during this event. Enables tool traceability for PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) and tool wear monitoring.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle unit being processed at the time of this event. Enables full build traceability from stamping through final assembly per NHTSA VIN requirements.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PP/MES work center (station, cell, or line segment) where this event occurred. Identifies the physical production resource.']
  agv_unit_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) unit involved in this event. Populated only for AGV arrival, departure, or fault events. Enables AGV fleet utilization and route efficiency analysis.']
  assembly_area varchar [note: 'Major manufacturing area where the event occurred. Supports area-level OEE reporting and quality gate management across stamping, body shop, paint, and final assembly. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint_shop|final_assembly|powertrain|chassis|trim — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shop floor event record was first ingested into the Databricks Silver layer. Distinct from event_timestamp (real-world event time). Used for data pipeline latency monitoring and audit trail.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the event type for reporting and analytics. Enables aggregated OEE loss categorization (availability, performance, quality) and safety incident tracking.']
  event_duration_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Duration of the shop floor event in seconds (e.g., station cycle time, weld duration, paint cure time). Principal quantitative measurement for cycle time analysis and OEE performance rate calculation.']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'Structured JSON string containing the full event-specific payload from the MES, PLC, or SCADA system. Stores additional context-specific parameters not captured in dedicated columns (e.g., weld schedule parameters, paint recipe ID, torque angle). Stored as STRING to comply with Silver layer no-complex-type constraint.']
  event_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating control system that generated this event (MES, PLC, SCADA, AGV controller, or manual entry). Critical for data lineage and root-cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MES|PLC|SCADA|AGV|TPMS|OBD|MANUAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shop floor event record. Indicates whether the event has been acknowledged, confirmed by the operator, placed on hold, or escalated for quality review.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the shop floor event occurred as recorded by the MES, PLC, or SCADA system. This is the principal real-world event time used for OEE calculation and production sequencing.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator classifying the kind of shop floor event. Drives downstream routing, OEE calculation, and quality gate logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: station_entry|station_exit|torque_confirmation|weld_completion|paint_cure|agv_arrival|hold_trigger|alarm|inspection_pass|inspection_fail|rework_start|rework_complete — promote to reference product]']
  fault_code varchar [note: 'Machine or process fault code generated by the PLC or SCADA system when an alarm or hold event occurs. Used for root-cause analysis, FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) updates, and maintenance work order creation.']
  fault_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the fault or alarm condition associated with this event. Populated from the PLC/SCADA alarm dictionary. Null for non-alarm events.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for a production hold triggered by this event (e.g., QUAL-FAIL, TORQUE-OOC, WELD-MISS, PART-MISSING). Populated only for hold_trigger event types. Drives quality containment and corrective action workflows.']
  is_rework boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event is associated with a rework operation (True) rather than a first-pass production operation (False). Used to calculate first-time quality (FTQ) and rework cost metrics.']
  is_within_spec boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value falls within the defined process control limits (True) or represents an out-of-specification condition (False). Drives automatic quality hold triggers and PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower statistical process control limit for the measured value at this station. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) to detect process drift and trigger quality holds.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Principal numeric measurement captured during the event (e.g., torque value in Nm, weld current in A, paint film thickness in µm, temperature in °C, pressure in bar). Null when the event type does not produce a measurement.']
  measured_value_unit varchar [note: 'Engineering unit of measure for the measured_value field (e.g., Nm for torque, A for weld current, µm for paint film thickness). Ensures dimensional correctness in SPC and quality analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Nm|A|V|bar|°C|µm|mm|kg|kN|rpm|% — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mes_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique event reference number assigned by the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system traceability and audit correlation.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit model year (MY) of the vehicle being produced at the time of this event. Supports model-year-level production analytics and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  oee_loss_category varchar [note: 'Classifies this event\'s contribution to OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) loss: availability loss (unplanned downtime), performance loss (speed reduction), quality loss (defects/rework), or no_loss (normal production). Enables direct OEE waterfall analysis per ISO 22400.']
  planned_cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard planned cycle time in seconds for this process step as defined in the SAP PP routing. Compared against event_duration_seconds to calculate performance rate for OEE.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain technology type of the vehicle being produced (ICE – Internal Combustion Engine, HEV – Hybrid Electric Vehicle, PHEV – Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle, BEV – Battery Electric Vehicle, FCEV – Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives process routing differences and regulatory compliance checks.']
  process_step_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific manufacturing process step (e.g., STAMP-001, BODY-WELD-045, PAINT-CURE-010, ASSY-TORQUE-120) within the routing. Aligns with SAP PP routing operations.']
  production_line_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific production line (e.g., BODY-LINE-1, PAINT-LINE-A, FINAL-ASSY-2) where the event occurred. Supports line-level throughput and OEE analysis.']
  program_number varchar [note: 'Version-controlled program number loaded on the PLC or robot controller at the time of this event. Critical for traceability of process parameter changes and engineering change management.']
  quality_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quality check associated with this event. Determines whether the vehicle unit proceeds to the next station, requires rework, or is scrapped. Feeds into IATF 16949 quality records and PPM reporting.']
  rework_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of rework attempts for this vehicle unit at this station. A value greater than 1 indicates repeated failures and triggers escalation per IATF 16949 nonconforming product procedures.']
  scrap_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for scrapping a component or vehicle unit at this event. Populated only when quality_result is \'scrap\'. Feeds into SAP CO scrap cost reporting and IATF 16949 quality records.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Production sequence number assigned to the vehicle unit in the JIS (Just-in-Sequence) build schedule. Used to validate correct part delivery sequencing and detect sequence deviations.']
  station_code varchar [note: 'Unique code of the specific work station or assembly point within the production line where the event was triggered. Enables station-level cycle time and downtime analysis.']
  station_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the work station (e.g., \'Underbody Weld Station 3\', \'E-Coat Oven Exit\', \'Engine Dress Final\'). Used in operator dashboards and quality reports.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this shop floor event record in the Silver layer (e.g., when quality result or hold status was updated post-event). Supports change data capture and audit compliance.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper statistical process control limit for the measured value at this station. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) to detect process drift and trigger quality holds.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the vehicle model or platform being assembled (e.g., F-150, RAV4, Model-3-LR). Enables model-mix production analysis and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) validation.']
  vin_number varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number of the vehicle unit being processed. Denormalized here for direct event-level traceability without requiring a join. Conforms to ISO 3779 and NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565.']

  Note: 'Granular transactional record capturing real-time shop floor events generated by MES, PLC, and SCADA systems during production. Includes event type (station entry, station exit, torque confirmation, weld completion, paint cure, AGV arrival, hold trigger, alarm), event timestamp, work center, production order reference, VIN reference, operator ID, and event payload. Enables real-time production monitoring and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation.'
}

Table manufacturing.wip_inventory {
  wip_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each WIP inventory snapshot or movement record on the shop floor. Primary key for this entity.']
  agv_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) or carrier skid currently transporting this WIP item on the shop floor. Null when the item is stationary at a work center or buffer zone.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: WIP valuation and inventory accounting require a direct FK to the finance cost center for accurate period‑end reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the shop floor operator who last performed or confirmed an operation on this WIP item. Sourced from MES operator login. Supports operator-level quality traceability per IATF 16949.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: WIP traceability requires linking inventory records to the inbound shipment that delivered the parts, supporting recall and quality investigations.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the shop floor operator who last performed or confirmed an operation on this WIP item. Sourced from MES operator login. Supports operator-level quality traceability per IATF 16949.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: WIP inventory must reference the authoritative part master for quality, cost, and compliance checks.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this WIP item resides. Aligns with SAP organizational unit (Werk) for plant-level WIP visibility.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'FK to manufacturing.production_line']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PP production order (Fertigungsauftrag) that authorized the manufacturing activity generating this WIP item. Links WIP to the planned production schedule.']
  quality_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality inspection lot or inspection record associated with this WIP item when wip_status = \'awaiting_inspection\' or after inspection completion. Links to SAP QM inspection lot.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production routing (Arbeitsplan) that defines the sequence of operations for manufacturing this WIP item. Sourced from SAP PP routing master data.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'FK to manufacturing.shift']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP material master record (Materialstamm) representing the sub-assembly, component, or vehicle body in this WIP record.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'FK to inventory.storage_location']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific work center (Arbeitsplatz) on the shop floor where the WIP item is currently being processed or staged. Supports JIS sequencing and real-time WIP location tracking.']
  actual_cycle_time_sec decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual time in seconds taken to complete the current operation on this WIP item, calculated from operation_start_timestamp to operation_end_timestamp. Compared against standard_cycle_time_sec for efficiency analysis.']
  assembly_stage varchar [note: 'Manufacturing stage or shop area where the WIP item currently resides. Reflects the major production zones: stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly, powertrain, chassis, trim, and inspection. Supports stage-level WIP throughput analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint|final_assembly|powertrain|chassis|trim|inspection — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  bom_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Bill of Materials (BOM) applicable to this WIP item at the time of production. Ensures traceability to the exact engineering configuration used during manufacturing.']
  buffer_zone_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the buffer or staging zone where the WIP item is temporarily held between work centers (e.g., pre-paint buffer, body shop exit buffer, final assembly feeder lane). Supports JIT/JIS material flow management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the wip_valuation_amount (e.g., USD, EUR, MXN). Supports multi-currency WIP valuation for global manufacturing plants.']
  engineering_change_number varchar [note: 'Engineering Change Number (ECN) applicable to this WIP item if it is being built to a revised engineering specification. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Ensures traceability of mid-production design changes.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason a WIP item has been placed on hold (e.g., quality defect, missing component, engineering change, supplier deviation). Populated only when wip_status = \'on_hold\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: quality_defect|missing_component|engineering_change|supplier_deviation|tooling_failure|safety_concern|awaiting_approval — promote to reference product]']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent quality inspection gate for this WIP item: \'pass\' = conforms to specification; \'fail\' = non-conforming; \'conditional_pass\' = accepted with deviation; \'pending\' = inspection not yet completed.']
  is_rework_item boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this WIP item has been routed to a rework station for corrective operations (True) or is following the standard production flow (False). Used to track rework rates and associated costs.']
  location_entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the WIP item entered the current work center, buffer zone, or AGV carrier position. Used to calculate dwell time and identify bottlenecks in the production flow.']
