Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) sequencing optimization to balance line loading and avoid consecutive high-complexity builds.']
  battery_capacity_kwh decimal(8,2) [note: 'Usable battery pack capacity in kilowatt-hours (kWh) for BEV, PHEV, and HEV variants. Null for pure ICE variants. Drives assembly sequencing for battery marriage station, WLTP (Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure) range certification, and EPA electric range labelling.']
  body_style_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the vehicle body configuration for this production variant (e.g., SEDAN, SUV, TRUCK, COUPE, HATCHBACK, MINIVAN). Determines stamping die sets, body shop tooling, and paint booth configuration requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SEDAN|SUV|TRUCK|COUPE|HATCHBACK|MINIVAN|CONVERTIBLE|WAGON|VAN|CROSSOVER — promote to reference product]']
  co2_emission_g_per_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Certified CO2 tailpipe emission in grams per kilometre (g/km) under WLTP or NEDC test cycle for this production variant. Used for EU CO2 fleet average compliance (Regulation (EU) 2019/631), CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) calculations, and vehicle taxation band classification in destination markets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production variant record was first created in the manufacturing master data system. Provides audit trail for data governance and IATF 16949 documented information control requirements.']
  destination_market_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the primary market for which this production variant is homologated and destined (e.g., \'USA\', \'DEU\', \'JPN\'). Governs regulatory compliance requirements (NHTSA FMVSS, Euro NCAP, UNECE), emission standards, and right-hand vs left-hand drive configuration.']
  drive_configuration varchar [note: 'Drivetrain configuration specifying which axles receive motive power: FWD (Front-Wheel Drive), RWD (Rear-Wheel Drive), AWD (All-Wheel Drive), or 4WD (Four-Wheel Drive). Determines chassis assembly routing, driveshaft fitment, and NVH (Noise Vibration Harshness) validation requirements.']
  engine_displacement_cc varchar [note: 'Total engine displacement in cubic centimetres (cc) for ICE (Internal Combustion Engine) and HEV/PHEV variants. Null for BEV and FCEV variants. Used for regulatory classification under EPA, CARB, and UNECE emissions standards, and for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) fleet averaging calculations.']
  engineering_change_level varchar [note: 'Current engineering change revision level for this production variant as managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or PTC Windchill (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'C1\', \'REV3\'). Tracks design maturity and ensures the correct BOM (Bill of Materials) version is applied during production. Incremented upon each approved engineering change notice (ECN).']
  eop_date date [note: 'Date on which this production variant is scheduled to cease production (EOP — End of Production). Drives last-order scheduling, tooling decommissioning planning, and spare parts provisioning for after-sales service obligations.']
  fuel_economy_mpg decimal(6,2) [note: 'EPA-certified combined fuel economy in miles per gallon (MPG) for ICE, HEV, and PHEV variants in NAFTA markets. Null for BEV/FCEV variants. Used for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) fleet compliance reporting to EPA and consumer window sticker labelling under 49 CFR Part 575.']
  gross_vehicle_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) in kilograms for this production variant. Used for regulatory classification under DOT FMVSS, UNECE weight regulations, and CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) fleet averaging. Also drives chassis assembly torque specifications and suspension tuning parameters.']
  hand_of_drive varchar [note: 'Steering wheel position for this production variant: LHD (Left-Hand Drive) for markets such as USA, Germany; RHD (Right-Hand Drive) for markets such as UK, Japan, Australia. Determines instrument panel assembly variant, steering column routing, and body shop tooling configuration.']
  homologation_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory type-approval and homologation status for this production variant in the target destination market: PENDING (application submitted), CERTIFIED (full approval granted), CONDITIONAL (provisional approval with conditions), EXPIRED (certification lapsed), NOT_REQUIRED. Governs whether the variant can be legally sold in the destination market.']
  is_ckd_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant can be produced as a CKD (Completely Knocked Down) kit for assembly at an overseas plant (True = CKD eligible). Drives export documentation, customs tariff classification, and overseas plant BOM (Bill of Materials) configuration in SAP MM.']
  is_jis_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant is eligible for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) supplier call-off sequencing (True = JIS eligible). JIS-eligible variants require sequenced component delivery synchronized to the build sequence, impacting supplier logistics contracts and MES call-off trigger configuration.']
  is_line_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant has been validated as feasible for assembly on the assigned production line (True = compatible, False = not compatible). Serves as the primary gate flag in MES (Manufacturing Execution System) build sequence feasibility validation and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) call-off authorization.']
  is_ota_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant supports OTA (Over-the-Air) software updates for ECU (Electronic Control Unit) and infotainment systems (True = OTA capable). Drives connected vehicle service provisioning in Geotab/Bosch IoT platform and UNECE UN R156 software update regulatory compliance.']
  max_consecutive_builds varchar [note: 'Maximum number of consecutive units of this production variant that can be scheduled back-to-back in the build sequence without violating line balancing or ergonomic constraints. Enforced by MES (Manufacturing Execution System) JIS (Just-in-Sequence) sequencing rules to prevent operator fatigue and tooling overload.']
  mes_variant_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier for this production variant in the Apriso/Dassault MES (Manufacturing Execution System). Used for real-time build sequence call-off, WIP (Work in Progress) tracking, and quality gate routing on the shop floor.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit model year (MY) for which this production variant is defined and authorized. Governs BOM (Bill of Materials) version applicability, regulatory homologation, and CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) compliance grouping. Range: 2000–2040.']
  ncap_safety_rating varchar [note: 'Overall NCAP (New Car Assessment Programme) safety star rating achieved by this production variant (1 to 5 stars, or NOT_RATED if not yet assessed). Applicable to Euro NCAP, NHTSA NCAP, or ANCAP depending on destination market. Used for regulatory compliance reporting and marketing communications.']
  option_package_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the bundled option package associated with this production variant (e.g., \'TECH_PKG\', \'SAFETY_PKG\', \'LUXURY_PKG\'). Maps to a specific set of BOM (Bill of Materials) components and assembly operations in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and SAP PP configurable material management.']
  paint_process_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the paint application process required for this production variant: STANDARD (solid colour), METALLIC, PEARL, MATTE, or TWO_TONE. Determines paint booth routing, cure cycle parameters, and paint material BOM (Bill of Materials) components in the body shop and paint shop assembly stages.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle powertrain technology for this variant: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), or FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives assembly process routing, tooling requirements, and EPA/CARB emissions compliance classification.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Current PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) approval status for this production variant. PPAP approval is a mandatory quality gate before SOP (Start of Production) under IATF 16949. Status values: NOT_REQUIRED, PENDING, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED. Drives production release authorization in MES.']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance region grouping for this production variant, used to apply the correct homologation ruleset: NAFTA (North America), EU (European Union), APAC (Asia-Pacific), LATAM (Latin America), MEA (Middle East & Africa), CHINA. Determines applicable NCAP (New Car Assessment Programme) standards, emissions regulations, and safety certification requirements.']
  sap_material_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA material master number (MM module) corresponding to this production variant\'s configurable material (KMAT). Used for MRP (Material Requirements Planning), production order creation in SAP PP, and cost object controlling in SAP CO.']
  sop_date date [note: 'Date on which this production variant is authorized to enter volume production (SOP — Start of Production). Used for production planning gate reviews, PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) milestone tracking, and supplier readiness confirmation.']
  standard_build_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard labor hours required to complete the full assembly of this production variant from stamping through final assembly and PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection). Used for production cost standard setting, workforce planning, and SAP CO (Controlling) cost object calculation.']
  takt_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Allocated takt time in seconds for building this production variant on the assigned production line. Represents the available production time divided by customer demand rate. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling, capacity planning, and bottleneck identification in MES (Manufacturing Execution System).']
  teamcenter_variant_code varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier for this production variant in Siemens Teamcenter PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) variant configuration module. Enables traceability between the manufacturing master data record and the engineering variant definition in the PLM system of record.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission fitted in this production variant: AUTOMATIC (conventional automatic), MANUAL (manual gearbox), CVT (Continuously Variable Transmission), DCT (Dual-Clutch Transmission), or SINGLE_SPEED (for BEV/FCEV). Drives assembly routing to the correct powertrain marriage station and torque tooling specification.']
  trim_level_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the equipment and feature trim level for this variant (e.g., \'BASE\', \'XLE\', \'PLATINUM\', \'SPORT\'). Determines option package applicability, BOM (Bill of Materials) component selection, and MSRP (Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price) tier in the commercial catalog.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production variant record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for engineering change audit trail compliance under IATF 16949 documented information control.']
  v2x_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant is equipped with V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) communication hardware (True = V2X capable). Drives telematics module assembly sequencing, spectrum licensing compliance, and connected mobility service activation in Geotab/Bosch IoT platform.']
  variant_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this production variant within the manufacturing system. Used as the primary business key in MES (Manufacturing Execution System), PLM (Product Lifecycle Management), and SAP PP (Production Planning) for variant identification and cross-system referencing. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter PLM variant configuration.']
  variant_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the production variant (e.g., \'F-Series SuperCrew 4x4 BEV Long Range\'). Used in build sequence planning, shop floor documentation, and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling displays.']
  variant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production variant within the manufacturing master data: ACTIVE (currently buildable), PLANNED (approved for future SOP), INACTIVE (not currently scheduled), DISCONTINUED (past EOP), ON_HOLD (temporarily suspended pending engineering change or regulatory review). Drives MES build feasibility checks.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) internal model code identifying the vehicle platform or nameplate (e.g., \'F150\', \'RAV4\', \'MODEL3\'). Links the production variant to the vehicle program in PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) and SAP MM (Materials Management).']
  wltp_range_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Official WLTP (Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure) certified driving range in kilometres for BEV and PHEV variants. Null for ICE and HEV variants. Used for EU regulatory labelling, EPA range certification cross-reference, and consumer-facing range claims.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the valid production variants (option combinations) that can be built on a specific production line for a given model year. Captures variant code, model, MY, powertrain type (ICE, HEV, PHEV, BEV), body style, trim level, market destination, and line compatibility flags. Supports build sequence feasibility validation and JIS sequencing rules. Distinct from the commercial product catalog — this is the manufacturing-feasible variant definition.'
}

Table manufacturing.scrap_record {
  scrap_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each scrap record transaction in the manufacturing domain. Primary key for the scrap_record data product.']
  authorizing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or quality engineer who authorized the scrap disposition. Required for IATF 16949 nonconforming output control and SAP CO posting authorization.']
  defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality defect or nonconformance record in the SAP QM or MES quality module that triggered this scrap event. Links scrap to the broader quality management workflow.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where the scrap event occurred. Used for plant-level PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate reporting and cost center allocation.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order under which the scrapped material or component was being processed. Links scrap to SAP PP production order for cost variance accounting.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or process station where the scrap event was detected and recorded. Enables work-center-level scrap analysis and bottleneck identification.']
  assembly_stage_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing assembly stage at which the scrap was detected. STAMP = Stamping, BODY = Body Shop, PAINT = Paint Shop, TRIM = Trim Assembly, CHASSIS = Chassis Assembly, FINAL = Final Assembly, PDI = Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI). Critical for stage-level scrap rate analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: STAMP|BODY|PAINT|TRIM|CHASSIS|FINAL|PDI — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  bom_component_number varchar [note: 'The BOM (Bill of Materials) component position number of the scrapped part within the vehicle BOM structure. Used for BOM-level scrap impact analysis and engineering change management in Siemens Teamcenter PLM.']
  co2_impact_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated CO2 equivalent emissions impact in kilograms associated with the scrapped material, based on the material\'s embodied carbon factor. Used for environmental sustainability reporting and EPA/ISO 14001 carbon footprint tracking.']
  corrective_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number to the corrective action report (CAR) or 8D problem-solving report initiated as a result of this scrap event. Links to the quality corrective action management system.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center code to which the scrap cost variance is posted. Derived from the work center\'s cost center assignment. Enables departmental cost accountability for scrap losses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scrap record was first created in the source system (SAP or MES). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the scrap_value and salvage_value amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, MXN). Aligns with the plant\'s company code currency in SAP S/4HANA.']
  detection_method_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the inspection or detection method used to identify the defect leading to scrap. VISUAL = visual inspection; GAUGE = gauge measurement; CMM = Coordinate Measuring Machine; SPC = Statistical Process Control (SPC) chart alert; FUNCTIONAL = functional test failure; AUTOMATED = automated vision system; AUDIT = quality audit finding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VISUAL|GAUGE|CMM|SPC|FUNCTIONAL|AUTOMATED|AUDIT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposal_method_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating how the scrapped material is to be or was physically disposed of. RECYCLE = sent to recycling; LANDFILL = disposed to landfill; REWORK = redirected to rework process; RETURN_SUPPLIER = returned to supplier; INCINERATE = incinerated; RESALE = sold as scrap metal or secondary material.']
  disposal_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the disposal authorization document, waste manifest, or environmental permit associated with the scrapped material disposal. Required for EPA and ISO 14001 environmental compliance documentation.']
  inspection_point_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific quality gate or inspection point in the production routing where the defect was detected and the scrap decision was made. Aligns with the APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) control plan.']
  is_hazardous_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scrapped material is classified as hazardous (e.g., battery electrolyte, paint solvents, hydraulic fluid). True = hazardous; requires special disposal handling per EPA and DOT regulations.']
  is_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scrapped component is a safety-critical part (e.g., airbag, brake component, steering system part). True = safety-related; triggers mandatory FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) review and NHTSA notification assessment.']
  is_warranty_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scrap event is relevant to warranty analysis, i.e., the defect type matches known warranty failure modes. True = relevant; used to correlate manufacturing scrap patterns with field warranty claims.']
  mes_transaction_code varchar [note: 'The unique transaction identifier assigned by the Apriso/Dassault MES system when the scrap event was recorded on the shop floor. Used for cross-system traceability between MES and SAP.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The vehicle model year (MY) associated with the production order or vehicle unit being scrapped. Used for model-year-level scrap cost reporting and CAFE/regulatory compliance tracking.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'The SAP material number or BOM (Bill of Materials) component part number of the scrapped item. Null if the scrapped item is a complete vehicle unit (VIN-level scrap). Used for BOM-level scrap analysis and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) adjustments.']
  ppm_contribution decimal(10,4) [note: 'The PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate contribution of this individual scrap event, calculated as (scrap_quantity / total production quantity) × 1,000,000. Used as a raw input for PPM defect rate KPI reporting to management and customers.']
  production_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific production line where the scrap event occurred. Used for line-level scrap rate trending and capacity impact analysis.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized root cause classification code assigned after investigation, used for corrective action tracking and FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) updates. Examples: TOOL_WEAR, PARAM_DRIFT, INCOMING_MATL, OPERATOR_SKILL, DESIGN_TOLERANCE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TOOL_WEAR|PARAM_DRIFT|INCOMING_MATL|OPERATOR_SKILL|DESIGN_TOLERANCE|MAINT_FAILURE|PROCESS_CHANGE|ENVIRONMENT — promote to reference product]']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The recoverable monetary value from the scrapped material after disposal (e.g., scrap metal resale, recycled material value). Net scrap cost = scrap_value minus salvage_value. Used in SAP CO variance reporting.']
  sap_movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP MM goods movement type used to post the scrap transaction. 551 = Scrapping from unrestricted stock; 552 = Reversal of scrapping; 553 = Scrapping from quality inspection stock; 554 = Reversal of scrapping from QI stock. Critical for SAP CO variance posting reconciliation.']
  sap_posting_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI/CO accounting document number generated when the scrap value was posted to the production variance account. Null if not yet financially posted. Used for financial audit trail and period-end closing reconciliation.']
  scrap_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the scrap event origin for management reporting and corrective action routing. \'process\' = manufacturing process failure; \'material\' = incoming material defect; \'design\' = engineering design issue; \'tooling\' = tooling or fixture failure; \'supplier\' = supplier-caused defect; \'operator_error\' = human error.']
  scrap_document_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable document number assigned to this scrap transaction, typically generated by SAP MM/PP goods movement (movement type 551/552). Used for audit trail and cross-system reconciliation.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of material or components scrapped in this transaction, expressed in the base unit of measure of the material (e.g., EA for each, KG for weight-based materials, M for length-based). Must be greater than zero.']
  scrap_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code classifying the root cause of the scrap event, sourced from the SAP QM defect catalog or MES reason code library. Examples: DMGD (Damaged), WELD_FAIL (Weld Failure), PAINT_DEF (Paint Defect), DIM_OOT (Dimensional Out of Tolerance), MATL_DEF (Material Defect). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DMGD|WELD_FAIL|PAINT_DEF|DIM_OOT|MATL_DEF|OPER_ERR|TOOLING|SETUP_ERR|DESIGN_DEF|SUPPLIER_DEF — promote to reference product]']
  scrap_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the scrap reason, providing additional context beyond the reason code. Entered by the operator or quality technician at the time of scrap recording in MES.']
  scrap_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scrap record. \'pending\' = awaiting supervisor confirmation; \'confirmed\' = approved and posted to SAP CO; \'disposed\' = material physically removed; \'reversed\' = scrap posting reversed; \'under_review\' = flagged for quality investigation.']
  scrap_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the scrap event was recorded on the shop floor via MES (Manufacturing Execution System). This is the principal business event timestamp, distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  scrap_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross standard cost value of the scrapped material or component, calculated as scrap quantity multiplied by the SAP standard cost rate. Posted to SAP CO production variance account. Used for financial reporting of manufacturing losses.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the production shift during which the scrap event occurred. D = Day, A = Afternoon, N = Night, E = Extended. Enables shift-level scrap pattern analysis for workforce and process improvement.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'SAP vendor code of the external supplier who provided the scrapped material or component, if the scrap root cause is supplier-related. Used for supplier PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate reporting and PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) review triggers.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the scrap quantity, aligned with SAP material master UoM configuration. EA = Each, KG = Kilogram, M = Meter, L = Liter, M2 = Square Meter, M3 = Cubic Meter, PC = Piece. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|M|L|M2|M3|PC — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this scrap record, such as status changes, quantity corrections, or authorization updates.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'Internal vehicle model or platform code associated with the scrapped component or vehicle unit (e.g., F150, RAV4, Model3). Enables model-level scrap rate analysis and BOM (Bill of Materials) impact assessment.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) of the vehicle unit being scrapped, if the scrap event applies to a complete or partially assembled vehicle. Null for component-level scrap. Conforms to ISO 3779 VIN standard.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical weight of the scrapped material in kilograms. Used for environmental reporting (ISO 14001 waste generation metrics), recycling tracking, and logistics planning for scrap removal.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing scrapped materials, components, or vehicle units during the manufacturing process. Tracks scrap item (part number or VIN), scrap quantity, scrap reason code, scrap location (work center, process stage), production order reference, scrap value, disposal method, and authorization reference. Feeds SAP CO production variance accounting and quality PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate reporting.'
}

Table manufacturing.manufacturing_supply_agreement {
  manufacturing_supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the SupplyAgreement association']
  packaging_specification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the packaging spec used for shipments to this plant']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the plant']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the agreement was approved']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the agreement (e.g., Approved, Pending)']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the agreement expires or is terminated']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the agreement becomes effective']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Freight terms agreed (e.g., FOB, CIF)']
  is_preferred_supplier boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this supplier is the preferred source for the plant']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from order to delivery for this plant‑supplier pair']
  manufacturing_supply_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the agreement (Active, Inactive, Suspended)']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity the supplier will accept for this plant']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Quality rating assigned to the supplier for this plant']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Preferred shipping method for deliveries']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual agreement between a manufacturing plant and a supplier. Each record captures the specific terms that apply to that plant‑supplier pair, such as lead time, minimum order quantity, pricing, and quality requirements.. Existence Justification: Each manufacturing plant can source materials from multiple suppliers, and each supplier can serve multiple plants. The business manages these plant‑supplier links through contracts that capture lead times, minimum order quantities, pricing, and other terms. The relationship is actively created, updated, and tracked as a distinct agreement.'
}

Table manufacturing.vehicle_mobility_subscription {
  vehicle_mobility_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the VehicleMobilitySubscription association']
  service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the mobility service offering']
  service_tier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pricing tier applicable to this subscription']
  vehicle_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vehicle build record']
  billing_amount decimal [note: 'Monetary amount billed for the subscription period']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle\'s subscription to the service ends or is scheduled to end']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle\'s subscription to the service begins']
  vehicle_mobility_subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the subscription (e.g., active, suspended, cancelled)']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual relationship between a vehicle build and a mobility service. Each record captures the period of subscription, billing details, status, and the service tier applicable to that vehicle.. Existence Justification: Each vehicle build can be subscribed to multiple mobility services (e.g., remote diagnostics, OTA updates) and each mobility service can be subscribed by many vehicle builds across the fleet. The subscription is an operational contract that is created, updated, and terminated by business users, and it carries its own attributes such as start/end dates, billing amount, and status.'
}

Table manufacturing.compliance_certification {
  compliance_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ComplianceCertification association']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the production order']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory requirement']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance was certified for this order‑requirement pair']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status for this order‑requirement pair (e.g., Compliant, Non‑Compliant, Pending)']
  evidence_document_path varchar [note: 'File system or URL path to the evidence document supporting compliance']

  Note: 'This association product records the compliance certification of a production order against a specific regulatory requirement. It captures the compliance status, supporting evidence document path, and the date the certification was achieved for each order‑requirement pair.. Existence Justification: Each production order must be verified against multiple regulatory requirements, and each regulatory requirement applies to many production orders. The compliance status, supporting evidence, and certification date are recorded for every order‑requirement pair, and quality engineers actively create, update, and delete these records as part of the manufacturing compliance process.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_order_allocation {
  production_order_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the production_order_allocation association']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the dealership']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the production order']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation was created or scheduled']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the allocation instance']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the allocation (e.g., pending, confirmed, shipped)']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Type of allocation (e.g., standard, expedited, special)']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Priority tier assigned to the allocation for fulfillment sequencing']

  Note: 'Represents the allocation of a manufacturing production order to a dealership. Each record links one production order to one dealership and captures allocation-specific details such as allocation number, status, date, priority tier, and type.. Existence Justification: Production orders are work orders that generate vehicles, and the allocation process assigns portions of these orders to multiple dealerships for distribution. A single production order can be split across several dealerships, and each dealership receives many production orders over time. The allocation itself carries attributes such as allocation number, status, date, priority tier, and type, which are managed as a distinct business entity.'
}

Table manufacturing.agv_route {
  agv_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for agv_route']
  end_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location where the AGV ends the route.']
  reverse_agv_route_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on agv_route (reverse_agv_route_id)']
  start_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location where the AGV begins the route.']
  allowed_load_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum load weight that can be safely transported on the route.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the route record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the route becomes operationally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the route is retired or superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  energy_consumption_kwh_per_cycle decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average electrical energy consumed by an AGV to complete the route once.']
  estimated_cycle_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Typical time for an AGV to complete one full traversal of the route.']
  is_bidirectional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the route can be traversed in both directions.']
  is_dynamic_route boolean [note: 'True if the route can be altered in real time based on production demands.']
  last_inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the route was last inspected for safety and compliance.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Scheduled time window during which the route may be taken offline for maintenance.']
  max_speed_m_per_s decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible speed for AGVs on this route.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information about the route.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority (1 = highest) used by the scheduler to rank routes.']
  route_category varchar [note: 'Business priority category that influences scheduling and resource allocation.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the route for lookup and integration with MES.']
  route_length_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total physical length of the route measured in meters.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the AGV route used for display and reporting.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the route purpose within the plant.']
  route_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking changes to the route definition.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety classification based on risk assessment for the route.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the route.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the route record.']
  waypoint_count varchar [note: 'Number of intermediate waypoints between start and end locations.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for agv_route. Referenced by route_id.'
}

Table manufacturing.routing {
  routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for routing']
  alternate_routing_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on routing (alternate_routing_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant to which the routing belongs.']
  change_approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the routing change.']
  change_approved_date date [note: 'Date when the routing change was formally approved.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent change to the routing.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable industry or internal compliance standard (e.g., ISO/TS 16949).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing record was first created in the master system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the routing purpose, scope and special instructions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the routing is retired or superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the routing becomes valid for production planning.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this routing is the default for its product family.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the routing was last reviewed for accuracy or compliance.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Primary material category processed by the routing (e.g., steel, aluminum, composite).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments, special handling instructions, or exceptions.']
  operation_count varchar [note: 'Total count of discrete work‑center steps defined in the routing.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'High‑level product family (e.g., SUV, Truck, EV) that uses this routing.']
  quality_check_required boolean [note: 'True if the routing mandates a quality inspection step.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the last review.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the routing used on shop floor displays and reports.']
  routing_code varchar [note: 'External code used in MES and shop‑floor systems to reference this routing.']
  routing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the routing.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Category of the routing based on the manufacturing process it supports.']
  safety_level varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the routing based on hazard analysis.']
  total_cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned total processing time for the routing, expressed in minutes.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the routing record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management of the routing.']
  work_center varchar [note: 'Primary work‑center or cell where the routing is executed.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who originally created the routing.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for routing. Referenced by routing_id.'
}

  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date on which the schedule becomes effective.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calendar.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the plant (e.g., "America/Detroit").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the calendar record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the calendar for change management.']
  week_number varchar [note: 'ISO week number within the calendar year.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for factory_calendar. Referenced by factory_calendar_id.'
}

Table manufacturing.operator_team {

  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line that the team works on.']
  average_cycle_time_seconds decimal(6,2) [note: 'Mean time taken to complete a single unit on the line.']
  average_experience_years decimal(4,2) [note: 'Mean years of experience of team members.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the team record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks about the team’s purpose, composition, or special considerations.']
  downtime_minutes_last_month varchar [note: 'Cumulative minutes of unplanned downtime for the team in the previous month.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the team is retired or disbanded (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the team starts operating or is officially recognized.']
  is_overtime_allowed boolean [note: 'True if the team may work overtime shifts.']
  last_safety_audit_date date [note: 'Date when the team last underwent a safety audit.']

  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the operator team (e.g., "Paint Line A Team").']
  overtime_rate_multiplier decimal(3,2) [note: 'Factor applied to hourly wage when overtime is worked.']
  quality_defect_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of produced units that failed quality inspection.']
  safety_training_completed boolean [note: 'True if all team members have completed required safety training.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Standard shift the team works (day, night, or flexible).']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Overall skill tier of the team based on competency assessments.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the team.']
  team_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the team in manufacturing execution systems.']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the team (e.g., assembly, paint, inspection).']
  total_output_units_last_month varchar [note: 'Number of finished units the team delivered in the prior month.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the team record.']
  work_order_capacity_per_shift varchar [note: 'Number of work orders the team can process in a standard shift.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for operator_team. Referenced by operator_team_id.'
}

  code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the packaging specification in ERP/MES systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created in the system.']
  default_package_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard weight of the packaged unit in kilograms.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the packaging specification, including purpose and usage notes.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Recommended disposal or end‑of‑life method for the packaging.']

  material_type varchar [note: 'Primary material used for the packaging.']
  max_load_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum permissible load weight for the package.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the packaging specification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks, exceptions, or special handling instructions.']
  packaging_color varchar [note: 'Standard color or color code of the packaging.']
  packaging_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of the packaging per unit in US dollars.']
  packaging_shape varchar [note: 'Geometric shape of the packaging.']
  recyclable_flag boolean [note: 'True if the packaging is recyclable according to company policy.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status with relevant packaging regulations.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the specification.']
  temperature_range_c varchar [note: 'Acceptable temperature range for the package, expressed as "min-max" in Celsius.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the specification indicating its role (e.g., primary packaging, secondary packaging, custom).']
  units_per_carton varchar [note: 'Number of individual units contained in one carton.']
  units_per_pallet varchar [note: 'Number of cartons that can be stacked on a standard pallet.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change management of the specification.']
  volume_per_unit_cubic_m decimal(12,6) [note: 'Physical volume occupied by a single packaged unit.']
  weight_per_unit_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of a single packaged unit.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'External width of the package in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for packaging_specification. Referenced by packaging_specification_id.'
}

Table supply.supplier_part_approval {
  supplier_part_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the supplier part approval record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the approver.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the approver.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: PPAP approval records must be tied to the supplier entity in the supply domain for audit and traceability.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the approval decision was recorded.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the approval record, often used in audit and reporting.']
  approval_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the PPAP review: approved, interim approval, or rejected.']
  approval_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting overall assessment of the part\'s compliance.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or manager who approved the part.']
  capability_study_status varchar [note: 'Status of the capability study element.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification indicating the importance of the part.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by reviewers or approvers.']
  control_plan_status varchar [note: 'Status of the control plan element.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Approved cost of the part in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approval record was first created in the system.']
  design_records_status varchar [note: 'Status of the design records element within the PPAP package.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the approval is no longer valid.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days for the part from supplier to plant.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the approval record.']
  msa_status varchar [note: 'Status of the MSA element.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Aggregated status of the approval record.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the part.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number of the component being approved.']
  pfmea_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Process FMEA element.']
  ppap_submission_level varchar [note: 'Level of PPAP submission (1‑5) indicating the depth of documentation provided.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Quality rating assigned to the part after approval.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for the creation event of the record.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for the most recent modification of the record.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part meets all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the part based on failure mode analysis.']
  sample_parts_status varchar [note: 'Status of the sample parts element.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the supplier submitted the PPAP package.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the supplier organization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the approval record.']

  Note: 'PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) record capturing the formal approval of a supplier-produced part for use in production. Includes approval ID, part number, supplier reference, PPAP submission level (1–5), submission date, approval date, approver, PPAP elements status (design records, PFMEA, control plan, MSA, capability study, sample parts), approval outcome (approved, interim approval, rejected), and expiry date. Mandated by IATF 16949 and AIAG PPAP standards for all production parts.'
}

Table supply.supply_supplier {
  supply_supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_supplier data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Supplier Jurisdiction Registry links a supplier to the jurisdiction where it operates for tax and regulatory reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record for all tier-1 and tier-2 suppliers in the automotive supply chain. Captures supplier identity, classification (direct/indirect, tier level), IATF 16949 certification status, DUNS number, geographic footprint, commodity codes, preferred currency, payment terms, and supplier lifecycle status (active, probation, disqualified). SSOT for supplier identity within the supply domain; integrates with SAP MM vendor master and PTC Windchill supplier collaboration.'
}

Table supply.supply_supplier_plant {
  supply_supplier_plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_supplier_plant data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link child plant to its supplier master for in‑domain hierarchy']

  Note: 'Represents the specific manufacturing or distribution plant/site of a supplier that ships parts to an OEM assembly plant. Captures plant address, plant code, production capabilities, capacity constraints, dock-to-dock lead time, customs zone, and plant-level quality certifications. Enables JIT/JIS scheduling at the plant-to-plant level and supports CKD/SKD kit origin tracking.'
}

Table supply.inbound_part {
  inbound_part_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inbound part record.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Part Certification Management process stores the compliance document (e.g., type‑approval) associated with each inbound part.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Material receipt cost is allocated to a Cost Center for inventory valuation and variance analysis.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Inbound receipt process matches each received part to the engineering part master for quality verification and BOM reconciliation.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory Requirement Mapping report links each inbound part to the regulatory requirement it must satisfy for emissions and safety compliance.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Receiving process matches inbound parts to SKU master to create accurate stock records; essential for inventory posting.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Connect inbound part to internal supplier master for analytics']
  average_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Weighted average purchase cost per unit of the part.']
  commodity_group varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the part for procurement and reporting.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the country where the part was manufactured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inbound part record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the part\'s cost.']
  customs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'HS‑8 tariff classification code used for import duties.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the part information is considered valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the part information is no longer valid (nullable).']
  engineering_change_level varchar [note: 'Level of engineering change applied to the part (e.g., A‑E).']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is classified as hazardous under regulatory rules.']
  height_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical height of the part in millimetres.']
  last_received_date date [note: 'Date when the part was most recently received at the plant.']
  last_received_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the part received on the most recent receipt.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from order placement to receipt.']
  length_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length of the part in millimetres.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the part within the supply chain.']
  lot_size varchar [note: 'Standard production batch size for the part.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Category indicating whether the part is raw material, sub‑assembly, CKD kit, finished good, or service item.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from the supplier.']
  oem_part_number varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer part number used internally for cross‑reference.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the purchased part.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Production Part Approval Process for the part.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for the price (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  reorder_point_quantity varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a new purchase order.']
  safety_stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Buffer inventory quantity maintained to protect against supply variability.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for the part data.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the external supplier to the part.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for ordering and inventory management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the record.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the part in kilograms.']
  width_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical width of the part in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Master record for every purchased part number sourced from external suppliers. Captures OEM part number, supplier part number cross-reference, commodity group, material type (raw, sub-assembly, CKD kit), unit of measure, PPAP approval status, engineering change level, hazardous material flag, country of origin, and customs tariff code. Bridges SAP MM material master and PTC Windchill parts classification for supply-domain-owned purchased parts.'
}

Table supply.sourcing_nomination {
  sourcing_nomination_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each sourcing nomination record.']
  buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal commodity buyer responsible for the nomination.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal commodity buyer responsible for the nomination.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link sourcing nomination to internal supplier master']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks entered by the buyer or sourcing team.']
  commodity varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the part or commodity being nominated (e.g., ENGINE, HVAC, ELECTRICAL).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the nomination record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the target piece price.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Optional date when the nomination expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the nomination becomes effective for planning purposes.']
  is_jis boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the nominated part is required under a JIS delivery strategy.']
  is_jit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the nominated part is required under a JIT delivery strategy.']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the part is supplied as a Completely Knocked Down (CKD) or Semi‑Knocked Down (SKD) kit, or not a kit.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year for which the part nomination applies.']
  nominated_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total quantity of the part the supplier is expected to deliver for the program.']
  nomination_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OEM formally recorded the nomination.']
  nomination_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business code assigned to the nomination for tracking and reference.']
  nomination_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the nomination.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number or PLM identifier for the component subject to nomination.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the nomination for execution planning.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the vehicle program or platform associated with the nomination (e.g., SUV2025, EVX).']
  region varchar [note: 'Region code indicating the primary supply geography for the nominated part.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of supply risk for the nominated part.']
  sor_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code linking to the SOR document that defines functional and performance requirements.']
  target_piece_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target unit price (per piece) the OEM aims to achieve with the nominated supplier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the nomination record.']

  Note: 'Records the formal OEM decision to nominate a specific supplier for a given part or commodity within a model year program. Captures nomination date, program/platform code, nominated supplier, awarded annual volume, target piece price, SOR (Statement of Requirements) reference, nomination status (nominated, confirmed, withdrawn), and the responsible commodity buyer. Precedes the RFQ and PPAP process. SSOT for sourcing award decisions; distinct from procurement domain\'s strategic sourcing strategy — this is the operational award record.'
}

Table supply.rfq {
  rfq_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the RFQ record.']
  buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal buying organization or department originating the RFQ.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal buying organization or department originating the RFQ.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: RFQ generation is driven by engineering part definitions; linking to part_master enables accurate pricing and specification alignment.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate RFQ with internal supplier master']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of internal approval workflow.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documents are attached to the RFQ.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code classifying the type of material or service requested.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality standards the supplier must meet (e.g., IATF 16949, ISO 14001).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RFQ record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Preferred delivery methodology (Just‑In‑Time, Just‑In‑Sequence, or standard).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount the supplier proposes relative to the target price.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the RFQ was issued to suppliers.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the RFQ status.']
  net_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net price after discounts, taxes, and fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the buyer.']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the part or service requested.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the RFQ.']
  program_model_year varchar [note: 'Vehicle program or model year for which the part is required.']
  quantity_estimate varchar [note: 'Projected annual volume the buyer expects to purchase.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'True if the part requires regulatory sign‑off before purchase.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the part must be delivered to the plant.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Last date on which suppliers may submit their quotations.']
  rfq_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible RFQ number assigned by the sourcing system.']
  rfq_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the RFQ.']
  rfq_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the RFQ request.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the RFQ (e.g., SAP_MM).']
  target_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum price the buyer is willing to pay for the quoted item (gross).']
  target_price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the target price.']
  tooling_description varchar [note: 'Details of the tooling requirements, if any.']
  tooling_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new tooling is needed for the part.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the RFQ record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who created the RFQ.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a Request for Quotation event issued to one or more suppliers for a specific part, assembly, or service. Captures RFQ number, issue date, response due date, commodity, target price, annual volume estimate, program/model year, tooling requirements, and RFQ status (open, closed, awarded, cancelled). Integrates with SAP MM RFQ (ME41) and PTC Windchill supplier collaboration portal.'
}

Table supply.rfq_response {
  rfq_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the RFQ response record.']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original Request for Quotation to which this response belongs.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate RFQ response with internal supplier master']
  amortization_term_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which the tooling cost is amortized.']
  capacity_commitment decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum production capacity the supplier commits to deliver per period.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the capacity commitment (e.g., units_per_month).']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or industry certifications attached to the quotation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RFQ response record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_address varchar [note: 'Physical address where the supplier will deliver the goods.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered on the quoted unit price.']
  exceptions_to_sor varchar [note: 'Supplier‑provided deviations or exceptions to the original SOR.']
  freight_included boolean [note: 'True if freight costs are included in the total price.']
  is_preferred_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is marked as a preferred source for this part.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the supplier needs to deliver the quoted items after order confirmation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition offered by the supplier.']
  quoted_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the quoted price.']
  quoted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of items the supplier is quoting for.']
  quoted_unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit quoted by the supplier.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory approvals for the quoted part.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments from the supplier.']
  response_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the supplier\'s quotation response.']
  response_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the supplier submitted the quotation.']
  rfq_response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RFQ response.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation for delivering the goods.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier submitted the quotation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated tax component included in the quotation.']
  tooling_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'One‑time cost for tooling or molds required to produce the part.']
  total_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount for the quoted quantity (unit price multiplied by quantity).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quoted quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the RFQ response record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Last date on which the quoted terms are valid.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'First date on which the quoted terms are valid.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Length of warranty coverage offered by the supplier.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Type of warranty provided.']

  Note: 'Captures a supplier\'s formal quotation response to an RFQ. Records quoted unit price, tooling cost, amortization terms, lead time, capacity commitment, exceptions to SOR, validity period, and response status (submitted, under review, accepted, rejected). Supports competitive bid analysis and supplier selection decisions. One RFQ may have multiple responses from competing suppliers.'
}

Table supply.supply_purchase_order {
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_purchase_order data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_model_year_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.model_year_program. Business justification: Purchase orders are tied to a specific model year program for budgeting, volume tracking, and compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PO cost allocation required for budgeting and expense posting in Cost Center; finance tracks spend per cost center.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link purchase order to internal supplier master']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: PRODUCTION PLANNING: Purchase orders are generated from vehicle orders; linking enables order‑to‑delivery traceability report required by manufacturing execution system.']

  Note: 'Legally binding procurement document issued to a supplier authorizing delivery of parts or materials at agreed price and schedule. Captures PO number, PO type (standard, blanket, scheduling agreement), supplier, plant, delivery terms (Incoterms), payment terms, total value, currency, and PO status (open, partially delivered, closed, cancelled). SSOT for purchase commitments; sourced from SAP MM (ME21N/ME22N).'
}

Table supply.supply_po_line {
  supply_po_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_po_line data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  build_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.build_spec. Business justification: INCLUDE: PO Line to Build Spec mapping is used in the Parts‑to‑Build reconciliation process, linking each PO line to the build spec it fulfills.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each PO line expense must be posted to a GL account for financial reporting; GL account determines expense classification.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_line. Business justification: FULFILLMENT MAPPING: Each PO line must reference the originating sales order line to reconcile parts consumption against sold vehicle configurations.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Purchase order line items specify the exact SKU to be received; linking enables automatic inventory allocation.']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: Connect PO line to its purchase order within supply domain']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link PO line to internal supplier master']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a purchase order representing a specific part number, quantity, unit price, delivery date, and plant destination. Captures line number, material number, ordered quantity, confirmed quantity, net price, delivery date, goods receipt quantity, and invoice quantity. Enables line-level tracking of delivery performance and invoice matching (3-way match) in SAP MM.'
}

Table supply.scheduling_agreement {
  scheduling_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the scheduling agreement record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Approval of scheduling agreements requires a designated employee; used in Scheduling Agreement Approval Log report.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Scheduling agreements are created per engineering part to lock volume, price, and delivery cadence with the supplier.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link scheduling agreement to internal supplier master']
  actual_otd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured on‑time delivery performance for the agreement period.']
  actual_ppm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured defect rate for parts delivered under the agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the scheduling agreement (e.g., contract number).']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scheduling agreement (e.g., framework, spot, consignment).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement received formal approval.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality compliance status of the agreement.']
  compliance_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number of the attached compliance documentation.']
  contract_scope varchar [note: 'High‑level description of the parts, services, or components covered.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the agreement when amendments are made.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scheduling agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for pricing.']
  delivery_rhythm varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency of deliveries defined in the agreement.']
  early_termination_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the agreement can be terminated before the end date.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement expires or is terminated (nullable for open‑ended contracts).']
  kanban_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement uses a Kanban pull system for JIT deliveries.']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the part covered by the agreement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed with the supplier.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing penalties for missed deliveries or quality breaches.']
  price_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price for one unit of the supplied part.']
  release_horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks in advance that delivery forecasts must be submitted.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Notice period required to exercise renewal option.']
  renewal_option boolean [note: 'Indicates if the agreement includes an automatic renewal provision.']
  scheduling_agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the purpose, scope and key terms of the agreement.']
  scheduling_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scheduling agreement.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement becomes binding.']
  target_otd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on‑time deliveries defined in the agreement.']
  target_ppm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable defect rate for supplied parts.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required to terminate the agreement early.']
  total_annual_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned total quantity of parts to be supplied over the contract year.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the volume (e.g., pieces, kilograms).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the scheduling agreement record.']

  Note: 'Long-term supply agreement with a supplier defining the framework for JIT/JIS delivery of parts over a model year or contract period. Captures agreement number, validity start/end dates, target annual volume, release horizon (firm/forecast weeks), delivery rhythm (daily, weekly), Kanban flag, and agreement status. The scheduling agreement is the backbone of JIT supply in SAP MM (ME31L/ME32L).'
}

Table supply.supply_delivery_schedule {
  supply_delivery_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_delivery_schedule data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  scheduling_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.scheduling_agreement. Business justification: Delivery schedule is issued under a scheduling agreement; linking via scheduling_agreement_id enables traceability to the agreement.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Delivery schedule belongs to a supplier; adding supplier_id FK creates the required parent relationship and consolidates supplier reference.']

  Note: 'JIT/JIS delivery schedule line issued against a scheduling agreement, specifying exact quantities and delivery dates/times for a part to a plant dock. Captures schedule line date, time, required quantity, cumulative quantity, schedule type (firm, forecast, JIS sequence), dock door, and transmission status (sent, acknowledged, revised). Drives supplier production and logistics planning. Sourced from SAP MM schedule lines (EKET).'
}

Table supply.supply_ppap_submission {
  supply_ppap_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_ppap_submission data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: PPAP Compliance Tracking stores the PPAP package document reference in compliance_document for each PPAP submission.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to procurement.supplier']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: REQUIRED: PPAP compliance dashboard requires each PPAP submission to be associated with the specific supplier contract governing the part.']

  Note: 'Production Part Approval Process submission record tracking the formal approval of a supplier\'s manufacturing process for a specific part. Captures PPAP level (1–5), submission date, part number, supplier, engineering change level, submission reason (new part, engineering change, tooling move), PPAP elements checklist status, PSW (Part Submission Warrant) status, and approval/rejection date. Integrates with PTC Windchill and SAP QM. SSOT for PPAP compliance.'
}

Table supply.ppap_element {
  ppap_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the PPAP element record.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier responsible for the part associated with this PPAP element.']
  supply_ppap_submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent PPAP submission that aggregates multiple elements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the element was approved by the quality reviewer.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality standard that the element satisfies (e.g., IATF 16949, AIAG PPAP).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the PPAP element record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PPAP element record was first created in the system.']
  defect_rate_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured parts‑per‑million defect rate associated with the element, if applicable.']
  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored PPAP element document.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Path or URL to the stored document that fulfills the PPAP element.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the element must be submitted for the PPAP package.']
  element_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the PPAP element type as defined by AIAG.']
  element_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the PPAP element (e.g., Design Records, PFMEA).']
  element_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PPAP element according to AIAG PPAP element taxonomy.']
  element_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the element document (e.g., v1.0, revA).']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the element contains confidential information requiring restricted handling.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the most recent reviewer of the element.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number to which the PPAP element applies.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the part version covered by the PPAP element.']
  quality_metric varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quality assessment for the element.']
  required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the element is mandatory for the current PPAP submission.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the element was last reviewed.']
  reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments entered by the reviewer during evaluation.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the element within the PPAP workflow.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the PPAP element record.']

  Note: 'Individual PPAP element or deliverable within a PPAP submission, such as Design Records, PFMEA, Control Plan, MSA, SPC studies, or PSW. Captures element type (per AIAG PPAP standard), required flag, submission status (not started, submitted, approved, rejected), reviewer comments, and document reference. Enables granular tracking of PPAP completeness and compliance against IATF 16949 requirements.'
}

Table supply.inbound_shipment {
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inbound shipment record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Carrier performance & cost reporting uses inbound shipment data; linking enables carrier KPI aggregation.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Shipment Documentation process records the customs/compliance document attached to each inbound shipment.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Shipment‑to‑PO matching process uses PO ID to verify that inbound shipments correspond to the correct purchase order.']
  receiving_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Receiving clerk records inbound shipment receipt; required for Receiving Activity Log.']
  receiving_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the OEM plant receiving the shipment.']
