  batch_management_flag boolean [note: 'True if the material is managed in batches.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch when batch management is active.']
  confirmation_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line was confirmed by the supplier.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a procurement contract governing this line.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PO line record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the line amounts.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for goods receipt as requested by the buyer.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Actual date when the material was received.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Line amount before tax (quantity * net_price).']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Internal order linked to the PO line for project accounting.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the supplier invoice linked to this line.']
  invoice_receipt_date date [note: 'Date the supplier invoice was recorded.']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'True if the line is currently blocked from processing.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'True if the line has been logically deleted.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the PO line.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the PO line.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the purchase order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the PO line.']
  net_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Line amount after tax (gross_amount - tax_amount).']
  net_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Net price (excluding tax) for a single unit of the material.']
  over_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed percentage over the ordered quantity.']
  ppap_level varchar [note: 'Production Part Approval Process level required for the part.']
  price_condition varchar [note: 'Condition type that determines pricing logic for the line.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer or group responsible for the procurement.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'True if a quality inspection is mandatory for the received goods.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(15,3) [note: 'Amount of material requested on this line.']
  release_number varchar [note: 'Release identifier for the purchase order (e.g., for multi‑release PO).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes entered by users.']
  short_text varchar [note: 'Brief free‑form text entered on the PO line.']
  source_of_supply varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the material is supplied internally, externally, or on consignment.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage bin where the material will be received.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s own part number for the material.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for the line.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applicable to the line.']
  under_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed percentage under the ordered quantity.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the ordered quantity.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a purchase order, representing a specific material, service, or part number being procured. Captures line number, material number, short text, quantity ordered, unit of measure, net price, delivery date, storage location, batch management flag, PPAP level required, over/under-delivery tolerance, and line-level confirmation status. Enables granular spend tracking and goods receipt matching at the part level. Sourced from SAP MM PO item table (EKPO).'
}

Table procurement.supplier_contract {
  supplier_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier contract record.']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Contracts embed quality control requirements; referencing the control plan ties contractual obligations to the actual quality control plan.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the contract.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Contractual spend must be mapped to a GL account for accruals and expense recognition.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier party associated with the contract.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Supplier contracts are scoped to specific vehicle programs, enabling contract compliance and program‑level spend tracking.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contract received formal approval.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Chronological notes of significant changes, approvals, or exceptions.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or industry standards the contract must satisfy (e.g., IATF 16949, ISO 14001).']
  contract_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the contract purpose (e.g., direct material, indirect services, capital expenditure).']
  contract_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description summarizing the purpose and scope of the contract.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic version of the signed contract document.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the contract by the organization or supplier.']
  contract_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the functional or product areas covered by the contract.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract based on its pricing and quantity structure.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number tracking amendments to the contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency used for contract amounts.']
  delivery_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of agreed delivery cadence, lead times, and sequencing.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract expires or is scheduled to end; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally binding.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the contract.']
  is_master_agreement boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this contract serves as a framework (master) agreement for multiple release orders.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO country code where the contract is executed or enforced.']
  last_amended_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent amendment to contract terms.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the contract record.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition code (e.g., NET30, NET60).']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing penalties for late delivery, quality breaches, or other defaults.']
  price_escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing any price adjustment mechanisms tied to indices or time.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contract renews automatically, requires manual action, or does not renew.']
  supplier_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the buyer must notify the supplier before terminating the contract.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of the contract over its full term.']
  volume_commitment_quantity varchar [note: 'Total quantity of goods or services the buyer commits to purchase under the contract.']
  volume_commitment_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the volume commitment (e.g., pieces, kilograms).']

  Note: 'Long-term procurement contract (outline agreement) with a supplier covering pricing, volume commitments, delivery schedules, quality requirements, and commercial terms for direct or indirect materials. Captures contract type (value contract, quantity contract, scheduling agreement), validity period, target value, release order documentation requirement, price escalation clauses, penalty terms, and contract status. Supports blanket PO releases and scheduling agreement delivery lines. Sourced from SAP MM contract (EKKO with doc type MK/WK).'
}

Table procurement.scheduling_agreement_line {
  scheduling_agreement_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the scheduling agreement line record.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Scheduling agreement lines are generated from purchase orders; linking to purchase_order provides parent reference and removes redundant order number fields.']
  scheduling_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent scheduling agreement header to which this line belongs.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Scheduling agreements schedule deliveries of specific SKUs; linking to SKU master enables MRP and capacity planning.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change or amendment affecting this line.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity that has been confirmed by the supplier for the delivery date.']
  confirmed_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the confirmed quantity.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the schedule line record was created in the system.']
  cumulative_received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity received to date for this line across all deliveries.']
  cumulative_received_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the cumulative received quantity.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the price.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for the delivery of the scheduled quantity.']
  delivery_window_end date [note: 'End date of the allowed delivery window for this line.']
  delivery_window_start date [note: 'Start date of the allowed delivery window for this line.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the schedule line becomes effective (often same as delivery_date).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Optional end date after which the schedule line is no longer valid.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms defining freight responsibilities for the delivery.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing delivery responsibilities.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a quality inspection is required for this line.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the line is currently backordered.']
  is_late boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the scheduled delivery is past its delivery date without receipt.']
  last_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent receipt for this line was recorded.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the schedule line.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the scheduling agreement, defining order of delivery lines.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the schedule line.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the material.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant or warehouse where the delivery is scheduled.']
  price_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Agreed price for one unit of the material on this line.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of quality inspection for the received material.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional notes or comments on the line.']
  schedule_line_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the line is a firm commitment or a forecasted quantity.']
  scheduled_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material planned for delivery on the delivery date.']
  scheduled_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the scheduled quantity (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  supplier_material_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-specific part number for the material.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage for this line.']

  Note: 'Delivery schedule line within a scheduling agreement (JIT/JIS delivery plan) specifying the exact quantity and delivery date for a specific part number at a specific plant. Captures schedule line number, delivery date, scheduled quantity, confirmed quantity, cumulative received quantity, and schedule line category (firm, forecast). Critical for JIT and JIS production supply in automotive assembly operations. Sourced from SAP MM scheduling agreement delivery schedule (EKET).'
}

Table procurement.procurement_goods_receipt {
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the goods receipt record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who posted the goods receipt.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier (vendor) from whom the goods were received.']
  receipt_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who posted the goods receipt.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Goods receipt confirmation must reference the specific inbound shipment for traceability and audit compliance.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Goods receipt posting updates stock balances; FK to SKU master ensures correct inventory item is credited.']
  accounting_document_number varchar [note: 'Financial accounting document generated for the receipt.']
  accounting_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year of the accounting document.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for the received material, if applicable.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the receipt cost is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods receipt record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total value of the receipt before taxes and discounts.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality inspection lot generated for this receipt.']
  invoice_match_status varchar [note: 'Three‑way match status between PO, receipt, and invoice.']
  is_blocked_stock boolean [note: 'True if the receipt created blocked stock (movement type 103).']
  is_quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a quality inspection is mandatory for this receipt.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code (e.g., 101 = standard receipt, 103 = blocked stock).']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net value after taxes and discounts.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant or warehouse where receipt took place.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the receipt was posted to financial accounting.']
  procurement_goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receipt.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center associated with the receipt.']
  purchase_order_item varchar [note: 'Line item number of the purchase order related to this receipt.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number against which the goods receipt is posted.']
  quality_inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quality inspection for the receipt.']
  quantity_received decimal(15,3) [note: 'Amount of material received, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the goods receipt (e.g., GR document number).']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the goods receipt event occurred in the plant.']
  receipt_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receipt (e.g., standard receipt, return, stock transfer).']
  slip_number varchar [note: 'Physical slip or document number associated with the receipt.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the record (e.g., SAP).']
  source_system_load_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was loaded from the source system into the lakehouse.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Specific storage location within the plant where goods were placed.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the receipt.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is measured.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the goods receipt record.']
  vendor_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number supplied by the vendor for the received goods.']

  Note: 'Record of physical receipt of materials or services at an Automotive plant or warehouse against a purchase order or scheduling agreement. Captures GR document number, posting date, material document number, plant, storage location, received quantity, unit of measure, batch number, quality inspection lot reference, GR slip number, and movement type (101 standard GR, 103 GR blocked stock). Triggers inventory update and initiates three-way match for invoice verification. Sourced from SAP MM material document (MSEG/MKPF).'
}

Table procurement.supplier_invoice {
  supplier_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for supplier invoice.']
  acquisition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_acquisition. Business justification: Supplier Invoice for a newly acquired asset must be reconciled with the Asset Acquisition record for financial and asset register consistency.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Invoice cost allocation to cost center is required for budgeting and profitability analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AP invoice posting must reference a GL account to record expense in the ledger.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier who issued the invoice.']
  accounting_document_number varchar [note: 'GL document number generated for the invoice.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if supporting documents are attached.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why invoice is blocked from payment.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes attached to the invoice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter currency code of the invoice amount.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the invoice.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be made according to payment terms.']
  ean_number varchar [note: 'European Article Number for the supplied product (if applicable).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert invoice currency to company currency.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year of the invoice posting.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the goods receipt linked to the invoice.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes and discounts.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Internal order identifier for project-related expenses.']
  invoice_currency_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount in invoice currency before conversion.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the supplier issued the invoice.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Unique invoice number assigned by supplier.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether invoice is for goods, services, or both.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of line items on the invoice.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount payable after taxes and discounts.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when payment was made.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle the invoice.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the payment transaction.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the invoice.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Terms defining payment schedule (e.g., Net 30).']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was posted to the general ledger.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center linked to the invoice.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Associated purchase order identifier.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Reference number used by supplier for internal tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the invoice data.']
  supplier_address_line varchar [note: 'Street address of the supplier.']
  supplier_city varchar [note: 'City where the supplier is located.']
  supplier_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the supplier.']
  supplier_invoice_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the invoice.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing tax jurisdiction and rate.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the invoice is tax-exempt.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of PO/GR/Invoice three-way match.']
  tolerance_check_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of tolerance check on price/quantity differences.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s VAT registration number.']

  Note: 'Supplier-submitted invoice for goods or services delivered to Automotive, processed through SAP MM invoice verification (Logistics Invoice Verification - LIV). Captures invoice number, supplier invoice reference, invoice date, posting date, gross amount, tax amount, currency, payment terms, due date, three-way match status (PO/GR/Invoice), tolerance check result, blocking reason, and payment status. Enables accounts payable processing and spend actuals capture. Sourced from SAP MM invoice document (RBKP/RSEG).'
}

Table procurement.procurement_invoice_line {
  procurement_invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the procurement_invoice_line data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  supplier_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_invoice. Business justification: A procurement invoice line is a line item of a supplier invoice; linking enables proper hierarchy and removes the need for duplicate invoice reference fields.']

  Note: 'Individual line item on a supplier invoice, matched against a specific PO line and goods receipt line. Captures invoice line number, material or service description, invoiced quantity, unit price, net amount, tax code, PO reference line, GR reference, quantity variance, price variance, and line-level match status. Supports granular three-way match reconciliation and dispute resolution at the part/service level.'
}

Table procurement.spend_category {
  spend_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for spend_category reference lookup (role: REFERENCE_LOOKUP)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the category.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Spend category GL mapping supports standardized chart‑of‑accounts reporting.']
  parent_category_spend_category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent category in the hierarchy.']
  primary_spend_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the category.']
  spend_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the spend category, aligned with UNSPSC or internal taxonomy.']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the spend category.']
  commodity_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of related spend categories (e.g., Materials, Services, Capital).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the category record was created in the system.']
  direct_indirect_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the spend category pertains to direct production parts or indirect materials/services.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the category definition becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the category definition expires or is superseded (null if still active).']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to external taxonomy or system identifier (e.g., SAP material group).']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth level of the category within the spend taxonomy (root = 0).']
  is_leaf boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the category has no child categories.']
  sourcing_strategy varchar [note: 'Recommended sourcing approach for the category.']
  spend_category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the spend category purpose and scope.']
  spend_category_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the category applicability.']
  spend_category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the spend category.']
  spend_category_type varchar [note: 'Classification of spend type for budgeting and reporting.']
  strategic_flag boolean [note: 'True if the category is considered strategic for sourcing and supplier management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the category record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the category record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Hierarchical classification taxonomy for procurement spend, organizing all purchased goods and services into commodity groups and sub-categories aligned with UNSPSC or Automotive-internal commodity codes. Captures category code, category name, parent category, commodity group, direct/indirect flag, strategic category flag, responsible category manager, and applicable sourcing strategy. Enables spend analytics, category management, and supplier rationalization. Reference data used across all procurement transactions.'
}

Table procurement.approved_vendor_list {
  approved_vendor_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each approved vendor list record.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the material or part covered by the AVL.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier authorized in this AVL.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying supplier contract governing the AVL.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the approval becomes effective (start of validity).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the AVL entry indicating if the supplier is approved, conditionally approved, or disqualified.']
  avl_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the approved vendor list entry.']
  backup_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'True if the supplier serves as a backup source for the material.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier-material combination meets regulatory and IATF 16949 requirements.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the AVL record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the AVL record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for any price‑related fields in the AVL.']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date the AVL record was initially created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the AVL entry expires or must be re‑validated.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Most recent date the AVL entry was reviewed for continued eligibility.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard supplier lead time in calendar days for the material.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered per purchase order for this AVL.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the AVL entry.']
  ppap_approval_level varchar [note: 'Production Part Approval Process level achieved for the supplier-material combination.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'True if the supplier is designated as a preferred source for the material.']
  price_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum unit price the supplier may charge for the material under this AVL.']
  quality_rating_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum quality rating percentage the supplier must maintain for this material.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'True if additional regulatory approval (e.g., EPA, NHTSA) is required for the material.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory AVL reviews.']
  single_source_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why a single source supplier is required.']
  source_list_flag boolean [note: 'True if the AVL entry is reflected in SAP MM source list (EORD).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the AVL record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the AVL record.']

  Note: 'Formally approved supplier-material combination (AVL) authorizing a specific supplier to supply a specific part number or commodity to Automotive plants. Captures AVL entry date, approval status (approved, conditional, disqualified), PPAP approval level, quality rating threshold, preferred supplier flag, backup supplier flag, single-source justification, and expiry date. Governs which suppliers are eligible to receive purchase orders for specific materials. Integrates with SAP MM source list (EORD).'
}

Table procurement.info_record {
  info_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the purchasing info record.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the material or service category covered by this info record.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier (vendor) associated with this info record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the info record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the info record becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the info record expires (null if open‑ended).']
  info_record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the info record, e.g., IR00001234.']
  info_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the info record.']
  info_record_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the info record (standard, contract, or framework agreement).']
  last_price_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent price change.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned delivery lead time in days from order to receipt.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered under this info record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions related to the info record.']
  order_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity.']
  over_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed percentage over the ordered quantity.']
  price_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Negotiated price per unit of the material or service.']
  price_valid_from date [note: 'Start date of the price validity period.']
  price_valid_until date [note: 'End date of the price validity period.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the material is procured as direct or indirect.']
  reminder_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before price expiry to trigger a reminder.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which the info record originates (e.g., SAP_MM).']
  under_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed percentage under the ordered quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the info record.']
  vendor_evaluation_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score (0‑5) reflecting supplier performance for this material.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the record.']

  Note: 'Purchasing info record storing the commercial relationship between a supplier and a specific material or service category, including the last negotiated price, price validity period, planned delivery time, over/under-delivery tolerance, reminder days, and vendor evaluation score. Serves as the default pricing and delivery condition source when creating purchase orders. Sourced from SAP MM purchasing info record (EINE/EINA/ME11).'
}

Table procurement.supplier_evaluation {
  supplier_evaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier evaluation record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or system that performed the evaluation.']
  evaluator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or system that performed the evaluation.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier being evaluated.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes from the evaluator providing context or observations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier meets regulatory and internal compliance requirements.']
  cost_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) for commercial performance (price competitiveness, invoice accuracy).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the evaluation record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) for delivery performance (OTD, schedule adherence).']
  development_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) for development dimension (responsiveness, engineering change support).']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'Date on which the evaluation was performed.']
  evaluation_method varchar [note: 'How the evaluation data was collected (automated, manual, or mixed).']
  evaluation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier for the evaluation (e.g., EV‑2024‑Q1).']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the evaluation record.']
  evaluation_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or trigger of the evaluation (annual, quarterly, or ad‑hoc).']
  evaluation_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the evaluation record to support revisions.']
  failed_criteria_count varchar [note: 'Number of criteria where the supplier fell below the required threshold.']
  invoice_accuracy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of invoices that match purchase order terms without discrepancies.']
  on_time_delivery_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries arriving on or before the promised date.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated score (0‑100) summarising supplier performance.']
  passed_criteria_count varchar [note: 'Number of criteria where the supplier met or exceeded the threshold.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the period covered by the evaluation.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the period covered by the evaluation.']
  ppm_defect_rate decimal(6,3) [note: 'Parts‑per‑million defect rate observed during the period.']
  price_variance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage difference between contracted price and actual invoiced price.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) for quality dimension (PPM defect rate, PPAP compliance).']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Suggested strategic response based on evaluation results.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification derived from evaluation outcomes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the evaluation data (e.g., SAP_QM, Ariba).']
  supplier_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the supplier (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2).']
  supplier_region varchar [note: 'ISO‑3 country code of the supplier\'s primary operating location.']
  total_criteria_count varchar [note: 'Number of evaluation criteria applied to the supplier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the evaluation record.']

  Note: 'Periodic formal assessment of supplier performance across quality (PPM defect rate, PPAP compliance), delivery (OTD - On-Time Delivery, schedule adherence), commercial (price competitiveness, invoice accuracy), and development (responsiveness, engineering change support) dimensions. Captures evaluation period, overall score, sub-scores by criterion, evaluator, evaluation method (automated from SAP QM/MM or manual), and recommended action (maintain, develop, reduce, disqualify). Supports supplier development programs and strategic sourcing decisions.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_development_plan {
  supplier_development_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier development plan.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved the plan.']
  apqp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: Supplier development is coordinated with APQP phases; linking ensures the development plan references the correct APQP plan.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the development engineer responsible for executing the plan.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: REQUIRED: Development plans are created to satisfy a particular compliance obligation, linking the plan to that obligation for tracking.']
  primary_supplier_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the development engineer responsible for executing the plan.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier for which the development plan is created.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier contract associated with the development plan.']
  actual_metric_timestamp date [note: 'Date when the actual metric was recorded.']
  actual_metric_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Current measured value of the primary KPI.']
  actual_progress_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current progress of the plan expressed as a percentage.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan was approved.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current budget status of the plan.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the latest version change of the plan.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance requirements linked to the plan (e.g., IATF 16949).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Planned end date of the plan (nullable for open‑ended).']
  investment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary investment committed to the development plan.']
  investment_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code of the investment amount (ISO 4217).']
  investment_type varchar [note: 'Source of the investment funding.']
  is_capex boolean [note: 'Indicates if the investment is classified as capital expenditure.']
  is_oem boolean [note: 'Indicates if the supplier is an original equipment manufacturer.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent plan review.']
  milestone_schedule varchar [note: 'Structured representation of milestones and their target dates (e.g., JSON or delimited string).']
  next_milestone_date date [note: 'Date of the upcoming milestone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes or comments regarding the plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External reference code for the development plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the development plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the development plan indicating its primary focus.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the plan for change tracking.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the development plan.']
  related_sop_date date [note: 'Target date for Start of Production readiness associated with the plan.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment level for the plan.']
  supplier_development_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the development plan.']
  target_metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary KPI the plan aims to improve (e.g., defect_rate, on_time_delivery).']
  target_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target metric (e.g., %, ppm).']
  target_metric_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target value for the primary KPI.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plan.']

  Note: 'Structured improvement program initiated for a supplier requiring capability development, quality improvement, or capacity expansion. Captures plan type (quality improvement, capacity ramp, technology development, cost reduction), target metrics, milestone schedule, responsible development engineer, investment commitment (Automotive-funded tooling or CapEx), and plan status. Tracks progress against agreed KPIs and supports SOP (Start of Production) readiness for new model launches.'
}

Table procurement.capex_requisition {
  capex_requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the capital expenditure requisition.']
  acquisition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_acquisition. Business justification: Capex Requisition leads to Asset Acquisition; linking provides end‑to‑end visibility of capital spend and asset onboarding.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Budget line item that funds the requisition.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the expense.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Department responsible for the request.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who created the CapEx requisition.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Capex request posting requires a GL account to capture capital expenditures in the ledger.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the asset will be used.']
  primary_capex_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who created the CapEx requisition.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Supplier selected to provide the capital asset.']
  project_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for overseeing the capital project.']
  supplier_quote_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier quote linked to the requisition.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Supplier selected to provide the capital asset.']
  afe_number varchar [note: 'Number of the AFE that authorizes the capital spend.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level required to approve the requisition.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the requisition received final approval.']
  approved_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget amount approved for the requisition.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Category of the asset to be acquired (e.g., tooling, equipment, facility).']
  asset_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the asset for planning purposes.']
  capex_requisition_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing business justification and details of the request.']
  capex_requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the requisition.']
  capitalized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the expense will be capitalized on the balance sheet.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status with internal and external regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  depreciation_life_years varchar [note: 'Number of years over which the asset will be depreciated.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation of the asset begins.']
  environmental_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the environmental impact of the capital project.']
  estimated_investment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected total cost of the capital investment.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funds for the capital expenditure.']
  justification_type varchar [note: 'Reason for the CapEx request such as new model development, replacement, or capacity expansion.']
  procurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to acquire the asset.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Code of the capital project or program to which the requisition belongs.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if regulatory approval is needed for the asset.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required regulatory approvals.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the requisition was initially submitted.']
  required_by_date date [note: 'Date by which the asset is needed for production.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the requisition for tracking and communication.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the capital investment.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the approved budget.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the requisition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the requisition record.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'WBS element code linking the requisition to project accounting hierarchy.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure procurement request for tooling, dies, fixtures, production equipment, or plant infrastructure required for new model programs or capacity expansion. Captures CapEx project code, asset category, justification type (new model, replacement, capacity), estimated investment, approved budget, plant, cost center, WBS element, AFE (Authorization for Expenditure) number, approval authority level, and CapEx status. Distinct from standard MRO or direct material purchase requisitions due to asset capitalization workflow.'
}

Table procurement.tooling_order {
  tooling_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the tooling order.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tooling orders are charged to a cost center for internal cost tracking and budgeting.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant that will use the tooling.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the tooling.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the capital project associated with the tooling.']
  tooling_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.tooling_registry. Business justification: Tooling Order fulfillment updates the Tool Registry; linking ensures tool lifecycle and cost are tracked from order to asset.']
  amortization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to amortize the tooling cost.']
  amortization_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied for per‑piece amortization.']
  amortization_term_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which the tooling cost is amortized.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the tooling order.']
  capital_expenditure_flag boolean [note: 'True if the tooling order is treated as a capital expenditure.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Certification(s) (e.g., ISO 9001) associated with the tooling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tooling order record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Projected end date of depreciation (nullable for open‑ended).']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation for the tooling asset.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation of the tooling begins.']
  expected_life_months varchar [note: 'Planned useful life of the tooling in months.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net amount payable after tax and any discounts.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the tooling order by the procurement system.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tooling order was created in the source system.']
  ownership varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the tooling is owned by the OEM or the supplier.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Part number that the tooling will be used to produce.']
  ppap_tryout_date date [note: 'Date of the Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) tryout for the tooling.']
  record_source_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp from the source system when the record was created or last updated.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of any regulatory approvals required for the tooling.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or MES system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the tooling order.']
  tool_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned identifier for the specific tool.']
  tooling_location varchar [note: 'Plant or facility of the supplier where the tooling will be manufactured.']
  tooling_order_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the tooling order.']
  tooling_type varchar [note: 'Category of tooling requested (e.g., die, mold, jig).']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary value of the tooling order before taxes and discounts.']
  tryout_schedule_date date [note: 'Planned date for the first production tryout of the tooling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the tooling order record.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Warranty duration provided by the supplier for the tooling.']

  Note: 'Purchase order specifically for supplier-owned or Automotive-owned tooling (dies, molds, jigs, fixtures, gauges) required for production of specific parts. Captures tooling type, tool number, associated part numbers, tooling ownership (OEM-owned vs supplier-owned), amortization method (lump sum vs per-piece), tooling location (supplier plant), tryout schedule, PPAP tryout date, and tooling approval status. Tooling orders follow a distinct procurement and capitalization workflow separate from production part POs.'
}

Table procurement.service_entry_sheet {
  service_entry_sheet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the service entry sheet record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service entry costs are allocated to cost centers for service profitability analysis.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Warranty service entry must reference the vehicle owner (customer) to process warranty claims and track service costs per customer.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site where the service was performed.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the service.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier contract governing the service.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center responsible for the service execution.']
  acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the service acceptance process.']
  acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service entry sheet was accepted.']
  acceptor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who accepted the service entry sheet.']
  account_assignment varchar [note: 'Account assignment object (e.g., cost object, internal order) used for financial posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service entry sheet record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Optional reference to an external system identifier.']
  invoice_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service entry sheet has triggered invoice verification (true) or not (false).']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value after tax and any discounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments entered by users.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the service purchase order linked to this entry sheet.']
  quantity_performed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual quantity of service units delivered (e.g., hours, days).']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the service type performed.']
  service_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the service was performed (principal business event date).']
  service_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service execution completed.']
  service_entry_sheet_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the service performed.']
  service_entry_sheet_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the service entry sheet.']
  service_line_item varchar [note: 'Identifier of the line item on the service purchase order.']
  service_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the service was performed.']
  service_order_number varchar [note: 'External service order number associated with the entry sheet.']
  service_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service execution began.']
  sheet_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the service entry sheet by the source system.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the service total.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the service for tax calculation purposes.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the services before taxes and discounts.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the performed quantity is measured.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the service entry sheet record.']

  Note: 'Confirmation document for services rendered by a supplier against a service purchase order, used for indirect procurement (maintenance services, contract labor, engineering services, logistics services). Captures service entry sheet number, service PO reference, service line items, actual quantities performed, acceptance status, acceptor name, acceptance date, and value accepted. Triggers invoice verification for service-based procurement. Sourced from SAP MM service entry sheet (ML81N/ESLH/ESLL).'
}

Table procurement.procurement_price_condition {
  procurement_price_condition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the procurement_price_condition data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: A procurement price condition is defined at the contract level; linking to supplier_contract provides contract context and avoids redundant contract reference columns.']

  Note: 'Negotiated pricing conditions and surcharges applicable to a supplier-material combination, including base price, freight surcharge, packaging surcharge, raw material index adjustment, currency exchange surcharge, and volume discount tiers. Captures condition type, validity period, scale basis, scale quantity, condition value, and currency. Enables accurate PO pricing and supports price variance analysis. Sourced from SAP MM condition records (KONP/KONH/MEK1).'
}

Table procurement.procurement_delivery_schedule {
  procurement_delivery_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the procurement_delivery_schedule data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: A delivery schedule always belongs to a purchase order; adding purchase_order_id FK to procurement_delivery_schedule links the schedule to its parent order.']

  Note: 'Inbound delivery schedule communicated to suppliers specifying exact delivery windows, quantities, and dock assignments for JIT and JIS supply to assembly plants. Captures schedule type (firm, forecast, JIT call-off), delivery date and time window, dock door assignment, carrier, packaging type, number of containers, and schedule transmission status (EDI 830/862 sent). Enables synchronized inbound logistics and production line feeding. Distinct from scheduling agreement lines which are the contractual commitment.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_nonconformance {
  supplier_nonconformance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the supplier nonconformance record.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier that provided the nonconforming part.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the nonconformance record.']
  closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the nonconformance was formally closed.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate action taken to contain the defect (e.g., quarantine, segregation).']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier must implement a corrective action.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the supplier corrective action request (SCAR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the nonconformance record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code that classifies the type of defect (e.g., surface, dimensional, functional).']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the observed defect.']
  detection_point varchar [note: 'Location in the value chain where the nonconformance was detected.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the defect was first recorded.']
  eight_d_report_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated 8‑D problem‑solving report.']
  nonconformance_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier assigned to the nonconformance event, used in reporting and communication.']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Brief textual description of the part.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number (or SKU) of the component that failed inspection.']
  ppm_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'Defect rate expressed in parts per million.']
  rejected_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units rejected because of the defect.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the root cause.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the underlying cause of the defect.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact rating of the nonconformance on product quality and safety.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the nonconformance record (e.g., SAP_QM, Ariba).']
  total_inspected_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of units inspected during the detection event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Formal record of a supplier quality nonconformance event, capturing the rejected part number, defect description, defect code, PPM count, detection point (incoming inspection, line-side, warranty), containment action, 8D report reference, root cause category, corrective action due date, and closure status. Triggers supplier corrective action requests (SCAR) and feeds into supplier evaluation scoring. Integrates with SAP QM quality notification (QM10).'
}

Table procurement.spend_transaction {
  spend_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the spend transaction record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the spend.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Cost‑to‑serve analysis requires attributing each spend transaction to the owning customer for margin and service level reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the spend.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Spend Transaction for equipment maintenance must be allocated to the specific equipment; linking supports cost accounting and OEE analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Spend transaction must be posted to a GL account for accurate financial reporting.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier invoice linked to this spend.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site where the spend was incurred.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase order that generated this spend transaction.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier associated with the spend.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center tracking of spend transactions supports segment profitability reporting.']
  purchase_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase requisition that originated the transaction.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: REQUIRED: Spend transactions are allocated to specific regulatory requirements for compliance budgeting and audit traceability.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Spend analytics and cost‑to‑serve reports aggregate spend by SKU; FK to SKU master provides consistent material identification.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier contract governing the transaction.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Provides \'Financial reporting per vehicle order\' linking spend entries to the exact vehicle order they support.']
  accounting_date date [note: 'Date used for posting the transaction to the general ledger.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the spend transaction.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the spend transaction received approval.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Standard commodity classification code (e.g., UNSPSC) for the purchased item.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center code used for allocating the spend.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the spend transaction record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the transaction.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency conversion rate to the corporate reporting currency at transaction time.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period or month within the fiscal year (e.g., 2023Q1).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year (e.g., FY2023) to which the spend is allocated.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'GL account number used for financial posting of the spend.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount including net amount and tax.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date printed on the supplier invoice.']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the spend line is blocked for payment or further processing.']
  is_service_line boolean [note: 'True if the spend line represents a service rather than a material.']
  is_tax_included boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether tax is already included in the unit price.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net spend amount (quantity × unit price) after discounts, before tax.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which payment for the transaction was made or scheduled.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms (e.g., Net 30, 2% 10 Net 30).']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal project or initiative funded by the spend.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of material or service units purchased.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Number of the goods receipt document linked to the spend.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system of the spend record.']
  source_system_load_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the source system record was loaded into the lakehouse.']
  spend_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of spend (direct, indirect, capital expenditure, or MRO).']
  spend_subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the spend category (e.g., raw material, tooling, consulting).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax component applied to the net amount.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code applied to the transaction.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the spend transaction (e.g., invoice or receipt number).']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the spend transaction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spend transaction was recorded in the source system.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'UOM for the quantity (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit before taxes and discounts, in transaction currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the spend transaction record.']

  Note: 'Actuals-level spend record aggregating confirmed procurement spend by supplier, commodity, plant, cost center, and period. Captures transaction date, PO reference, invoice reference, material or service description, spend category, quantity, unit price, net spend amount, currency, tax amount, payment date, and spend classification (direct, indirect, CapEx, MRO). Serves as the foundational spend cube for category management, supplier rationalization, and savings tracking. Derived from confirmed goods receipts and posted invoices.'
}

Table procurement.savings_initiative {
  savings_initiative_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each savings initiative.']
  buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal buyer or procurement owner responsible for the initiative.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal buyer or procurement owner responsible for the initiative.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier associated with the savings initiative.']
  actual_savings_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Realized monetary savings after the initiative has been completed.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the initiative received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the initiative.']
  baseline_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual spend amount for the commodity/supplier before any savings actions, expressed in the functional currency.']
  commodity varchar [note: 'Primary commodity or material group targeted by the initiative (e.g., steel, electronics).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center responsible for the spend and savings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the savings initiative record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary values in this record.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the initiative is considered closed; may be null for open‑ended initiatives.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the initiative becomes active and savings can start to be realized.']
  initiative_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the savings initiative for reporting and search.']
  initiative_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier or code assigned to the savings initiative.']
  initiative_type varchar [note: 'Category of the initiative indicating the nature of the expected savings.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or rationale related to the initiative.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or site linked to the initiative.']
  program_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the initiative is assigned for budgeting and reporting.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region of the initiative.']
  savings_initiative_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the initiative.']
  savings_validation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the reported savings are accurate and compliant.']
  target_savings_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected monetary savings to be achieved by the initiative.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Procurement cost savings project tracking negotiated price reductions, value engineering changes, demand reduction, specification changes, or payment term improvements. Captures initiative type (hard savings, cost avoidance, payment terms), commodity, supplier, baseline spend, target savings amount, actual realized savings, savings validation method, responsible buyer, program year, and initiative status (identified, approved, in-negotiation, realized, cancelled). Supports procurement KPI reporting and annual savings target tracking.'
}

Table procurement.payment_run {
  payment_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment run batch.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payment run aggregates payments to a GL account for cash‑flow and reconciliation reporting.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier whose invoices are being paid in this run.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the run.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the payment run.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run received approval.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch within the automatic payment program.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments or remarks entered by the processor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment run.']
  currency_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert amounts to the reporting currency.']
  discount_taken_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any discount was applied in the run.']
  execution_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the automatic payment program finished processing.']
  execution_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the automatic payment program started processing.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total processing fees charged for the run.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'True if the run was generated by an automated schedule, false if manually triggered.']
  number_of_invoices varchar [note: 'Count of supplier invoices cleared in this payment run.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to execute the payments.']
  payment_run_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description or notes for the payment run.']
  processing_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the run in seconds (end minus start).']
  reference varchar [note: 'Reference number from external banking or clearing house, if applicable.']
  run_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the payment run was executed.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the payment run by the source system.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment run.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the payment run was initiated.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the run (e.g., regular scheduled, ad‑hoc, or correction).']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the funds are expected to settle in the supplier\'s account.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current settlement status of the payment run.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source ERP/system that generated the payment run record.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status, e.g., error details.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount included in the payment run.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate discount amount applied across all invoices in the run.']
  total_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall discount percentage applied across the run.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all invoice gross amounts before any discounts or fees.']
  total_invoices_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate gross amount of all invoices before discounts.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount paid after discounts, taxes, and fees.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment run record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the payment run record.']

  Note: 'Automated payment execution batch for supplier invoice payments, capturing payment run date, payment method (ACH, wire, check, virtual card), bank account, total payment amount, currency, number of invoices cleared, discount taken, and payment run status. Represents the final step of the procure-to-pay cycle. Sourced from SAP FI automatic payment program (F110/PAYR). Distinct from individual supplier invoices — this is the payment execution event.'
}

Table procurement.approval {
  approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the procurement approval record.']
  approval_delegated_to_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or user to whom the approval was delegated.']
  approval_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee or user who performed the approval.']
  auto_approval_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule that triggered automatic approval.']
  delegated_to_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or user to whom the approval was delegated.']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the underlying procurement document (e.g., requisition ID, PO number).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee or user who performed the approval.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the approval group or committee responsible for this step.']
  approval_action varchar [note: 'Result of the approval decision for this step.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable identifier for the approval workflow instance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the approval record.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approval decision was recorded.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that was approved in this step.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role of the individual responsible for this approval step.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documents are attached to the approval record.']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'Link to the location of attached supporting documentation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments provided by the approver regarding the decision.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the approval must satisfy regulatory or internal compliance requirements.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual description of specific compliance criteria applicable to this approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approval record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  deadline date [note: 'Date by which the approval decision must be completed.']
  decision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided for the approval decision, especially if rejected.']
  delegation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval was delegated to another individual (True) or performed by the assigned approver (False).']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of procurement document subject to this approval.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for escalating the approval to a higher authority.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approval request expires if no action is taken.']
  is_auto_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval was granted automatically by system rules.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the procurement item is classified as critical for business operations.']
  is_final_approval boolean [note: 'True if this step represents the final required approval in the workflow.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the delegation of authority policy applied to this approval.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the approval policy.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0-100) associated with the procurement request.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system where the approval record was created.']
  spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount requested for the procurement document subject to approval.']
  step_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this approval step within the overall workflow.']
  threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Spend threshold that triggers this approval level according to delegation of authority policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the approval record.']

  Note: 'Workflow approval record for procurement documents requiring authorization (purchase requisitions, purchase orders, contracts, CapEx requisitions) based on spend thresholds, commodity type, or strategic significance. Captures document reference, approval step sequence, approver role, approver identity, approval action (approved, rejected, escalated), approval timestamp, comments, and delegation flag. Supports Automotive\'s procurement governance and delegation of authority (DOA) policy enforcement.'
}

Table procurement.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vendor']
  parent_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor (parent_vendor_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the vendor\'s headquarters or main office.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, etc.) for the vendor.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s bank account number for payments.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution holding the vendor\'s account.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the vendor\'s primary location is situated.']
  commodity_specialization varchar [note: 'Specific commodity or product line the vendor specializes in.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the vendor\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vendor record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure approved for the vendor.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for vendor transactions.']
