  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier for the vendor organization.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the vendor record is no longer active (null for indefinite).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the vendor record becomes active.']
  iatf16949_cert_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s IATF 16949 certification.']
  iatf16949_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds a valid IATF 16949 certification.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing delivery responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  iso14001_cert_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s ISO 14001 certification.']
  iso14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds ISO 14001 environmental management certification.']
  iso9001_cert_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s ISO 9001 certification.']
  iso9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is ISO 9001 certified.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from order placement to delivery for the vendor.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the vendor entity for regulatory and tax purposes.']
  max_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered from the vendor in a single transaction.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from the vendor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the vendor.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was first onboarded into the procurement system.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the vendor (e.g., Net 30).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the vendor\'s primary address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Language used for communication with the vendor.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary vendor contact used for electronic communication.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact for the vendor.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the primary vendor contact.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the vendor master data (e.g., SAP, Ariba).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the vendor\'s primary location.']
  supplier_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the vendor\'s primary offering.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'International bank identifier for cross‑border payments.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is exempt from sales tax.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the vendor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the vendor record.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'Value‑Added Tax registration number for the vendor.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal business name of the vendor as used in contracts and purchase orders.']
  vendor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor master record.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor based on its role in the supply chain.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the vendor.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for vendor. '
}

Table procurement.program_supplier_contract {
  program_supplier_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ProgramSupplierContract association']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vehicle program']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the contract']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Type of contract (e.g., supply, service, joint‑development)']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is terminated']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes effective']
  price_escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Clause describing price escalation mechanisms']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual agreement between a vehicle development program and a supplier. Each record links one vehicle_program to one supplier and stores contract‑specific data that is relevant only to that pairing.. Existence Justification: Vehicle development programs engage multiple suppliers for parts, services, and components, and each supplier can provide for many different vehicle programs. The business creates and manages a contract for each program‑supplier pairing, capturing contract details such as number, type, effective dates, and price escalation clauses.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_supply_agreement {
  procurement_supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the supply_agreement association']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier master']
  spare_parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spare parts catalog entry']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Lead time in days for this supplier to deliver the part']
  unit_price decimal [note: 'Agreed price for the part from this supplier']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual relationship between a supplier and a spare part catalog entry. Each record links one supplier to one spare part and stores attributes that are specific to that supplier‑part combination, such as price and lead time.. Existence Justification: A supplier can provide many spare parts, and each spare part can be sourced from multiple approved suppliers. The business actively manages a supply agreement for each supplier‑part pair, capturing unit price, lead time, and contract identifier. These agreements are created, updated, and retired as part of procurement planning.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_regulatory_compliance {
  supplier_regulatory_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the SupplierRegulatoryCompliance association']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory requirement']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which the supplier must achieve compliance for this requirement']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the supplier for the requirement (e.g., compliant, non‑compliant, pending)']
  evidence_document_path varchar [note: 'File path or URL to the evidence document supporting compliance']

  Note: 'This association captures the compliance relationship between a supplier and a regulatory requirement, including the supplier\'s compliance status, supporting evidence, and deadline for each requirement.. Existence Justification: Suppliers must meet multiple regulatory requirements, and each regulatory requirement applies to many suppliers. The compliance team actively tracks the status, evidence, and deadlines for each supplier‑requirement pair, creating a many‑to‑many operational entity.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_document {
  procurement_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for procurement_document']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the document.']
  predecessor_procurement_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on procurement_document (predecessor_procurement_document_id)']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier associated with the document.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the document received final approval.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the document\'s sensitivity.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the contract in months.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description or summary of the document\'s purpose.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount percentage granted on the gross amount.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier or reference number assigned to the document.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement document (e.g., contract, purchase order, invoice, statement of requirements).']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the document.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the document becomes legally binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the document expires or is no longer in effect; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  file_checksum varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 checksum used to verify file integrity.']
  file_path varchar [note: 'Storage location or URI of the digital document file.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the document was formally issued by the organization.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after taxes and discounts.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be received.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed for the document.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'Classification of spend (direct, indirect, capital expenditure, operating expenditure).']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the document was received by the counter‑party or internal stakeholder.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is set for automatic renewal.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiry that renewal notice must be issued.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the document must be retained before archival or deletion.']
  signed_by varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual who signed the document.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date the document was signed by the authorized signatory.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the document.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the document before taxes, discounts, or adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for procurement_document. Referenced by document_id.'
}

Table procurement.approval_group {
  approval_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for approval_group']
  parent_approval_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on approval_group (parent_approval_group_id)']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating the hierarchy of the group within the approval chain.']
  approval_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that must be met or exceeded before the group can approve a purchase.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the approval group within the organization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approval group record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department that owns and maintains the approval group.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, and rules of the approval group.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the approval group becomes active for use in procurement processes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the approval group is retired or no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  escalation_policy varchar [note: 'Policy that defines how approvals are escalated when thresholds are exceeded.']
  is_system_defined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval group is a standard system‑defined group (true) or a custom user‑defined group (false).']
  max_approvers varchar [note: 'Maximum number of approvers allowed in this group for a single transaction.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the approval group used in procurement workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the approval group.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region to which the approval group applies.']
  requires_multi_approval boolean [note: 'True if the group requires more than one approver for a transaction.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the approval group.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the approval group indicating its primary purpose (e.g., financial, operational).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the approval group record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who initially created the approval group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for approval_group. Referenced by group_id.'
}

Table procurement.auto_approval_rule {
  auto_approval_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for auto_approval_rule']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or team responsible for maintaining the rule.']
  superseded_auto_approval_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on auto_approval_rule (superseded_auto_approval_rule_id)']
  applies_to_purchase_category varchar [note: 'Code of the purchase category (e.g., raw_materials, MRO) to which the rule applies.']
  applies_to_supplier_segment varchar [note: 'Supplier segment (e.g., tier‑1, strategic) for which the rule is relevant.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating which approval tier the rule triggers (e.g., 1 = front‑line, 2 = manager).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the threshold amount.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule purpose and logic.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes active and enforceable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires; null indicates an open‑ended rule.']
  escalation_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum hours allowed before the rule escalates to the next approval level.']
  execution_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the rule has been evaluated.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule must be satisfied for a purchase to be approved.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last time the rule was evaluated against a purchase request.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments about the rule.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority determining rule evaluation order; lower numbers evaluate first.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the rule across systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rule used in procurement configuration.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule logic (e.g., amount threshold, supplier rating).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold value used by amount‑based rules (e.g., maximum purchase amount).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for auto_approval_rule. Referenced by auto_approval_rule_id.'
}

Table quality.apqp_plan {
  apqp_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the APQP plan record.']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: APQP planning requires referencing the homologation approval to align release dates with regulatory clearance.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: APQP planning is defined per vehicle model; linking APQP plan to model enables model‑specific quality goals and gate tracking.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: APQP planning is executed per vehicle program; linking aligns plans with program metadata and eliminates program_name duplication.']
  actual_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured parts-per-million defect rate observed after validation.']
  actual_release_date date [note: 'Actual date when the product was released to production.']
  apqp_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the APQP process for the plan.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'Category of the APQP plan based on product scope.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current IATF 16949 compliance status of the APQP plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the APQP plan record was created in the system.']
  documentation_link varchar [note: 'URL or path to the primary APQP documentation repository.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the APQP plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the APQP plan expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  eop_date date [note: 'Date when series production ended for the vehicle/program.']
  gate_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the milestone gate was completed.']
  gate_due_date date [note: 'Planned due date for the milestone gate.']
  gate_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the milestone gate.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the APQP plan.']
  milestone_gate varchar [note: 'Specific APQP milestone gate associated with the plan.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Description of actions taken to mitigate identified risks.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year (calendar year) of the vehicle model associated with the APQP plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal owner or manager of the APQP plan.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part or component covered by the APQP plan (e.g., OEM part number).']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the APQP plan, often used in documentation and reporting.']
  quality_goal_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target parts-per-million defect rate defined for the APQP plan.']
  responsible_team varchar [note: 'Team or department accountable for executing the APQP plan.']
  risk_assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment for the APQP plan was performed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification for the APQP plan.']
  sop_date date [note: 'Date when series production started for the vehicle/program.']
  target_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned PPM level to achieve during the APQP process.']
  target_release_date date [note: 'Planned date for the product release associated with the APQP plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the APQP plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the APQP plan record.']

  Note: 'Advanced Product Quality Planning master record governing the structured quality planning process for new vehicle programs and components. Tracks APQP phases (concept, design, process, validation, launch), milestone gates, responsible teams, and completion status per program/part. Aligns with IATF 16949 requirements and links to engineering change and SOP timelines.'
}

Table quality.fmea {
  fmea_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the FMEA record.']
  action_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FMEA action owner must be a responsible employee; linking enables tracking accountability in corrective action reports.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vehicle component or part to which the FMEA applies.']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: FMEA is performed based on design specs; linking provides traceability for risk analysis reports.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: FMEA must be linked to applicable regulatory requirements to verify compliance of failure modes.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date the recommended action was actually completed.']
  analysis_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the FMEA analysis, used for tracking across systems.']
  analysis_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the FMEA is a Design FMEA (DFMEA) or Process FMEA (PFMEA).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the FMEA analysis received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or manager who approved the FMEA analysis.']
  cause varchar [note: 'Underlying cause(s) that could lead to the failure mode.']
  control_effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Score (1‑10) assessing how well the current control detects or prevents the failure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FMEA record was first created in the system.']
  current_control varchar [note: 'Description of existing controls that mitigate the failure mode.']
  detection_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the detection rating.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method or technique used by the current control to detect the failure.']
  detection_rating varchar [note: 'Score (1‑10) indicating the ability of current controls to detect the failure before it reaches the customer.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the FMEA analysis is considered active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the FMEA analysis is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  failure_effect varchar [note: 'Impact of the failure mode on vehicle performance, safety, or compliance.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Description of the way the component could potentially fail.']
  fmea_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FMEA record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the FMEA.']
  occurrence_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the occurrence rating.']
  occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Likelihood score (1‑10) that the cause will occur.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Proposed corrective or preventive action to reduce severity, occurrence, or improve detection.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version of the FMEA record after each update or re‑analysis.']
  rpn varchar [note: 'Calculated risk priority number (S × O × D) used to prioritize corrective actions.']
  severity_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the severity rating.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Severity score (1‑10) reflecting seriousness of the failure effect.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the FMEA record (e.g., Teamcenter PLM, SAP QM).']
  subsystem varchar [note: 'Higher‑level subsystem or system grouping the component belongs to (e.g., Powertrain, Chassis).']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the recommended action should be completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the FMEA record.']

  Note: 'Failure Mode and Effects Analysis master record capturing potential failure modes, their effects, causes, current controls, severity/occurrence/detection ratings, and Risk Priority Numbers (RPN) for vehicle components, subsystems, and manufacturing processes. Supports both Design FMEA (DFMEA) and Process FMEA (PFMEA) per AIAG-VDA FMEA methodology. Tracks recommended actions and revised RPN after corrective actions.'
}

Table quality.control_plan {
  control_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the quality control plan.']
  associated_fmea_id varchar [note: 'Link to the Process FMEA that this control plan supports.']
  associated_pfmea_fmea_id varchar [note: 'Link to the Process FMEA that this control plan supports.']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: Control plans are derived from design specifications; FK enables automatic extraction of spec requirements for quality control.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: CONTROL PLAN EXECUTION: Measurement equipment used in control plans must be tracked for maintenance and traceability.']
  primary_control_inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed inspection plan referenced by this control plan.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Control plans are line‑specific for process control; required for the Production Control Review report.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Control plans are project‑tracked in the WBS for capital budgeting and cost control.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the control plan.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Employee identifier who approved the control plan.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the change control record associated with this plan.']
  control_method varchar [note: 'Statistical or inspection method used to control the characteristic.']
  control_plan_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the purpose, scope, and key characteristics of the control plan.']
  control_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control plan per IATF 16949.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control plan record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan is retired or superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan becomes effective for production.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control plan is mandatory for the operation.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the characteristic.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the control characteristic (e.g., mm, psi). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mm|cm|inch|mm2|g|kg|psi|kpa|percent — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the control plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the control plan (e.g., CP‑2024‑001).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of manufacturing operation the plan applies to.']
  reaction_plan varchar [note: 'Defined corrective actions if a measurement falls outside specification limits.']
  responsible_function varchar [note: 'Organizational function accountable for the control plan.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the control plan.']
  sample_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which sampling is performed.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units inspected per sampling event as defined by the plan.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Desired nominal value for the controlled characteristic.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the control plan record.']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the characteristic.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the employee who authored the control plan.']

  Note: 'Quality control plan defining the process controls, inspection methods, measurement systems, reaction plans, and control characteristics for each manufacturing operation or assembly step. Links to PFMEA and inspection plans. Specifies sample sizes, frequencies, control methods (SPC, attribute, visual), and responsible functions per IATF 16949 requirements.'
}

Table quality.inspection_plan {
  inspection_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection plan.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inspection Cost Allocation report assigns inspection plan expenses to the responsible cost center.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.functional_location. Business justification: INSPECTION PLANNING: Plans are assigned to specific functional locations (stations) to satisfy ISO/TS 16949 inspection scheduling.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Inspection plans are defined per production line to meet line‑specific quality standards; used in the Line Inspection Planning process.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Textual definition of pass/fail conditions.']
  applicable_process varchar [note: 'Manufacturing process to which the inspection plan applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the inspection plan was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the approver.']
  associated_quality_system varchar [note: 'Quality system or methodology linked to the plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection plan record was first created.']
  criticality_level varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the inspection characteristic.']
  department_responsible varchar [note: 'Organizational department owning the inspection plan.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the inspection plan expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the inspection plan becomes effective.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the inspection is performed.']
  gauge_type varchar [note: 'Specific gauge or instrument type used.']
  inspection_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the inspection activity.']
  inspection_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose and scope of the inspection plan.']
  inspection_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection plan.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection is performed by automated equipment.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent plan review.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the measurement.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for tolerances and results.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year (calendar year) of the vehicle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part or component the inspection plan targets.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the inspection plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the inspection plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan (e.g., incoming material, in‑process, final vehicle).']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where the plan is executed.']
  regulatory_compliance varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory framework(s) for the inspection.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'True if the inspection results must be formally approved.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory reviews.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for change tracking.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units to be inspected per batch.']
  tolerance_lower decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable measurement value.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable measurement value.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the inspection plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection plan.']
  vehicle_model varchar [note: 'Vehicle model designation for which the plan is applicable.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label of the inspection plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the inspection plan.']

  Note: 'Detailed inspection plan specifying the characteristics to be measured, measurement methods, gauges/instruments, tolerances, sample sizes, and acceptance criteria for incoming material, in-process, and final vehicle inspections. Supports incoming material inspection (IQC), in-process quality gates, and PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection). Linked to SAP QM inspection lots.'
}

Table quality.inspection_lot {
  inspection_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inspection lot record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed or supervised the inspection.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: LOT INSPECTION: Inspection lots are performed on specific equipment; linking supports maintenance planning and defect attribution.']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Required for the \'Inbound Part Inspection Lot\' report that ties each inspection lot to the specific inbound part received.']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inspection plan governing this lot.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed or supervised the inspection.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Traceability: inspection lot results must be linked to the specific shipment for recall, OTD and quality KPI reporting.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Inspection lots are executed per part; adding sku_master_id supports the Lot Traceability Report linking lots to the specific SKU.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Needed for the \'Supplier Inspection Summary\' dashboard aggregating inspection lots per supplier.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Supports the Inspection Lot Traceability report, associating each lot with its vehicle order to ensure inspection results are linked to the correct customer order.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier associated with the material or component.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action must be completed.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action must be initiated for the identified defect.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection lot record was first created in the system.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Final usage decision for the lot after inspection.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for any defect identified during inspection.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the defect.']
  inspection_lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection lot.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the inspection.']
  inspection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the inspection was triggered (e.g., SOP, PPAP, random sampling).']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection activity was performed.']
  lot_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the inspection lot was closed after final decision.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the inspection lot, often matching the SAP lot number.']
  lot_origin varchar [note: 'Origin of the lot (e.g., goods receipt, production order).']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lot being inspected (e.g., incoming material, work‑in‑process assembly, finished vehicle, prototype).']
  measurement_result_status varchar [note: 'Result of the measurement against specification limits.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the measured value (e.g., mm, V).']
  measurement_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantitative measurement recorded during inspection (e.g., dimension, voltage).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the inspection took place.']
  quantity_accepted varchar [note: 'Number of units/items that passed inspection criteria.']
  quantity_inspected varchar [note: 'Total number of units/items examined in this inspection lot.']
  quantity_rejected varchar [note: 'Number of units/items that failed inspection criteria.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes captured by the inspector.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the inspected unit, if applicable.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the originating document such as goods receipt, production order, or delivery.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection lot record.']
  work_center varchar [note: 'Work center responsible for the production step associated with the lot.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a quality inspection event triggered for a batch of incoming materials, WIP assemblies, or finished vehicles. Captures lot origin (goods receipt, production order, delivery), inspection type, quantity inspected, inspection start/end timestamps, assigned inspector, and overall usage decision (accept, reject, conditional release). Sourced from SAP QM inspection lot management.'
}

Table quality.inspection_result {
  inspection_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection result record.']
  characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspected characteristic or measurement point.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the inspection.']
  gauge_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the measurement instrument used.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection lot (header) to which this result belongs.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the inspection.']
  instrument_gauge_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the measurement instrument used.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or observations recorded by the inspector.']
  cp_value decimal(5,3) [note: 'Cp value calculated for the characteristic.']
  cpk_value decimal(5,3) [note: 'Cpk value calculated for the characteristic.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection result record was created.']
  deviation_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric difference between measured value and nearest specification limit.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement was taken.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates if the characteristic is critical to vehicle safety or compliance.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the characteristic within the inspection lot.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the characteristic.']
  measurement_accuracy decimal(10,4) [note: 'Accuracy specification of the measurement instrument.']
  measurement_condition varchar [note: 'Environmental condition during measurement.']
  measurement_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at time of measurement.']
  measurement_location varchar [note: 'Physical location on the vehicle or part where measurement was taken.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the measurement.']
  measurement_resolution decimal(10,4) [note: 'Resolution of the measurement instrument.']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the measurement data (e.g., internal sensor, external lab).']
  measurement_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in Celsius at time of measurement.']
  measurement_tool varchar [note: 'Tool or equipment used for the measurement.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated uncertainty of the measurement.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measured value (e.g., millimeter, kilogram).']
  measurement_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value recorded for the characteristic.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the record (e.g., active, archived).']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Pass/fail outcome of the measurement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the characteristic.']

  Note: 'Individual characteristic measurement result recorded during an inspection lot. Captures the measured value or attribute outcome, tolerance limits, pass/fail status, measurement instrument used, and inspector ID for each characteristic within an inspection plan. Supports SPC data collection and statistical analysis of process capability (Cp, Cpk).'
}

Table quality.defect_record {
  defect_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the defect record.']
  apqp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality plan or inspection plan associated with the defect.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: COPQ report requires assigning each defect to the responsible cost center for financial impact analysis.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Defect records are frequently generated from dealer warranty claims; linking enables root‑cause analysis and dealer‑specific defect trends.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Required for the Defect Impact Analysis report that ties each defect record to the originating sales opportunity to assess customer exposure and trigger recalls.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Defect records must reference the canonical part master for root‑cause analysis; removes redundant part_number/name.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external supplier linked to the defect, if applicable.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Defect records are tied to the specific production order where the defect was detected, essential for the Defect Tracking Dashboard.']
  recall_defect_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.recall_defect_report. Business justification: Defect records that trigger a recall are linked to the recall defect report for regulatory tracking.']
  reported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Defect records must capture the employee who reported the issue for corrective‑action workflow.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Supplier performance: each defect is tied to the shipment that delivered the part, enabling root‑cause and logistics KPI analysis.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part or component associated with the defect.']
  telemetry_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.telemetry_event. Business justification: Root cause analysis links each defect to the specific telemetry event that triggered it, required for the Defect Investigation Report.']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: REQUIRED: Recall & warranty management needs to associate each defect record with the owning customer\'s vehicle record to trigger service actions.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Defect records are tied to a specific VIN; FK to vin_registry allows traceability from defect to exact vehicle for warranty and recall actions.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate action taken to contain the defect and prevent further impact.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed action to eliminate the root cause of the defect.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action must be completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the defect record was initially created in the system.']
  defect_category varchar [note: 'Stage of the product lifecycle where the defect was detected.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the defect for tracking and reporting.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the non‑conformance or quality issue.']
  defect_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the defect record.']
  defect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the defect by its origin or nature.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the defect was first observed.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method or channel through which the defect was discovered.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the defective item after evaluation.']
  investigation_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigation was concluded.']
  investigation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the root‑cause investigation began.']
  is_repeat_defect boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this defect has been observed previously.']
  location_zone varchar [note: 'Vehicle zone or assembly area where the defect was identified (e.g., body, powertrain).']
  ppm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Defect rate expressed in parts per million for quality metrics.']
  quantity_affected varchar [note: 'Number of units or parts impacted by the defect.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis result describing why the defect occurred.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level indicating impact on safety, performance, or compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system of the defect data (e.g., Apriso MES, SAP QM).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the defect record.']
  vin varchar [note: 'VIN of the vehicle in which the defect was found.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a quality defect or non-conformance identified at any stage — incoming material, in-process assembly, final inspection, or field. Captures defect code, defect description, location on vehicle (zone/component), severity classification, detection method, quantity affected, containment action taken, and disposition (rework, scrap, use-as-is). Sourced from Apriso/Dassault MES quality control module.'
}

Table quality.spc_chart {
  spc_chart_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the SPC chart configuration record.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: SPC ANALYSIS: Charts are generated from data collected by a particular piece of equipment; linking enables root‑cause tracing.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: SPC charts monitor key characteristics of each work center; required for the Work Center SPC Dashboard.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SPC chart configuration was formally approved.']
  center_line decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target center line (mean) for the SPC chart.']
  chart_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the SPC chart for a specific CTQ characteristic.']
  chart_type varchar [note: 'Type of SPC chart used to monitor the characteristic (e.g., X‑bar/R, p‑chart).']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level applied when setting control limits, expressed as a percentage.']
  control_limit_lcl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical lower control limit for the chart, calculated from process data.']
  control_limit_ucl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical upper control limit for the chart, calculated from process data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SPC chart record was first created in the system.']
  ctq_characteristic varchar [note: 'Name of the critical‑to‑quality (CTQ) characteristic being monitored (e.g., bolt torque, paint thickness).']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplies the raw measurement data for the SPC chart.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the SPC chart configuration becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the SPC chart configuration is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the monitored characteristic (e.g., mm, psi). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mm|cm|mmHg|psi|rpm|kg|%|mm/s — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for any extra information or remarks.']
  ocap varchar [note: 'Defined actions to be taken when the chart signals an out‑of‑control condition.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the manufacturing process step where the SPC chart is applied.']
  process_step_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the process step (e.g., ASSEMBLY_01).']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Total number of samples used to compute the chart\'s control limits.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which samples are taken for the SPC chart.']
  spc_chart_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context for the chart.']
  spc_chart_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SPC chart configuration.']
  statistical_method varchar [note: 'Statistical technique used to calculate control limits (e.g., Shewhart, EWMA).']
  subgroup_size varchar [note: 'Number of units in each sampling subgroup used for chart calculations.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Desired target value for the characteristic (center line target).']
  tolerance_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower acceptable tolerance bound for the characteristic.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper acceptable tolerance bound for the characteristic.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the SPC chart record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the chart configuration, incremented on changes.']

  Note: 'Statistical Process Control chart master record defining the SPC monitoring configuration for a critical-to-quality (CTQ) characteristic at a specific process step. Specifies chart type (X-bar/R, X-bar/S, p-chart, c-chart), control limits (UCL, LCL), center line, subgroup size, sampling frequency, and out-of-control action plan (OCAP). Supports IATF 16949 SPC requirements.'
}

Table quality.spc_data_point {
  spc_data_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each SPC measurement data point.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator who supervised or initiated the measurement.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production machine or equipment that generated the measurement.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant floor location where the measurement occurred.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator who supervised or initiated the measurement.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant.']
  quality_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective action record triggered by this out‑of‑control event.']
  spc_chart_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SPC chart to which this data point belongs.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: SPC data points are collected per work center for statistical analysis; needed for the SPC Data Collection process.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the material or component being inspected.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the sensor was last calibrated.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled calibration date for the sensor.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for operator or system notes.']
  cp_value decimal(6,3) [note: 'Cp value calculated for the process at the time of measurement.']
  cpk_value decimal(6,3) [note: 'Cpk value calculated for the process at the time of measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SPC data point record was created in the system.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the raw data quality after validation.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Code of the defect identified when the point is out of control.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the measurement relates to a critical quality characteristic.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot identifier associated with the measurement.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical lower control limit for the SPC chart.']
  lower_warning_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Optional warning limit below which a warning is raised (but not out‑of‑control).']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement was captured automatically or manually.']
  measurement_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the measurement within the subgroup.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement was recorded on the shop floor.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the SPC variable being measured.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the measurement value.']
  measurement_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value recorded for the SPC variable.']
  out_of_control_flag boolean [note: 'True when the data point violates any Western Electric rule.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'OEM part number (BOM reference) linked to the SPC measurement.']
  pp_value decimal(6,3) [note: 'Pp value representing short‑term capability.']
  ppk_value decimal(6,3) [note: 'Ppk value representing short‑term capability index.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Production step or operation where the measurement was taken.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Sequential sample number within the subgroup.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units in the subgroup used for this measurement.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sensor device that recorded the measurement.']
  sensor_type varchar [note: 'Technology type of the sensor used for the measurement.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the measurement was taken.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system that supplied the measurement.']
  spc_data_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the data point record.']
  subgroup_number varchar [note: 'Logical subgroup index used for SPC calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SPC data point record.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical upper control limit for the SPC chart.']
  upper_warning_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Optional warning limit above which a warning is raised (but not out‑of‑control).']
  western_electric_rule_violation varchar [note: 'Specific Western Electric rule that was violated, if any.']

  Note: 'Individual SPC measurement data point recorded against an SPC chart for a specific subgroup or sample. Captures measured value, subgroup number, timestamp, operator, shift, machine, and out-of-control signal flags (Western Electric rules violations). Enables real-time process monitoring and capability trending (Cp, Cpk, Pp, Ppk).'
}

Table quality.quality_ppap_submission {
  quality_ppap_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality_ppap_submission data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  apqp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: PPAP submission belongs to an APQP plan; linking via apqp_plan_id enables reuse of part_number and plan details.']
  supply_ppap_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_ppap_submission. Business justification: PPAP Submission Tracking links the quality PPAP record to the original supply PPAP submission for compliance audits.']

  Note: 'Production Part Approval Process submission record for a supplier part or internally manufactured component. Tracks PPAP level (1-5), submission reason (new part, engineering change, tooling change), submission date, approval status, and the 18 PPAP elements status (design records, PFMEA, control plan, MSA, capability study, etc.). Supports IATF 16949 supplier quality management.'
}

Table quality.quality_corrective_action {
  quality_corrective_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality_corrective_action data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record managing the structured problem-solving process for quality escapes and non-conformances. Supports 8D (Eight Disciplines) and 5-Why methodologies. Captures problem statement, containment actions (D3), root cause analysis (D4/5-Why), permanent corrective actions (D5), verification of effectiveness (D6), and preventive action deployment (D7). Tracks open/closed status and due dates.'
}

Table quality.audit {
  audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quality audit record.']
  auditee_location_plant_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key for the audited facility.']
  auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the auditor (links to employee master).']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit plan that defines scope, criteria, and schedule.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Audit Expense Tracking links each audit to the cost center bearing the audit cost for financial statements.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Dealer Quality Audit process requires linking each audit to the specific dealership for compliance reporting and corrective action tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the auditor (links to employee master).']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: AUDIT VERIFICATION: Audits often verify calibration and condition of equipment; FK enables audit‑to‑asset traceability.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key for the audited facility.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Quality audits are performed per production line; the Line Audit Summary report depends on this linkage.']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_standard. Business justification: Quality standards are referenced by audits; adding standard_id to quality_audit creates the needed lookup.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Quality audits are scheduled per vehicle program; linking provides program context for audit findings and compliance reporting.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the audit was performed.']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the audit was performed internally, by an external party, or a third‑party provider.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable audit identifier used in reports and communications.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the functional or geographic scope covered by the audit.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit (system, process, product, or layered process audit).']
  auditee_location varchar [note: 'Code of the plant, facility, or site where the audit was conducted.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor responsible for the audit.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit was formally closed.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Current status of audit closure activities.']
  compliance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific standard(s) (e.g., IATF 16949, ISO 9001) the audit addresses.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completing all required corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any findings require corrective action.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current progress status of corrective actions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  documents_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the audit record.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time spent conducting the audit, measured in minutes.']
  findings_major varchar [note: 'Count of findings classified as major.']
  findings_minor varchar [note: 'Count of findings classified as minor.']
  findings_severe varchar [note: 'Count of findings classified as severe (high impact).']
  findings_total varchar [note: 'Total number of findings identified during the audit.']
  non_conformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of non‑conformances identified during the audit.']
  non_conformance_severity varchar [note: 'Highest severity level among identified non‑conformances.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form observations, comments, or remarks recorded by the auditor.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing overall audit performance.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing body or standard applicable to the audit.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating derived from audit findings.']
  score_category varchar [note: 'Qualitative categorisation of the overall audit score.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']

  Note: 'Quality system and process audit record capturing planned and unplanned audits conducted at plants, supplier facilities, or dealer service centers. Tracks audit type (system, process, product, layered process audit — LPA), audit scope, audit date, lead auditor, findings count by severity, overall audit score, and closure status. Supports IATF 16949 internal audit requirements and customer-specific requirements (CSR).'
}

Table quality.quality_audit_finding {
  quality_audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality_audit_finding data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: Audit finding records belong to a quality audit; adding quality_audit_id creates the required parent relationship.']
  remote_diagnostic_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.remote_diagnostic_session. Business justification: Audit findings often reference remote diagnostic sessions used to verify software compliance; this link supports the Audit Findings Documentation.']

  Note: 'Individual finding or observation raised during a quality audit. Captures finding type (major non-conformance, minor non-conformance, observation, opportunity for improvement), clause reference (IATF 16949, ISO 9001, customer-specific), finding description, evidence, assigned owner, due date for corrective action, and closure verification status. Linked to the parent quality audit record.'
}

Table quality.quality_pdi_inspection {
  quality_pdi_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality_pdi_inspection data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: PDI inspection is part of a quality audit process; linking to quality_audit provides audit context.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Pre‑Delivery Inspection results are recorded per vehicle; linking to the connected vehicle enables traceability for OTA readiness and compliance reporting.']
  logistics_pdi_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_pdi_inspection. Business justification: PDI hand‑over: quality PDI record must reference the logistics PDI execution for final vehicle acceptance.']

  Note: 'Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) record for a finished vehicle prior to handover to dealer or customer. Captures VIN, inspection date, plant or PDI center, inspector ID, checklist version, total defects found, defect categories (cosmetic, functional, safety), rework required flag, and final pass/fail disposition. Supports dealer network quality standards and customer satisfaction targets.'
}

Table quality.ncap_test_result {
  ncap_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the NCAP test result record.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: NCAP test results are safety audit outcomes; linking to quality_audit captures audit association.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change (ECN) associated with the vehicle configuration.']
  ncap_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ncap_submission. Business justification: NCAP test results feed directly into the NCAP submission report required for safety certification.']
  test_operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: NCAP test results require the test operator\'s identity for regulatory traceability.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key for the vehicle under test.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key for the vehicle under test.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination against applicable safety regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that supplied the test result data.']
  homologation_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory approval status of the vehicle based on the test outcome.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle variant tested.']
  rating_scale varchar [note: 'Scale definition used for the star rating (e.g., 5‑star).']
  raw_score_front decimal(5,2) [note: 'Raw numeric score for the frontal offset impact test.']
  raw_score_pole decimal(5,2) [note: 'Raw numeric score for the pole impact test.']
  raw_score_side decimal(5,2) [note: 'Raw numeric score for the side impact test.']
  star_rating varchar [note: 'Overall star rating assigned by the NCAP program (0‑5).']
  test_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes captured by the test team.']
  test_condition varchar [note: 'Environmental and operational conditions during the test (e.g., temperature, humidity).']
  test_configuration varchar [note: 'Detailed configuration of the vehicle for the test (e.g., seat position, restraint settings).']
  test_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the crash test was performed.']
  test_facility varchar [note: 'Name of the laboratory or proving ground where the test took place.']
  test_observer varchar [note: 'Name of the independent observer or auditor present during testing.']
  test_program varchar [note: 'The NCAP program under which the vehicle was tested.']
  test_report_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the testing authority for the test report.']
  test_result_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the test result record.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Category of crash test performed.']
  test_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the test protocol used.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the test result record.']
  vehicle_variant varchar [note: 'Specific model variant or trim level of the vehicle.']
  vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique vehicle identifier used to link the test result to a specific vehicle.']

  Note: 'New Car Assessment Programme (NCAP) and regulatory crash/safety test result record for a vehicle model variant. Captures test program (Euro NCAP, NHTSA, ANCAP), test date, test facility, vehicle configuration tested, test type (frontal offset, side impact, pole test, pedestrian, AEB), raw scores, star ratings, and homologation status. Supports regulatory compliance and product safety reporting.'
}

Table quality.wltp_test_result {
  wltp_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each WLTP test result record.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: WLTP test results are part of quality audit evidence; linking to quality_audit provides audit traceability.']
  test_operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: WLTP test data must be linked to the operator to satisfy emissions reporting standards.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle (or prototype) that was tested.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle (or prototype) that was tested.']
  ambient_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity measured during the test.']
  ambient_pressure_kpa decimal(6,2) [note: 'Atmospheric pressure at the test site.']
  ambient_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature recorded during the test.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result record was first created in the system.']
  electric_range_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum distance the electric or plug‑in hybrid powertrain can travel on a full charge under WLTP conditions.']
  extra_high_speed_co2_g_per_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'CO2 emissions measured during the extra‑high‑speed phase of the WLTP cycle.']
  extra_high_speed_fuel_l_per_100km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Fuel consumption recorded in the extra‑high‑speed phase.']
  high_speed_co2_g_per_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'CO2 emissions measured during the high‑speed phase of the WLTP cycle.']
  high_speed_fuel_l_per_100km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Fuel consumption recorded in the high‑speed phase.']
  homologation_certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date the homologation certificate was issued.']
  homologation_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official certificate identifier issued after successful type‑approval.']
  homologation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the homologation process for this test result.']
  low_speed_co2_g_per_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'CO2 emissions measured during the low‑speed phase of the WLTP cycle.']
  low_speed_fuel_l_per_100km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Fuel consumption recorded in the low‑speed phase.']
  medium_speed_co2_g_per_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'CO2 emissions measured during the medium‑speed phase of the WLTP cycle.']
  medium_speed_fuel_l_per_100km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Fuel consumption recorded in the medium‑speed phase.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the vehicle model is designated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations recorded by the test operator.']
  nox_mg_per_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured nitrogen‑oxides emissions for the test cycle.']
  particulate_mg_per_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured particulate matter emissions for the test cycle.']
  powertrain_variant_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the specific powertrain configuration (e.g., engine, hybrid, electric) used in the test.']
  test_facility varchar [note: 'Name of the laboratory or proving ground where the WLTP test was conducted.']
  test_methodology varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the test was conducted on a chassis dynamometer (lab) or in‑vehicle.']
  test_result_status varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the test execution.']
  test_standard_version varchar [note: 'Version of the WLTP standard applied (e.g., WLTP v2022).']
  test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the WLTP test was performed.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the test methodology applied (e.g., WLTP, Real‑Driving Emissions (RDE), ECE).']
  total_co2_g_per_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Overall CO2 emissions for the complete WLTP cycle.']
  total_fuel_l_per_100km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Overall fuel consumption for the complete WLTP cycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the test result record.']

  Note: 'Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure (WLTP) and emissions/fuel economy test result record for a vehicle powertrain variant. Captures test date, test facility, drive cycle phases (low, medium, high, extra-high), CO2 emissions (g/km), fuel consumption (L/100km), electric range (km for EV/PHEV), NOx and particulate values, and homologation certificate reference. Supports EPA, CARB, and EU type-approval compliance.'
}

Table quality.gauge_msa {
  gauge_msa_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the gauge MSA record.']
  gauge_gauge_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the gauge or measurement device being studied.']
  gauge_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the gauge or measurement device being studied.']
  acceptability_status varchar [note: 'Result classification based on %GRR thresholds: acceptable (<10%), marginal (10‑30%), unacceptable (>30%).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the MSA study was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the MSA study results.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the MSA study.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration of the gauge prior to the study.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next calibration of the gauge.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MSA record was first created in the system.']
  gauge_msa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gauge MSA record.']
  gauge_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label of the gauge.']
  gauge_type varchar [note: 'Category of gauge based on measurement principle.']
  grr_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall %GRR result from the study.']
  measurement_range_max decimal(10,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the gauge\'s measurable range.']
  measurement_range_min decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the gauge\'s measurable range.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement used by the gauge (e.g., millimeters).']
  ndc varchar [note: 'Count of distinct measurement categories (e.g., size bins) used in the study.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the study.']
  operator_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct operators who performed measurements in the study.']
  part_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct parts measured during the study.']
  study_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the MSA study was conducted.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Type of MSA study performed on the gauge.']
  trial_count varchar [note: 'Number of measurement repetitions per operator‑part combination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the MSA record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the MSA record.']

  Note: 'Measurement System Analysis (MSA) study record for a gauge, instrument, or measurement device used in quality inspection. Captures gauge ID, study type (Gauge R&R, linearity, bias, stability), study date, number of operators, number of parts, number of trials, %GRR result, number of distinct categories (ndc), and acceptability status (acceptable <10%, marginal 10-30%, unacceptable >30%). Supports IATF 16949 MSA requirements.'
}

Table quality.gauge_master {
  gauge_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each gauge master record.']
  custodian_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Gauge custodianship is assigned to a specific employee; linking enables maintenance responsibility tracking.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: CALIBRATION: Gauges are physical equipment tracked for maintenance; linking enables calibration schedule and asset lifecycle management.']
  accuracy decimal(8,4) [note: 'Maximum permissible deviation from true value, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal barcode or RFID tag used to track the gauge in asset management systems.']
  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the most recent calibration certificate issued by the lab.']
  calibration_due_flag boolean [note: 'True when the current date is past the next_calibration_date and the gauge has not been recalibrated.']
  calibration_history_available boolean [note: 'True if detailed calibration logs are retained for this gauge.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibrations as defined by the calibration plan.']
  calibration_method varchar [note: 'Technique or standard employed to calibrate the gauge (e.g., laser interferometry, dead‑weight).']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the gauge with respect to its calibration schedule.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Quality or environmental standard that dictates calibration and usage requirements for the gauge.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the gauge master record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the gauge was placed into service.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the gauge was decommissioned or taken out of service (null if still active).']
  gauge_master_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the gauge, its application and special characteristics.']
  gauge_master_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the gauge within the quality system.']
  gauge_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier printed on the gauge.']
  gauge_type varchar [note: 'Category of measurement instrument (e.g., Coordinate Measuring Machine, caliper, torque wrench, vision system, laser tracker).']
  inspection_capability varchar [note: 'Main type of measurement the gauge provides for quality inspections.']
  is_critical_gauge boolean [note: 'True when the gauge is used for measurements that affect product safety or regulatory compliance.']
  last_calibrated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or external lab that performed the most recent calibration.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the gauge was last calibrated.']
  line varchar [note: 'Specific line or cell within the plant that uses the gauge.']
  location varchar [note: 'General area (e.g., warehouse, line, lab) where the gauge is stored or used.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that produced the gauge.']
  measurement_range_max decimal(12,4) [note: 'Highest value the gauge can accurately measure.']
  measurement_range_min decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lowest value the gauge can accurately measure.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model identifier for the gauge.']
  next_calibration_date date [note: 'Projected date for the next required calibration based on interval.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or special handling instructions for the gauge.']
