  plant varchar [note: 'Code or name of the manufacturing plant where the gauge resides.']
  purchase_date date [note: 'Calendar date the gauge was acquired by the organization.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the gauge hardware.']
  station varchar [note: 'Exact station or work‑cell where the gauge is deployed.']
  tolerance decimal(8,4) [note: 'Acceptable deviation range for measurements taken with the gauge.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the gauge\'s measurement range.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the gauge master record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty for the gauge ends.']

  Note: 'Master record for all measurement instruments, gauges, fixtures, and test equipment used in quality inspection and SPC. Captures gauge number, description, type (CMM, caliper, torque wrench, vision system), calibration interval, last calibration date, next calibration due date, calibration status, location (plant/line/station), and responsible custodian. Supports gauge calibration management per IATF 16949.'
}

Table quality.ppm_record {
  ppm_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the PPM defect rate record.']
  gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_gate. Business justification: PPM records are evaluated at quality gates; linking to quality_gate enables gate‑level analysis.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the part.']
  recorded_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PPM records must capture who entered the data for auditability and data‑quality checks.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: PPM reporting is SKU‑specific; linking to sku_master enables the Supplier Part Quality Dashboard and accurate ppm aggregation per part.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the PPM record was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the PPM record.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch associated with the PPM measurement.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or observations about the PPM record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the PPM record was first created in the system.']
  defect_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the defect cause.']
  defect_category_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects attributed to the selected defect category.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of inspection (e.g., incoming, in‑process, final, pre‑delivery).']
  is_outlier boolean [note: 'True if the PPM value is flagged as an outlier by statistical rules.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material lot linked to the PPM record.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the PPM measurement was recorded.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year the vehicle model was released.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant where the parts were produced.']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the manufacturing plant.']
  ppm_record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the PPM record.']
  ppm_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Defects per million parts (PPM) for the reporting period.']
  ppm_variance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual PPM minus target PPM; positive indicates over‑target.']
  quality_gate_passed boolean [note: 'True if the part passed the defined quality gate for the period.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country/region code where the measurement originated.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'Month (YYYY‑MM) or quarter (Qn‑YYYY) for which the PPM is calculated.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Incremental revision number of the PPM record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the PPM data.']
  target_ppm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable PPM value as defined by the quality goal.']
  total_defective_parts varchar [note: 'Number of parts that failed quality inspection in the reporting period.']
  total_parts_received varchar [note: 'Total number of parts received from the supplier or produced in‑process during the reporting period.']
  trend_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the PPM trend is improving, declining, or stable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the PPM record.']
  vehicle_model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the vehicle that uses the part.']

  Note: 'Parts Per Million (PPM) defect rate record tracking supplier or internal process quality performance over a defined period. Captures supplier or plant code, part number, reporting period (month/quarter), total parts received or produced, total defective parts, PPM value, defect category breakdown, and trend vs. target. Supports supplier quality scorecards and IATF 16949 supplier monitoring requirements.'
}

Table quality.gate {
  gate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quality gate record.']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Link to the control plan that governs this quality gate.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quality Gate Cost Allocation report tracks gate execution costs per cost center for budgeting.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: GATE OPERATION: Each quality gate station relies on specific equipment; linking supports equipment downtime and audit records.']
  gate_associated_fmea_id varchar [note: 'Link to the Failure Mode and Effects Analysis that this gate supports.']
  gate_associated_inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Link to the inspection plan that defines the detailed checks for this gate.']
  gate_fmea_id varchar [note: 'Link to the Failure Mode and Effects Analysis that this gate supports.']
  gate_inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Link to the inspection plan that defines the detailed checks for this gate.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the gate definition was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the gate definition.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the gate\'s compliance audit.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the change control record governing this gate revision.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality standard that this gate satisfies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the gate record was first created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP/MES system that supplied the gate data (e.g., SAP QM, Apriso).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the gate definition is retired (null if indefinite).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the gate definition becomes active.']
  equipment_required varchar [note: 'Specific tools or fixtures needed to execute the gate.']
  escalation_rule varchar [note: 'Procedure and responsible function if the gate fails.']
  gate_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the gate for reference in MES and PLM systems.']
  gate_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the quality gate used in reports and UI.']
  gate_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the gate definition.']
  gate_type varchar [note: 'Category of the gate indicating the kind of check performed (e.g., dimensional, functional, torque, water, roll, end‑of‑line).']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the gate check.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failure at this gate triggers a production stop.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gate is compulsory for the associated production process.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the gate definition.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the gate (e.g., planned, in‑progress, completed, closed).']
  line varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line within the plant.']
  location varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the physical location (plant, line, station) where the gate is applied.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the measured characteristic.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for quantitative criteria (e.g., mm, N·m, psi).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the gate.']
  operator_role varchar [note: 'Job role or qualification required for the operator performing the gate.']
  pass_fail_criteria varchar [note: 'Definition of the acceptance limits that determine pass or fail outcomes.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant (e.g., Plant A, Plant B).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used for scheduling and escalation (higher = more critical).']
  required_checks varchar [note: 'Narrative of the mandatory inspections or measurements that must be completed at this gate.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory reviews of the gate.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision count for the gate record.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the gate is scheduled.']
  station varchar [note: 'Specific workstation or station where the gate is executed.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target numeric value that the gate measurement should achieve.']
  tolerance decimal(12,4) [note: 'Allowed deviation from the target value (e.g., ±0.5%).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the gate record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the gate record.']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the measured characteristic.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change management of the gate definition.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the gate record.']

  Note: 'In-process quality gate master record defining the mandatory quality checkpoints within the vehicle assembly or manufacturing process. Captures gate name, gate type (dimensional check, functional test, torque audit, water test, roll test, end-of-line EOL test), location (plant, line, station), required checks, pass/fail criteria, and escalation rules. Linked to control plans and MES work order routing.'
}

Table quality.gate_result {
  gate_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each gate result record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shop‑floor operator who performed the gate scan.']
  gate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality gate where the vehicle was inspected.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shop‑floor operator who performed the gate scan.']
  trip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.trip. Business justification: Gate results during final testing are associated with the test trip record to correlate inspection outcomes with vehicle movement, needed for the Final Gate Report.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Needed for the Production Gate Tracking dashboard linking gate results to the specific vehicle order to monitor delivery schedules and quality release dates.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Gate results are captured per vehicle during production; linking to vin_registry supports gate‑by‑VIN reporting and rework analysis.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action must be initiated due to a fail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the gate result record was first created in the system.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct defects recorded during the gate inspection.']
  gate_location varchar [note: 'Plant or line code where the gate is physically located.']
  gate_status varchar [note: 'Overall result of the gate inspection; pass indicates compliance, fail indicates one or more defects.']
  gate_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the gate (e.g., assembly, paint, final).']
  inspection_result_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the detailed outcome of the inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the operator.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) summarizing overall quality performance for this gate.']
  rework_loop_count varchar [note: 'Number of rework cycles the vehicle has undergone prior to this gate.']
  scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vehicle passed the gate and was scanned.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Shift during which the gate scan occurred.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the gate result (e.g., Apriso).']
  time_at_gate_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration in seconds that the vehicle spent at the gate for inspection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the gate result record.']
  vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique 17‑character identifier assigned to the vehicle.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a vehicle or assembly unit passing through a quality gate during production. Captures VIN or assembly serial number, gate ID, scan timestamp, operator ID, shift, overall pass/fail status, number of defects found, rework loop count, and time-at-gate. Sourced from Apriso/Dassault MES shop floor traceability. Enables first-time quality (FTQ) and direct run rate calculations.'
}

Table quality.supplier_quality_event {
  supplier_quality_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier quality event.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal owner responsible for investigation.']
  investigation_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal owner responsible for investigation.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: REQUIRED: Supplier quality events trigger charge‑back invoices; FK ties event to the specific invoice for audit and payment reconciliation.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where defect was detected.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Supplier Quality Event Management uses the supply‑domain supplier master for root‑cause analysis and reporting.']
  actual_resolution_time_days varchar [note: 'Actual number of days taken to resolve the issue.']
  affected_quantity varchar [note: 'Total quantity of parts received in the shipment.']
  attached_documentation varchar [note: 'Reference to attached documents (e.g., photos, test reports).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or notes.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality standard relevant to the event.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate action taken to contain the defect.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for implementing corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned corrective actions to resolve the defect.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was created.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the defect observed.']
  defect_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units affected by the defect.']
  defect_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the defect.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the delivery note associated with the part.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to detect the defect.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the quality event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality event was recorded.']
  expected_resolution_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days to resolve the issue.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the event is classified as critical.']
  is_repeat_issue boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this defect has occurred previously for the same part/supplier.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part or material supplied.']
  quality_system varchar [note: 'Quality system or standard applicable (e.g., IATF 16949).']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the defect impacts regulatory compliance (e.g., NHTSA).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the event was resolved.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the event resolution.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating associated with the defect.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Analysis identifying the root cause of the defect.']
  sca_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Supplier Corrective Action Request.']
  sca_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal SCAR was issued to the supplier.']
  supplier_response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier must respond with corrective action.']
  supplier_response_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the supplier\'s response.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record.']

  Note: 'Supplier quality incident or deviation event record raised when a supplier-delivered part or material fails incoming inspection or causes a production disruption. Captures supplier code, part number, delivery note reference, defect description, quantity affected, severity (critical, major, minor), containment action, supplier response due date, and resolution status. Supports supplier corrective action request (SCAR) workflow.'
}

Table quality.warranty_quality_signal {
  warranty_quality_signal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the warranty quality signal record.']
  aftersales_warranty_claim_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warranty claim that generated the signal.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the direct customer complaint record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Warranty signals are tied to the specific telematics unit of the vehicle for recall actions and service planning, required by the Warranty Claim Processing workflow.']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.credit_memo. Business justification: REQUIRED: Warranty cost‑recovery process creates a credit memo to recover supplier costs; linking signal to credit_memo enables automated financial tracking.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the end‑customer owner of the vehicle.']
  dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer where the vehicle was serviced or sold.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer where the vehicle was serviced or sold.']
  field_return_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the field return record linked to the signal.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the end‑customer owner of the vehicle.']
  quality_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the corrective action plan associated with the signal.']
  root_cause_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the root cause analysis record.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Field failure analysis: warranty signals are linked to the originating shipment to identify logistics‑related issues.']
  tsb_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the TSB that triggered the signal, if applicable.']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: REQUIRED: Warranty signal processing must link to the specific customer vehicle record for follow‑up service and parts provisioning.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Warranty quality signals reference a specific vehicle VIN; FK enables correlation of field failures with original vehicle data.']
  affected_vin_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct VINs impacted by this signal.']
  associated_vehicle_model varchar [note: 'Vehicle model (e.g., Corolla, F-150) linked to the signal.']
  associated_vehicle_variant varchar [note: 'Specific variant or trim level of the vehicle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the signal record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the impact amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|JPY|GBP|CAD|AUD|CHF|CNY|INR|BRL|... — promote to reference product]']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Logical data source name or database where the raw record resides.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the quality issue was first detected in the field.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation to corrective action processes.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the observed failure or defect.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Technical classification of how the part failed (e.g., fracture, leak).']
  impact_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the quality issue.']
  is_repeat_signal boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this signal has been observed previously.']
  is_systemic boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the issue is considered systemic across multiple vehicles.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle associated with the signal.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional context or analyst comments.']
  occurrence_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the failure has been reported in the field.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the part or system linked to the signal.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'OEM part number of the component associated with the quality signal.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact severity assigned to the signal.']
  severity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric severity rating (e.g., 0.00–10.00) derived from internal scoring models.']
  signal_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the quality signal, used for tracking and reporting.']
  signal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quality signal.']
  source_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating record in the source system (e.g., claim number, return ID).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating IT system (e.g., SAP, DMS, MES) that supplied the source data.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Origin of the signal: warranty claim, field return, technical service bulletin trigger, or direct customer complaint.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the signal record.']

  Note: 'Field quality signal derived from warranty claims and field returns that indicates a potential systemic quality issue requiring engineering or manufacturing investigation. Captures signal source (warranty claim, field return, TSB trigger, customer complaint), affected part or system, failure description, field occurrence count, affected VIN population, signal severity, and escalation status to corrective action. Bridges after-sales warranty data with quality engineering.'
}

Table quality.standard {
  standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quality standard record.']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or plant scope where the standard applies.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the organization against this standard.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the standard record was first created in the system.']
  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored standard document.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Web address or repository link to the electronic version of the standard.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the standard becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the standard expires or is superseded (nullable if open‑ended).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with this standard is mandatory for the organization.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the standard (e.g., ISO, IATF, EPA, NHTSA).']
  language varchar [note: 'Language in which the standard document is written.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the standard record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the standard.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority associated with the standard (e.g., NHTSA, EPA, UNECE).']
  regulatory_section varchar [note: 'Specific section or clause of the regulation that the standard addresses.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the latest revision of the standard was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Internal revision identifier for updates to the standard document.']
  standard_code varchar [note: 'Official alphanumeric code assigned by the issuing body (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949).']
  standard_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope, purpose and content of the standard.']
  standard_type varchar [note: 'Category of the standard (e.g., quality management, environmental, safety, emissions, regulatory, process).']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the quality standard or specification.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the standard record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the standard (e.g., 1.0, 2.3).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Reference master for quality standards, specifications, and regulatory requirements applicable to the business. Captures standard code (IATF 16949, ISO 9001, ISO 14001, FMVSS, ECE regulations), standard title, issuing body, version, effective date, expiry date, applicability scope (global, regional, plant-specific), and compliance status. Used to link quality plans, audits, and test results to governing requirements.'
}

Table quality.defect_code {
  defect_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for defect_code reference table; role inferred as REFERENCE_LOOKUP, no minimum categories required.']
  affected_system varchar [note: 'Vehicle system or subsystem impacted by the defect.']
  affected_zone varchar [note: 'Physical zone of the vehicle where the defect occurs.']
  applicable_stage varchar [note: 'Process stage(s) where the defect can be recorded.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action is mandatory for this defect.']
  cost_impact_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the defect per occurrence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the defect code record was created.']
  defect_code_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the defect type.']
  defect_code_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric identifier for the defect (e.g., D001).']
  defect_code_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the defect.']
  defect_code_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the defect.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Typical method used to detect the defect.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the defect code is no longer valid (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the defect code becomes valid.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the defect code is currently active for use.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the defect code.']
  regulatory_compliance varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) the defect code complies with.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Primary cause category for the defect.']
  severity_default varchar [note: 'Default severity rating assigned to the defect.']
  typical_resolution_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days to resolve the defect after detection.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the defect code record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the defect code definition for change control.']
  warranty_implication varchar [note: 'Impact of the defect on warranty obligations.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the defect code record.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of standardized defect codes and classifications used across all quality inspection, defect recording, and warranty processes. Captures defect code, defect name, defect category (dimensional, cosmetic, functional, safety, NVH), affected system or zone, severity default, detection method, and applicable process stage (incoming, in-process, final, field). Ensures consistent defect classification across plants and suppliers.'
}

Table quality.notification {
  notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quality notification record.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Quality notifications are often addressed to a specific dealership to trigger corrective actions or safety recalls.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality engineer responsible for the case.']
  notification_assigned_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  notification_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality engineer responsible for the case.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Exception handling: quality notifications reference the shipment they affect, required for logistics exception dashboards.']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the issue was actually closed or resolved.']
  affected_material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number of the part implicated in the quality issue.']
  affected_vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'VIN of the vehicle affected by the quality problem.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents or images attached to the notification.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline for completing the corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to remediate the issue and prevent recurrence.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the notification record was created in the system.']
  defect_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the defect type.']
  department_responsible varchar [note: 'Organizational department accountable for the notification.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation applied to the notification.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the issue is deemed critical to product quality or safety.']
  is_regulatory_compliance boolean [note: 'True if the issue is subject to regulatory reporting requirements.']
  is_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issue impacts vehicle safety.']
  last_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the notification.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the notification, used for tracking and reference.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quality notification.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the quality issue.']
  originator_contact varchar [note: 'Email address of the originator for follow‑up communication.']
  originator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person or entity that raised the notification.']
  originator_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the notification was raised internally, by a customer, dealer, or supplier.']
  plant_location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where the issue originated.']
  ppm_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured defect rate associated with the issue.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the notification for handling urgency.']
  problem_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the quality issue as reported.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing authority relevant to the notification.']
  related_claim_number varchar [note: 'Warranty or insurance claim identifier associated with the notification.']
  related_order_number varchar [note: 'Sales order number linked to the quality issue, if applicable.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Summary of the identified root cause for the quality problem.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the record (e.g., SAP QM, MES).']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the issue should be resolved.']

  Note: 'SAP QM quality notification record capturing formal quality alerts, complaints, and problem reports raised internally or from customers/dealers. Captures notification type (complaint, defect, deviation), originator, affected material or vehicle, problem description, priority, assigned quality engineer, target resolution date, and status (open, in-process, completed). Sourced from SAP QM notification management module.'
}

Table quality.calibration_event {
  calibration_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the calibration event record.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or assembly line where the gauge is installed.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or assembly line where the gauge is installed.']
  gauge_gauge_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the gauge or measurement instrument being calibrated.']
  gauge_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the gauge or measurement instrument being calibrated.']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who performed the calibration.']
  as_found_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured value of the gauge before any adjustment.']
  as_left_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured value of the gauge after adjustment (post‑calibration).']
  calibration_event_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the calibration event.']
  calibration_lab varchar [note: 'Name of the internal or external laboratory that performed the calibration.']
  calibration_method varchar [note: 'Method used for calibration – internal (in‑house) or external (third‑party lab).']
  calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Standard or procedure used for the calibration (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949).']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the calibration record.']
  calibration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the calibration activity was performed.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the calibration certificate issued after successful calibration.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory or quality standard governing the calibration (e.g., IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.5).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration event record was created in the system.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference number for the calibration event.']
  gauge_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial or part number of the gauge.']
  lab_accreditation varchar [note: 'Accreditation body or standard (e.g., ISO/IEC 17025) that the calibration lab holds.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location (plant, line, or station) where the gauge is used or calibrated.']
  measurement_range_max decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum measurable value of the gauge according to its specification.']
  measurement_range_min decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum measurable value of the gauge according to its specification.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(12,4) [note: 'Stated uncertainty of the gauge measurement after calibration.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration of the gauge.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the calibration event.']
  result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the calibration activity.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the as‑found and as‑left values (e.g., mm, in, µm).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calibration event record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a gauge or measurement instrument calibration activity. Captures gauge ID, calibration date, calibration laboratory (internal or accredited external), calibration standard used, as-found condition, as-left condition, calibration result (pass/fail/adjusted), certificate number, next due date, and technician ID. Supports IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.5 measurement equipment traceability requirements.'
}

Table quality.target {
  target_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quality target record.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Quality targets are set per dealership (e.g., service CSI scores); the link supports performance scorecards and incentive eligibility.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team accountable for achieving the target.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant to which the target applies.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier whose parts or processes are covered by the target.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific production line or cell.']
  responsible_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team accountable for achieving the target.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle model or program associated with the target.']
  alignment_policy varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate quality policy or objective that this target supports.']
  baseline_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Historical performance value used as a reference point.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the target record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the quality target becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the quality target expires or is superseded.']
  measurement_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the period over which the target is measured.']
  measurement_period_start date [note: 'First day of the period over which the target is measured.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target (e.g., PPM, Percent, Rating).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments, rationale, or special conditions for the target.']
  stretch_target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Optional higher‑performance goal beyond the primary target.']
  target_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the target in external systems (e.g., SAP QM).']
  target_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the quality objective.']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the quality target.']
  target_type varchar [note: 'Category of the quality metric the target represents.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Planned numeric value for the quality metric.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the target record.']

  Note: 'Quality performance target master record defining the planned quality objectives for a plant, production line, supplier, or vehicle program over a defined period. Captures target type (PPM, FTQ, warranty rate per 1000 vehicles — R/1000, audit score, NCAP star rating), target value, stretch target, baseline value, measurement period, responsible owner, and alignment to corporate quality policy. Supports IATF 16949 quality objectives management.'
}

Table quality.defect_code_assignment {
  defect_code_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the defect_code_assignment association']
  defect_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the defect code']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the SKU master']
  defect_category varchar [note: 'Category of the defect specific to this SKU assignment']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of how the defect manifests on the SKU']
  ppm_rate decimal [note: 'Parts per million failure rate for this defect on the SKU']
  quantity_affected varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU affected by the defect']

  Note: 'Association product that records the relationship between defect codes and SKUs, capturing impact metrics for each pairing.. Existence Justification: Engineers assign defect codes to individual SKUs to capture which parts are impacted by a given quality issue. A single defect code can affect many SKUs, and a single SKU can be linked to multiple defect codes, each with its own impact metrics. The assignment is actively managed and stores attributes such as quantity affected and ppm rate.'
}

Table quality.characteristic {
  characteristic_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for characteristic']
  parent_characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on characteristic (parent_characteristic_id)']
  category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the characteristic within quality domains.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the characteristic record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the characteristic record was first created.']
  criticality_level varchar [note: 'Business impact rating of the characteristic on product quality.']
  data_type varchar [note: 'Data type of the characteristic\'s measured value.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what the characteristic measures or represents.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the characteristic becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the characteristic is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the characteristic is measured or evaluated.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to capture the characteristic value.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the characteristic (e.g., mm, kg, sec).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the quality characteristic.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the characteristic.']
  related_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable quality or regulatory standard linked to the characteristic.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating IT system or application that defines the characteristic.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the characteristic.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target or nominal value that the characteristic should achieve.']
  tolerance_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable deviation below the target value.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable deviation above the target value.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the characteristic record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the characteristic record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for characteristic. Referenced by characteristic_id.'
}

Table quality.root_cause_analysis {
  root_cause_analysis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for root_cause_analysis']
  related_root_cause_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on root_cause_analysis (related_root_cause_analysis_id)']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the corrective action was actually completed.']
  category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the root cause source.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the root cause in reports and work orders.']
  corrective_action_owner varchar [note: 'Person or team accountable for executing the corrective action plan.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned corrective actions to eliminate or mitigate the root cause.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the root cause analysis record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the root cause, its symptoms, and context.']
  detection_phase varchar [note: 'Process phase in which the root cause was first detected.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the root cause analysis definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the root cause analysis definition is retired (nullable).']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Specific failure mode linked to the root cause (e.g., "Crack in chassis").']
  impact_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the business or safety impact caused by the root cause.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the root cause (e.g., "Incorrect Torque Specification").']
  occurrence_phase varchar [note: 'Process phase where the root cause actually occurred.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name of the person or team responsible for investigating and resolving the root cause.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to address the root cause.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating associated with the root cause.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1‑5) of the impact severity of the root cause on product quality.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the root cause analysis.']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective action should be completed.']
  type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cause is systemic, occasional, or a one‑off event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the root cause analysis record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action resolved the root cause.']
  verification_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the verification activity.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for root_cause_analysis. Referenced by root_cause_analysis_id.'
}

Table quality.field_return {
  field_return_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for field_return']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer associated with the returned vehicle.']
  original_field_return_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on field_return (original_field_return_id)']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the end‑customer who owned the vehicle at the time of return.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service center that received the returned vehicle.']
  additional_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra information related to the field return.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed corrective action to address the defect.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the defect or failure observed.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the defect associated with the return.']
  gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount associated with the return before deductions.']
  labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total labor hours spent on repairing the returned item.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly labor rate applied to the repair work (in the currency of net_amount).']
  mileage_at_return varchar [note: 'Odometer reading (in miles) at the time the vehicle was returned.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final monetary amount after tax and any adjustments.']
  parts_replaced_count varchar [note: 'Number of parts replaced during the repair of the returned item.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this field return record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this field return record.']
  repair_completion_date date [note: 'Date when repair work on the returned item was completed.']
  repair_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any repair work performed on the returned item.']
  return_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the field return, used for tracking and communication.']
  return_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the vehicle or component was returned from the field.']
  return_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the field return was officially recorded.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return reason such as warranty, recall, or customer complaint.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis result for the defect leading to the return.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the field return.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the gross amount.']
  vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'Unique 17‑character identifier of the vehicle being returned.']
  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the warranty claim linked to this return, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for field_return. Referenced by field_return_id.'
}

Table logistics.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each outbound vehicle shipment.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the carrier entity.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Required for Shipment Compliance Documentation Report linking each shipment to its approved customs/compliance document.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for freight cost allocation per shipment in the Transportation Cost Allocation Report.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Shipment Creation Report to record the logistics coordinator responsible for initiating each shipment.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Required for Shipment Delivery Performance Report linking each shipment to its destination dealership; enables OTD and cost tracking per dealer.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Required for Shipment Tracking Report to associate each shipment with the primary vehicle asset for warranty and depreciation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Needed to post transportation expenses to the appropriate GL account in the General Ledger.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Parts Shipment Tracking requires linking each shipment to the specific part master to satisfy regulatory traceability and cost allocation reports.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: PO‑based inbound logistics tracking ties each shipment to the purchase order it fulfills, essential for supply chain KPI dashboards.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Direct supplier‑shipment link enables supplier on‑time delivery KPI and performance reporting for inbound parts.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Order fulfillment shipping links each shipment to its originating production order for traceability and cost accounting.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Order fulfillment tracking report ties each physical shipment to its originating sales vehicle order, essential for delivery status and revenue recognition.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Required for Shipment Planning Report to allocate vehicles by program; logistics teams need program context for routing and dealer allocation.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the truck, rail car, or vessel used for the shipment.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real timestamp when the shipment was received at the destination.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real timestamp when the shipment left the origin plant.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification result for the shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'Standard container identification number when applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for customs clearance documents.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any delay beyond the planned arrival.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location (city) of the destination dealer or yard.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the freight cost.']
  export_import_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is an export, import, or domestic movement.']
  freight_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross transportation cost before discounts or adjustments.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous goods.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International commercial term governing responsibilities and costs.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment load.']
  net_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final freight cost after discounts and adjustments.']
  number_of_units varchar [note: 'Count of individual vehicles or kits included in the shipment.']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location (city) of the origin plant.']
  origin_plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the shipment originates.']
  otd_flag boolean [note: 'True if the shipment arrived on or before the planned arrival date.']
  planned_arrival_date date [note: 'Estimated date the shipment should arrive at the destination.']
  planned_departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for shipment departure from the origin plant.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible shipment number assigned by SAP SD for tracking and reference.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment.']
  temperature_control_flag boolean [note: 'True if the shipment requires temperature‑controlled transport.']
  tracking_url varchar [note: 'Web link to real‑time shipment tracking information.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the shipment record.']
  vin_list varchar [note: 'List of VINs for all vehicles in the shipment, separated by commas.']
  volume_cbm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cargo volume of the shipment in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Core master record for each outbound vehicle shipment from plant to dealer or distribution point. Captures shipment origin (plant/compound), destination (dealer/port/yard), transport mode (rail, truck, vessel, CKD/SKD), shipment status, planned and actual departure/arrival dates, OTD tracking, and associated VINs. Primary operational entity for finished vehicle logistics. Sourced from SAP SD outbound delivery and MES traceability.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_leg {
  shipment_leg_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each shipment leg record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Multi‑modal shipment leg tracking requires a FK to the carrier entity for each leg.']
  destination_facility_functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility where the leg terminates.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility where the leg originates (plant, yard, port, etc.).']
  goods_movement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_movement. Business justification: Enables Shipment Leg Cost Allocation: reconciles each logistics leg with the corresponding inventory goods movement for cost analysis.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent shipment (transaction header) to which this leg belongs.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Allows detailed Shipment Leg analysis tying each leg to the VIN master record for emissions, fuel consumption, and compliance reporting.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real‑time arrival date‑time recorded by carrier or IoT device.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real‑time departure date‑time recorded by carrier or IoT device.']
  container_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping container or trailer used for the leg.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for this leg (freight, duty, handling, etc.).']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the leg cost.']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Category of cost represented by cost_amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment leg record was created in the system.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the customs clearance document for cross‑border legs.']
  customs_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance for the leg.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why a leg is delayed, if applicable.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical distance covered in this leg, measured in kilometers.']
  emissions_kg_co2 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated carbon dioxide emissions for the leg.']
  fuel_consumption_liters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total fuel used by the vehicle during the leg.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling notes for the cargo on this leg.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the leg carries hazardous materials.']
  hazardous_material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of hazardous material (e.g., flammable, corrosive).']
  leg_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the leg within the multi‑modal shipment.']
  leg_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the leg.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of cargo load for the leg.']
  odometer_end varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the end of the leg (kilometers).']
  odometer_start varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the start of the leg (kilometers).']
  planned_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled arrival date‑time for the leg.']
  planned_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled departure date‑time for the leg.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of individual vehicles or units moved in this leg.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to containers or trailers.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo requires temperature‑controlled transport.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature setting for the leg when temperature control is required.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature setting for the leg when temperature control is required.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the leg.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport used for this leg (e.g., truck, rail, ship, air, pipeline).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment leg record.']
  volume_cubic_meters decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total cargo volume for the leg, measured in cubic meters.']
  weight_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of cargo transported on this leg, expressed in metric tons.']

  Note: 'Individual transport leg within a multi-modal shipment, representing a discrete movement segment (e.g., plant to rail yard, rail yard to port, port to dealer compound). Tracks leg sequence, transport mode, carrier assignment, origin/destination facility, planned and actual departure/arrival timestamps, distance, leg-level status, and mode-specific details (rail car number/type for rail legs, vessel name/IMO/voyage for ocean legs). After merges, this product also carries rail car assignment details and vessel voyage references for their respective transport modes. Enables end-to-end multi-modal visibility for each VIN or batch.'
}

Table logistics.vehicle_transport_order {
  finished_vehicle_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the transport order.']
  vehicle_transport_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vehicle_transport_order']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logistics carrier responsible for the shipment.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Transport order costs are charged to a cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Needed for Transport Order Execution Dashboard to associate each order with the receiving dealership; supports dealer‑level on‑time delivery metrics.']
  dispatcher_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Dispatch planning process assigns a dispatcher employee to each transport order for tracking and accountability.']
  load_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the load planning record associated with this transport order.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the vehicles are shipped from.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: A transport order represents the execution of a shipment; linking it to the parent shipment enables traceability of cost and status.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Transport planning aligns with the specific sales vehicle order to ensure correct routing, timing, and cost allocation.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Needed for Transport Order Management to group orders by vehicle program, enabling capacity planning and compliance with program launch schedules.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment was actually received at the destination.']
  carrier_contact varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the carrier\'s dispatch desk.']
  compliance_regulation_code varchar [note: 'Code of the regulatory requirement applicable to the shipment (e.g., IATF, EPA).']
  confirmed_pickup_date date [note: 'Date the carrier confirmed for vehicle pickup.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of container or trailer used for the shipment (e.g., flatbed, container, roll‑on/roll‑off).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the transport cost.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customs declaration for cross‑border shipments.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for delivery to the destination dealer.']
  distance_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total distance in kilometers for the shipment route.']
  emission_co2_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated CO₂ emissions for the shipment based on distance and vehicle type.']
  estimated_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System‑estimated timestamp when the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination.']
  export_import_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is an export or import movement.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Dominant fuel type of the vehicles being shipped.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'True if the shipment is marked for expedited handling.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by logistics planners.']
  on_time_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment was delivered within the agreed on‑time window.']
  order_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transport order was initially created in the system.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the transport order by the logistics system.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transport order.']
  origin_compound varchar [note: 'Name of the storage yard or compound where vehicles are staged before pickup.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the transport order.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first captured for audit purposes.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  requested_pickup_date date [note: 'Date the shipper requested the carrier to pick up the vehicles.']
  shipping_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling or routing instructions for the carrier.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number provided by the carrier for real‑time shipment visibility.']
  transport_cost_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross cost charged by the carrier before taxes and discounts.']
  transport_cost_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net amount payable to the carrier after taxes and discounts.']
  transport_cost_tax decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component of the transport cost.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
  vehicle_count varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles included in this transport order.']
  vin_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of VINs for the vehicles being shipped.']
  weight_tons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total weight of all vehicles in the shipment.']

  Note: 'Transport order issued to a carrier for the movement of finished vehicles. Captures order number, issuing plant, carrier reference, transport mode, vehicle count, VIN list, origin compound, destination, requested pickup date, confirmed pickup date, and order status. Represents the contractual instruction to move vehicles and is the primary document linking shipments to carrier execution. Sourced from SAP TM or SAP SD.'
}

Table logistics.logistics_delivery_schedule {
  finished_vehicle_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the delivery schedule record.']
  logistics_delivery_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery_schedule data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logistics carrier responsible for transportation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Scheduled delivery cost estimates are allocated to a cost center for financial planning.']
  dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer receiving the vehicles.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer receiving the vehicles.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or distribution compound where the vehicles are dispatched.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the transportation route used for the delivery.']
  scheduler_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Delivery Scheduling system records which employee created/maintains each schedule for audit and performance metrics.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Delivery schedule planning references the shipment that will deliver vehicles to the dealership.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Delivery schedule generation uses sales order details to plan dealer deliveries and measure on‑time delivery performance.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Delivery Schedule Dashboard aggregates deliveries per program; linking ensures on‑time delivery metrics are tied to the correct vehicle program.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real timestamp when the shipment arrived at the dealer.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real timestamp when the shipment actually left the plant.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the vehicles to arrive at the dealer location.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the compliance status of the shipment with relevant regulations.']
  cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final cost incurred for the delivery after execution.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected cost for the scheduled delivery, before execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured for audit purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost fields.']
  customs_document_required boolean [note: 'True if customs paperwork is required for the shipment.']
  delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the agreed delivery time window at the dealer.']
  delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the agreed delivery time window at the dealer.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the vehicles to leave the plant or compound.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Projected delivery date based on current schedule and carrier commitments.']
  export_import_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is export, import, or domestic.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'Classification of freight for tariff and handling purposes.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing the delivery responsibilities.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery is expedited (true) or standard (false).']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  last_mile_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the final leg of delivery to the dealer or customer.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between departure and arrival, calculated for performance analysis.']
  logistics_delivery_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the delivery schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional remarks.']
  otd_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when the delivery was completed for OTD measurement.']
  otd_target_date date [note: 'Target date for achieving on‑time delivery performance.']
  planned_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles (or units) scheduled for delivery.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the delivery schedule.']
  schedule_horizon varchar [note: 'Planning horizon for the schedule (e.g., weekly, monthly).']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the delivery schedule, used for tracking and communication with dealers and carriers.']
  schedule_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery schedule was initially created in the system.']
  scheduled_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned exact timestamp for arrival, including time of day.']
  scheduled_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned exact timestamp for departure, including time of day.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Any additional handling or routing instructions for the shipment.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'True if temperature‑controlled transport is required.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'External tracking number provided by the carrier.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the delivery.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the delivery schedule record.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Type of transport vehicle allocated for the shipment.']
  volume_cbm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Aggregate cargo volume in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Aggregate weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Planned delivery schedule for finished vehicles from plant or distribution compound to dealer network. Captures scheduled delivery windows, dealer allocation, vehicle model/trim/color mix, planned quantities, transport mode, and scheduling horizon (weekly/monthly). Supports OTD planning, dealer inventory replenishment, and logistics capacity allocation. Linked to dealer orders and production scheduling.'
}

Table logistics.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the carrier record.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the carrier\'s primary business address (optional).']
  average_cost_per_mile decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average transportation cost incurred per mile for this carrier.']
  average_on_time_delivery_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average percentage of shipments delivered on or before the promised delivery date.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the transport carrier as registered with authorities.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the carrier within the logistics network.']
  carrier_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic tier assigned to the carrier based on volume, reliability, and strategic importance.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the carrier entity.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person for the carrier.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number for the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the carrier\'s master service agreement (null if open‑ended).']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for the carrier\'s master service agreement.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the carrier\'s master service agreement.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the carrier record is considered active for reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the carrier record is no longer active (null if indefinite).']
  environmental_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental compliance certifications held by the carrier.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Main type of equipment the carrier uses for shipments.']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Number of transport units (e.g., trucks, railcars) owned or operated by the carrier.']
  iatf_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current IATF 16949 quality‑management compliance status of the carrier.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the carrier\'s liability insurance.']
  insurance_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance company covering the carrier\'s operations.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or safety audit performed on the carrier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the carrier.']
  operating_regions varchar [note: 'Geographic regions (e.g., continents, countries) where the carrier provides service.']
  performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Overall performance score (0‑5) derived from on‑time delivery, safety, and cost metrics.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety performance rating based on regulatory audits and incident history.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four‑letter code uniquely identifying the carrier in North American logistics.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the carrier.']
  transport_modes varchar [note: 'Transport modes the carrier is authorized to operate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the carrier record.']
  website varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the carrier.']

  Note: 'Master record for transport carriers (road haulers, rail operators, ocean shipping lines, port logistics providers) engaged for finished vehicle and CKD/SKD kit logistics. Captures carrier legal name, SCAC code, DOT number, transport modes supported, operating regions, contract reference, insurance certificate details, IATF 16949 compliance status, carrier tier classification, and performance rating. SSOT for carrier identity within the logistics domain. Referenced by transport orders, shipment legs, freight invoices, damage claims, and rate contracts.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_performance {
  carrier_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each carrier performance scorecard row.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the logistics carrier whose performance is being measured.']
  reviewed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Carrier performance reviews are performed by a specific employee; link enables reviewer accountability.']
  average_transit_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean number of calendar days shipments spent in transit for the period.']
  carrier_performance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the performance record is active, inactive, or archived.']
  claim_frequency varchar [note: 'Count of loss or damage claims submitted for the carrier on this lane during the period.']
  cost_per_shipment_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average transportation cost incurred per shipment for the carrier on this lane.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the carrier performance row was initially loaded into the lakehouse.']
  damage_rate_ppm varchar [note: 'Number of damaged units per one million units shipped during the period.']
  fuel_consumption_l_per_100km decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average liters of fuel used per 100 kilometers by the carrier on this lane.']
  lane_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier for the transportation lane (e.g., "DET‑LAX").']
  lane_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lane is domestic, export, or import.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional textual comments regarding carrier performance, exceptions, or observations for the period.']
  on_time_delivery_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of shipments delivered within the agreed delivery window, calculated independently of OTD for cross‑validation.']
  otd_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of shipments delivered on or before the committed delivery date for the period.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Composite score (1‑5) summarizing carrier performance across all metrics.']
  performance_month date [note: 'Calendar month (first day) representing the reporting period for the carrier performance metrics.']
