  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from kanban signal to material arrival.']
  lot_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is managed by lot/lot number.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or operational instructions related to the kanban.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the kanban card for production scheduling.']
  production_line varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific production line that consumes the kanban.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Inventory level at which a new kanban card is generated to trigger replenishment.']
  replenishment_strategy varchar [note: 'Method used to replenish the downstream inventory when the kanban signals empty.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum safety stock quantity maintained for the SKU to protect against variability.']
  serial_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is serialized (individual serial numbers tracked).']
  signal_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to convey the kanban signal (physical card, electronic signal, barcode scan).']
  supply_area varchar [note: 'Logical area or zone where the supply source for the kanban is located (e.g., warehouse, buffer, supplier dock).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity fields (e.g., pieces, kilograms).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the kanban record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Kanban card master record governing pull-based replenishment signals for line-side and supermarket inventory in lean manufacturing environments. Captures kanban card number, associated SKU, supply area, consuming work center, container quantity, number of cards in circulation, replenishment strategy (internal, external, in-house production), kanban status (empty, in-transit, full), and signal method (physical, electronic, barcode). Supports JIT/JIS production systems and reduces WIP inventory.'
}

Table inventory.ckd_skd_kit {
  ckd_skd_kit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the CKD/SKD kit record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the kit record.']
  primary_ckd_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the kit record.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to procurement.supplier']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the kit record.']
  vehicle_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: CKD kit planning requires associating each kit with the vehicle model it assembles, used in production scheduling and customs documentation.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: CKD/SKD kits are shipped per vehicle order; linking enables the Kit‑to‑Order allocation dashboard used by assembly planners.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Date the kit was received at the destination plant.']
  bom_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the BOM that defines the parts included in the kit.']
  completeness_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether all required parts are present in the kit.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping container that holds the kit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the kit record was first created in the system.']
  customs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'HS or tariff code used for customs duty calculation.']
  customs_valuation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Customs‑declared value of the kit for duty calculation.']
  customs_valuation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the customs valuation.']
  documentation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required export documentation.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date of arrival at the destination plant.']
  export_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the country to which the kit is exported.']
  export_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to export paperwork (e.g., commercial invoice, export license).']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the kit contains hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  inventory_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage indicating how accurately the kit quantity matches system records.']
  is_jis_flag boolean [note: 'True if the kit follows a JIS sequencing requirement.']
  is_jit_flag boolean [note: 'True if the kit is produced/shipped under a JIT strategy.']
  is_obsolete_flag boolean [note: 'True if the kit is marked obsolete and should not be used for production.']
  kit_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the kit as Completely Knocked Down (CKD), Semi‑Knocked Down (SKD), or a mixed configuration.']
  kit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the kit, often reflecting vehicle model and configuration.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the kit for tracking and customs documentation.']
  kit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the kit.']
  last_inventory_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count for the kit.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of the kit batch.']
  missing_parts_list varchar [note: 'Free‑text list of parts that are absent from a partially complete kit.']
  packing_list_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the packing list generated for the kit shipment.']
  serial_number_flag boolean [note: 'True if individual parts in the kit are tracked by serial number.']
  serial_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of serial numbers for serialized components in the kit.']
  shipping_date date [note: 'Date the kit was loaded onto the outbound carrier.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any additional handling notes (e.g., fragile, keep upright).']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the supplier providing the kit components.']
  target_assembly_plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the overseas assembly plant that will receive and assemble the kit.']
  target_assembly_plant_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the destination assembly plant.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the kit must be kept within a controlled temperature range during transport.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of the kit, expressed in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all components in the kit, expressed in kilograms.']
  transfer_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Inter‑company transfer price applied to the kit for tax and profitability reporting.']
  transfer_price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the transfer price.']
  transfer_price_incoterms varchar [note: 'Incoterm governing the transfer price responsibilities.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the kit record.']
  valuation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary valuation of the kit for accounting and customs purposes.']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the valuation amount.']

  Note: 'CKD (Completely Knocked Down) and SKD (Semi Knocked Down) kit inventory record for vehicles and assemblies exported to overseas assembly plants. Captures kit number, target assembly plant, kit BOM reference, packing list, container assignment, export documentation reference, kit completeness status, missing parts list, and customs valuation. Supports international manufacturing operations, transfer pricing, and customs compliance for cross-border kit shipments.'
}

Table inventory.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inventory adjustment transaction.']
  adjustment_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the adjustment record.']
  adjustment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the inventory adjustment.']
  adjustment_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last modified the adjustment record.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the adjustment record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the inventory adjustment.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: inventory_adjustment adjusts quantities for a SKU; linking to sku_master consolidates SKU reference.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Adjustment records reference a storage location; replace string with FK']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last modified the adjustment record.']
  accounting_document_number varchar [note: 'Financial document number generated for the adjustment in the general ledger.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the adjustment for tracking and reference.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the adjustment transaction.']
  adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory adjustment event occurred in the business process.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Category describing why the inventory adjustment was performed.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment was approved.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who approved the adjustment.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier when the adjustment applies to a specific production batch.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Optional free-text notes entered by the user.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the financial impact of the adjustment is charged.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the cost impact amount.']
  cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary impact of the adjustment on inventory valuation.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date when the adjustment becomes effective, if applicable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date when the adjustment ceases to be effective, if applicable.']
  is_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates if the adjustment has received final approval.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the adjustment was entered manually (true) or generated by an automated process (false).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where the adjustment took place.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the adjustment is posted to financial ledgers.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Project identifier if the adjustment is linked to a specific internal project.']
  quantity_adjusted decimal(18,3) [note: 'Signed quantity change; positive for additions, negative for reductions.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the business reason for the adjustment.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the adjustment reason.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the adjustment record.']
  related_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to an external document (e.g., PPAP, audit report) linked to the adjustment.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of a uniquely tracked item affected by the adjustment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the adjustment record.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for the adjustment, if applicable.']
  tax_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment is subject to tax.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity adjusted.']
  valuation_area_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the valuation area used for inventory accounting.']

  Note: 'Inventory adjustment transaction record capturing manual corrections to stock quantities resulting from cycle count variances, system reconciliation, damage write-offs, or process corrections. Captures adjustment type (count variance, damage, theft, system error, process correction), affected SKU/batch/serial, adjusted quantity (positive or negative), adjustment reason code, approver, financial impact, and posting date. Provides audit trail for inventory accuracy management and SOX compliance.'
}

Table inventory.warehouse_task {
  warehouse_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the warehouse execution task.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the task record.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_line. Business justification: Warehouse tasks are created to pick/pack specific order lines; the Order Line Fulfillment report requires this FK for performance tracking.']
  primary_warehouse_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the task record.']
  source_bin_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.bin. Business justification: Warehouse task source bin should reference bin entity']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the task record.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or distribution center where the task occurs.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier when the material is batch‑controlled.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task was successfully completed.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with executing the task (labor, AGV usage, etc.).']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the task cost.']
  destination_bin_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse bin code where the material is to be placed.']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of why the task did not complete as planned.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions for handling the material (e.g., fragile, keep upright).']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is classified as hazardous and requires special handling.']
  is_jis boolean [note: 'Indicates if the task follows a JIS production sequence.']
  is_jit boolean [note: 'Indicates if the task is part of a JIT inventory strategy.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier when the material is lot‑controlled.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant associated with the warehouse.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority level for the task.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of units of the SKU to be moved or processed.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the task record.']
  resource_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator (employee) or AGV unit assigned to the task.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the task is performed by a human operator or an automated guided vehicle.']
  serial_number_flag boolean [note: 'True if the material is serialized and each unit has a unique serial number.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Master identifier of the material or component being moved.']
  special_handling_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates any additional non‑standard handling requirements.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the operator or AGV began executing the task.']
  task_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Business event time when the warehouse task was generated/assigned.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Category of the physical activity performed in the warehouse.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'True if the material must be stored or moved under temperature-controlled conditions.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., pcs, kg).']
  warehouse_task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the task.']

  Note: 'Warehouse execution task record representing a discrete physical activity assigned to a warehouse operator or AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle), such as putaway, picking, replenishment, or relocation. Aligned with SAP EWM warehouse task. Captures task type, assigned resource (operator ID or AGV unit), source and destination bin, SKU and quantity, priority, creation time, start time, completion time, and exception reason if incomplete. Supports warehouse labor management and AGV fleet coordination.'
}

Table inventory.movement_type {
  movement_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for each inventory movement type record. [canonical_skip_reason: REFERENCE_LOOKUP - classification table for movement types]']
  account_determination_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the movement type triggers automatic account determination for financial posting.']
  allowed_destination_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of location permitted as the destination for this movement.']
  allowed_source_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of location permitted as the source for this movement (e.g., plant, warehouse, vendor).']
  batch_management_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if batch management must be maintained for movements of this type.']
  cost_center_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if a cost center must be supplied when posting this movement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the movement type record was initially created in the source system.']
  default_quantity_sign varchar [note: 'Default sign (+1 or -1) applied to quantity for this movement type.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the movement adds stock (inbound), removes stock (outbound), or moves stock between locations (transfer).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the movement type becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the movement type is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  hazardous_material_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hazardous materials may be moved using this movement type.']
  inventory_accounting_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the movement type affects inventory valuation and accounting.']
  inventory_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the movement increases, decreases, or does not change stock levels.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the movement type is currently active and usable in transactions.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether postings of this movement type can be generated automatically by system processes.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'True if the movement type is deprecated and should not be used for new transactions.']
  is_jis boolean [note: 'True if the movement type supports Just‑In‑Sequence production scheduling.']
  is_jit boolean [note: 'True if the movement type is used in Just‑In‑Time procurement processes.']
  is_lot_tracked boolean [note: 'True if lot (batch) tracking is mandatory for this movement type.']
  is_serial_tracked boolean [note: 'True if serial number tracking is mandatory for this movement type.']
  movement_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category grouping of movement types.']
  movement_reason_code varchar [note: 'Optional code that provides additional reason for the movement (e.g., scrap, rework).']
  movement_type_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the movement type (e.g., 101 for goods receipt, 261 for goods issue).']
  movement_type_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the movement type and its business purpose.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the movement type.']
  price_control varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory valuation for this movement type.']
  profit_center_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if a profit center must be supplied for this movement type.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a quality inspection record is mandatory for this movement.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether movements of this type must be approved before posting.']
  reversal_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this movement type can be reversed by another movement.']
  reversal_movement_type_code varchar [note: 'Code of the movement type that reverses this movement (e.g., 102 reverses 101).']
  serial_number_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if serial numbers must be captured for this movement type.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source ERP system that defines the movement type (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Identifies special stock categories (e.g., blocked stock, quality inspection stock) associated with the movement.']
  system_defined boolean [note: 'True if the movement type is delivered by SAP and cannot be deleted.']
  tax_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether tax calculation is applicable for this movement.']
  transaction_event varchar [note: 'Business event that typically generates this movement type (e.g., purchase order receipt, production order issue).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the movement type record.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Key used in valuation to determine price control and account assignment.']

  Note: 'Reference classification table defining all valid inventory movement types used in goods movement transactions. Captures movement type code, description, movement direction (inbound/outbound/transfer), account determination relevance, reversal movement type, and applicable business scenarios (e.g., 101=GR for PO, 261=GI for production order, 311=transfer posting). Aligned with SAP MM movement type configuration. Provides standardized classification for all goods movement transactions.'
}

Table inventory.dealer_sku_stock {
  dealer_sku_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the DealerSkuStock association']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'FK to the dealership record']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to the SKU master record']
  cost_per_unit decimal [note: 'Cost per unit of the SKU for this dealership']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the cost per unit']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Lead time in days for replenishing the SKU at the dealership']
  quantity_on_hand varchar [note: 'Current quantity of the SKU available at the dealership']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a reorder for the SKU at the dealership']
  safety_stock varchar [note: 'Safety stock quantity maintained for the SKU at the dealership']

  Note: 'Represents the inventory allocation of a specific SKU at a specific dealership. Each record captures operational stock attributes that are unique to the SKU‑dealership pairing.. Existence Justification: A SKU can be stocked at many dealership locations and a dealership stocks many SKUs. The business actively manages the stock level, reorder point, safety stock, lead time, cost and currency for each SKU at each dealership, creating a distinct inventory record per SKU‑dealership pair.'
}

Table inventory.bin {
  bin_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bin']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or plant location where the bin resides.']
  parent_bin_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bin (parent_bin_id)']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Bin belongs to a storage location; add FK for hierarchy']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Bin is located within a warehouse; add FK to warehouse']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle designation within the warehouse.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Machine‑readable barcode identifier printed on the bin.']
  bin_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of bins for reporting or planning (e.g., zone, area).']
  capacity_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the bin can hold, expressed in the unit defined by capacity_unit.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the bin\'s capacity (e.g., kilograms, pieces).']
  code varchar [note: 'Business code assigned to the bin (e.g., alphanumeric location code).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bin record was first created in the system.']
  current_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current total weight of items stored in the bin.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the bin, its purpose, or special handling notes.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the bin became operationally available.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the bin is scheduled to be retired or decommissioned (null if open‑ended).']
  hazardous_material_allowed boolean [note: 'True if the bin may store hazardous components (e.g., batteries, chemicals).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bin is the default location for unspecified SKUs.']
  is_reserved boolean [note: 'True if the bin is reserved for a specific SKU or production order.']
  last_inventory_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count for the bin.']
  last_inventory_count_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity recorded during the last inventory count.']
  level varchar [note: 'Vertical level of the bin on the rack.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature for temperature‑controlled bins.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight the bin can safely support.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature for temperature‑controlled bins.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label or name of the bin used on warehouse floor.']
  occupancy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of bin capacity currently utilized.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the bin.']
  rack varchar [note: 'Rack designation within the aisle.']
  reserved_for_sku varchar [note: 'SKU identifier for which the bin is reserved (if any).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bin.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bin is climate‑controlled.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of bin indicating its physical configuration or purpose.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bin record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for bin. Referenced by source_bin_id.'
}

Table inventory.warehouse {
  warehouse_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for warehouse']

  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the warehouse street address (optional).']
  capacity_cubic_m decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total usable volume capacity in cubic meters.']
  capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total usable floor space in square feet.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the warehouse is located.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date when the warehouse ceased operations (null if still active).']
  code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the warehouse in ERP and logistics systems.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the warehouse resides.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warehouse record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional details about the warehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the warehouse information is considered effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the warehouse information remains effective (null for open‑ended).']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the warehouse (decimal degrees).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the warehouse (decimal degrees).']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Contact email address for the warehouse manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person responsible for warehouse operations.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the warehouse manager.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the warehouse.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date when the warehouse began operations.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating hours (e.g., 08:00-17:00).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the warehouse address.']
  region varchar [note: 'Higher‑level geographic region grouping (e.g., North America, EMEA).']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification of the warehouse based on access controls and monitoring.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the warehouse location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warehouse.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse provides temperature‑controlled storage.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of warehouse based on its primary function.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the warehouse record.']
  zone varchar [note: 'Internal zone or area identifier within the warehouse complex.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for warehouse. Referenced by warehouse_id.'
}

Table asset.equipment_registry {
  equipment_registry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each equipment record.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Classify each equipment record by linking to asset_class.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for Cost Allocation Report linking each equipment to its cost center for depreciation and expense tracking, a standard automotive finance requirement.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.functional_location. Business justification: Equipment belongs to a functional location; linking equipment_registry to functional_location eliminates duplicated location strings and enables hierarchy navigation.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Asset management records equipment supplier to manage warranty contracts, service agreements, and depreciation schedules.']
  asset_condition varchar [note: 'Current physical condition of the equipment as assessed during inspection.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Company‑assigned tag or barcode used to uniquely identify the equipment on the shop floor.']
  calibration_last_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was last calibrated.']
  calibration_next_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration.']
  calibration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment requires periodic calibration.']
  capital_expenditure_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial capital cost incurred to acquire the equipment.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the equipment became operational and entered service.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the equipment record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was retired or removed from service (nullable if still active).']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date depreciation calculations end (typically end of useful life).']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the equipment.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date depreciation calculations begin for the equipment.']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average energy consumption of the equipment per operating hour.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'Operational category indicating the primary production area the equipment supports.']
  equipment_registry_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the equipment\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  equipment_registry_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the equipment or asset.']
  equipment_registry_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the equipment.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of equipment based on function or technology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: machinery|robot|agv|plc|scada|press|welding_station|paint_booth — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was physically installed at the plant.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date the most recent maintenance work was completed.']
  lifecycle_phase varchar [note: 'Stage of the equipment\'s lifecycle within the enterprise.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days between routine maintenance activities.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Chosen maintenance approach for the equipment.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that produced the equipment.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the equipment manufacturer.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operating time between equipment failures.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the equipment after a failure.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the upcoming maintenance activity.']
  operating_cost_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to operate the equipment per hour, including energy and labor.']
  power_rating_kw decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical power rating of the equipment.']
  regulatory_compliance_expiry date [note: 'Date when the equipment\'s current regulatory compliance certification expires.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment meets applicable regulatory requirements.']
  safety_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the equipment\'s safety certification.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of safety certifications applicable to the equipment.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑provided serial number for the equipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the equipment record.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment warranty period expires.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment warranty period begins.']
  warranty_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the equipment warranty.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical weight of the equipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all manufacturing assets and equipment across all plants, including production machinery, robots, AGVs, PLCs, SCADA systems, presses, welding stations, paint booths, and assembly line equipment. Serves as the SSOT for equipment identity, classification, location, and lifecycle status. Aligned with SAP PM Equipment Master (EQUI) and supports IATF 16949 asset traceability requirements.'
}

Table asset.functional_location {
  functional_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the functional location.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Supports Facility Cost Center reporting, enabling allocation of location‑based overhead and depreciation in financial statements.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Each functional location falls under a specific jurisdiction; linking supports location‑based regulatory reporting.']
  parent_location_functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent location in the hierarchy.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the functional location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, etc.).']
  area_sqm double [note: 'Physical floor area occupied by the functional location.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Date by which equipment calibration must be performed.']
  capacity_units_per_hour double [note: 'Maximum production output capacity of the location, measured in units per hour.']
  capital_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial capital expenditure required to acquire and install the location.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the functional location is situated.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the functional location became operational after installation.']
  compliance_iso9001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location complies with ISO 9001 quality standards.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the location resides.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the functional location was retired or taken out of service.']
  depreciation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount allocated for depreciation over the asset\'s useful life.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the location is fully depreciated.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation of the location\'s capital cost begins.']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to identify the functional location in external systems.']
  functional_location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the functional location\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  functional_location_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the functional location.']
  functional_location_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the functional location.']
  functional_location_type varchar [note: 'Category of the functional location within the plant hierarchy.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the location within the plant hierarchy (e.g., 1=Plant, 2=Shop).']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the functional location was physically installed.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the location is deemed critical to production continuity.']
  is_operational boolean [note: 'True if the location is currently operational.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which maintenance was performed.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the functional location.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the functional location.']
  maintenance_cost_yearly decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated yearly cost for maintaining the location.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval between scheduled maintenance activities, expressed in days.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Approach used for maintaining the functional location.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the equipment installed at the location.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the manufacturer.']
  mtbf_days double [note: 'Average number of days between successive failures of equipment at this location.']
  mttr_hours double [note: 'Average time required to repair equipment after a failure, expressed in hours.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next preventive or predictive maintenance activity.']
  oee_overall_pct double [note: 'Combined availability, performance, and quality metric expressed as a percentage.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the functional location.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department that owns operational responsibility for the location.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of safety certifications for the location.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the equipment.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the functional location.']

  Note: 'Hierarchical plant topology model representing the physical and functional structure of manufacturing facilities — plant, shop, line, station, and position levels. Each functional location is a named node in the plant hierarchy where equipment is installed and maintained. Directly maps to SAP PM Functional Location (IFLOT) and supports spatial asset management, maintenance planning, and OEE reporting by location.'
}

Table asset.tooling_registry {
  tooling_registry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each tooling record.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Links tooling to the specific vehicle program it supports; replaces the denormalized vehicle_program string for 3NF compliance.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Tooling procurement process requires linking each tooling asset to its supplier for warranty, cost, and compliance reporting.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset tag used for inventory and tracking within the plant.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date the tool was last calibrated or verified for accuracy.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next calibration activity.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the tool\'s calibration lifecycle.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Capital cost of the tool in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tooling record was first created in the system.']
  custodian_plant varchar [note: 'Plant responsible for the tool\'s custody and maintenance.']
  dimensions_mm varchar [note: 'Physical dimensions of the tool expressed as L×W×H in millimetres.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the tool last underwent preventive or corrective maintenance.']
  maintenance_schedule varchar [note: 'Planned maintenance frequency for the tool.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the tool (e.g., H13 steel, aluminum).']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operational hours between tool failures, calculated from historical data.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the tool after a failure.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance activity.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or facility where the tool is physically located.']
  purchase_date date [note: 'Date the tool was acquired by the organization.']
  remaining_shots varchar [note: 'Estimated remaining shots before tool replacement or refurbishment is required.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number of the tool.']
  shots_used varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of shots the tool has performed to date.']
  tool_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the tool, often used in shop‑floor systems and engineering drawings.']
  tool_life_shots varchar [note: 'Maximum number of shots/strokes the tool is engineered to perform.']
  tool_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the tooling item (e.g., "Engine Block Stamping Die").']
  tool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tooling item indicating its functional category.']
  tooling_registry_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the tool, including purpose and key characteristics.']
  tooling_registry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tool within the manufacturing environment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tooling record.']
  vehicle_model varchar [note: 'Specific vehicle model or variant associated with the tooling.']
  vendor_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the contract under which the tool was purchased.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external supplier that provided the tool.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the tool\'s manufacturer warranty ends.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the tool in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all production tooling, dies, molds, fixtures, gauges, and jigs used in manufacturing and assembly operations. Tracks tool identity, classification (stamping die, injection mold, welding fixture, CMM fixture), associated vehicle programs, tool life (shots/strokes remaining), and current custodian plant. Supports PPAP tooling documentation and die maintenance scheduling.'
}

Table asset.maintenance_plan {
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the maintenance plan.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or contractor responsible for the plan.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: A maintenance plan is defined for specific equipment; adding equipment_registry_id to maintenance_plan creates the parent‑child link needed for plan assignment.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Needed for Maintenance Planning Dashboard that schedules activities per vehicle nameplate, ensuring program‑specific maintenance windows.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Plant‑level maintenance plans are created per manufacturing plant; required for the Plant Maintenance Planning KPI.']
  responsible_person_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or contractor responsible for the plan.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality standard that the maintenance plan must satisfy.']
  cost_estimate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of the total cost to execute the maintenance plan.']
  cost_estimate_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the cost estimate for the maintenance plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance plan record was created.']
  downtime_estimate_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated duration of equipment downtime for the maintenance activity.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Official start date of the agreement portion of the maintenance plan.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Official end date of the agreement portion of the maintenance plan (nullable).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance plan expires or is retired (nullable).']
  interval_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maintenance interval.']
  interval_value varchar [note: 'Numeric value of the maintenance interval.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance was performed under this plan.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to prepare before the maintenance window.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the maintenance plan within the organization.']
  maintenance_plan_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the plan (e.g., routine, major overhaul).']
  maintenance_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance plan purpose and scope.']
  maintenance_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the maintenance plan.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Category of maintenance performed under the plan.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Preferred time window for performing the maintenance.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next maintenance activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the maintenance plan.']
  parts_list_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier for the parts list associated with the maintenance plan.']
  parts_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether spare parts are required for the maintenance task.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the maintenance plan in SAP PM and other systems.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the maintenance plan.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the maintenance plan.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department accountable for executing the maintenance plan.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for safety hazards associated with the maintenance activity.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Method used to trigger maintenance (time, usage counter, or condition).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the maintenance plan originates.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance plan becomes effective.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the maintenance plan record.']

  Note: 'Preventive and predictive maintenance plan defining scheduled maintenance strategies for equipment and functional locations. Captures maintenance cycle (time-based, counter-based, condition-based), task list references, maintenance intervals, lead times, and scheduling parameters. Aligned with SAP PM Maintenance Plan (MPLAN) and supports IATF 16949 preventive maintenance program requirements.'
}

Table asset.maintenance_order {
  maintenance_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the maintenance order.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved the maintenance order.']
  approver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved the maintenance order.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Maintenance orders often require regulatory approvals; attaching the relevant compliance document ensures traceability.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allows linking maintenance orders to cost centers for accurate labor/material expense posting in the Maintenance Cost Ledger.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or department that requested the maintenance work.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset being maintained.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset being maintained.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the functional location where maintenance is performed.']
  maintenance_work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_work_center. Business justification: Replace free‑text work_center with FK to maintenance_work_center for proper relational integrity.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Required for Maintenance Cost Allocation Report linking downtime costs to specific vehicle programs, a standard process in automotive manufacturing.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Service orders for customer‑owned vehicles require the owning party for billing, warranty tracking, and service history.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the maintenance is performed.']
  primary_maintenance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or department that requested the maintenance work.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when maintenance work was completed.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when maintenance work began.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the maintenance activity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance order record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes the equipment was unavailable due to maintenance.']
  external_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of third‑party service provider if external resources were used.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'True if the order was created as an emergency response.']
  labor_cost_actual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual labor cost incurred.']
  labor_cost_estimated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned labor cost based on estimated hours and rates.']
  labor_hours_actual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual labor hours spent on the maintenance work.']
  labor_hours_estimated decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned labor hours required to complete the maintenance work.']
  maintenance_order_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance task and scope.']
  maintenance_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance order.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy governing the maintenance order: run-based, time-based, or condition-based.']
  material_cost_actual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost of materials consumed during maintenance.']
  material_cost_estimated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned cost of materials required for the maintenance order.']
  mttr_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time taken to repair equipment, calculated from this order.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by technicians.']
  oem_part_number varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer part number for parts used.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the maintenance order.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance work: corrective, preventive, or predictive.']
  parts_used varchar [note: 'List of part numbers and quantities used, stored as free text or JSON string.']
  planned_finish_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for completion of maintenance work.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the start of maintenance work.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level indicating urgency of the maintenance work.']
  safety_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether safety procedures were required for the maintenance work.']
  total_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of labor and material costs for the order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the maintenance order record.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance work is covered under warranty.']

  Note: 'Work order issued for corrective, preventive, or predictive maintenance activities on equipment or functional locations. Captures order type (PM01 corrective, PM02 preventive, PM03 predictive), priority, planned/actual start and finish times, assigned work center, estimated and actual labor hours, material costs, and completion status. Core transactional entity in SAP PM module. Drives MTTR calculation and maintenance cost tracking.'
}

Table asset.maintenance_notification {
  maintenance_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the maintenance notification record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator, technician, or system that raised the notification.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset to which the notification relates.']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance plan governing this notification.']
  reported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator, technician, or system that raised the notification.']
  activity_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the maintenance activity to be performed.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real completion time when work finished.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real start time when work began on the notification.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Human‑readable tag or serial number of the asset.']
  breakdown_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the notification represents a complete equipment breakdown.']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the root cause of the malfunction.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the issue triggers regulatory or internal compliance actions.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed action to resolve the issue.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected monetary cost of the maintenance activity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notification record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the cost estimate.']
  downtime_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code explaining why production was halted.']
  estimated_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Projected duration of production loss in minutes.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity performed on the asset.']
  location varchar [note: 'Plant, line, or area where the asset is located.']
  maintenance_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification such as preventive, corrective, predictive.']
  maintenance_group varchar [note: 'Organizational group responsible for the maintenance activity.']
  maintenance_notification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the notification.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Historical average time between failures for the asset.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the asset after a failure.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next preventive maintenance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra information relevant to the notification.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the notification (e.g., NM123456).']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the notification was originally created in the source system.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the notification as a malfunction report, activity report, or maintenance request.']
  oem_notification_flag boolean [note: 'True if the original equipment manufacturer must be notified.']
  parts_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers required.']
  parts_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if spare parts are needed to complete the maintenance.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled completion time for the maintenance activity.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start time for the maintenance activity.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business‑defined urgency level for handling the notification.']
  regulatory_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the notification must be reported to a governing body (e.g., NHTSA).']
  reported_by_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the person or system that reported the issue.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause identified after investigation.']
  safety_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issue poses a safety hazard.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Impact level of the reported issue on production.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Production shift during which the issue was observed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the notification record.']
  work_center varchar [note: 'Specific work center or production cell responsible for the asset.']

  Note: 'Maintenance notification (malfunction report, activity report, or maintenance request) raised by operators, technicians, or automated condition monitoring systems to report equipment faults, defects, or required maintenance activities. Captures notification type, reported damage/cause/activity codes, breakdown indicator, and priority. Maps to SAP PM Notification (QMEL) and is the trigger for maintenance order creation.'
}

Table asset.equipment_downtime {
  equipment_downtime_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each equipment downtime event.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Enables downtime cost attribution to the responsible cost center for OEE and financial impact analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator or technician who reported or handled the downtime.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment or machine that experienced downtime.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment or machine that experienced downtime.']
  maintenance_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work order linked to the downtime event.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Supports Downtime Impact Analysis per vehicle program, a regulatory and internal KPI report.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator or technician who reported or handled the downtime.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line or cell affected by the downtime.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: Downtime tracking must attribute loss to the work center where the equipment resides for OEE and cost analysis.']
  cause_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the root cause, used for prioritization of corrective actions.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by the operator or maintenance team.']
  cost_of_downtime decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the downtime event.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost of downtime.']
  downtime_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of why the equipment was down.']
  downtime_event_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier or ticket number assigned to the downtime event.']
  downtime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of scheduled production time lost, expressed as a percentage.']
  downtime_reason_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of root cause categories for reporting.']
  downtime_reported_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who initially reported the downtime.']
  downtime_reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the downtime was first reported in the system.']
  downtime_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that recorded the downtime (e.g., MES, SAP PM).']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total length of the downtime event expressed in whole minutes.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the equipment resumed operation.']
  equipment_downtime_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the downtime record.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the downtime event had a critical impact on production or safety.']
  lost_units varchar [note: 'Number of production units that could not be produced because of the downtime.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime was scheduled (planned) or unexpected (unplanned).']
  oee_impact_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated impact of the downtime on Overall Equipment Effectiveness, expressed as a percentage.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the downtime record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the downtime record.']
  repair_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action performed to restore equipment.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the detailed root cause of the downtime.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the underlying cause of the downtime.']
  scheduled_maintenance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the downtime was part of a pre‑planned maintenance window.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Shift during which the downtime occurred.']
  spare_part_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the spare part consumed in the repair.']
  spare_part_used varchar [note: 'Part number of any spare component used during repair.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the equipment stopped operating.']

  Note: 'Operational record of unplanned and planned equipment downtime events on the shop floor. Captures downtime start and end timestamps, duration (minutes), downtime category (mechanical failure, electrical fault, tooling change, planned maintenance, material shortage), affected production line, lost production units, and root cause classification. Primary source for MTBF, MTTR, and OEE availability calculations. Integrates with MES (Apriso/Dassault) downtime tracking.'
}

Table asset.calibration_record {
  calibration_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the calibration record.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset or measurement equipment that was calibrated.']
  maintenance_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work order that triggered or recorded the calibration.']
  calibration_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for the calibration activity.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date on which the calibration activity was performed.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between successive calibrations as defined by the calibration plan.']
  calibration_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label or title for the calibration event.']
  calibration_method varchar [note: 'Method used for calibration: performed in‑house or by an external provider.']
  calibration_number varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned identifier or code for the calibration record, used in reports and work orders.']
  calibration_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calibration record.']
  calibration_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital calibration report or certificate.']
  calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the calibration standard or traceability document used for the calibration.']
  calibration_type varchar [note: 'Category of calibration performed, e.g., dimensional, electrical, functional, temperature, or pressure.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations recorded by the calibrator.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for calibration_cost.']
  due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the next calibration is required.']
  lab_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate or accreditation number issued to the laboratory for this calibration.']
  laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited laboratory or internal facility that performed the calibration.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(10,4) [note: 'Quantified uncertainty of the measurement after calibration, expressed in the unit defined by uncertainty_unit.']
  result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the calibration: pass, fail, or conditional.']
  uncertainty_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for measurement_uncertainty, e.g., micrometers, millimeters, nanometers, or percent.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calibration record.']

  Note: 'Calibration and measurement system analysis (MSA) record for gauges, measurement instruments, test equipment, and CMM (Coordinate Measuring Machine) assets. Tracks calibration date, due date, calibration standard used, calibration result (pass/fail/conditional), measurement uncertainty, calibration interval, and accredited laboratory reference. Mandatory for IATF 16949 clause 7.1.5 measurement equipment management and PPAP dimensional reporting.'
}

Table asset.equipment_counter {
  equipment_counter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each equipment counter reading record.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment (machine, tool, or asset) to which the counter reading belongs.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment (machine, tool, or asset) to which the counter reading belongs.']
  measurement_point_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific measurement point on the equipment (e.g., sensor or counter location).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number associated with the production run (if applicable).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reading record was created in the data lake.']
  cumulative_counter decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative total of the counter up to this reading (if applicable).']
  delta_counter decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between this reading and the previous reading.']
  equipment_counter_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reading record.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the counter reading was captured.']
  maintenance_action varchar [note: 'Recommended maintenance action associated with this reading.']
  maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the recommended maintenance action should be completed.']
  maintenance_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reading triggers a maintenance action based on predefined thresholds.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Plant or facility where the equipment is located.']
  reading_quality varchar [note: 'Quality status of the reading as assessed by the system or operator.']
  reading_type varchar [note: 'Category of the counter measurement.']
  reading_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the counter reading (e.g., operating hours, cycles).']
  recorded_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that recorded the reading.']
  recorded_by_role varchar [note: 'Role of the recorder (human operator or automated system).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes about the reading.']
  sensor_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the sensor was last calibrated.']
  sensor_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled calibration date for the sensor.']
  sensor_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the sensor providing the reading.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Production shift during which the reading was taken.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the reading value is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reading record.']
  work_center varchar [note: 'Work center responsible for the equipment.']

  Note: 'Counter and measurement point readings for equipment — tracks operating hours, production cycles, stroke counts, mileage, and other counter-based metrics used to trigger condition-based maintenance. Each reading is time-stamped and associated with a specific measurement point on the equipment. Maps to SAP PM Measurement Document (IK11) and supports predictive maintenance trigger logic.'
}

Table asset.spare_parts_catalog {
  spare_parts_catalog_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each spare parts catalog record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links spare part inventory valuation to cost centers for inventory costing and budgeting in automotive plants.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Spare parts inventory tracks which supplier provides each part to enable procurement planning, cost analysis, and recall traceability.']
  cad_file_reference varchar [note: 'Path or identifier for the CAD file stored in the PLM system.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or quality certifications applicable to the part.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the catalog record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the listed price.']
  dimensions_mm varchar [note: 'Physical dimensions expressed as LxWxH in millimetres.']
  drawing_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAD drawing that defines the part geometry.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the part information is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the part information remains valid; null for indefinite.']
  eol_date date [note: 'Planned date when the part will no longer be manufactured or supported.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the part must not be used.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the part is critical to equipment operation and requires special handling.']
  is_obsolete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is obsolete and should not be used for new maintenance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from order to receipt of the part.']
  list_price_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard catalog price in US dollars.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse or bin identifier where the part is stored.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability.']
  maintenance_interval_hours varchar [note: 'Recommended operating hours between preventive maintenance actions.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Preferred maintenance approach for the part.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactures the part.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number assigned by the original equipment manufacturer.']
  max_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average operating time between part failures.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from the supplier.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of parts (e.g., hydraulics, electronics).']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the spare part, including function and key characteristics.']
  part_family varchar [note: 'Family of parts sharing a common design platform.']
  part_group varchar [note: 'Logical group of parts that are interchangeable or used together.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the spare part.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or OEM assigned part number used to uniquely identify the spare part.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for engineering changes to the part.']
  part_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the part category.']
  part_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the part by functional domain.']
  regulatory_approval_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating compliance with applicable safety or emissions regulations.']
  reorder_point_quantity varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety classification based on internal or external assessment.']
  safety_stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum inventory level to protect against stockouts.']
  spare_parts_catalog_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the part in the catalog.']
  stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the part currently on hand.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the preferred supplier for this part.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for inventory and ordering.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the record.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Manufacturer warranty duration expressed in months.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Net weight of the part in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Catalog of spare parts and MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) materials associated with specific equipment and functional locations. Defines the bill of materials for maintenance — critical spare parts, recommended stock quantities, lead times, and supplier references. Links equipment to the materials required for its maintenance. Supports SAP PM Equipment BOM (CS15) and MRP planning for maintenance materials.'
}

Table asset.maintenance_task_list {
  maintenance_task_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the maintenance task list.']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_plan. Business justification: Tie maintenance task lists to their parent maintenance plan.']
  applicable_asset_type varchar [note: 'Type of asset (e.g., CNC machine, robot, conveyor) the task applies to.']
  author varchar [note: 'Name of the person who authored the task list.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard governing the task.']
  cost_estimate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to perform the task.']
  cost_estimate_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the cost estimate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task list record was created.']
  criticality varchar [note: 'Impact level of task failure on operations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the task list is no longer valid (null if indefinite).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the task list is valid.']
  environmental_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task has environmental impact considerations.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of equipment (e.g., mechanical, electrical, hydraulic).']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration to complete the task, in minutes.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated labor effort in hours.']
  frequency_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between recurring executions of the task.']
  frequency_interval_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between recurring executions of the task.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates if the task list is deprecated and should not be used for new work orders.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who last modified the task list.']
  maintenance_task_list_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work to be performed.']
  maintenance_task_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the task list.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Category of maintenance strategy the task belongs to.']
  overtime_allowed boolean [note: 'Whether overtime work is permitted for this task.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level indicating urgency.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulation or guideline reference (e.g., NHTSA §123).']
  required_materials varchar [note: 'Materials or consumables needed for the task.']
  required_skill_level varchar [note: 'Skill level required for personnel performing the task.']
  required_tools varchar [note: 'List of tools or equipment needed to execute the task.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates if task execution requires managerial approval.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date of the latest revision of the task list.']
  safety_instructions varchar [note: 'Safety precautions and personal protective equipment required.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Overall safety risk rating for the task.']
  task_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the task within the maintenance catalog.']
  task_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the maintenance task.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the task list.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the task list definition.']
  work_order_template_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order template that uses this task list.']

  Note: 'Standardized task list (operation list) defining the sequence of maintenance activities, required labor skills, estimated durations, safety instructions, and material requirements for a specific maintenance strategy. Reusable template referenced by maintenance plans and orders. Maps to SAP PM Task List (PLMK) and supports standardization of preventive maintenance procedures across plants.'
}

Table asset.acquisition {
  acquisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the asset acquisition record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Critical for capital expenditure allocation to the proper cost center during asset acquisition accounting.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or facility where the asset is installed.']
