  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: When equipment is sold to external customers, acquisition records must capture the buyer party for revenue recognition and contract compliance.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the asset.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the asset.']
  acquisition_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier or reference number for the acquisition transaction.']
  acquisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the acquisition process.']
  acquisition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the acquisition event was recorded (e.g., purchase order issuance).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the acquisition was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the acquisition request.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Business category grouping for the asset (e.g., production, auxiliary).']
  asset_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the asset at the time of acquisition.']
  asset_subcategory varchar [note: 'More detailed classification within the asset category.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label or tag assigned to the asset for identification on the shop floor.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the acquired asset.']
  budget_line_item varchar [note: 'Budget line item code used for the acquisition funding.']
  capex_project_code varchar [note: 'Capital expenditure project identifier linked to the acquisition.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was officially commissioned and ready for production.']
  cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base purchase price of the asset before taxes, fees, and installation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the asset over its useful life.']
  depreciation_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years over which the asset is depreciated.']
  expected_mtbf_hours double [note: 'Planned Mean Time Between Failures for the asset, expressed in operating hours.']
  expected_mttr_hours double [note: 'Planned Mean Time To Repair for the asset, expressed in hours.']
  finance_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the acquisition cost is posted.']
  installation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost incurred to install and commission the asset at the plant.']
  maintenance_contract_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a separate maintenance contract exists for the asset.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that produced the asset.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model designation of the asset as defined by the manufacturer.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the purchase order associated with the acquisition.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the acquisition record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the acquisition record.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number of the asset.']
  sop_readiness_date date [note: 'Target date when the asset is expected to be ready for Start of Production.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applied to the acquisition.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount of the acquisition (cost + installation + taxes).']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount payable after taxes and any discounts.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate tax amount applied to the acquisition.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated operational lifespan of the asset for planning purposes.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the vendor supplying the asset.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor warranty period expires.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor warranty period begins.']

  Note: 'Capital asset acquisition record capturing the procurement and commissioning of new manufacturing equipment, tooling, and plant infrastructure. Tracks CapEx authorization reference, purchase order reference, vendor, acquisition cost, installation cost, commissioning date, SOP (Start of Production) readiness date, and depreciation method. Links to finance domain for fixed asset accounting and supports CapEx project tracking.'
}

Table asset.asset_valuation {
  asset_valuation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each asset valuation record.']
  asset_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manufacturing asset being valued.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tracks which employee performed the valuation; required for financial audit and change‑control documentation.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manufacturing asset being valued.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recorded to date for the asset.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount representing the current book value of the asset.']
  asset_valuation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the valuation record.']
  book_value_after_valuation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Asset book value after applying the current valuation adjustments.']
  book_value_before_valuation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Asset book value prior to the current valuation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the valuation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the valuation amount.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Projected or actual date when the asset will be fully depreciated.']
  depreciation_expense_current_year decimal(18,2) [note: 'Depreciation expense recognized for the asset in the current fiscal year.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation for the asset.']
  depreciation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation of the asset began.']
  impairment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of impairment applied to the asset value, if any.']
  impairment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the valuation includes an impairment adjustment.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Asset book value after subtracting accumulated depreciation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the valuation.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for performing the valuation (e.g., annual review, sale, impairment).']
  residual_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated salvage value of the asset at the end of its useful life.']
  revaluation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary increase or decrease applied during a revaluation.']
  revaluation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the valuation represents a revaluation (as opposed to routine depreciation).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the valuation data.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or process identifier that last modified the valuation record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the valuation record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the asset in years for depreciation purposes.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset valuation was performed.']
  valuation_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title for the valuation record.']
  valuation_number varchar [note: 'Business reference number assigned to the valuation event.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Category of valuation: initial, periodic, impairment, or revaluation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or process identifier that created the valuation record.']

  Note: 'Financial valuation record for manufacturing assets tracking book value, net book value, accumulated depreciation, depreciation method (straight-line, declining balance), useful life, residual value, and revaluation history. Supports SAP FI-AA (Asset Accounting) integration and provides the financial dimension of asset management for CapEx reporting, impairment testing, and insurance valuation.'
}

Table asset.maintenance_cost {
  maintenance_cost_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each maintenance cost record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Ensures each maintenance cost line is charged to the correct cost center for financial consolidation.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or functional location incurring the cost.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or functional location incurring the cost.']
  maintenance_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance order to which this cost line belongs.']
  spare_parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.spare_parts_catalog. Business justification: Link maintenance cost lines to the specific spare part used.']
  consumables_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of consumable items (e.g., lubricants, cleaning supplies).']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost element (e.g., labor, material, external service).']
  cost_object_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SAP CO cost object (e.g., order, WBS element) for settlement.']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cost is planned (budget) or actual incurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance cost record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the cost line.']
  downtime_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total production downtime caused by the maintenance, measured in minutes.']
  external_service_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of third‑party services or contractors.']
  functional_location varchar [note: 'Plant or line location code where the equipment resides.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the external invoice was issued.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice identifier for third‑party services or parts.']
  labor_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount for labor (labor_hours * labor_rate).']
  labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total labor hours spent on the maintenance activity.']
  labor_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Hourly rate applied to labor hours, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  line_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units represented by this cost line (e.g., number of labor entries).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the cost line within the maintenance order.']
  maintenance_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact end time of the maintenance activity.']
  maintenance_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact start time of the maintenance activity.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the maintenance work.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Category of maintenance activity performed.']
  material_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of materials used.']
  material_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of material consumed for the maintenance task.']
  material_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the material quantity.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operating hours between successive failures for the equipment.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the equipment after a failure.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the cost is posted to financial ledgers.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of cost settlement against the cost object.']
  spare_parts_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of spare parts consumed during maintenance.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the total cost.']
  total_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregated total cost for the line (sum of all cost components).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the maintenance cost record.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor or service provider.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment warranty expires.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance is covered under warranty (true) or not (false).']
  work_center varchar [note: 'Work center responsible for performing the maintenance activity.']

  Note: 'Maintenance cost record aggregating actual labor, material, and external service costs incurred per maintenance order and per equipment/functional location. Captures cost element breakdown (internal labor, external contractor, spare parts, consumables), cost center allocation, and settlement to production cost objects. Supports SAP CO cost object controlling and maintenance budget vs. actual variance analysis.'
}

Table asset.equipment_reliability {
  equipment_reliability_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each equipment reliability record.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment (machine, tool, or fixture) to which the reliability metrics apply.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment (machine, tool, or fixture) to which the reliability metrics apply.']
  availability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of scheduled production time that the equipment was available for operation (Uptime / (Uptime + Downtime)).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the reliability record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score (0‑1) indicating the completeness and accuracy of the source data used to compute the reliability metrics.']
  failure_rate_per_hour decimal(10,4) [note: 'Number of equipment failures occurring per operating hour during the reporting period.']
  maintenance_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost incurred for all maintenance activities on the equipment in the reporting period.']
  maintenance_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the maintenance cost amount.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average elapsed operating time between successive failures of the equipment, expressed in hours.']
  mean_time_to_repair_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to restore equipment to operational condition after a failure, expressed in hours.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for additional observations or remarks about the reliability record.']
  number_of_failures varchar [note: 'Count of distinct failure events recorded for the equipment during the reporting period.']
  oee_availability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Availability portion of OEE, reflecting planned production time versus actual uptime.']
  oee_performance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance portion of OEE, measuring actual production speed against ideal speed.']
  oee_quality_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality portion of OEE, representing good units produced versus total units processed.']
  overall_oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite metric combining availability, performance, and quality percentages for the equipment.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site where the equipment is installed.']
  record_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the reliability record, typically combining plant, equipment, and period (e.g., "PlantA_Stamping_2023Q1").']
  reliability_category varchar [note: 'Type of maintenance or failure mode that the reliability record reflects.']
  reliability_code varchar [note: 'Business code used by external systems or reports to reference this reliability record.']
  reliability_status varchar [note: 'Current health classification of the equipment based on its reliability metrics.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar day of the period for which reliability metrics are calculated.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'First calendar day of the period for which reliability metrics are calculated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the raw data for this reliability record (e.g., "SAP PM").']
  total_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Cumulative minutes the equipment was non‑operational due to failures or planned maintenance within the reporting period.']
  total_uptime_minutes varchar [note: 'Cumulative minutes the equipment was operational and producing within the reporting period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the reliability record.']

  Note: 'Reliability performance record for equipment tracking MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures), MTTR (Mean Time To Repair), availability percentage, failure rate, and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) components (availability, performance, quality) over defined reporting periods. Computed from downtime and maintenance order actuals. Supports reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) programs and IATF 16949 equipment effectiveness monitoring.'
}

Table asset.condition_monitoring {
  condition_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each condition monitoring measurement event.']
  asset_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manufacturing asset or equipment to which the sensor is attached.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who validated or acknowledged the measurement.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manufacturing asset or equipment to which the sensor is attached.']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Link to the maintenance plan applicable to the asset at the time of measurement.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who validated or acknowledged the measurement.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Connected‑vehicle telemetry alerts must be associated with the vehicle owner for proactive service and safety compliance.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: Condition‑monitoring sensor data is tied to the work center where the equipment operates, needed for predictive maintenance dashboards.']
  anomaly_detected boolean [note: 'True when the anomaly_score exceeds the configured alert threshold.']
  anomaly_score double [note: 'Numeric score from the anomaly detection model indicating deviation from normal behavior.']
  battery_level_percent varchar [note: 'Remaining battery charge of the sensor expressed as a percent.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration state of the sensor.']
  comment varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional observations or operator notes.']
  condition_monitoring_status varchar [note: 'Overall health status derived from the measurement and thresholds.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the measurement record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assessment of the measurement record.']
  equipment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the asset at the time of measurement.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sensor measurement was captured on the shop floor.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the sensor firmware at event time.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location code or description where the sensor is installed.']
  maintenance_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement triggers a maintenance work order.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the measured parameter (e.g., vibration, temperature, oil analysis).']
  measurement_value double [note: 'Numeric value recorded for the measurement_type at event_timestamp.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or facility.']
  predicted_time_to_failure_hours double [note: 'Estimated remaining operating time before failure, as predicted by the model.']
  recorded_by_system varchar [note: 'Source system that transmitted the measurement to the data lake.']
  reliability_score double [note: 'Composite score representing the overall reliability of the asset based on recent measurements.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the IoT sensor that generated the measurement.']
  signal_strength_dbm double [note: 'Radio signal strength of the sensor transmission.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the measurement data.']
  threshold_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True when measurement_value exceeds threshold_value.']
  threshold_value double [note: 'Configured limit for the measurement; breach indicates abnormal condition.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Engineering unit associated with measurement_value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the measurement record.']

  Note: 'Condition monitoring and predictive maintenance sensor data record capturing real-time and periodic equipment health parameters — vibration levels, temperature, oil analysis results, acoustic emission, current draw, and pressure readings from IoT sensors (Bosch IoT / Geotab). Each record represents a measurement event with timestamp, sensor ID, measured value, engineering unit, and threshold breach indicator. Feeds predictive maintenance algorithms.'
}

Table asset.asset_tooling_usage {
  asset_tooling_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset_tooling_usage data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Link asset_tooling_usage to equipment_registry to associate usage records with the specific equipment.']
  tooling_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.tooling_registry. Business justification: Link asset_tooling_usage to tooling_registry to capture which tooling is used for each asset usage record.']

  Note: 'Operational usage record for production tooling and dies tracking actual strokes, shots, cycles, or impressions consumed per production run. Captures tool life consumed vs. total rated life, wear indicators, last refurbishment date, and remaining life percentage. Drives tool change scheduling, die maintenance triggers, and PPAP tool life validation. Integrates with MES press and stamping line data.'
}

Table asset.equipment_transfer {
  equipment_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each equipment transfer record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer accountable for the transfer execution.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Add equipment_registry reference to equipment_transfer to know which asset is moved.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Tracks equipment moves between functional locations tied to specific vehicle programs for cost and warranty tracking.']
  responsible_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer accountable for the transfer execution.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory or quality standard governing the transfer (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transfer record was first created in the system.']
  depreciation_amount_after decimal(12,2) [note: 'Accumulated depreciation value of the equipment after the transfer.']
  depreciation_amount_before decimal(12,2) [note: 'Accumulated depreciation value of the equipment before the transfer.']
  destination_functional_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the functional location or plant where the equipment is being transferred to.']
  equipment_condition_after varchar [note: 'Recorded condition of the equipment at the destination after move.']
  equipment_condition_before varchar [note: 'Recorded condition of the equipment at the source location prior to move.']
  equipment_transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the transfer (e.g., pending, in progress, completed, cancelled).']
  installation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment was installed after arrival.']
  mtbf_hours_after decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean time between failures for the equipment measured after the transfer.']
  mtbf_hours_before decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean time between failures for the equipment measured before the transfer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the transfer.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason for the equipment transfer.']
  source_functional_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the functional location or plant where the equipment originated.']
  transfer_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for moving the equipment.']
  transfer_cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the transfer cost (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  transfer_date timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the equipment was moved from source to destination.']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the equipment transfer event.']
  transfer_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an external work order or purchase order linked to the transfer.']
  transport_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the equipment move.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the transfer record.']
  warranty_status_after varchar [note: 'Warranty state of the equipment at the destination location.']
  warranty_status_before varchar [note: 'Warranty state of the equipment at the source location.']

  Note: 'Record of equipment relocation and transfer events between plants, production lines, or functional locations. Captures source and destination functional locations, transfer date, reason code (line rebalancing, plant consolidation, program changeover), transport method, installation status at destination, and responsible engineer. Supports asset location history and plant capacity planning.'
}

Table asset.maintenance_work_center {
  maintenance_work_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each maintenance work center.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allows work‑center overhead allocation to finance cost centers for accurate cost accounting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person responsible for the work center.']
  responsible_person_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person responsible for the work center.']
  capacity_per_shift decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum production capacity of the work center per shift (units per hour).']
  certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specific certifications are mandatory for staff.']
  compliance_iso9001 boolean [note: 'True if the work center complies with ISO 9001 quality management standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the work center record was created.']
  downtime_estimate_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical downtime expected for scheduled maintenance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the work center becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the work center ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'Category of equipment primarily used in the work center.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Higher‑level functional area (e.g., Assembly, Paint, Body Shop) the work center belongs to.']
  headcount varchar [note: 'Number of personnel assigned to the work center.']
  hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard hourly labor cost for the work center (currency defined by hourly_rate_currency).']
  hourly_rate_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the hourly labor rate.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the work center is deemed critical to production continuity.']
  is_operational boolean [note: 'True if the work center is currently operational.']
  labor_cost_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard labor cost per hour for the work center.']
  labor_cost_rate_currency varchar [note: 'Currency for the labor cost rate.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days between routine maintenance activities.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategic approach for maintaining the work center.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Preferred time window for performing maintenance.']
  maintenance_work_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the work center\'s responsibilities.']
  maintenance_work_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work center.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operating hours between failures for the work center.']
  mean_time_to_repair_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to restore the work center after a failure.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next maintenance activity.']
  oee_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OEE percentage for the work center.']
  parts_list_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code to the parts list associated with the work center.']
  parts_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether spare parts are required for scheduled work.']
  plant_location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the plant or functional location where the work center is assigned.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for scheduling work center capacity.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department accountable for the work center\'s performance.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of safety certifications for the work center.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed safety risk associated with the work center.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Shift arrangement for the work center staff.']
  skill_level_required varchar [note: 'Minimum skill level required for personnel assigned to the work center.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the work center record.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in SAP and other systems.']
  work_center_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of work centers for planning purposes.']
  work_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the work center (e.g., Electrical Assembly Team).']
  work_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work center by craft or functional area.']

  Note: 'Master data for maintenance work centers (craft groups) — electrical, mechanical, hydraulics, robotics, tooling, and facilities maintenance teams. Defines available capacity (headcount, shift patterns), skill requirements, cost rates, and plant assignment. Maps to SAP PM Work Center (CR01) and is used for maintenance order scheduling, capacity leveling, and labor cost calculation.'
}

Table asset.inspection {
  inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset inspection record.']
  asset_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset/equipment inspected.']
  checklist_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection checklist used.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Inspection reports are stored as compliance documents for regulatory audit; linking provides direct access to the document.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspector who performed the inspection.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset/equipment inspected.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspector who performed the inspection.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Allows inspection results to be traced to the vehicle program, required for quality audit and compliance reporting.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or facility where the inspection took place.']
  plant_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or facility where the inspection took place.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: Inspections are performed at a work center; linking enables Inspection‑by‑Work‑Center compliance reports.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Asset tag or barcode associated with the inspected equipment.']
  attached_documents varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of document identifiers attached to the inspection record.']
  calibration_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether calibration is required following inspection.']
  checklist_version varchar [note: 'Version of the checklist applied during inspection.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations recorded by the inspector.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards framework applicable to the inspection.']
  corrective_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for corrective actions linked to this inspection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection record was first created in the system.']
  downtime_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection caused production downtime.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total production downtime minutes attributable to the inspection.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time spent performing the inspection, in minutes.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of findings from the inspection.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the inspection as used in operational systems.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection was performed.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of inspection performed.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the inspector.']
  maintenance_order_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of maintenance order generated from inspection findings.']
  non_conformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of non-conformances identified during the inspection.']
  non_conformance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection resulted in any non-conformances.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk severity assigned based on inspection findings.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Result of safety certification checks during inspection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection record.']

  Note: 'Formal inspection record for equipment, tooling, and plant infrastructure covering safety inspections, regulatory compliance inspections (OSHA, pressure vessel, crane), quality system audits (IATF 16949), and pre-production readiness checks. Captures inspection type, inspector, inspection date, checklist results, findings, non-conformances raised, and corrective action references. Distinct from calibration (which is measurement-specific) and maintenance orders (which are repair-focused).'
}

Table asset.warranty_claim_equipment {
  warranty_claim_equipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for each equipment warranty claim record.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or tooling item for which the warranty claim is filed.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or tooling item for which the warranty claim is filed.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the OEM or supplier that provided the equipment under warranty.']
  claim_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the system.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Business visible claim reference number assigned by the warranty claim system.']
  claim_resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim was resolved (approved, rejected, or settled).']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warranty claim.']
  claim_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the warranty claim was submitted by the maintenance organization.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the claim outcome requested (repair, replacement, or credit).']
  claim_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the claim record.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who created the claim.']
  credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary credit awarded to the plant for the claim (if applicable).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary fields.']
  defect_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the reported defect.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the equipment defect or failure reported.']
  downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total equipment downtime (in hours) attributable to the defect.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer supplied serial number of the equipment.']
  equipment_tag varchar [note: 'Plant asset tag or barcode assigned to the equipment.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment failure is classified as critical to production.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the equipment for this claim.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the claim.']
  parts_used varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers or SKUs used during repair.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the claim for handling.']
  repair_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the repair or replacement work performed.']
  repair_decision varchar [note: 'Decision taken by the supplier on how to resolve the claim.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the root cause of the equipment failure.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the identified root cause.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement amount was paid to the plant.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the claim settlement payment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the claim record (e.g., SAP PM, MES).']
  total_repair_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for repairing the equipment (excluding warranty credit).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who last modified the claim.']
  warranty_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number provided by the supplier or OEM for tracking the claim.']
  warranty_coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment warranty coverage expires.']
  warranty_coverage_start_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment warranty coverage began.']

  Note: 'Warranty claim record for manufacturing equipment and tooling under OEM or supplier warranty. Tracks claim submission date, equipment defect description, warranty coverage period, supplier/OEM claim reference, repair or replacement decision, credit amount received, and claim settlement status. Distinct from vehicle warranty (owned by aftersales domain) — this covers plant equipment warranties managed by the maintenance organization.'
}

Table asset.shutdown_plan {
  shutdown_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the shutdown plan record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for managing the shutdown project.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.functional_location. Business justification: Associate shutdown plans with the functional location they affect.']
  project_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for managing the shutdown project.']
  affected_line_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of production line identifiers impacted by the shutdown.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the shutdown plan.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shutdown plan was approved or rejected.']
  cost_estimate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost of the shutdown plan.']
  cost_estimate_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost estimate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shutdown plan record was first created in the system.']
  downtime_estimate_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Projected total downtime in hours for the shutdown.']
  estimated_production_loss_units varchar [note: 'Projected number of vehicle units not produced due to the shutdown.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the shutdown plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business number or code assigned to the shutdown plan.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled end date and time for the shutdown event.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start date and time for the shutdown event.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the shutdown plan.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Brief description of the work to be performed during the shutdown.']
  shutdown_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shutdown plan.']
  shutdown_type varchar [note: 'Category of shutdown event, e.g., annual maintenance, SOP retool, emergency, or capital installation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the record, e.g., SAP PM.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the shutdown plan record.']

  Note: 'Planned plant or line shutdown event record for major maintenance turnarounds, model year changeovers, retooling, and capital installation projects. Captures shutdown type (annual maintenance shutdown, SOP retool, emergency shutdown), planned start/end dates, affected production lines, estimated production loss (units), scope of work summary, and responsible project manager. Supports production planning and CapEx project coordination.'
}

Table asset.lubrication_schedule {
  lubrication_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the lubrication schedule record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links lubrication activities to cost centers for budgeting of preventive maintenance expenses.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person (e.g., maintenance planner) responsible for the schedule.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or machine to which this lubrication schedule applies.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or machine to which this lubrication schedule applies.']
  responsible_person_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person (e.g., maintenance planner) responsible for the schedule.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Lubrication scheduling must reference the lubricant supplier to ensure specification compliance and track supplier performance.']
  application_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the lubricant is applied to the equipment.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is being followed as required.']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Time unit for the lubrication interval.']
  frequency_value varchar [note: 'Numeric part of the lubrication interval (e.g., 8).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the lubrication point is critical to equipment safety or performance.']
  last_lubrication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the equipment was last lubricated.']
  lubricant_spec varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑specified grade or standard for the lubricant (e.g., ISO VG 46).']
  lubrication_point_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the specific point or component to be lubricated.']
  lubrication_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the schedule.']
  lubrication_type varchar [note: 'Category of lubricant used (e.g., grease, oil, synthetic).']
  maintenance_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of lubrication schedules for planning (e.g., primary, secondary).']
  next_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the next lubrication activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the schedule.']
  quantity_amount decimal(10,3) [note: 'Amount of lubricant applied each interval.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the lubricant quantity.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule record.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department accountable for executing the schedule.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the lubrication activity.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the lubrication schedule in maintenance planning.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the lubrication schedule.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the schedule (e.g., SAP PM, MES).']

  Note: 'Lubrication management schedule defining lubrication points on equipment, lubricant type (grease, oil, specification grade), application method, frequency, and quantity. Tracks last lubrication date, next due date, and compliance status. Supports autonomous maintenance programs (TPM — Total Productive Maintenance) and prevents equipment failures attributable to inadequate lubrication. Managed as a distinct schedule from general PM plans due to high frequency and operator-executed nature.'
}

Table asset.class {
  class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each asset class record.']
  default_compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Asset class defines a default compliance standard document; linking provides quick reference for class‑level compliance.']
  default_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Provides default cost center for new assets of this class, supporting automated cost allocation in ERP.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category grouping for the asset class.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibrations.']
  calibration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class must be calibrated periodically.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the asset class in ERP and PLM systems.']
  class_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the asset class purpose and scope.']
  class_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the asset class (e.g., "Robotic Arm").']
  class_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the asset class.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the asset class record was first created.']
  default_capacity_units_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical production capacity associated with the asset class.']
  default_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost amount associated with the asset class (e.g., average purchase price).']
  default_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for default cost amounts.']
  default_energy_consumption_kwh_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Typical energy usage for assets of this class, used for cost and sustainability calculations.']
  default_environmental_compliance varchar [note: 'Standard environmental regulation applicable to the asset class.']
  default_lifecycle_phase varchar [note: 'Typical lifecycle phase where assets of this class reside.']
  default_location_type varchar [note: 'Common location type for assets of this class.']
  default_maintenance_cost_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard labor cost applied to maintenance activities for this class.']
  default_maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval between scheduled maintenance events.']
  default_mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical MTBF used for planning and reliability analysis.']
  default_mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical MTTR used for downtime estimation.']
  default_power_rating_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard power rating for the asset class.']
  default_responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that typically owns assets of this class.']
  default_safety_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class must hold a safety certification.']
  default_spare_part_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing spare part inventory for this asset class.']
  default_supplier varchar [note: 'Preferred supplier for procurement of assets in this class.']
  default_warranty_years varchar [note: 'Standard warranty duration offered for assets of this class.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation ends (end of useful life).']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate assets of this class.']
  depreciation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation begins for newly created assets of this class.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the asset class definition is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the asset class definition is no longer valid (nullable).']
  is_depreciable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class are subject to depreciation.']
  is_maintainable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets of this class require maintenance planning.']
  maintenance_strategy_default varchar [note: 'Standard maintenance approach applied to assets of this class.']
  sap_asset_class_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding SAP S/4HANA PM asset class code for integration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the asset class record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected economic life of assets in this class, in years.']

  Note: 'Reference classification taxonomy for manufacturing assets defining asset classes (production machinery, robots, conveyors, tooling, gauges, vehicles, buildings, utilities) with associated depreciation parameters, maintenance strategy defaults, calibration requirements, and SAP asset class codes. Provides standardized classification used across equipment registry, asset valuation, and maintenance planning. Passes First-Class Entity Test with 8+ unique business attributes beyond id/name/code.'
}

Table asset.equipment_service_subscription {
  equipment_service_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the equipment_service_subscription association']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to the equipment registry record']
  service_id varchar [note: 'FK to the mobility service record']
  billing_amount decimal [note: 'Monetary amount billed for the subscription period']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the billing amount']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the subscription ends or is terminated']
  equipment_service_subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscription (active, suspended, cancelled)']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the subscription becomes effective']
  subscription_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the subscription']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual subscription of a manufacturing equipment asset to a mobility service offering. Each record links one equipment_registry to one mobility_service and stores subscription-specific data such as dates, status, and billing.. Existence Justification: Each manufacturing equipment asset can be subscribed to multiple mobility services, and each mobility service can be subscribed to by many equipment assets. The subscription is an operational record that humans create, update, and delete, capturing start/end dates, status, and billing details.'
}

Table asset.compliance_assessment {
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ComplianceAssessment association']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the equipment registry']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory requirement']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance assessment was performed']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the equipment for this requirement']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments about the assessment']

  Note: 'Represents the assessment of a specific equipment asset against a particular regulatory requirement, recording the compliance status, assessment date, and any notes. Each record links one equipment_registry to one regulatory_requirement.. Existence Justification: Each piece of manufacturing equipment must be evaluated against multiple regulatory requirements, and each regulatory requirement applies to many pieces of equipment. The compliance team actively creates, updates, and deletes records linking equipment to regulations, capturing status, assessment dates, and notes for each pairing.'
}

Table asset.inspection_checklist {
  inspection_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inspection_checklist']
  parent_inspection_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inspection_checklist (parent_inspection_checklist_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the checklist.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or role that approved the checklist.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the checklist was approved.']
  associated_asset_type varchar [note: 'Type of asset (e.g., robot, press, conveyor) the checklist applies to.']
  author varchar [note: 'Name of the person or role that authored the checklist.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent change to the checklist.']
  checklist_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the checklist in SAP PM and other systems.']
  checklist_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the inspection checklist.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the checklist record was first created.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the checklist.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the checklist purpose and scope.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the checklist becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the checklist is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Typical time in minutes required to complete the checklist.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the checklist is scheduled to be performed.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of inspection the checklist supports.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the checklist must be performed for the asset.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the checklist was last reviewed for relevance.']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'External regulation or standard the checklist satisfies (e.g., ISO 26262).']
  required_certification varchar [note: 'Specific certification or training required to execute the checklist.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory reviews of the checklist.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety risk rating associated with the checklist.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the checklist.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the checklist.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the checklist (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for inspection_checklist. Referenced by checklist_id.'
}

Table asset.measurement_point {
  measurement_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for measurement_point']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset (machine, line, or equipment) to which the measurement point belongs.']
  parent_measurement_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on measurement_point (parent_measurement_point_id)']
  calibration_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the next required calibration.']
  calibration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent calibration of the measurement point.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the measurement point record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the measurement point purpose and characteristics.']
  installation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement point was installed on the asset.']
  location varchar [note: 'Plant, line, or station where the measurement point is physically installed.']
  max_value double [note: 'Upper bound of acceptable measurement values for this point.']
  measurement_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement is captured automatically by a sensor, entered manually, or derived from other data.']
  min_value double [note: 'Lower bound of acceptable measurement values for this point.']
  point_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the measurement point in external systems.']
  point_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the measurement point.']
  point_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines what physical quantity the point measures.']
  reading_frequency_hz varchar [note: 'How often the measurement point records data, expressed in hertz.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sensor device that provides data for this measurement point.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the measurement point.']
  tolerance double [note: 'Allowed deviation from the nominal value before flagging an alert.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the measurement is recorded (e.g., °C, bar, mm/s², L/min).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the measurement point record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for measurement_point. Referenced by measurement_point_id.'
}

Table customer.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the party record.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program the party is enrolled in.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the party\'s primary mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the party\'s primary mailing address (optional).']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary address (e.g., billing, shipping).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the primary mailing address.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for outbound communications.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the primary mailing address.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned to the party for risk assessment.']
  customer_lifetime_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total net revenue expected from the party over the relationship.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification for analytics and targeting.']
  data_residency_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the party\'s personal data is stored to satisfy data‑sovereignty requirements.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of an individual party, used for age verification and KYC.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for electronic communication.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Additional external reference used for cross‑system reconciliation.']
  gdpr_consent_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has consented to receive email communications under GDPR.']
  gdpr_consent_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has consented to receive SMS communications under GDPR.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date the organization was legally formed.']
  industry_classification varchar [note: 'Standard industry code (NAICS or SIC) describing the party\'s primary business.']
  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party is exempt from sales tax.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of KYC verification for the party.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the party\'s information was last validated against source data.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or organization as recorded for contracts and compliance.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the party within the business relationship.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level within the loyalty program.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has consented to receive marketing communications.']
  net_promoter_score varchar [note: 'Latest NPS rating captured for the party.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by business users about the party.']
  onboarding_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the party was onboarded.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the party was first created in the system.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the party is an individual customer or an organization (e.g., fleet operator, dealer).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for voice or SMS contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the primary mailing address.']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code the party prefers for billing and pricing.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code for the party\'s preferred communication language.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Method most frequently used to reach the party.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the party record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the party record.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number for an organization (e.g., corporate registration, dealer license).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the party record (e.g., Salesforce, CDK).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the party in the source system.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the primary mailing address.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or code for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier (e.g., EIN, VAT) for the party.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Doing‑business‑as name or brand name used by the organization.']

  Note: 'SSOT master entity for all customer identities served by Automotive — retail buyers (B2C), fleet operators, corporate accounts, government entities, and dealer principals. Stores the unified identity record including party type (individual/organization), legal name, tax identification, registration number, preferred language, preferred currency, date of birth (for individuals), incorporation date (for organizations), industry classification (NAICS/SIC), credit rating, KYC status, GDPR consent flags, data residency region, source system (Salesforce Automotive Cloud party ID), onboarding channel, onboarding date, lifecycle status (prospect/active/inactive/churned), and last verified date. This is the anchor entity for all customer-domain relationships.'
}

Table customer.individual {
  individual_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the individual.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer the individual prefers for service or purchase.']
  preferred_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer the individual prefers for service or purchase.']
  salesforce_contact_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the individual in Salesforce Automotive Cloud.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the individual\'s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (apartment, suite, etc.).']
  annual_income_band varchar [note: 'Income range band used for segmentation and marketing.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the individual\'s mailing address.']
  cltv_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total future revenue attributable to the individual.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the individual gave marketing consent.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the mailing address.']
  country_of_residence varchar [note: 'Country where the individual currently resides.']
  customer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the individual as B2C, B2B, government, or fleet.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the individual, used for age‑based analytics and compliance.']
  driver_license_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the driver license.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued driver license identifier.']
  driver_license_state varchar [note: 'State or province that issued the driver license.']
  education_level varchar [note: 'Highest completed education level of the individual.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for electronic communications.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment situation of the individual.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the individual.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑identified gender of the individual.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking individuals belonging to the same household.']
  individual_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the individual record.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the individual.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent vehicle or service purchase.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current loyalty program tier of the individual.']
  marital_status varchar [note: 'Current marital status of the individual.']
  marketing_opt_in_email boolean [note: 'True if the individual has consented to receive marketing emails.']
  marketing_opt_in_phone boolean [note: 'True if the individual has consented to receive marketing phone calls.']
  marketing_opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'True if the individual has consented to receive marketing SMS messages.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the individual, if any.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Country of citizenship of the individual.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Latest NPS rating provided by the individual (‑100 to 100).']
  occupation varchar [note: 'Primary occupation or job title of the individual.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for voice contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the mailing address.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Individual\'s preferred channel for communications.']
  primary_spoken_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 code of the individual\'s primary language.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the individual record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the individual record.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment used for marketing and analytics (e.g., luxury, economy).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the record (e.g., Salesforce, SAP).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the mailing address.']
  suffix varchar [note: 'Suffix such as Jr., Sr., III, if applicable.']
  vehicle_ownership_count varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles currently owned by the individual.']

  Note: 'B2C retail customer profile extending the party master for natural persons. Captures personal identity attributes: first name, middle name, last name, suffix, gender, marital status, employment status, occupation, annual income band, education level, driver license number, driver license state/country, driver license expiry, primary spoken language, nationality, country of residence, household_id (FK to household), loyalty tier, NPS score (latest), CLTV estimate, preferred contact method, marketing opt-in flags, and Salesforce Contact ID. Supports personalized marketing, loyalty management, and customer journey analytics for retail vehicle buyers.'
}

Table customer.organization_account {

  salesforce_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account in Salesforce Automotive Cloud.']

  accounts_payable_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the accounts payable contact.']
  accounts_payable_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the accounts payable contact.']
  accounts_payable_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the accounts payable contact.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the organization\'s primary address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the organization\'s primary address.']
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual revenue of the organization in US dollars.']

  duns_number varchar [note: 'Unique DUNS identifier for the organization.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the account became effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the account expires or is terminated, if applicable.']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles owned or managed by the organization.']
  government_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of government entity, if applicable.']
  industry_codes varchar [note: 'Additional industry-specific codes (e.g., CAGE, NCAGE).']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the organization.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'North American Industry Classification System code for the organization.']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total number of employees in the organization.']
  organization_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organization account.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the organization.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the organization\'s primary address.']
  preferred_oem_programs varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of preferred OEM programs for the organization.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for general communications with the organization.']
