  procurement_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the procurement contact.']
  procurement_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary procurement contact for the organization.']
  procurement_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the procurement contact.']
  sap_customer_number varchar [note: 'Customer number in SAP S/4HANA Finance module.']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Standard Industrial Classification code for the organization.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the organization\'s primary address.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number for the organization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the organization account record.']

  Note: 'B2B customer profile for corporate accounts, fleet operators, government entities, and leasing companies. Extends the party master with organization-specific attributes: legal entity name, DBA name, parent company party_id (for corporate hierarchy), DUNS number, SIC/NAICS code, annual revenue, number of employees, fleet size, procurement contact name, accounts payable contact, credit limit, payment terms, preferred OEM programs, government entity type (federal/state/municipal), contract vehicle type (GSA/state contract), Salesforce Account ID, SAP customer number, and account tier (strategic/key/standard). Enables B2B fleet sales, government procurement, and corporate account management.'
}

Table customer.household {
  household_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the household.']
  individual_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party designated as primary decision maker for household purchases.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program the household is enrolled in.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party designated as primary decision maker for household purchases.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (e.g., apartment, suite).']
  census_block varchar [note: 'Census block identifier for geographic segmentation.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the household residence.']
  cltv decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected total net revenue from the household over its relationship lifespan.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the household.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the household record was first created.']
  estimated_income_band varchar [note: 'Income band estimate used for marketing segmentation.']
  formation_date date [note: 'Date the household entity was first created in the system.']
  household_name varchar [note: 'Legal or commonly used name of the household (e.g., Smith Family).']
  household_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the household record.']
  household_type varchar [note: 'Classification of household composition.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent interaction with the household.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has opted in to marketing communications.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total count of individual members in the household.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information about the household.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Latest NPS rating associated with the household.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the household.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for household communications.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for household contact.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the household data.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the household record in the source system.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the household residence.']
  street_address varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the household.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the household record.']
  vehicle_count varchar [note: 'Count of vehicles registered to members of the household.']

  Note: 'Groups individual customers into household units for B2C relationship management, multi-vehicle ownership tracking, and household-level marketing. Stores household name, primary address, estimated household income band, number of members, number of vehicles owned, primary decision maker party_id, household formation date, household type (single/family/multi-generational), geographic census block, and Salesforce Household ID. Enables household-level CLTV calculation, conquest and retention campaigns, and multi-vehicle loyalty program management.'
}

Table customer.contact_point {
  contact_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each contact point record.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Contact points belong to a party (customer or organization). Adding contact_point.party_id creates the required inbound link and eliminates isolation.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the mailing address (optional).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Preferred channel associated with the contact point for outbound messaging.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the mailing address.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification indicating the nature of the party owning the contact point.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Type of communications the party consents to receive via this contact point.']
  contact_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact point.']
  contact_point_type varchar [note: 'Category of the contact point indicating the medium (e.g., email, phone, mailing address, social media handle).']
  contact_point_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The raw value of the contact point (e.g., email address, phone number, full mailing address, social handle).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the mailing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contact point record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the contact point becomes valid for use.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Email address used for electronic communication.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the contact point is no longer valid.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact point is the primary method for reaching the party.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'True when the contact point has been successfully verified.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications sent to this contact point.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent timestamp when this contact point was used for communication.']
  opt_in_status boolean [note: 'True when the party has consented to receive communications via this contact point.']
  opt_out_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the party withdrew consent for this contact point.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Telephone number including country code, used for voice or SMS contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the mailing address.']
  preferred_contact_hours varchar [note: 'Typical daily time window (e.g., 09:00-17:00) when the party prefers to be contacted.']
  preferred_time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier representing the party\'s preferred time zone for contact.']
  social_handle varchar [note: 'Identifier for the party on a social media platform (e.g., @user).']
  social_platform varchar [note: 'Social media platform associated with the social handle.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the contact point record (e.g., Salesforce, CDK).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the mailing address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contact point record.']
  verification_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contact point was verified.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the contact point (e.g., email link, SMS code).']

  Note: 'Stores all contact information for a party (individual or organization) including email addresses, phone numbers, mailing addresses, and social media handles. Each record captures: contact point type (email/phone/address/social), contact point value, is_primary flag, is_verified flag, verification date, verification method, opt-in status, opt-out date, channel (voice/SMS/email/mail/push), preferred time zone, preferred contact hours, source system, and effective/expiry dates. Supports omnichannel communication, regulatory compliance (TCPA, CAN-SPAM, GDPR), and Salesforce Automotive Cloud contact synchronization.'
}

Table customer.vehicle_ownership {
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each vehicle ownership record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Required for OTA campaign and warranty management linking each ownership record to its connected vehicle entity.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Required for warranty/service eligibility reports linking each ownership record to the dealer that sold the vehicle; dealer_code is denormalized and replaced.']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: Needed for regulatory compliance and recall management to link each vehicle to the exact design specification it was built from.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (party) that owns or leases the vehicle.']
  telematics_device_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.telematics_device. Business justification: Service and OTA update processes need to know which telematics device is installed on each owned vehicle.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Links ownership record to the original sales order, enabling warranty, service, and revenue attribution reports.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Required for warranty, recall and production tracking reports that map each owned vehicle to its engineering program.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Required for ownership verification and warranty claim processing; links each ownership record to the VIN registry entry to retrieve vehicle specs.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'Source through which the vehicle was obtained.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the customer acquired the vehicle (purchase, lease start, etc.).']
  acquisition_dealer_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer that facilitated the acquisition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ownership record was first created.']
  current_odometer varchar [note: 'Most recent mileage reported for the vehicle.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle was disposed of (sold, traded, etc.).']
  disposition_odometer varchar [note: 'Mileage recorded at the time of disposition.']
  disposition_type varchar [note: 'How the ownership relationship ended.']
  insurance_carrier varchar [note: 'Company providing the vehicle\'s insurance coverage.']
  insurance_expiry date [note: 'Date the insurance coverage ends.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the insurance policy covering the vehicle.']
  is_primary_vehicle boolean [note: 'True if this vehicle is the primary vehicle for the customer household.']
  lien_holder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution or entity holding a lien on the vehicle.']
  odometer_at_acquisition varchar [note: 'Vehicle mileage recorded at the time of acquisition.']
  ownership_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the ownership agreement between the customer and the manufacturer/dealer.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the vehicle is held by the customer.']
  purchase_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount paid by the customer for the vehicle at acquisition.']
  registration_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the registration jurisdiction.']
  registration_expiry date [note: 'Date the vehicle registration expires.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official license plate number assigned to the vehicle.']
  registration_state varchar [note: 'State or province where the vehicle is registered.']
  title_number varchar [note: 'Legal document number proving ownership.']
  title_state varchar [note: 'State that issued the title.']
  trade_in_vin varchar [note: 'VIN of a vehicle traded in as part of the acquisition, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ownership record.']
  vehicle_ownership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ownership record.']

  Note: 'Tracks the ownership history of vehicles by customers — the authoritative record linking a customer (party) to a VIN. Captures: VIN, party_id (owner), ownership type (retail purchase/lease/fleet/government), acquisition date, acquisition channel (dealer/direct/auction/fleet), acquisition dealer code, purchase price, trade-in VIN, registration state/country, registration number, registration expiry, title number, title state, lien holder name, insurance carrier, insurance policy number, insurance expiry, odometer at acquisition, current odometer (last reported), is_primary_vehicle flag, disposition type (sold/traded/totaled/repossessed), disposition date, and disposition odometer. SSOT for customer-vehicle relationship; cross-references vehicle domain VIN master.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_membership {
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the loyalty membership record.']
  enrollment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Loyalty Membership Enrollment Tracking records the employee who enrolled the party into the loyalty program.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking members belonging to the same household.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program to which the member is enrolled.']
  primary_loyalty_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (party) that holds the membership.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty membership record was first created.']
  current_tier varchar [note: 'Current loyalty tier of the member based on points qualification.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer enrolled (e.g., online, in‑store).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the customer enrolled in the loyalty program.']
  is_primary_member boolean [note: 'Indicates if this member is the primary account holder in a household linkage.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent activity (e.g., point accrual, redemption) by the member.']
  last_redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent points redemption.']
  lifetime_points_earned decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total points the member has earned over the lifetime of the membership.']
  membership_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the membership itself expires if not renewed.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the member within the loyalty program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes about the membership.']
  points_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current balance of loyalty points available for redemption.']
  points_balance_last_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Points balance at the end of the previous fiscal year.']
  points_earned_this_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Points accrued by the member during the current calendar year.']
  points_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current points balance will expire if not used.']
  points_redeemed_this_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Points redeemed by the member during the current calendar year.']
  preferred_redemption_category varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred category for redeeming points.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the membership within the loyalty program.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the record for data‑governance purposes.']
  redemption_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is currently eligible to redeem points.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code used by the member to refer new customers.']
  salesforce_loyalty_member_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member record in Salesforce Automotive Cloud Loyalty Management.']
  tier_benefits_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of benefits associated with the current tier.']
  tier_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the current tier expires if not renewed.']
  tier_points_required decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of points required to reach the next tier level.']
  tier_qualification_date date [note: 'Date the member qualified for the current tier.']
  total_points_redeemed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate number of points the member has redeemed.']
  total_redemptions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of redemption transactions performed by the member.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the loyalty membership record.']

  Note: 'Manages customer enrollment and status in Automotive loyalty programs (e.g., owner rewards, EV early adopter program, fleet loyalty). Captures: program_id, party_id, membership number, enrollment date, enrollment channel, current tier (bronze/silver/gold/platinum), tier qualification date, tier expiry date, points balance, lifetime points earned, points expiry date, redemption eligibility flag, referral code, referred_by party_id, preferred redemption category, program status (active/suspended/expired/cancelled), and Salesforce Loyalty Management program member ID. Enables tier-based benefits, points accrual/redemption, and retention marketing.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_transaction {
  loyalty_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the loyalty transaction record.']
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program membership to which this transaction belongs.']
  processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Loyalty Points Accrual Audit tracks which employee processed each loyalty transaction at point‑of‑sale.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion or campaign that generated the points, if applicable.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Administrative approval state of the transaction for fraud control and audit.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the transaction was recorded (dealer, online portal, mobile app, or call center).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the system.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Code of the dealer or service location associated with the transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or remarks about the transaction.']
  points_amount varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty points affected by the transaction (positive for earn, negative for redeem/expire).']
  points_balance_after varchar [note: 'Running total of loyalty points for the membership after this transaction is applied.']
  points_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which earned points are scheduled to expire, if applicable.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External document identifier linked to the transaction (e.g., invoice number, repair order number, VIN).']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the transaction (e.g., transaction code used in reporting).']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transaction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the loyalty event actually occurred.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the transaction indicating points earned, redeemed, adjusted, expired, or transferred.']
  triggering_event_type varchar [note: 'Business event that caused the points transaction (e.g., vehicle purchase, service visit, referral, promotion, OTA enrollment, EV charge).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']

  Note: 'Records every points accrual and redemption event within a loyalty program membership. Captures: membership_id, transaction type (earn/redeem/adjust/expire/transfer), transaction date, points amount (positive for earn, negative for redeem/expire), triggering event type (vehicle purchase/service visit/referral/promotion/OTA enrollment/EV charge), reference document number (e.g., RO number, invoice number, VIN), dealer code, channel (dealer/online/app/call center), promotion_id, expiry date of earned points, running points balance after transaction, and approval status. Supports loyalty program P&L, fraud detection, and member engagement analytics.'
}

Table customer.customer_segment {
  customer_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each customer segment record.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence (0‑100) that the segment definition accurately captures the target customers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose and characteristics of the segment.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the segment becomes effective for use.']
  estimated_segment_size varchar [note: 'Projected number of customers that belong to this segment.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the segment is retired or no longer valid.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic extent of the segment\'s relevance.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the segment is currently active for assignment.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the segment was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year included in the segment\'s relevance window.']
  model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year included in the segment\'s relevance window.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Internal team or business unit responsible for maintaining the segment.']
  primary_vehicle_interest varchar [note: 'Dominant vehicle powertrain or body style interest for customers in this segment (e.g., ICE, HEV, PHEV, EV, truck, SUV, commercial). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ICE|HEV|PHEV|EV|truck|SUV|commercial — promote to reference product]']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to order segments when multiple apply to a customer.']
  qualifying_criteria_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the rules or metrics used to assign customers to this segment.']
  revenue_potential_band varchar [note: 'Estimated revenue range the segment is expected to generate.']
  segment_category varchar [note: 'Taxonomy category that defines the basis of the segmentation.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the segment in reporting and downstream systems.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the customer segment.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the segment definition originated.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Free‑form tags or keywords associated with the segment for ad‑hoc filtering.']
  target_customer_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the segment applies to retail consumers, business accounts, or both.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the segment record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the segment definition, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Defines the segmentation taxonomy used to classify customers for marketing, sales, and product planning. Each segment record captures: segment code, segment name, segment category (demographic/behavioral/psychographic/firmographic/lifecycle/vehicle-type), description, target customer type (B2C/B2B/both), qualifying criteria summary, estimated segment size, revenue potential band, primary vehicle interest (ICE/HEV/PHEV/EV/truck/SUV/commercial), geographic scope, model year relevance, is_active flag, effective date, and expiry date. Reference data consumed by individual and organization_account entities for segmentation assignment.'
}

Table customer.segment_membership {
  segment_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each segment membership record.']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer segment to which the party is assigned.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (party) assigned to a segment.']
  assigned_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the segment assignment.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer was assigned to the segment.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign the customer to the segment: rule‑based, manual, or machine‑learning model.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence level (0‑1) indicating the certainty of the segment assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment membership record was first created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the segment membership becomes inactive or is scheduled to expire.']
  is_primary_segment boolean [note: 'True if this segment is the primary segment for the customer; otherwise false.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the ML model that performed the assignment, if assignment_method is ml_model.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the segment membership record.']

  Note: 'Association entity recording which customers belong to which segments at any point in time. Captures: party_id, customer_segment_id, assignment date, assignment method (rule-based/manual/ML-model), model version (if ML-assigned), confidence score (0-1), is_primary_segment flag, expiry date, and assigned_by user. Supports dynamic segmentation, A/B testing cohort assignment, and historical segment membership tracking for campaign attribution and regulatory audit.'
}

Table customer.preference {
  preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each preference entry.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (person or organization) to whom the preference applies.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for delivering communications when the preference_category is \'communication\'.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence level (0.0000‑1.0000) that the stored preference accurately reflects the customer\'s intent.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the preference record was first created in the system.']
  data_origin_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the source system that supplied the preference record.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the preference becomes active.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the preference is no longer valid; null if indefinite.']
  is_opt_out boolean [note: 'True if the customer has explicitly opted out of the indicated preference; otherwise false.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the preference record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form comments or remarks about the preference.']
  preference_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the preference (e.g., communication, vehicle feature, service scheduling, digital experience, privacy).']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the preference (e.g., active, inactive, expired, pending review).']
  preference_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value assigned to the preference key; stored as text and interpreted according to value_data_type.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the preference record: self‑declared by the customer, inferred by analytics, or imported from an external system.']
  value_data_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the native data type of preference_value (e.g., string, boolean, integer, list).']

  Note: 'Stores customer-declared and inferred preferences for communications, vehicle features, service scheduling, and digital experiences. Each record captures: party_id, preference category (communication/vehicle/service/digital/privacy), preference key (e.g., preferred_fuel_type, preferred_body_style, preferred_service_day, email_frequency), preference value, value data type (string/boolean/integer/list), source (self-declared/inferred/imported), confidence score, effective date, expiry date, and last updated timestamp. Supports personalized marketing, product recommendation engines, and connected vehicle feature configuration.'
}

Table customer.customer_consent_record {
  customer_consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer_consent_record data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']

  Note: 'SSOT for all customer consent and privacy preference records required under GDPR, CCPA, TCPA, and CAN-SPAM regulations. Captures: party_id, consent type (marketing_email/marketing_sms/telematics_data/data_sharing/profiling/cookies), consent status (granted/withdrawn/pending), consent version (policy version at time of consent), consent date, withdrawal date, consent channel (web/app/dealer/call_center/paper), IP address at consent, geolocation at consent, consent text snapshot, legal basis (consent/legitimate_interest/contract/legal_obligation), data processing purpose, third_party_sharing_flag, retention period (days), and regulatory jurisdiction (GDPR/CCPA/PIPEDA/LGPD). Critical for regulatory compliance and privacy-by-design.'
}

Table customer.nps_response {
  nps_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each NPS survey response record.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service case created if the response was escalated.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (person or organization) who provided the NPS response.']
  survey_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the NPS survey definition that was administered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the NPS response record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Code of the dealer or service location linked to the survey touchpoint.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the response requires follow‑up action (true) or not (false).']
  follow_up_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any follow‑up activity triggered by the response.']
  nps_response_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the NPS response record.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating provided by the respondent on a 0‑10 scale.']
  promoter_category varchar [note: 'Categorization of the NPS score: promoter (9‑10), passive (7‑8), or detractor (0‑6).']
  response_date date [note: 'Date on which the respondent submitted the completed survey.']
  salesforce_survey_response_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the response record in Salesforce Survey system.']
  survey_channel varchar [note: 'Medium through which the survey was delivered to the respondent.']
  survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the survey was presented to the respondent.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the NPS survey is transactional (post‑purchase) or relational (brand experience).']
  touchpoint varchar [note: 'Business interaction point at which the NPS survey was triggered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the NPS response record.']
  verbatim_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑text comment supplied by the respondent providing qualitative feedback.']
  vin varchar [note: 'Unique 17‑character identifier of the vehicle associated with the response, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Captures Net Promoter Score survey responses from customers across all touchpoints in the automotive ownership journey. Records: party_id, VIN (if vehicle-specific), survey_id, survey type (transactional/relational), touchpoint (vehicle_purchase/service_visit/delivery/recall/roadside/connected_app), dealer code, nps_score (0-10), promoter category (promoter/passive/detractor), verbatim comment, survey channel (email/SMS/app/IVR), survey date, response date, follow-up required flag, follow-up status, case_id (if escalated), and Salesforce Survey Response ID. Feeds NPS KPI reporting and closed-loop customer recovery workflows.'
}

Table customer.cltv_record {
  cltv_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each CLTV record snapshot.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (party) to which this CLTV estimate belongs.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the CLTV estimate was generated.']
  calculation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the CLTV record.']
  churn_probability decimal(5,4) [note: 'Likelihood (0‑1) that the customer will stop doing business within the horizon.']
  cltv_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Present value of the total expected net revenue from the customer over the forecast horizon.']
  cltv_horizon_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which the CLTV is projected.']
  data_completeness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0‑100) reflecting how complete the underlying data was for this CLTV estimate.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual discount rate (as a decimal) applied to future cash flows to compute present value.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the predictive model used for the CLTV calculation.']
  projected_accessories_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue from future accessories and add‑on sales.']
  projected_connected_services_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue from OTA updates, telematics subscriptions, and other connected mobility services.']
  projected_finance_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue from financing, leasing, and related financial products.']
  projected_parts_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue from future parts sales to the customer.']
  projected_service_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue from future service and maintenance activities.']
  projected_vehicle_purchases varchar [note: 'Estimated count of future vehicle purchases by the customer within the horizon.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when this CLTV snapshot was inserted into the Silver layer.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to this CLTV record.']
  retention_probability decimal(5,4) [note: 'Likelihood (0‑1) that the customer will remain active throughout the horizon.']
  revenue_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative revenue earned from the customer up to the calculation date.']
  segment_at_calculation varchar [note: 'Business segment classification of the customer when the CLTV was calculated.']

  Note: 'Stores calculated Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) estimates and their historical snapshots for each customer party. Captures: party_id, calculation date, calculation model version, cltv_amount (present value), cltv_horizon_months, revenue_to_date, projected_vehicle_purchases, projected_service_revenue, projected_parts_revenue, projected_accessories_revenue, projected_finance_revenue, projected_connected_services_revenue, discount_rate, churn_probability, retention_probability, segment_at_calculation, and data_completeness_score. Supports strategic customer investment decisions, retention program targeting, and dealer incentive allocation. NOT an analytics aggregate — this is the operational CLTV record used in CRM workflows.'
}

Table customer.journey_touchpoint {
  journey_touchpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each touchpoint event.']
  agent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales or service agent handling the touchpoint.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign linked to the touchpoint.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales or service agent handling the touchpoint.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program associated with the touchpoint, when applicable.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (person or organization) associated with the touchpoint.']
  salesforce_activity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding activity record in Salesforce Automotive Cloud.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Customer journey analytics associate each touchpoint with a specific vehicle; linking to VIN registry provides model and feature context.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Medium through which the touchpoint was delivered.']
  connected_app_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the connected mobile or in‑vehicle application activated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the touchpoint record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the dealer involved in the touchpoint, if applicable.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the customer for digital interactions.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of the interaction in seconds for digital or call channels.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'System of record that generated the touchpoint event (e.g., Salesforce, DMS, IoT platform).']
  feedback_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual feedback supplied by the customer.']
  feedback_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating provided by the customer for the interaction (1‑5).']
  interaction_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the interaction from the customer\'s perspective.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client device for online interactions.']
  is_assisted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the touchpoint involved human assistance (true) or was fully automated (false).']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City where the touchpoint took place, if location is applicable.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the touchpoint occurred.']
  location_state varchar [note: 'State or province code for the touchpoint location.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty points used during the interaction.']
  marketing_attribution varchar [note: 'Attribution source for the touchpoint (e.g., email, paid search, referral).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations captured for the touchpoint.']
  ota_update_version varchar [note: 'Software version applied during an Over‑The‑Air update touchpoint.']
  repurchase_flag boolean [note: 'True if the touchpoint represents a repeat purchase by the customer.']
  session_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the digital session associated with the touchpoint.']
  touchpoint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the touchpoint occurred.']
  touchpoint_type varchar [note: 'Category of the interaction event in the customer journey.']
  trade_in_vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'VIN of the vehicle offered as a trade‑in during the touchpoint.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the touchpoint record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string for the digital session.']

  Note: 'Records every significant interaction event in a customer\'s lifecycle journey with Automotive — from initial lead through ownership and repurchase. Captures: party_id, VIN (if applicable), touchpoint type (lead_inquiry/test_drive/quote_request/vehicle_purchase/delivery/service_visit/recall_notification/loyalty_redemption/connected_app_activation/OTA_update/trade_in/repurchase), touchpoint date, channel (dealer/online/app/call_center/event/social), dealer code, campaign_id, interaction outcome, duration_seconds (for digital/call interactions), agent_id (for assisted channels), device_type (for digital), session_id (for digital), and Salesforce Activity ID. Enables customer journey mapping, attribution modeling, and next-best-action recommendations.'
}

Table customer.customer_fleet_account {

  Note: 'Specialized master entity for fleet customer accounts — commercial, government, and rental fleet operators — extending organization_account with fleet-specific attributes. Captures: organization_account_id, fleet type (commercial/government/rental/utility/emergency), fleet size (total vehicles), active_fleet_size, annual_replacement_volume, preferred_vehicle_segments (sedan/SUV/truck/van/EV/commercial), preferred_powertrain (ICE/HEV/PHEV/EV), fleet management system name, telematics provider, upfitter name, dedicated fleet sales rep, fleet pricing tier, volume discount percentage, fleet program code, government contract number, GSA schedule number, state contract number, annual spend, and fleet account status. Supports fleet sales operations, volume pricing, and government procurement compliance.'

Table customer.customer_fleet_vehicle_assignment {
  customer_fleet_vehicle_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer_fleet_vehicle_assignment data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  fleet_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fleet Vehicle Assignment Log tracks which fleet manager assigned a vehicle to a fleet account.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Each fleet vehicle assignment is performed by a driver (party). Adding party_id links the assignment to the driver entity and removes isolation.']

  Note: 'Tracks the assignment of specific VINs to fleet accounts and their designated operators/drivers within a fleet. Enables fleet utilization tracking, driver accountability, and fleet billing reconciliation.'
}

Table customer.identity_resolution {
  identity_resolution_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each identity resolution event.']
  primary_identity_golden_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the surviving (golden) party record after resolution.']
  match_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence level (0‑1) indicating how certain the match is, as calculated by the MDM engine.']
  match_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to determine the match: deterministic, probabilistic, or manual review.']
  match_rule_set_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the matching rule set used for this resolution.']
  matched_attributes varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded list of attribute names that were used to compute the match (e.g., ["email","phone","address"]).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this identity resolution record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this identity resolution record.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation why a resolution was rejected, if applicable.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken after the match: merge (combine records), link (associate), suppress (deactivate duplicate), or review (manual investigation required).']
  resolution_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the identity resolution decision was recorded.']
  resolved_by varchar [note: 'User name or system identifier that performed the resolution.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current review state of the resolution: automatically approved, pending manual review, or rejected.']
  source_system_a varchar [note: 'Name or code of the first source system contributing the party record (e.g., Salesforce, SAP, CDK).']
  source_system_b varchar [note: 'Name or code of the second source system contributing the party record.']

  Note: 'Records the results of identity resolution and deduplication processes that match, merge, and link customer party records across source systems (Salesforce, SAP, CDK DMS, dealer systems). Captures: golden_party_id (surviving record), duplicate_party_id (merged/suppressed record), source_system_a, source_system_b, match_rule_set_version, match_confidence_score (0-1), match_method (deterministic/probabilistic/manual), matched_attributes (JSON list of attributes used), resolution_action (merge/link/suppress/review), resolution_date, resolved_by (user or system), review_status (auto-approved/pending_review/rejected), and rejection_reason. Supports MDM governance, SSOT maintenance, and regulatory right-to-erasure compliance.'
}

Table customer.communication_subscription {
  communication_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the communication subscription record.']
  customer_consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consent record that captures the legal basis for this subscription.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (customer) who holds the subscription.']
  preference_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preference record that defines channel preferences for the party.']
  subscription_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Subscription Management Oversight tracks which employee manages a customer\'s communication subscription.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription record was first created.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Code of the dealer associated with a dealer‑specific subscription.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for delivering the communication.']
  external_subscription_code varchar [note: 'External identifier used by third‑party systems to reference this subscription.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the communication is sent to the party.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription is for testing purposes only.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language of the communication.']
  last_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent communication sent under this subscription.']
  marketing_cloud_key varchar [note: 'Unique key used by Salesforce Marketing Cloud to identify the subscription.']
  next_scheduled_send timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next communication to be sent.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the subscription (e.g., Salesforce Automotive Cloud).']
  subscription_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription became effective.']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscription.']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Category of communication subscription (e.g., service_reminder, recall_alert, loyalty_newsletter, ev_charging_tips, ota_update_notification, promotional_offers, dealer_newsletter, safety_bulletin). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: service_reminder|recall_alert|loyalty_newsletter|ev_charging_tips|ota_update_notification|promotional_offers|dealer_newsletter|safety_bulletin — promote to reference product]']
  unsubscribe_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription was terminated by the party.']
  unsubscribe_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the party for unsubscribing, if any.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subscription record.']

  Note: 'Manages customer subscriptions to specific communication programs, newsletters, and notification channels — distinct from consent_record (which captures legal basis) and preference (which captures channel preferences). Captures: party_id, subscription type (service_reminder/recall_alert/loyalty_newsletter/ev_charging_tips/ota_update_notification/promotional_offers/dealer_newsletter/safety_bulletin), subscription status (active/paused/unsubscribed), subscription date, unsubscribe date, unsubscribe reason, delivery channel (email/SMS/push/mail), frequency (daily/weekly/monthly/event-triggered), language, dealer code (for dealer-specific subscriptions), and Salesforce Marketing Cloud subscription key. Enables targeted communication management and CAN-SPAM/GDPR unsubscribe compliance.'
}

Table customer.party_relationship {
  party_relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the party relationship record.']
  from_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source party in the relationship (e.g., parent company, household head, driver).']
  relationship_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Customer Relationship Owner tracking records the employee overseeing a specific party‑to‑party relationship.']
  to_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the target party in the relationship (e.g., subsidiary, household member, authorized driver).']
  authorization_level varchar [note: 'Level of authorization granted to the target party for actions on behalf of the source party.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance verification status of the relationship with respect to regulatory requirements.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'External contract or agreement identifier that governs the relationship, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the relationship record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the relationship becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the relationship ends or is scheduled to terminate.']
  inverse_role varchar [note: 'Corresponding role of the target party from its perspective.']
  is_legal_entity boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the relationship is recognized as a legal entity relationship (e.g., corporate ownership) rather than informal.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates if this relationship is the primary link among multiple relationships of the same type for the source party.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the legal jurisdiction of the relationship.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the relationship details were last reviewed for accuracy or compliance.']
  party_relationship_description varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments describing the nature of the relationship.']
  party_relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the relationship.']
  relationship_role varchar [note: 'Role of the source party within this relationship (e.g., owner, contact, driver, guarantor).']
  relationship_strength varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1-5) indicating the assessed strength or importance of the relationship.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the link (e.g., parent_company, subsidiary, household_member, authorized_driver, fleet_contact, dealer_contact, co_owner, guarantor, beneficiary, partner, vendor, customer, employee). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parent_company|subsidiary|household_member|authorized_driver|fleet_contact|dealer_contact|co_owner|guarantor|beneficiary|partner|vendor|customer|employee — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system that created or maintains this relationship record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or process identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the relationship record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or process identifier that initially created the relationship record.']

  Note: 'Models relationships between party entities — corporate hierarchies (parent/subsidiary), household member relationships, authorized drivers, fleet account contacts, and dealer-customer relationships. Captures: from_party_id, to_party_id, relationship type (parent_company/subsidiary/household_member/authorized_driver/fleet_contact/dealer_contact/co_owner/guarantor/beneficiary), relationship role (from_party perspective), inverse_role (to_party perspective), effective date, expiry date, is_primary flag, authorization level, and source system. Enables corporate account hierarchy navigation, household relationship mapping, and authorized user management for connected vehicle services.'
}

Table customer.pdi_record {
  pdi_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the PDI record.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the vehicle.']
  pdi_technician_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who performed the inspection.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Pre‑delivery inspection must validate vehicle configuration against the VIN registry to ensure correct build and compliance.']
  connected_services_activated boolean [note: 'True if connected vehicle services (e.g., telematics) were activated at delivery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PDI record was first created in the system.']
  customer_signature_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer signed the PDI acceptance form.']
  customer_walkthrough_completed boolean [note: 'True if the dealer completed the customer walkthrough of vehicle features.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the dealer that performed the PDI.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Official date the vehicle was handed over to the customer.']
  floor_mat_count varchar [note: 'Count of floor mats provided with the vehicle.']
  fuel_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fuel level recorded at delivery expressed as a percentage of tank capacity.']
  inspection_checklist_version varchar [note: 'Version of the PDI checklist used during the inspection.']
  inspection_items_json varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing individual checklist item results and comments.']
  key_count varchar [note: 'Count of vehicle keys handed over to the customer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional observations or comments from the technician.']
  odometer_km varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the time of delivery, in kilometers.']
  ota_baseline_version varchar [note: 'Baseline over‑the‑air software version installed on the vehicle at delivery.']
  overall_pass boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the vehicle passed the overall PDI (true) or failed (false).']
  owner_manual_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner’s manual was supplied to the customer.']
  pdi_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the PDI was conducted.']
  pdi_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the PDI event, used for tracking and reference.']
  pdi_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PDI record.']
  pdi_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the PDI inspection was performed.']
  tpms_calibrated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Tire Pressure Monitoring System was calibrated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the PDI record.']

  Note: 'Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) record capturing the formal inspection and handover checklist completed by the dealer before vehicle delivery to the customer. Captures: VIN, party_id (receiving customer), dealer code, PDI date, PDI technician ID, inspection checklist version, overall PDI pass/fail status, individual inspection item results (JSON), fuel level at delivery, odometer at delivery, key count, floor mat count, owner manual provided flag, connected services activation status, OTA baseline software version, TPMS calibration status, customer walkthrough completed flag, customer signature date, and delivery date. Links vehicle domain (VIN) and dealer domain (dealer code) to customer delivery event.'
}

Table customer.customer_test_drive {
  customer_test_drive_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer_test_drive data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Test drive events belong to a customer party; adding party_id FK links test drive to party and eliminates the silo.']
  sales_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Test‑Drive Management Report requires linking each test drive to the salesperson who conducted it.']

  Note: 'Records customer test drive events at dealerships or OEM-hosted drive events. Captures: party_id, VIN (demo vehicle), dealer code, event type (dealer_showroom/OEM_drive_event/home_delivery_test/virtual), test drive date, scheduled start time, actual start time, actual end time, duration_minutes, sales consultant ID, vehicle model, trim level, powertrain type (ICE/HEV/PHEV/EV), customer feedback score (1-5), interest level (hot/warm/cold), follow-up action (quote_requested/brochure_sent/no_action), campaign_id (if event-driven), driver license verified flag, insurance verified flag, and Salesforce Activity ID. Feeds sales pipeline and product interest analytics.'
}

Table customer.case {
  case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the customer service case.']
  assigned_agent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service agent currently assigned to the case.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service agent currently assigned to the case.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (individual, fleet, corporate) who raised the case.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Needed for service case management to pull vehicle specifications, warranty status, and recall info from VIN registry.']
  case_category varchar [note: 'Primary business category for the case (e.g., Service, Sales, Technical).']
  case_description varchar [note: 'Full free‑text description of the issue, request or complaint.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible case number assigned at creation.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the case purpose.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was finally closed.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post‑resolution satisfaction rating provided by the customer (e.g., 1‑10).']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer handling the case.']
  dynamics_case_reference varchar [note: 'Native Microsoft Dynamics 365 identifier for the case.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation applied to the case, if any.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the case was escalated.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was initially created.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level indicating urgency (P1 highest).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the case record was first persisted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the case record.']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing how the case was resolved.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the resolution actions taken.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was marked as resolved.']
  salesforce_case_reference varchar [note: 'Native Salesforce identifier for the case record.']
  sla_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target date/time by which the case should be resolved per Service Level Agreement.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the case was originally submitted.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'More detailed sub‑category within the main category.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief title or subject line summarising the case.']
  total_handle_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Cumulative time spent handling the case, measured in minutes.']

  Note: 'Customer service case record tracking complaints, inquiries, warranty claims, recall notifications, and roadside assistance requests raised by customers through any channel. Captures: party_id, VIN (if vehicle-related), case number, case type (complaint/inquiry/warranty_claim/recall/roadside/goodwill/lemon_law/regulatory), case category, case sub-category, subject, description, priority (P1-P4), status (new/in_progress/pending_customer/resolved/closed/escalated), opened date, SLA due date, resolved date, closed date, resolution code, resolution description, dealer code (if dealer-handled), assigned agent ID, escalation level, escalation reason, total handle time (minutes), customer satisfaction score (post-resolution), Salesforce Case ID, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 Case ID. SSOT for customer service operations.'
}

Table customer.connected_service_enrollment {
  connected_service_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each connected service enrollment record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing account to which fees for this enrollment are charged.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (person or organization) that owns the enrollment.']
  service_advisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Connected Services Activation Log records the service advisor who enrolls a customer in a connected service.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Connected service enrollment requires confirming vehicle eligibility and retrieving OTA capabilities from the VIN registry.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date the service became active after any trial or provisioning period.']
  auto_renew boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription automatically renews at the end of the term.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of recurring billing for the subscription.']
  connected_app_user_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user account within the mobile or web app that accesses the service.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was first created in the system.']
  data_plan_gb decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allocated data volume per billing cycle for data‑enabled services.']
  data_usage_gb decimal(7,2) [note: 'Total data consumed by the service during the current billing cycle.']
  device_imei varchar [note: 'International Mobile Equipment Identity of the telematics device installed in the vehicle.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Origin of the enrollment request (e.g., online portal, dealer, mobile app).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the customer initially signed up for the service (trial or paid).']
  enrollment_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference number used in partner systems or printed on invoices.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the enrollment.']
  last_ota_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent over‑the‑air software update applied to the vehicle.']
  monthly_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Recurring monthly charge for the selected service plan.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  service_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code that uniquely identifies the subscription plan for the service type.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of the connected service the customer is enrolled in.']
  sim_iccid varchar [note: 'Integrated Circuit Card Identifier of the SIM used for cellular connectivity.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status (e.g., payment failure, user request).']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date the paid subscription period ends or is scheduled to terminate.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date the paid subscription period begins.']
  telematics_provider varchar [note: 'Company that supplies the telematics connectivity service.']
  trial_end_date date [note: 'Date the free trial period expires, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the enrollment record.']

  Note: 'Tracks customer enrollment in connected vehicle and mobility services — telematics subscriptions, remote access apps, OTA update programs, EV charging network memberships, and V2X services. Captures: party_id, VIN, service_type (telematics/remote_access/ev_charging/ota_updates/wifi_hotspot/autonomous_feature/roadside_connect/v2x), service plan code, enrollment date, activation date, trial_end_date, subscription_start_date, subscription_end_date, auto_renew flag, billing_account_id (FK to billing domain), monthly_fee, data_plan_gb, connected_app_user_id, device_IMEI, SIM_ICCID, telematics_provider (Geotab/Bosch_IoT), enrollment channel, and enrollment status (trial/active/suspended/cancelled/expired). Bridges customer domain with mobility and billing domains.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_program {
  loyalty_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the loyalty program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the program record was first created in the system.']
  earn_rate_points_per_dollar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of loyalty points earned for each US dollar spent.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which customers may join the program.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the loyalty program is terminated or superseded.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Date after which new enrollments are no longer accepted.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'Date when new members may begin enrolling in the program.']
  is_partner_program boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program is co‑branded with an external partner.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the program definition was last reviewed for relevance or compliance.']
  loyalty_program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the program purpose, benefits and target audience.']
  loyalty_program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the loyalty program.']
  loyalty_program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the loyalty program.']
  loyalty_program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the program based on reward mechanism.']
  min_redemption_points decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum number of points a member must have before a redemption can be processed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or operational comments.']
  partner_redemption_options varchar [note: 'Description of third‑party partners where points can be redeemed.']
  points_earn_rate_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the earn rate is expressed.']
  points_expiry_months varchar [note: 'Number of months after which earned points expire if not redeemed.']
  points_redemption_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the redemption rate is expressed.']
  program_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification such as owner rewards, EV loyalty, fleet loyalty, or partner co‑branded.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the program in contracts and marketing materials.']
  program_owner varchar [note: 'Internal team or business unit responsible for the program.']
  redemption_rate_points_per_dollar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of points required to redeem one US dollar of reward.']
  salesforce_loyalty_program_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the program in Salesforce Automotive Cloud.']
  sponsoring_brand varchar [note: 'Brand or sub‑brand that sponsors the loyalty program.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the loyalty program becomes effective for members.']
  target_customer_type varchar [note: 'Customer segment the program is designed for.']
  tier_structure varchar [note: 'JSON string describing tier names, thresholds and associated benefits.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the program record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier for change management.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all loyalty program definitions offered by Automotive — owner rewards, EV loyalty, fleet loyalty, and partner co-branded programs. Captures: program code, program name, program type (points/tier/cashback/hybrid), target customer type (B2C/B2B/fleet), sponsoring brand, program description, enrollment eligibility criteria, tier structure (JSON: tier names, thresholds, benefits), points earn rate (per dollar spent), points redemption rate (points per dollar), minimum redemption threshold, points expiry policy (months), partner redemption options, program start date, program end date, is_active flag, and Salesforce Loyalty Program ID. Reference data consumed by loyalty_membership.'
}

Table customer.privacy_request {
  privacy_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the privacy request record.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Link to the related service case or ticket in the case management system.']
  handling_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GDPR Request Handling requires linking each privacy request to the employee responsible for processing it.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (individual, fleet, corporate, or government) who submitted the privacy request.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date the request was fully processed and closed.']
  data_domains_affected varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded list of business data domains (e.g., vehicle, sales, after‑sales) impacted by the request.']
  due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the request must be fulfilled.']
  fulfillment_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing actions taken to satisfy the request.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request involves high‑risk data (e.g., biometric, financial).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the request for handling urgency.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the privacy request record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the privacy request record.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Legal framework governing the request, such as GDPR, CCPA, PIPEDA, or LGPD.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation why a request was rejected or partially fulfilled.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the request for tracking and reference.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the request.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Type of privacy right exercised by the subject (e.g., erasure, access, portability, rectification, restriction, opt‑out).']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the privacy request was received.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the privacy request was submitted by the subject.']
  systems_notified varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded list of source systems (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce) that were informed of the request.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the identity of the requestor before processing.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or system that performed the identity verification.']

  Note: 'Tracks formal customer privacy rights requests submitted under GDPR (right to erasure, right of access, right to portability, right to rectification), CCPA (right to delete, right to know, right to opt-out), and other applicable privacy regulations. Captures: party_id, request type (erasure/access/portability/rectification/restriction/opt_out), regulatory_basis (GDPR/CCPA/PIPEDA/LGPD), submission date, submission channel (web_portal/email/call_center/mail), request status (received/in_progress/completed/rejected/partially_fulfilled), due_date (regulatory deadline), completion_date, fulfillment_notes, rejection_reason, data_domains_affected (JSON list), systems_notified (JSON list), verification_method (identity verified before processing), verified_by, and case_id (linked service case). Mandatory for privacy compliance operations.'
}

Table customer.dealer_customer_link {
  dealer_customer_link_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each dealer-customer relationship record.']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Dealer‑Customer Relationship Management assigns a dealer account manager employee to each customer link.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (person or organization) linked to the dealer.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'How the dealer was assigned to the customer: chosen by the customer, automatically by the system, or claimed by the dealer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the dealer‑customer link record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the dealership within the CDK Global DMS.']
  dealer_customer_link_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the relationship (active, inactive, terminated, pending).']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dealer is the primary dealer for the customer (true) or a secondary/auxiliary dealer (false).']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the customer received service at this dealer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for comments, special conditions, or business remarks about the relationship.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the dealer-customer relationship was terminated or is scheduled to end (nullable for active relationships).']
  relationship_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the dealer‑customer agreement, used in contracts and reporting.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the dealer-customer relationship became effective.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dealer-customer link (e.g., primary selling dealer, service dealer, preferred dealer, conquest dealer).']
  salesforce_account_dealer_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this record to the corresponding account‑dealer relationship in Salesforce Automotive Cloud.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the relationship data.']
  total_service_visits varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of service appointments the customer has completed at this dealer.']
  total_spend_at_dealer decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount (USD) the customer has spent on sales, service, parts, and accessories at this dealer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the dealer‑customer link record.']
  vin varchar [note: '17‑character vehicle identifier when the relationship is specific to a particular vehicle.']

  Note: 'Association entity recording the relationship between a customer party and a specific dealership — capturing primary dealer assignment, service dealer preference, selling dealer history, and conquest/retention dealer transitions. Captures: party_id, dealer_code, relationship type (selling_dealer/service_dealer/preferred_dealer/conquest_dealer), VIN (if vehicle-specific relationship), relationship start date, relationship end date, is_primary flag, assignment method (customer_selected/system_assigned/dealer_claimed), last_service_date, total_service_visits, total_spend_at_dealer, and Salesforce Account-Dealer relationship ID. Enables dealer performance attribution, customer retention by dealer, and dealer network territory analysis.'
}

Table customer.promotion {
  promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for promotion']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the promotion record.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broader marketing campaign that owns this promotion.']
  predecessor_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on promotion (predecessor_promotion_id)']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the promotion record.']
  applicable_geographies varchar [note: 'Geographic regions (country codes) where the promotion is valid.']
  applicable_vehicle_models varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of vehicle models eligible for the promotion.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary budget allocated for the promotion.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary distribution channel through which the promotion is offered.']
  cltv_impact_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated change in Customer Lifetime Value attributable to the promotion.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the promotion for tracking and lookup.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values associated with the promotion.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification for the promotion\'s target customers.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the discount_value is a percentage of the transaction amount or a fixed monetary amount.']
  discount_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount offered; interpretation depends on discount_type (percentage or fixed amount).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that define which transactions or customers qualify for the promotion.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date after which the promotion is no longer valid.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the promotion cannot be combined with other promotions.']
  loyalty_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is part of a loyalty program.']
  max_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper limit on the discount value that can be applied per transaction.']
  min_purchase_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount required for the promotion to be applicable.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the promotion as used in marketing materials.']
  nps_impact_estimate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated effect of the promotion on Net Promoter Score (percentage points).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to rank promotions when multiple are applicable.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of the promotion indicating the mechanism used to provide value to the customer.']
  promotional_message varchar [note: 'Marketing copy displayed to customers describing the promotion.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Running total of how many times the promotion has been redeemed.']
  required_product_category varchar [note: 'Product category that must be purchased for the promotion to apply.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the promotion becomes effective and can be redeemed.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Segment of customers for whom the promotion is intended.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Legal and operational rules governing the promotion.']
