  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the MSRP amounts (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, MXN).']
  destination_charge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard destination and delivery charge applied to transport the vehicle from manufacturing plant to dealer. Mandated by federal regulation to be uniform within a market region.']
  drivetrain varchar [note: 'Drivetrain configuration: FWD (Front-Wheel Drive), RWD (Rear-Wheel Drive), AWD (All-Wheel Drive), 4WD (Four-Wheel Drive).']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this MSRP schedule becomes effective. Typically aligned with model year launch or mid-cycle pricing adjustments.']
  engine_code varchar [note: 'Internal engine code or designation (e.g., 2GR-FE, EcoBoost 3.5L V6). Links to engineering specifications.']
  epa_fuel_economy_city varchar [note: 'EPA-certified city fuel economy rating in miles per gallon (MPG) for ICE/HEV/PHEV, or MPGe (miles per gallon equivalent) for BEV/FCEV. Used for CAFE compliance and consumer disclosure.']
  epa_fuel_economy_combined varchar [note: 'EPA-certified combined fuel economy rating (55% city, 45% highway) in MPG or MPGe. Primary metric for CAFE and consumer comparison.']
  epa_fuel_economy_highway varchar [note: 'EPA-certified highway fuel economy rating in miles per gallon (MPG) or MPGe. Used for CAFE compliance and consumer disclosure.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this MSRP schedule expires and is superseded by a new schedule. Null if currently active with no planned end date.']
  gas_guzzler_tax decimal(10,2) [note: 'Federal gas guzzler tax applied to vehicles with EPA fuel economy below statutory thresholds. Zero if not applicable.']
  holdback_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Manufacturer holdback amount paid to dealer after vehicle sale, typically 2-3% of MSRP. Used to support dealer cash flow and inventory financing. Confidential business data.']
  invoice_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The dealer invoice price (wholesale cost to dealer) for this vehicle configuration. Used for dealer margin calculations and incentive programs. Confidential business data.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market region or sales territory where this MSRP schedule applies (e.g., USA, Canada, Mexico, Europe). MSRP may vary by region due to regulatory requirements, taxes, and market conditions.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year (MY) for which this MSRP schedule is effective. Typically ranges from current year to next year.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this MSRP schedule record was last modified. Updated on any change to pricing, dates, or status.']
  nameplate varchar [note: 'The vehicle nameplate or model name (e.g., Camry, F-150, Model 3). Denormalized for reporting convenience.']
  ncap_safety_rating varchar [note: 'Overall NCAP safety rating (1-5 stars) for this vehicle configuration. Used for marketing and consumer safety disclosure. Null if not yet rated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this MSRP schedule, such as rationale for mid-cycle adjustments, competitive positioning, or special market conditions.']
  option_package_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the factory-installed option package or bundle (e.g., Premium Package, Technology Package, Winter Package). May be null for base configuration.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'The powertrain type: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle).']
  powertrain_warranty_miles varchar [note: 'Extended powertrain warranty duration in miles (e.g., 60,000 miles). Covers engine, transmission, drivetrain components.']
  powertrain_warranty_months varchar [note: 'Extended powertrain warranty duration in months (e.g., 60 months / 5 years). Typically longer than bumper-to-bumper warranty.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this MSRP schedule was published to dealer systems and public-facing channels (website, configurator, dealer portals).']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the MSRP schedule, typically assigned at model year launch or mid-cycle refresh. Format: MSRP-NNNNNN.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MSRP schedule: draft (pending approval), active (currently in effect), superseded (replaced by newer schedule), archived (historical record).']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Type of MSRP schedule: launch (initial model year pricing), mid_cycle (mid-year adjustment), promotional (temporary special pricing), fleet (commercial/fleet pricing).']
  total_msrp decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total MSRP including base price, destination charge, gas guzzler tax, and any mandatory fees. Excludes dealer-installed accessories and local taxes.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission: manual, automatic, CVT (Continuously Variable Transmission), DCT (Dual-Clutch Transmission).']
  trim_level varchar [note: 'The trim level or grade of the vehicle (e.g., Base, LE, XLE, Limited, Platinum). Defines the standard equipment package.']
  warranty_miles varchar [note: 'Standard manufacturer warranty duration in miles (e.g., 36,000 miles). Warranty expires at the earlier of months or miles threshold.']
  warranty_months varchar [note: 'Standard manufacturer warranty duration in months (e.g., 36 months / 3 years). Used for warranty cost accrual and customer communication.']

  Note: 'Official Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price schedule for each vehicle nameplate, model year, trim level, and option package combination. Captures base MSRP, destination and delivery charge, gas guzzler tax (if applicable), effective date, market region, and currency. Serves as the pricing reference baseline for all quotes, orders, and incentive calculations. Updated at model year launch and mid-cycle adjustments.'
}

Table sales.sales_territory {
  sales_territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales territory. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regional director overseeing this territory. Represents the next level of sales leadership hierarchy.']
  primary_sales_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales manager responsible for this territory. Links to employee or sales representative master data.']
  tertiary_sales_regional_director_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regional director overseeing this territory. Represents the next level of sales leadership hierarchy.']
  annual_quota_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual revenue quota target for this territory in the base currency. Represents expected sales value including MSRP and incentives.']
  annual_quota_units varchar [note: 'Annual sales quota for this territory measured in vehicle units. Used for performance tracking and incentive calculation.']
  competitive_intensity varchar [note: 'Assessment of competitive pressure in the territory based on number of competing brands, dealer density, and market share dynamics.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the territory operates (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, DEU, CHN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this territory record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dealer_count varchar [note: 'Number of authorized dealerships operating within this territory. Used for dealer network density analysis and coverage planning.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Primary distribution channel used in this territory. Direct represents OEM-owned sales, dealer represents franchised network, fleet serves corporate buyers, online covers digital sales, and export handles international shipments.']
  district_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the district within the region. Represents a mid-level geographic or organizational subdivision.']
  district_name varchar [note: 'Name of the district (e.g., \'Northeast District\', \'Southern California District\').']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this territory configuration expires or is superseded. Null for currently active territories. Supports territory history and audit trails.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this territory configuration becomes active. Used for territory realignment and historical tracking.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the territory configuration and quotas are defined (e.g., FY2024, FY2025). Aligns with corporate financial planning cycles.']
  household_count varchar [note: 'Number of households in the territory. Key metric for retail automotive market potential analysis.']
  incentive_program_code varchar [note: 'Code of the primary sales incentive program applicable to this territory. Links to dealer incentive and rebate programs.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Primary language code for customer communication and marketing materials in this territory (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh). Follows ISO 639-1 two-letter codes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this territory record. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Primary market segment focus for this territory. Determines product mix, pricing strategy, and customer targeting approach.']
  median_household_income decimal(12,2) [note: 'Median household income in the territory. Used for market segmentation and product mix planning (luxury vs. mass market).']
  model_year_focus varchar [note: 'Primary model year of vehicles being sold in this territory (e.g., MY2024, MY2025). Reflects inventory and product launch timing.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this territory record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special considerations, territory-specific strategies, market conditions, or operational instructions for sales teams.']
  population_count varchar [note: 'Total population within the territory boundaries. Used for market sizing and potential customer base estimation.']
  postal_code_range varchar [note: 'Postal code or ZIP code ranges covered by this territory (e.g., \'90001-90099, 91001-91199\'). Used for geographic assignment of leads and customers.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Strategic priority level assigned to this territory for resource allocation, investment, and management attention. Strategic territories receive highest focus and support.']
  quota_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for quota and revenue targets (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY, JPY).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the parent region in the geographic hierarchy (e.g., national, regional, district level). Used for roll-up reporting and regional performance tracking.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Name of the parent region (e.g., \'North America\', \'Western Europe\', \'Asia Pacific\').']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction or compliance region (e.g., EPA, CARB, Euro NCAP, UNECE). Determines applicable emissions standards, safety requirements, and homologation rules.']
  sales_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the sales manager assigned to this territory for quick reference and reporting.']
  sales_office_code varchar [note: 'Code of the primary sales office serving this territory. Links to SAP SD sales organization structure.']
  sales_organization_code varchar [note: 'SAP sales organization code responsible for this territory. Represents the legal entity or business unit managing sales operations.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'State or province code within the country (e.g., CA for California, ON for Ontario, TX for Texas). May contain multiple values if territory spans multiple states.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the sales territory used across systems and reporting. Typically alphanumeric format following regional coding standards.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the sales territory (e.g., \'Northeast Region - Metro\', \'California Fleet Sales Zone\').']
  territory_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the territory. Active territories are operational, inactive are closed, suspended are temporarily paused, planned are future territories, consolidating are merging with others, and splitting are being divided.']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory by sales channel or customer segment. Retail serves individual consumers, fleet serves corporate fleet buyers, export covers international markets, commercial targets business customers, government serves public sector, and dealer_network represents indirect sales through franchised dealers.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Primary time zone for the territory (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Shanghai). Used for scheduling and operational coordination.']
  vehicle_category_focus varchar [note: 'Primary vehicle categories sold in this territory (e.g., \'SUV, Truck\', \'Sedan, EV\', \'Commercial Vehicles\'). Reflects regional demand patterns and product strategy.']
  vehicle_registration_count varchar [note: 'Total number of registered vehicles in the territory. Indicates market saturation and replacement demand potential.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the zone within the district. Represents the most granular level of the territory hierarchy.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Name of the zone (e.g., \'Manhattan Zone\', \'Orange County Zone\').']

  Note: 'Geographic or channel-based sales territory definition used to assign sales representatives, dealers, and regional managers to specific market areas. Captures territory code, territory name, region hierarchy (national/regional/district/zone), assigned sales manager, territory type (retail/fleet/export), and effective dates. Supports regional sales performance tracking and quota allocation.'
}

Table sales.rep {
  rep_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OEM direct sales representative. Primary key.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.channel. Business justification: Normalize channel reference; replace string attribute with FK to channel.']
  rep_crm_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier in Salesforce Automotive Cloud. Used to link sales activities, opportunities, and customer interactions to this representative.']
  rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Human Resources employee identifier linking this sales representative to the workforce management system. Sourced from SuccessFactors HR system.']
  rep_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the direct manager or regional sales manager overseeing this sales representative. Used for reporting hierarchy and approval workflows.']
  active_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sales representative is currently active and eligible for lead assignment and quota tracking. False indicates inactive (terminated, on leave, or transferred out of sales).']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current sales certification expires and recertification is required. Null if certification does not expire or is not required for the role.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the representative\'s sales certification or training completion. Certified indicates all required product, compliance, and sales methodology training is current. Used for dealer-facing role authorization and advanced product sales eligibility.']
  commission_plan_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the incentive compensation plan assigned to the representative. Links to commission structure, bonus tiers, and payout rules in the compensation management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales representative record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  crm_user_code varchar [note: 'User identifier in Salesforce Automotive Cloud. Used to link sales activities, opportunities, and customer interactions to this representative.']
  dealer_count varchar [note: 'Number of dealerships assigned to this representative for relationship management and sales support. Applicable primarily to retail field reps and dealer development roles.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary corporate email address for the sales representative. Used for customer communication and system notifications.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the sales representative.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the current quota is assigned. Used for year-over-year performance comparison and quota planning.']
  fleet_account_count varchar [note: 'Number of fleet or commercial accounts assigned to this representative. Applicable primarily to fleet account managers and national account executives.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the sales representative was hired by the OEM. Used for tenure calculation and eligibility for tenure-based incentives.']
  language_proficiency varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of languages the representative is proficient in (e.g., English, Spanish, Mandarin). Used for customer matching and international account assignment.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name of the sales representative.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number for field sales representative. Used for on-the-go communication and CRM mobile app access.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales representative record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information about the sales representative, such as special skills, awards, territory transition notes, or temporary assignment details.']
  office_location varchar [note: 'Primary office or branch location where the representative is based (e.g., Detroit HQ, Los Angeles Regional Office, Remote). Used for travel planning and regional coordination.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Current performance classification based on quota attainment, customer satisfaction (NPS), and other KPIs. Used for recognition programs, incentive multipliers, and career development planning.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number for the sales representative. Used for dealer and customer contact.']
  product_specialization varchar [note: 'Primary product line or vehicle segment specialization (e.g., Electric Vehicles, Commercial Trucks, Luxury Sedans, SUVs, Performance Vehicles). Used for lead routing and expert assignment in Salesforce Automotive Cloud.']
  quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual or period-based sales quota target assigned to the representative, expressed in the base currency. Used for performance measurement and incentive compensation calculation.']
  quota_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quota amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Supports multi-currency sales operations.']
  quota_period varchar [note: 'Time period over which the quota is measured (annual, quarterly, or monthly). Aligns with fiscal year and performance review cycles.']
  region varchar [note: 'Higher-level geographic region grouping multiple territories (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Latin America). Used for regional performance rollup and management hierarchy.']
  sales_role varchar [note: 'Functional sales role classification defining the representative\'s primary responsibility area. Retail field reps manage dealer relationships, fleet account managers handle commercial fleet sales, national account executives manage large enterprise accounts, export sales covers international markets, regional sales managers oversee territories, and dealer development focuses on dealer network expansion.']
  sales_start_date date [note: 'Date the representative began their current sales role or territory assignment. May differ from hire_date if the employee transferred from another department or role.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Link sales_rep to its sales territory for proper reporting; replace string code with FK.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the sales representative\'s employment or sales role ended. Null for active representatives. Used for historical performance analysis and territory reassignment.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the assigned sales territory (e.g., Northeast Region, California Central, Germany South, National Fleet Accounts).']
  work_arrangement varchar [note: 'Primary work location arrangement. Field-based representatives spend majority of time visiting dealers or customers; office-based work from regional offices; hybrid split time; remote work from home.']

  Note: 'Master record for OEM direct sales representatives and field sales personnel responsible for managing dealer relationships, fleet accounts, and direct sales channels. Captures employee reference, assigned territory, sales role (retail field rep, fleet account manager, national account executive, export sales), quota assignment, certification status, and active status. Distinct from dealer-side sales staff managed in the dealer domain.'
}

Table sales.quota {
  quota_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales quota record. Primary key.']
  assignee_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the entity assigned this quota. Interpretation depends on assignee_type: references sales_representative, dealership, territory, region, or team table.']
  dealer_incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the incentive program associated with this quota. Null if quota is not tied to a specific incentive program.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the dealership this quota is assigned to. Null if quota is not dealership-specific.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the entity assigned this quota. Interpretation depends on assignee_type: references sales_representative, dealership, territory, region, or team table.']
  rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the individual sales representative this quota is assigned to. Null if quota is not individual-specific.']
  sales_representative_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the individual sales representative this quota is assigned to. Null if quota is not individual-specific.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the sales territory this quota is assigned to. Null if quota is not territory-specific.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the quota: pending (awaiting approval), approved (management approved), rejected (not approved), revision_required (sent back for changes).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or executive who approved this quota. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the quota was approved by management. Null if not yet approved.']
  assignee_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the quota assignee (representative name, dealer name, territory name, etc.) for reporting and display purposes.']
  assignee_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity to which the quota is assigned: sales_representative (individual salesperson), dealer (dealership entity), territory (geographic sales territory), region (multi-territory region), district (sales district), zone (sales zone), team (sales team). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_representative|dealer|territory|region|district|zone|team — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quota record was first created in the system, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Fiscal month within the fiscal year (M01 through M12). Null if quota is quarterly or annual.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter within the fiscal year (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4). Null if quota is annual or monthly.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the quota is assigned, in format FYYYYYYY (e.g., FY2024). Aligns with corporate fiscal calendar.']
  incentive_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether achievement of this quota makes the assignee eligible for incentive compensation (True) or is a non-incentivized target (False).']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of vehicles covered by this quota (e.g., MY2024). Used when quota is specific to a model year production run. Null if quota spans multiple model years.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified the quota record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quota record was last modified, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the quota assignment, special conditions, or context for sales management and reporting.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the quota performance period. Defines when quota measurement concludes.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the quota performance period. Defines when quota measurement begins.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain type this quota applies to: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle), all (applies to all powertrains). Null if not powertrain-specific.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Specific product line or brand this quota applies to (e.g., premium brand, economy brand, commercial line). Null if quota applies to all product lines.']
  quota_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the quota assignment, e.g., \'Q1 FY2024 Northeast Region EV Target\' or \'Annual MY2024 Fleet Sales Goal\'.']
  quota_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the quota assignment, typically following format QTA-YYYYNNNN.']
  quota_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quota: draft (under review), approved (management approved), active (in effect), suspended (temporarily paused), closed (period ended), cancelled (voided before completion).']
  quota_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quota by measurement type: unit_volume (vehicle units sold), revenue (dollar/currency target), ev_mix (Electric Vehicle penetration percentage), fleet_penetration (commercial fleet sales), market_share (regional market share target), model_mix (specific model line targets).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter geographic region code where quota applies (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, DEU, GBR, CHN, JPN). Follows ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or internal regional codes.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the quota record, incremented each time the quota is revised or adjusted. Initial version is 1.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent revision to the quota (e.g., \'Market conditions adjustment\', \'Territory realignment\', \'Product launch delay\'). Null if no revisions have been made.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel this quota applies to: retail (individual consumer sales), fleet (corporate fleet sales), commercial (business sales), government (government contracts), direct (OEM direct sales), dealer_network (indirect dealer sales). Null if applies to all channels.']
  target_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target_value: units (vehicle count), currency codes (USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY), percentage (for mix/share targets), market_points (for market share basis points). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: units|usd|eur|gbp|jpy|cny|percentage|market_points — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric target value for the quota. Interpretation depends on quota_type: unit count for unit_volume, currency amount for revenue, percentage for ev_mix or market_share.']
  threshold_minimum decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum performance threshold (as percentage of target_value or absolute value) required for any incentive payout or recognition. Null if no minimum threshold applies.']
  threshold_stretch decimal(18,2) [note: 'Stretch performance threshold (as percentage of target_value or absolute value) that triggers maximum incentive payout or recognition. Null if no stretch threshold applies.']
  vehicle_segment varchar [note: 'Vehicle segment or category this quota applies to (e.g., sedan, SUV, truck, commercial, luxury). Null if quota applies to all segments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sedan|suv|truck|crossover|van|commercial|luxury|compact|midsize — promote to reference product]']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created the quota record.']

  Note: 'Sales volume and revenue quota assigned to a sales representative, dealer, sales territory, or regional team for a defined period (monthly, quarterly, annual). Captures quota type (unit volume, revenue, EV mix, fleet penetration), target value, model year or fiscal period, assigned entity type and reference, and quota approval status. Enables sales performance tracking against targets.'
}

Table sales.activity {
  activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales activity record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that generated or is associated with this sales activity.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.channel. Business justification: Channel identifies the sales channel used for the activity; a FK to sales.channel provides a controlled reference and eliminates the free‑text column.']
  competitor_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.competitor_vehicle. Business justification: Standardize competitor vehicle reference; replace free‑text with FK to competitor_vehicle.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific contact person involved in this sales activity.']
  contact_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific contact person involved in this sales activity.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Activity logging audit needs the employee who created the sales activity.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with this sales activity.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership location where the sales activity took place or is associated with.']
  lead_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead record associated with this sales activity, if the activity is related to a lead.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the opportunity record associated with this sales activity, if the activity is related to an opportunity.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with this sales activity.']
  rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative who performed or is responsible for this sales activity.']
  sales_representative_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative who performed or is responsible for this sales activity.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Sales activity occurs within a sales territory; linking to sales_territory enables consistent reporting and removes the free‑text territory column.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle that was the subject of this sales activity, if applicable (e.g., test drive, demo).']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle that was the subject of this sales activity, if applicable (e.g., test drive, demo).']
  activity_date date [note: 'The date on which the sales activity occurred or is scheduled to occur.']
  activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description or notes about the sales activity, capturing key discussion points, customer feedback, and action items.']
  activity_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the sales activity, typically system-generated in format ACT-########.']
  activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sales activity indicating whether it is scheduled, in progress, completed, cancelled, no-show, or rescheduled.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales activity indicating the nature of the interaction (call, email, test drive, demo, site visit, proposal presentation, follow-up, negotiation, or other). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: call|email|meeting|test_drive|demo|site_visit|proposal_presentation|follow_up|negotiation|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  competitor_mentioned varchar [note: 'Name of any competitor brand or product mentioned by the customer during the sales activity.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this sales activity record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales activity record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated deal value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_sentiment varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the customer\'s sentiment or attitude during the sales activity (very positive, positive, neutral, negative, very negative).']
  demo_completed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a product demonstration was completed as part of this sales activity.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The duration of the sales activity in minutes, calculated or manually entered.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the sales activity ended or is scheduled to end.']
  estimated_deal_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of the potential deal discussed or progressed during this sales activity, in the base currency.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter in which the sales activity occurred (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the sales activity occurred, in format FY#### (e.g., FY2024).']
  is_commercial_vehicle boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this sales activity involves a commercial vehicle.']
  is_fleet_sale boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this sales activity is related to a fleet sale opportunity.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the sales activity took place (e.g., dealership showroom, customer site, online).']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle discussed in the sales activity, in format MY#### (e.g., MY2024).']
  modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this sales activity record in the system.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales activity record was last modified in the system.']
  next_action varchar [note: 'Description of the next planned action or follow-up step resulting from this sales activity.']
  next_action_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next follow-up action or activity.']
  objection_raised varchar [note: 'Description of any objections or concerns raised by the customer during the sales activity.']
  objection_resolved boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer\'s objection was successfully resolved during the activity.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The result or outcome of the sales activity indicating whether it was successful, unsuccessful, pending, no contact made, customer interested, not interested, or callback requested. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: successful|unsuccessful|pending|no_contact|interested|not_interested|callback_requested — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the sales activity indicating its urgency and importance (low, medium, high, urgent).']
  quote_presented boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a price quote or proposal was presented during this sales activity.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region where the activity took place or is associated with.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the sales activity started or is scheduled to start.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject or title of the sales activity describing the purpose or topic of the interaction.']
  test_drive_completed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a test drive was completed as part of this sales activity.']

  Note: 'Record of a sales-related interaction or activity logged against a lead, opportunity, or customer account. Captures activity type (call, email, test drive, demo, site visit, proposal presentation), activity date and time, outcome, next action, associated opportunity or lead reference, and sales rep performing the activity. Sourced from Salesforce Automotive Cloud Activity/Task/Event objects. Supports pipeline velocity and conversion analysis.'
}

Table sales.sales_test_drive {
  sales_test_drive_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales_test_drive data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: A sales test drive is generated as part of a sales opportunity; adding opportunity_id FK links each test drive to its originating opportunity, eliminating the silo and enabling reporting across the sa']

  Note: 'Record of a scheduled or completed vehicle test drive event conducted with a prospect or customer. Captures prospect reference, vehicle VIN or demo vehicle identifier, nameplate and trim driven, test drive date and duration, conducting dealer or direct sales location, prospect feedback rating, and conversion outcome (converted to opportunity or not). Supports lead nurturing and conversion tracking.'
}

Table sales.trade_in {
  trade_in_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trade-in transaction record.']
  appraiser_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or appraiser who evaluated the trade-in vehicle.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who traded in the vehicle.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership that appraised and accepted the trade-in vehicle.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or appraiser who evaluated the trade-in vehicle.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who traded in the vehicle.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated new vehicle purchase order for which this trade-in was accepted.']
  accepted_date date [note: 'Date when the customer and dealership agreed to the trade-in terms and the trade-in was officially accepted.']
  accident_history_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trade-in vehicle has a reported accident history (True/False).']
  allowance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Agreed-upon credit amount applied toward the new vehicle purchase, which may differ from appraised value due to negotiation.']
  appraisal_date date [note: 'Date when the trade-in vehicle was appraised and evaluated by the dealership.']
  appraised_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fair market value of the trade-in vehicle as determined by the dealership appraiser, before negotiation.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'Body style classification of the trade-in vehicle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sedan|coupe|suv|truck|van|wagon|convertible|hatchback — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  condition_grade varchar [note: 'Overall condition assessment of the trade-in vehicle based on physical inspection (excellent, good, fair, poor).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade-in record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this trade-in transaction.']
  disposition_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual sale or disposal amount realized when the trade-in vehicle was disposed of, used to calculate trade-in profitability.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date when the trade-in vehicle was sold, transferred, or otherwise disposed of by the dealership.']
  disposition_type varchar [note: 'Planned or actual disposition channel for the trade-in vehicle after acceptance (wholesale auction, certified pre-owned program, retail resale, internal fleet, scrap).']
  exterior_color varchar [note: 'Exterior paint color of the trade-in vehicle.']
  exterior_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the exterior body condition of the trade-in vehicle (paint, body panels, glass).']
  inspection_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a full mechanical and safety inspection was completed on the trade-in vehicle (True/False).']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the detailed inspection of the trade-in vehicle was completed.']
  interior_color varchar [note: 'Interior upholstery color of the trade-in vehicle.']
  interior_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the interior condition of the trade-in vehicle (seats, dashboard, carpets, controls).']
  lien_holder varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution or entity holding a lien on the trade-in vehicle, if applicable.']
  make varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or brand of the trade-in vehicle (e.g., Ford, Toyota, Honda).']
  mechanical_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the mechanical and operational condition of the trade-in vehicle (engine, transmission, drivetrain).']
  model varchar [note: 'Model name of the trade-in vehicle (e.g., F-150, Camry, Accord).']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the trade-in vehicle as designated by the manufacturer.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade-in record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments regarding the trade-in vehicle, appraisal, condition, or negotiation details.']
  odometer_reading varchar [note: 'Mileage reading on the trade-in vehicle at the time of appraisal, in miles or kilometers as per regional standard.']
  odometer_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the odometer reading (miles or kilometers).']
  outstanding_loan_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining loan balance owed on the trade-in vehicle at the time of trade, if applicable.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Type of powertrain in the trade-in vehicle: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle).']
  reconditioning_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to recondition the trade-in vehicle for resale (repairs, detailing, inspection).']
  service_records_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether complete service and maintenance records are available for the trade-in vehicle (True/False).']
  tire_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the tire condition and tread depth on the trade-in vehicle.']
  title_status varchar [note: 'Legal title status of the trade-in vehicle (clean, salvage, rebuilt, lemon, flood).']
  trade_in_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transaction number assigned to the trade-in for tracking and reference purposes.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission in the trade-in vehicle (manual, automatic, CVT, dual-clutch).']
  trim_level varchar [note: 'Trim or package level of the trade-in vehicle (e.g., LX, EX, Limited, Sport).']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number of the trade-in vehicle, uniquely identifying the vehicle being traded.']

  Note: 'Record of a customer vehicle trade-in evaluated and accepted as part of a new vehicle purchase transaction. Captures trade-in vehicle details (VIN, make, model, year, mileage, condition grade), appraised value, agreed trade-in allowance, appraisal date, appraising dealer, and disposition (wholesale auction, certified pre-owned, retail resale). Links to the associated vehicle order.'
}

Table sales.delivery_appointment {
  delivery_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery appointment record. Primary key for the delivery appointment entity.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who will take delivery of the vehicle.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership or delivery center where the vehicle handover will occur.']
  delivery_specialist_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or delivery specialist assigned to conduct the vehicle handover and customer orientation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or delivery specialist assigned to conduct the vehicle handover and customer orientation.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who will take delivery of the vehicle.']
  pdi_technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the technician who performed the Pre-Delivery Inspection.']
  rescheduled_from_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous delivery appointment if this appointment is a rescheduled version.']
  service_subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.service_subscription. Business justification: Delivery appointment must verify and activate the customer\'s subscribed connectivity services; link provides the exact subscription record.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Appointment execution requires the shipment tracking number to confirm the correct vehicle is being delivered to the customer.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the confirmed vehicle order for which this delivery appointment is scheduled.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle unit being delivered, linked by VIN (Vehicle Identification Number).']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle unit being delivered, linked by VIN (Vehicle Identification Number).']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vehicle was actually handed over to the customer and delivery was completed.']
  appointment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected duration of the delivery appointment in minutes, including vehicle walkthrough, paperwork, and handover.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique appointment reference number used for customer communication and tracking. Format: DA-YYYYMMDD-sequence.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery appointment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show|rescheduled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for cancellation if the delivery appointment was cancelled.']
  connected_services_activated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether connected vehicle services and mobile app access were activated during delivery.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the delivery appointment record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the delivery appointment record was first created in the system.']
  customer_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer arrived at the delivery location for their appointment.']
  customer_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the customer has confirmed their attendance at the scheduled delivery appointment.']
  customer_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer confirmed the delivery appointment.']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the delivery location if delivery is not at a standard dealership facility.']
  delivery_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (suite, apartment, building) for the delivery location.']
  delivery_city varchar [note: 'City name for the delivery location.']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the delivery location.']
  delivery_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery location where the vehicle will be handed over to the customer.']
  delivery_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the delivery location.']
  delivery_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Customer satisfaction rating for the delivery experience, typically on a scale of 1-10, collected immediately after delivery.']
  delivery_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the delivery location.']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery service level provided to the customer.']
  digital_owner_manual_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the digital owner manual and vehicle information was sent to the customer.']
  documentation_status varchar [note: 'Status of delivery documentation preparation including title, registration, warranty cards, and owner manuals.']
  financing_status varchar [note: 'Status of financing approval and funding for the vehicle purchase if applicable.']
  handover_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual duration of the vehicle handover process from customer arrival to departure.']
  last_reminder_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent appointment reminder was sent to the customer.']
  modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the delivery appointment record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the delivery appointment record was last modified.']
  pdi_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the Pre-Delivery Inspection was completed and the vehicle was cleared for delivery.']
  pdi_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Pre-Delivery Inspection process ensuring the vehicle meets quality standards before customer handover.']
  reminder_sent_count varchar [note: 'Number of appointment reminder notifications sent to the customer via email, SMS, or phone.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the vehicle delivery appointment is scheduled to occur.']
  scheduled_delivery_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the customer is expected to arrive for vehicle handover.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special instructions or requirements for the delivery appointment such as accessibility needs, language preferences, or specific requests.']
  trade_in_status varchar [note: 'Status of trade-in vehicle processing if the customer is trading in a vehicle as part of the transaction.']
  vehicle_orientation_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery specialist completed the vehicle features and controls orientation with the customer.']
  vehicle_preparation_status varchar [note: 'Status of vehicle preparation activities including cleaning, fueling, accessory installation, and final detailing before delivery.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number uniquely identifying the vehicle being delivered. ISO 3779 standard format.']

  Note: 'Scheduled vehicle delivery appointment for a confirmed vehicle order, coordinating the handover of a new vehicle to the customer at a dealer or direct delivery point. Captures scheduled delivery date and time, delivery location, assigned delivery specialist, PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection) completion status, customer confirmation status, and actual delivery completion timestamp. Triggers post-delivery customer satisfaction follow-up.'
}

Table sales.channel {
  channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales channel. Primary key.']
  applicable_powertrain_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of powertrain types eligible for this channel (e.g., ICE, EV, HEV, PHEV, FCEV). Used for product eligibility and incentive program alignment.']
  applicable_vehicle_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of vehicle types that can be sold through this channel (e.g., sedan, SUV, truck, EV, HEV, PHEV, commercial). Used for channel eligibility rules in lead and opportunity management.']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the channel is direct (OEM-owned, no intermediary), indirect (dealer-mediated or third-party), or hybrid (combination of direct and indirect elements).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the sales channel (e.g., RETAIL_DEALER, DIRECT_CONSUMER, FLEET_COMM, GOVT, EXPORT_CKD, ONLINE_CONFIG, CPO). Used for operational reporting and system integration.']
  channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the sales channel, including its purpose, target customer segments, operational model, and any special characteristics or restrictions.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the sales channel (e.g., Retail Dealer Network, Direct-to-Consumer, Fleet and Commercial Sales, Government Sales, Export/CKD, Online Configurator, Certified Pre-Owned).']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the sales channel: active (currently operational), inactive (temporarily not in use), suspended (on hold pending review), planned (future channel not yet launched), discontinued (permanently retired).']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the sales channel: retail (traditional dealer), direct (OEM direct-to-consumer), fleet (commercial/fleet sales), government (public sector contracts), export (international/CKD/SKD), online (digital configurator/e-commerce), cpo (certified pre-owned). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|direct|fleet|government|export|online|cpo — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commission_model varchar [note: 'Type of commission structure applied to this channel: fixed (flat fee per unit), percentage (percent of sale price), tiered (volume-based tiers), hybrid (combination), none (no commission, direct OEM).']
  country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this channel operates (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, DEU, GBR, CHN, JPN). Used for geographic sales attribution and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales channel record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  crm_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is integrated with the CRM system (Salesforce Automotive Cloud) for lead and opportunity tracking (True) or operates independently (False).']
  default_commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default commission rate percentage applied to sales through this channel (e.g., 3.50 for 3.5%). Null if commission model is fixed or none.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'SAP SD distribution channel code associated with this sales channel. Used for order processing, pricing, and logistics routing.']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP SD division code representing the product line or business unit served by this channel (e.g., passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, parts and accessories).']
  dms_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is integrated with the Dealer Management System (CDK Global DMS) for inventory and order management (True) or not (False).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this sales channel was or will be discontinued. Null for currently active channels with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this sales channel became or will become operational. Used for temporal validity and historical reporting.']
  erp_sales_channel_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding sales channel code in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA SD module). Used for order-to-cash integration and financial reporting.']
  incentive_eligibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether sales through this channel are eligible for OEM incentive programs: eligible (fully eligible), ineligible (excluded from incentives), conditional (eligible under specific conditions).']
  is_dealer_mediated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel involves a dealer or distributor intermediary (True) or is a direct OEM channel (False). Used for dealer network analytics and commission calculations.']
  is_direct_oem boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is a direct OEM-owned channel (True) or an indirect dealer-mediated channel (False). Used for revenue attribution and margin analysis.']
  lead_source_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for lead routing to this channel (1 = highest priority). Used in lead assignment and opportunity allocation logic.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market region(s) where this sales channel is applicable (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, Middle East Africa). May be a comma-separated list for multi-region channels.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales channel record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the sales channel, including operational considerations, historical context, or special instructions.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit or individual responsible for managing this sales channel. Used for accountability and escalation.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing approach used in this channel: msrp (Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price with dealer negotiation), negotiated (fully negotiated pricing), fixed (no-haggle fixed price), dynamic (algorithm-driven pricing), auction (competitive bidding).']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP SD sales organization code responsible for this channel. Links to the organizational hierarchy for revenue reporting and territory management.']
  supports_financing boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel offers financing options (True) or requires cash/external financing (False).']
  supports_fleet_sales boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel supports fleet or commercial bulk sales (True) or is limited to individual retail sales (False).']
  supports_leasing boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel offers leasing options (True) or does not support leasing (False).']
  supports_online_configuration boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel supports online vehicle configuration and ordering (True) or requires in-person interaction (False).']
  supports_trade_in boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel accepts trade-in vehicles as part of the transaction (True) or does not support trade-ins (False).']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the sales channels through which vehicles are sold, including retail dealer, direct-to-consumer, fleet/commercial, government, export/CKD, online configurator, and certified pre-owned channels. Captures channel code, channel name, channel type, applicable market regions, and whether the channel is direct (OEM) or indirect (dealer-mediated). Used for channel attribution across leads, opportunities, and orders.'
}

Table sales.price_adjustment {
  price_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price adjustment record. Primary key.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving the price adjustment.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership applying or requesting the price adjustment.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or executive who authorized this price adjustment.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price Adjustment Posting: adjustments must be posted to a specific GL account for audit and financial impact tracking.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving the price adjustment.']
  primary_price_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or executive who authorized this price adjustment.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales quote to which this price adjustment applies.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle order to which this price adjustment applies.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle configuration or unit to which this price adjustment applies.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle configuration or unit to which this price adjustment applies.']
  adjusted_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'The resulting price after this adjustment is applied. May be further adjusted by subsequent adjustments in the pricing waterfall.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the price adjustment in the transaction currency. Negative values represent discounts or reductions; positive values represent surcharges or premiums (rare but possible for special equipment or expedited delivery).']
  adjustment_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the adjustment type for reporting and analysis purposes. Distinguishes between manufacturer-funded incentives, dealer-funded discounts, and other adjustment sources. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturer_incentive|dealer_discount|regional_program|customer_loyalty|fleet_commercial|employee_affiliate|promotional_campaign|competitive_response|inventory_management|executive_override — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  adjustment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the adjustment is calculated: fixed dollar amount, percentage of MSRP or invoice, tiered based on volume or other criteria, or formula-based calculation.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the price adjustment, used for tracking and audit purposes.']
  adjustment_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate of the price adjustment when the adjustment method is percentage-based. Stored as a decimal (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price adjustment indicating the program or reason for the non-standard pricing. Includes dealer discretionary discounts, manufacturer-authorized regional allowances, conquest and loyalty programs, fleet volume discounts, employee purchase programs, and other special pricing categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dealer_discount|regional_allowance|conquest_bonus|loyalty_discount|fleet_volume_discount|employee_purchase_program|military_discount|first_responder_discount|college_graduate_discount|lease_subvention|finance_subvention|trade_assistance|competitive_conquest|model_year_clearance|inventory_reduction|executive_authorization — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  applied_date date [note: 'Date on which the price adjustment was actually applied to the quote or order.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the price adjustment was approved by the authorizing manager.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the price adjustment request. Tracks the adjustment through the authorization workflow.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date on which this price adjustment was reviewed during an audit process.']
  audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price adjustment has been flagged for audit review due to unusual amount, authorization level, or other risk factors.']
  audit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the audit review for this price adjustment, if audited.']
  authorization_level varchar [note: 'The organizational level required to approve this price adjustment. Higher-value or non-standard adjustments require escalated authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dealer_manager|regional_manager|zone_director|national_sales_director|vice_president|executive_authorization|automated_program — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  base_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'The original price (MSRP or invoice price) before this adjustment is applied, providing context for the adjustment magnitude.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the financial impact of this price adjustment is allocated for internal accounting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the adjustment amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, MXN).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this price adjustment is no longer valid. Null for adjustments without expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this price adjustment becomes valid and can be applied to transactions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year when the adjustment was applied.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the price adjustment was applied, used for financial reporting and incentive accrual tracking.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for funding the price adjustment. Distinguishes between manufacturer-funded incentives and dealer-funded discounts.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the price adjustment amount is posted for financial reporting.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and supporting explanation for the price adjustment. Provides audit trail and context for non-standard pricing decisions.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle to which this price adjustment applies. Adjustments often vary by model year for inventory clearance purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price adjustment record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the price adjustment, providing supplementary context beyond the structured justification field.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Reference code for the manufacturer or dealer incentive program under which this adjustment is authorized, if applicable.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the incentive program associated with this price adjustment.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region code where the adjustment is applicable. Regional allowances and programs vary by market.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date on which the price adjustment request was rejected, if applicable.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the approving authority for rejecting the price adjustment request.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the sale is being made. Different channels may have different adjustment authorization rules and limits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|fleet|commercial|government|employee|direct|online|auction — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stacking_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price adjustment can be combined (stacked) with other adjustments and incentives. Some programs are mutually exclusive.']
  submitting_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or dealer user who submitted the price adjustment request.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Sales territory code within the region where the adjustment is applicable.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character unique identifier for the vehicle to which this price adjustment applies, if the vehicle has been assigned a VIN at the time of adjustment.']

  Note: 'Record of a price adjustment applied to a vehicle order or quote beyond standard MSRP and published incentive programs. Captures adjustment type (dealer discount, regional allowance, conquest bonus, loyalty discount, fleet volume discount, employee purchase program), adjustment amount or percentage, authorization level, approving manager, and justification. Provides audit trail for non-standard pricing decisions.'
}

Table sales.order_status_event {
  order_status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order status event record. Primary key.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed the order. Enables customer-specific order tracking and communication.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership or dealer location that placed or is fulfilling the order.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The user or system account that triggered the status change. May be a human user ID or a system service account identifier.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed the order. Enables customer-specific order tracking and communication.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle order that experienced the status change. Links to the parent order entity.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Internal unique identifier for the vehicle master record associated with this order.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Internal unique identifier for the vehicle master record associated with this order.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date when the vehicle was delivered to the dealer or customer. Populated when the order transitions to delivered status.']
  actual_production_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when the vehicle completed assembly and exited the production line. Populated when the order transitions to built status.']
  actual_production_start_date date [note: 'The actual date when the vehicle entered the production line. Populated when the order transitions to in_production status.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The name of the logistics carrier or transportation provider responsible for shipping the vehicle. Relevant for shipped and in_transit status events.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this order status event record was first created in the data system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  delay_days varchar [note: 'The number of days the order is delayed relative to the original schedule. Calculated when a status transition occurs later than planned.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'The destination location where the vehicle is being delivered. Typically the dealership code or customer delivery address reference.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'The estimated date when the vehicle is expected to arrive at the dealer or customer location. Updated as the order progresses through logistics.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments associated with the status change event. Provides additional context or details not captured in structured fields.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the order status transition occurred in the source system. This is the business event time, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the status event. Distinguishes routine status progressions from exceptions, cancellations, or amendments.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status event represents an exception or deviation from the normal order fulfillment process. True if exception, False otherwise.']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the exception or deviation, if exception_flag is True. Examples include production delay, quality hold, parts shortage, logistics delay, customer request.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the status event occurred. Used for financial and operational reporting alignment.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year of the vehicle associated with the order. Used for product lifecycle and sales analytics.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this order status event record was last modified in the data system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The order status after this transition event. Captures the to-state in the order lifecycle. Enables end-to-end order tracking through the fulfillment pipeline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: confirmed|scheduled_for_production|in_production|built|quality_released|shipped|in_transit|at_dealer|delivered|cancelled — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the customer of the status change. Examples include email, SMS, mobile app push notification, customer portal, phone call.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification was sent for this status change event. True if notification sent, False otherwise.']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'The location from which the vehicle was shipped. Typically the manufacturing plant or distribution center code.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The code of the manufacturing plant or assembly facility where the vehicle is being produced. Relevant for production and post-production status events.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The order status immediately before this transition event. Captures the from-state in the order lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: placed|confirmed|scheduled_for_production|in_production|built|quality_released|shipped — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  production_line varchar [note: 'The specific production line or assembly line within the plant where the vehicle is being built. Captured during in-production status events.']
  quality_release_date date [note: 'The date when the vehicle passed final quality inspection and was released for shipment. Populated when the order transitions to quality_released status.']
  region varchar [note: 'The sales region or geographic market where the order is being fulfilled. Used for regional performance tracking and logistics planning.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The business unit, department, or role responsible for the status transition. Examples include Production Planning, Manufacturing, Quality Assurance, Logistics, Dealer Network.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the order was placed. Examples include retail (dealer), fleet sales, commercial vehicle sales, direct-to-consumer, online sales.']
  scheduled_production_date date [note: 'The planned date when the vehicle is scheduled to enter production. Populated when the order transitions to scheduled_for_production status.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'The date when the vehicle was shipped from the manufacturing plant or distribution center. Populated when the order transitions to shipped status.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The tracking number or shipment reference provided by the carrier for end-to-end logistics visibility.']
  triggering_system varchar [note: 'The source system or application that initiated the status change event. Examples include SAP SD (Sales and Distribution), MES (Manufacturing Execution System), TMS (Transportation Management System), WMS (Warehouse Management System), DMS (Dealer Management System), Salesforce Automotive Cloud, or manual entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP_SD|MES|WMS|TMS|DMS|SALESFORCE|MANUAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  triggering_user_code varchar [note: 'The user or system account that triggered the status change. May be a human user ID or a system service account identifier.']
  vin varchar [note: 'The 17-character Vehicle Identification Number assigned to the vehicle associated with this order. Populated once the vehicle enters production and a VIN is assigned.']

  Note: 'Lifecycle status change event record for a vehicle order, capturing each transition through the order fulfillment pipeline (placed → confirmed → scheduled for production → in production → built → quality released → shipped → in transit → at dealer → delivered). Records event timestamp, previous status, new status, triggering system (SAP SD, MES, logistics), and responsible party. Enables end-to-end order tracking and customer communication.'
}

Table sales.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the sales campaign record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative or manager responsible for the campaign.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative or manager responsible for the campaign.']
  actual_spent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount actually spent on the campaign to date.']
  actual_spent_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the actual spent amount.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned monetary budget allocated to the campaign.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the campaign goals, scope, and key messages.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the campaign used in reporting and UI.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Category of the campaign indicating its strategic purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created.']
  dealer_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dealers are required to participate in the campaign.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign is scheduled to end.']
  kpi_market_share_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed market‑share percentage change achieved by the campaign.']
  kpi_market_share_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target market‑share percentage increase for the nameplate during the campaign period.']
  kpi_sales_volume_actual varchar [note: 'Actual number of vehicles sold attributable to the campaign.']
  kpi_sales_volume_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of vehicles to be sold as a result of the campaign.']
  marketing_channels varchar [note: 'Primary channels used to deliver the campaign messaging.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks.']
  objective varchar [note: 'High‑level business objective the campaign aims to achieve (e.g., increase sales volume, improve market share).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the campaign.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code representing the primary geographic region of the campaign.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign becomes active.']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment the campaign is designed for.']
  target_model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle nameplate targeted by the campaign.']
  target_nameplate varchar [note: 'Vehicle nameplate (model family) that the campaign is aimed at.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Internal code for the sales territory covered by the campaign.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a structured sales and marketing campaign targeting specific customer segments, nameplates, or market regions to drive vehicle sales. Captures campaign name, campaign type (model launch, clearance, seasonal, conquest, loyalty), target nameplate and model year, target customer segment, campaign period, budget allocation, participating dealers or regions, and campaign objectives. Distinct from incentive programs — campaigns coordinate multi-channel outreach while incentive programs define financial offers.'
}

Table sales.campaign_response {
  campaign_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign response record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales or marketing campaign that generated this response.']
