  Note: 'The formal dealer order guide defining which SKUs, trim levels, option packages, and colors are orderable by dealers for a given MY, market, and sales channel. Captures order guide version, effective date range, market/region, sales channel, orderable SKU list, option availability matrix, minimum/maximum order constraints, and order guide approval status. Published to dealer DMS systems and governs what dealers can order from the factory.'
}

Table aftersales.homologation_variant {
  homologation_variant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the homologation variant record. Primary key.']
  aftersales_trim_level_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific trim level or configuration within the nameplate that this homologation variant applies to. Nullable if variant applies to all trims.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle nameplate (model line) that this homologation variant applies to. Links to the nameplate master data.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the base commercial SKU that this homologation variant is derived from. Links to the product catalog SKU master.']
  adas_level varchar [note: 'SAE automation level for the ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) features included in this homologation variant, ranging from Level 0 (no automation) to Level 5 (full automation).']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency, regulatory body, or authorized technical service that issued the homologation approval. Examples include NHTSA, EPA, CARB, TÜV, or national transport ministries.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority officially granted homologation approval for this variant. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Official approval or certification number issued by the regulatory authority upon successful homologation. This number is typically required for vehicle registration and sales in the target market.']
  bom_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this homologation variant requires changes to the base SKU Bill of Materials. True if BOM modifications are needed, False if only calibration or documentation changes are required.']
  cafe_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this homologation variant is certified for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) compliance in the United States market. Applicable to US market variants only.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to regulatory compliance, testing requirements, or market-specific considerations for this homologation variant.']
  cost_delta_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Incremental cost associated with implementing this homologation variant compared to the base SKU, including parts, labor, testing, and certification fees. Expressed in the currency specified in cost_delta_currency.']
  cost_delta_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost_delta_amount field.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this homologation variant record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this homologation variant becomes valid and vehicles can be legally sold or registered in the target market. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  emissions_standard_target varchar [note: 'Specific emissions standard or tier that this homologation variant is designed to meet, such as Euro 6d, EPA Tier 3, CARB LEV III, or China 6b.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this homologation approval expires or becomes invalid, requiring renewal or re-certification. Nullable for approvals without expiration. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this homologation variant record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical lead time in days required to obtain homologation approval for this variant from the date of submission to the regulatory authority.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year designation for which this homologation variant is applicable. Aligns with the automotive industry model year convention.']
  modification_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the technical modifications, component changes, or configuration adjustments required to meet the target market regulatory requirements. May reference specific parts, calibrations, or design changes.']
  modification_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the type of modification required for homologation compliance, such as hardware changes, software updates, calibration adjustments, documentation updates, labeling changes, or additional testing.']
  ncap_rating varchar [note: 'Safety rating achieved by this homologation variant under the applicable NCAP (New Car Assessment Programme) testing protocol, such as Euro NCAP, US NCAP, or ANCAP. Typically expressed as a star rating or score.']
  ota_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this homologation variant includes OTA (Over-the-Air) update capability for software and firmware updates. Relevant for regulatory compliance with evolving software update requirements.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Type of powertrain for which this homologation variant is certified. ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle).']
  regulatory_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary regulatory domain this variant addresses, such as safety standards, emissions controls, environmental compliance, or fuel economy requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Safety|Emissions|Environmental|Fuel Economy|Crash Test|Lighting|Braking|Occupant Protection — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory standard or framework that this homologation variant must comply with. Examples include UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe), FMVSS (Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards), ADR (Australian Design Rules), CARB (California Air Resources Board). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UNECE|FMVSS|ADR|CARB|EPA|Euro NCAP|IATF 16949|ISO 14001 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this homologation approval requires periodic renewal or re-certification. True if renewal is mandated by the regulatory authority, False if approval is perpetual or tied to model year.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or technical specialist responsible for managing the homologation process and regulatory compliance for this variant.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application from which this homologation variant record originated, such as PLM, ERP, or regulatory compliance management system.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the homologation application and supporting documentation were submitted to the regulatory authority for review. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  target_market_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country code representing the target market or jurisdiction for which this homologation variant is certified.']
  target_region varchar [note: 'Broader geographic region classification for the target market, used for regional compliance grouping and reporting.']
  test_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the official test report(s) submitted as part of the homologation application. Links to laboratory test results, crash test data, emissions testing, or other certification evidence.']
  testing_laboratory varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited testing laboratory or technical service that conducted the homologation testing and issued the test reports.']
  variant_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying this specific homologation variant within the product catalog. Used for internal tracking and cross-referencing with regulatory submissions.']
  variant_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation for this homologation variant, typically including market and regulatory standard identifiers.']
  variant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the homologation variant in the approval and production process. Tracks progression from initial submission through approval, active use, and eventual expiration or withdrawal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Submitted|Under Review|Approved|Active|Suspended|Expired|Withdrawn|Rejected — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  wltp_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this homologation variant has been tested and certified under the WLTP (Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure) standard for fuel consumption and emissions.']

  Note: 'Defines market-specific homologation variants of a base SKU required to meet local regulatory standards (UNECE, FMVSS, ADR, etc.). Captures homologation variant code, base SKU reference, target market/country, regulatory standard (UNECE/FMVSS/ADR/CARB), required modification description, homologation approval number, approval authority, approval date, expiry date, and variant status. Bridges the commercial product catalog to the compliance domain\'s regulatory approval records.'
}

Table aftersales.product_lifecycle_event {
  product_lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product lifecycle event record. Primary key for this entity.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Lifecycle event approvals must record the approving employee to satisfy regulatory audit trails and internal change control.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lifecycle events (e.g., recall, launch) trigger cost allocations; linking to cost_center enables event‑driven expense tracking.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Lifecycle events are tied to a nameplate; the FK anchors events to the correct product entity.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Recall and safety event notifications require linking each product lifecycle event to the affected customer party to generate regulatory compliance reports and direct communications.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit impact of lifecycle events is captured via profit_center to reflect margin changes in financial statements.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when the lifecycle event occurred, used for variance analysis against planned date.']
  affected_entity_code varchar [note: 'Business code or identifier of the affected entity (e.g., nameplate code, program code, SKU code) for human-readable reference and reporting.']
  affected_entity_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific product entity affected by this event. References the primary key of the entity type specified in affected_entity_type.']
  affected_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of product entity affected by this lifecycle event (e.g., nameplate, model year program, SKU, trim level, platform, powertrain).']
  announcement_date date [note: 'Date when the lifecycle event was publicly or internally announced, which may differ from the actual event date.']
  announcement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event announcement scope: internal (company-only), public (press release), regulatory (mandatory filing), or confidential (restricted distribution).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the lifecycle event was formally approved by the required authority.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether executive or board approval was required for this lifecycle event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lifecycle event record was first created in the data platform.']
  customer_communication_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether direct customer communication or notification is required for this lifecycle event (e.g., for recalls, service campaigns, or discontinuations affecting existing owners).']
  dealer_notification_date date [note: 'Date when dealers were notified of this lifecycle event.']
  dealer_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dealer network notification is required for this lifecycle event.']
  engineering_change_number varchar [note: 'Engineering Change Notice or Engineering Change Order number associated with this lifecycle event, if applicable.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the lifecycle event: milestone (key program achievement), phase gate (APQP gate), regulatory (compliance-driven), commercial (market-facing), or operational (internal process).']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of lifecycle event (e.g., PROG_APPR, DSGN_FRZ, SOP, JOB1, MCR_ANN, EOP, DISC).']
  event_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the lifecycle event occurred or is scheduled to occur. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the lifecycle event: planned (scheduled but not confirmed), confirmed (approved and scheduled), completed (occurred), cancelled, or postponed.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the lifecycle event was recorded or occurred, including time zone information.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the lifecycle event. Captures major milestones such as program approval, design freeze, Start of Production (SOP), Job 1 (first production unit), mid-cycle refresh announcement, End of Production (EOP), or nameplate discontinuation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: program_approval|design_freeze|sop|job_1|mid_cycle_refresh|eop|nameplate_discontinuation|phase_gate|homologation_approval|ppap_approval — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial impact of this lifecycle event in the specified currency (e.g., CapEx investment for SOP, cost savings from EOP, write-off for discontinuation).']
  financial_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  impact_level varchar [note: 'Business impact level of this lifecycle event on operations, supply chain, sales, and customer commitments.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lifecycle event record was last updated in the data platform.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region affected by this lifecycle event (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Global).']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year associated with the affected product entity at the time of this lifecycle event.']
  new_state varchar [note: 'The lifecycle state or phase of the affected entity after this event (e.g., approved, frozen, in-production, discontinued). Represents the state transition resulting from this event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or context about this lifecycle event for audit trail and business reference.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Originally planned or target date for the lifecycle event, which may differ from the actual event date if the event was rescheduled.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or assembly facility code where the lifecycle event is relevant (e.g., for SOP or EOP events).']
  platform_code varchar [note: 'Vehicle platform or architecture code associated with the affected product entity.']
  press_release_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number, URL, or document identifier for the associated press release or public announcement, if applicable.']
  prior_state varchar [note: 'The lifecycle state or phase of the affected entity immediately before this event occurred (e.g., concept, development, pre-production, production, phase-out).']
  production_volume_impact varchar [note: 'Estimated change in production volume (units) resulting from this lifecycle event. Positive for ramp-up events, negative for phase-out or discontinuation.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Internal program code or project identifier for the vehicle program associated with this lifecycle event.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason or driver for this lifecycle event (e.g., market demand, regulatory requirement, cost reduction, quality improvement, end of platform life).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the business rationale, circumstances, or drivers behind this lifecycle event.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to any regulatory filing or submission associated with this event (e.g., NHTSA recall notice, EPA certification, homologation approval number).']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or key of this event record in the source system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or code of the operational system that originated this lifecycle event record (e.g., Teamcenter PLM, SAP PP, Windchill, internal event management system).']
  supplier_impact_count varchar [note: 'Number of suppliers affected by this lifecycle event (e.g., new suppliers onboarded for SOP, suppliers phased out for EOP).']
  triggering_authority varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person, role, or organizational unit that authorized or triggered this lifecycle event (e.g., Chief Product Officer, Program Manager, Executive Committee).']
  triggering_authority_reference varchar [note: 'System identifier for the person or role that authorized this event, linking to employee or role master data.']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between planned and actual event dates. Positive values indicate delay; negative values indicate early completion.']

  Note: 'Captures significant lifecycle state transitions for nameplates, model year programs, and SKUs (e.g., program approval, design freeze, SOP, Job 1, mid-cycle refresh announcement, EOP, nameplate discontinuation). Captures event type, affected entity type and ID, event date, prior state, new state, triggering authority, announcement type (internal/public), and associated press release or regulatory filing reference. Provides a complete audit trail of commercial product lifecycle decisions.'
}

Table aftersales.aftersales_repair_order {
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the repair_order data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_change. Business justification: Repair orders must reference the engineering change that introduced the fix, enabling field impact tracking and change effectiveness reporting.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Remote Diagnostic Session process requires linking a repair order to the specific connected vehicle for OTA updates and diagnostics.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service revenue posting requires linking each repair order to a cost center for cost allocation in the Service Revenue Report.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the vehicle.']
  dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer/service center handling the repair.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer/service center handling the repair.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service advisor who created the repair order.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: General Ledger posting of repair order revenue requires a GL account reference for the Service Revenue GL Posting process.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Repair orders are created when a quality inspection lot fails; linking provides the Inspection‑to‑Repair Traceability Report.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the vehicle.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Needed for Repair Order Cost Tracking report to associate parts spend on a repair with the originating purchase order.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Traceability report linking each repair order to the original production order enables warranty and recall investigations per VIN.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis per repair order uses profit center to track service margin in the Service Profitability Dashboard.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a recall campaign linked to this repair.']
  service_advisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service advisor who created the repair order.']
  service_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a service campaign (e.g., TSB) associated with this repair.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical service location.']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary technician assigned to perform the work.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Required for warranty eligibility and service cost attribution; repair orders must reference the original vehicle order to verify coverage and allocate service revenue.']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: Service order processing uses the ownership record to confirm warranty coverage and apply owner‑specific service rules.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Required for warranty verification and recall tracking in the Repair Order processing workflow, linking each order to the vehicle\'s VIN registry record.']
  actual_completion_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the repair was completed.']
  aftersales_repair_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the repair order.']
  appointment_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle arrived at the service center.']
  appointment_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle left the service center.']
  appointment_scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service appointment was scheduled.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the repair order was closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the repair order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  customer_feedback_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1-5) provided by the customer after service.']
  customer_signature_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer signed off on the repair order.']
  diagnostic_code varchar [note: 'Standard OBD diagnostic code recorded during service.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the repair order.']
  is_estimate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record is an estimate prior to work being performed.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard labor rate applied per hour.']
  labor_total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of labor before taxes and discounts.']
  labor_total_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total labor hours recorded for the repair order.']
  mileage_at_service varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the time of service.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after tax and discount.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the repair order was opened.']
  parts_total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of parts consumed before taxes and discounts.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the customer to settle the repair order.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment for the repair order.']
  promised_completion_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time promised to the customer for repair completion.']
  ro_number varchar [note: 'External repair order number assigned by the service center.']
  service_center_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the service center is located.']
  service_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by service advisor describing work performed.']
  service_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the repair order.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of service performed.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the repair order.']
  technician_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the technician during repair.']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the repair order before tax and discount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the repair order record.']
  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the warranty claim associated with this repair.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair is covered under warranty.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Type of warranty covering the repair.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record capturing a vehicle service or repair event at an authorized service center or dealership. Tracks RO number, vehicle VIN, customer, service advisor, open/close dates, labor operations performed, parts consumed, total labor cost, total parts cost, total RO value, payment method, warranty vs. customer pay vs. internal pay type, DMS source (CDK Global), mileage at write-up, promised completion time, actual completion time, technician assignments, and RO status lifecycle (open, in-progress, quality-check, closed, invoiced).'
}

Table aftersales.repair_order_line {
  repair_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the repair order line.']
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent repair order header to which this line belongs.']
  aftersales_service_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service appointment linked to this line.']
  aftersales_warranty_claim_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warranty claim associated with this line, if applicable.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.batch_record. Business justification: REQUIRED: When batch‑controlled parts are used in service, the batch_record must be recorded for quality and recall traceability.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_change. Business justification: Specific line items may be affected by an engineering change; linking supports detailed root‑cause analysis and cost allocation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each line\'s labor/parts expense is posted to a specific GL account for detailed expense reporting.']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: REQUIRED: Parts Traceability Report for warranty investigations needs to link each repair line to the inbound part record it originated from.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: REQUIRED: Repair Order Shipment Tracking report ties repaired parts to the specific inbound shipment they arrived on for logistics audit.']
  labor_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.labor_time_standard. Business justification: Repair order line should reference the standard labor time definition for the operation code.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor performing the sublet work.']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: REQUIRED: Serialized components (e.g., battery modules) installed during repair need a FK to the serialized_unit for warranty and service history.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authorized service center where work was performed.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Parts used in a repair order line must be tracked against the inventory SKU master for cost, stock deduction, and compliance reporting.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: REQUIRED: Service parts are issued from a specific warehouse bin; linking to storage_location enables inventory location traceability for service.']
  sublet_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor performing the sublet work.']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who performed the labor.']
  actual_technician_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual labor hours logged by the technician for this line.']
  cause_complaint varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the customer complaint or issue that initiated the service.']
  correction varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the corrective action taken to resolve the complaint.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the repair order line record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values on the line.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to this line, if any.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the labor type performed.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly labor rate applied to the technician for this operation.']
  labor_skill_level varchar [note: 'Skill level required for the technician to perform the operation.']
  labor_time_standard decimal(5,2) [note: 'Flat‑rate standard labor time in hours for the operation code.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the repair order for ordering.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the repair order line.']
  line_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount for the line (labor_rate * actual_technician_hours) before discounts and taxes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes entered by the technician or service advisor.']
  operation_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the specific labor operation performed (e.g., oil change, brake inspection).']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'True if overtime rates were applied to this labor line.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(3,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the labor_rate when overtime_flag is true (e.g., 1.5).']
  part_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard price per unit of the part at the time of service.']
  part_quantity decimal(6,2) [note: 'Quantity of the part used for this line.']
  parts_used_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any parts were consumed on this line.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the service work was performed.']
  sublet_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost charged by the sublet vendor for this line.']
  sublet_flag boolean [note: 'True if the work was performed by an external subcontractor.']
  tax_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Tax applied to the line total.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the repair order line record.']
  vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'Unique 17‑character identifier of the vehicle serviced.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the labor line is covered under warranty (true) or not (false).']

  Note: 'Individual labor operation or job line within a repair order. Captures operation code, labor time standard (flat-rate hours), actual technician hours, labor rate, line total, technician ID, cause/complaint/correction (3C) narrative, warranty flag, sublet flag, and line status. Supports granular cost analysis and technician productivity tracking per CDK Global DMS job line structure.'
}

Table aftersales.aftersales_warranty_claim {
  aftersales_warranty_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the warranty_claim data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  claim_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Associates warranty claim creation with the responsible employee for accountability and claim processing KPI.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Warranty claims must reference the originating sales contract to verify coverage terms and reconcile financials between sales and aftersales.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle owner who is the warranty claimant.']
  dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer or service center submitting the claim.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer or service center submitting the claim.']
  defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect_record. Business justification: Warranty claim investigation requires linking each claim to the originating quality defect record (Warranty Claim Investigation Report).']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle owner who is the warranty claimant.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_recall_campaign. Business justification: Regulatory warranty claim reporting mandates associating claims arising from a recall campaign with that campaign for audit and reimbursement.']
  remote_diagnostic_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.remote_diagnostic_session. Business justification: Warranty Claim Investigation often uses a remote diagnostic session to verify fault, linking claim to the session record.']
  service_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recall or service campaign linked to the claim.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authorized service center performing the repair.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: Warranty Claim Settlement process requires linking each claim to the supplier contract that governs parts coverage.']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: Claims processing must reference the specific ownership period to determine coverage limits and calculate accurate claim payouts.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Aggregating warranty claims by vehicle program is required for reliability analysis and program improvement decisions.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Needed for claim eligibility validation against the vehicle\'s VIN registry during Warranty Claim adjudication.']
  warranty_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.warranty_policy. Business justification: A warranty claim is filed under a specific warranty policy; linking directly to warranty_policy provides direct access to policy terms without needing to traverse vehicle_warranty.']
  warranty_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.warranty_reserve. Business justification: Warranty claim settlements are charged against a warranty reserve record for IFRS warranty liability tracking.']
  adjudication_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the OEM\'s review of the claim.']
  adjusted_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the adjustment applied to the claim.']
  approved_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount approved for labor services.']
  approved_labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of labor hours approved for reimbursement.']
  approved_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount approved for parts used in the repair.']
  claim_adjusted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim amount was later adjusted.']
  claim_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the claim type.']
  claim_created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who entered the claim.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'External claim identifier assigned by the OEM or dealer for tracking.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warranty claim.']
  claim_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the warranty claim was submitted to the OEM.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the claim amounts.']
  failure_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle failure that triggered the warranty claim occurred.']
  goodwill_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim is processed as goodwill (no reimbursement).']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard labor rate used for calculating labor cost.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes entered by the service advisor or adjudicator.']
  parts_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax amount applied to the approved parts cost.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the claim record.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a claim was rejected, if applicable.']
  repair_date date [note: 'Date the repair work was performed on the vehicle.']
  repair_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the service repair order linked to the claim.']
  total_claim_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the claim (labor + parts).']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle\'s warranty coverage expires.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle\'s warranty coverage began.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Category of warranty coverage applicable to the claim.']

  Note: 'Warranty claim submitted by a dealer or authorized service center to the OEM for reimbursement of warranty-covered repairs. Tracks claim number, VIN, repair order reference, failure date, repair date, claim submission date, claim status (submitted, approved, rejected, adjusted, paid), approved labor hours, approved parts cost, total claim amount, rejection reason code, goodwill flag, campaign/recall linkage, and OEM adjudication outcome. Integrates with SAP SD warranty module and CDK Global DMS.'
}

Table aftersales.warranty_policy {
  warranty_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the warranty policy record.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Warranty administration reports need to associate each warranty policy with the exact nameplate model to determine coverage eligibility and claim validation.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Warranty policies are defined per vehicle program; the link supports cost forecasting and compliance tracking per program.']
  authorized_dealer_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether warranty service must be performed at an authorized dealer (true) or can be performed at any qualified service center (false).']
  claim_limit_per_year varchar [note: 'Maximum number of warranty claims allowed per vehicle per calendar year.']
  claim_limit_total varchar [note: 'Overall maximum number of warranty claims permitted during the policy term.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the primary country of warranty applicability.']
  coverage_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of what components or systems are covered under the policy.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Category of coverage provided by the warranty (e.g., basic, powertrain, corrosion, emissions, EV battery, ADAS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the warranty policy record was first created in the system.']
  deductible_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount the customer must pay for each covered repair under the warranty.']
  duration_months varchar [note: 'Length of the warranty coverage expressed in calendar months.']
  effective_end_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the warranty coverage expires (typically EOP or calculated from duration).']
  effective_start_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the warranty coverage becomes effective (typically SOP).']
  eop_date date [note: 'The production end date of the vehicle model to which the warranty applies.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'List of components, conditions, or events excluded from coverage.']
  extension_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the warranty may be extended beyond the original term under defined conditions.']
  extension_terms varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the conditions, cost, and duration for extending the warranty.']
  inclusions varchar [note: 'List of components, systems, or services explicitly covered.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the warranty policy record.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region where the warranty is offered (e.g., North America, EU).']
  mileage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum distance the vehicle may travel while the warranty remains valid, expressed in miles.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle for which the warranty is defined.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or remarks about the warranty policy.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the warranty policy, used in contracts and customer communications.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warranty policy.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Type of powertrain the warranty applies to (e.g., ICE, HEV, PHEV, EV).']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing authority that mandates or oversees the warranty (e.g., NHTSA, EPA).']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory filing or certification linked to the warranty.']
  renewal_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty can be renewed after expiration.']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Details of renewal options, pricing, and eligibility.']
  service_center_network varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service‑center network eligible for warranty work (e.g., OEM network, third‑party network).']
  sop_date date [note: 'The production start date of the vehicle model to which the warranty applies.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty can be transferred to a subsequent owner (true) or is non‑transferable (false).']

  Note: 'Master definition of warranty coverage terms applicable to a vehicle nameplate, model year, powertrain type, or market. Captures warranty type (basic/bumper-to-bumper, powertrain, corrosion, emissions, EV battery, ADAS), coverage duration in months, coverage distance in miles/km, deductible amount, transferability flag, market/region applicability, SOP and EOP dates, and governing regulatory body (NHTSA, EPA). Serves as the authoritative reference for warranty eligibility determination.'
}

Table aftersales.vehicle_warranty {
  vehicle_warranty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the vehicle warranty record.']
  aftersales_warranty_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.warranty_policy. Business justification: Vehicle warranty must reference the applicable warranty policy; adds inbound to warranty_policy and outbound from vehicle_warranty.']
  dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer that sold the vehicle or issued the warranty.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer that sold the vehicle or issued the warranty.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Warranty administration requires linking each warranty to the owning party for eligibility verification and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a recall record that may affect the warranty.']
  recall_recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a recall record that may affect the warranty.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authorized service center handling warranty work.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service contract linked to the warranty.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Connects warranty contracts to the VIN registry to validate coverage periods and claim eligibility.']
  claims_last_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the most recent warranty claim.']
  claims_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent warranty claim.']
  coverage_area varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the warranty coverage.']
  coverage_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of what components and services are covered.']
  coverage_end_mileage varchar [note: 'Maximum mileage at which warranty coverage ends.']
  coverage_start_mileage varchar [note: 'Mileage reading at which warranty coverage begins.']
  cpo_warranty_flag boolean [note: 'True if the warranty applies to a Certified Pre‑Owned vehicle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warranty record was first created in the system.']
  duration_months varchar [note: 'Total length of the warranty in months.']
  eligible_for_recall boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle is eligible for a safety recall under this warranty.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the warranty coverage ends.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Text describing items or conditions excluded from coverage.']
  extended_warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty has been extended beyond the original terms.']
  last_transfer_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent warranty transfer.']
  mileage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum mileage allowed under the warranty.']
  original_purchase_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle was originally purchased by the first owner.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the warranty policy.']
  program_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the warranty program.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the warranty program (e.g., New Vehicle, CPO, Extended).']
  remaining_mileage varchar [note: 'Mileage remaining before the warranty limit is reached.']
  remaining_months varchar [note: 'Number of months remaining before warranty expiration.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date on which the warranty was renewed or is scheduled to renew.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty is eligible for renewal.']
  service_plan varchar [note: 'Name of the service plan associated with the warranty.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the warranty coverage became effective (in‑service date).']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the warranty is in its current status.']
  transfer_allowed boolean [note: 'True if the warranty may be transferred to a subsequent owner.']
  transfer_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the warranty has been transferred to a new owner.']
  transfer_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged for transferring the warranty to a new owner.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the warranty record.']
  vehicle_warranty_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warranty.']
  warranty_claims_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all warranty claims.']
  warranty_claims_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims filed to date.']
  warranty_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic copy of the warranty contract.']
  warranty_number varchar [note: 'External warranty contract number assigned by the manufacturer or dealer.']
  warranty_terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the warranty terms document.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Category of coverage provided by the warranty.']

  Note: 'VIN-level warranty entitlement record linking a specific vehicle to its applicable warranty policies. Tracks warranty start date (in-service date), expiration date, remaining months, remaining mileage, warranty status (active, expired, voided), extended warranty flag, CPO (Certified Pre-Owned) warranty flag, and any warranty transfer history. This is the SSOT for whether a specific vehicle is under warranty at any point in time.'
}

Table aftersales.service_campaign {
  service_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the service campaign record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_campaign. Business justification: Service campaigns are often launched from sales campaigns; linking tracks campaign effectiveness and regulatory reporting across domains.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Service campaigns are planned per vehicle program; linking enables program‑level impact analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  affected_model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year in the range of vehicles covered.']
  affected_model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year in the range of vehicles covered.']
  affected_vin_population varchar [note: 'Estimated count of VINs that fall within the campaign scope.']
  campaign_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected total cost to execute the campaign (currency assumed USD).']
  campaign_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments from campaign managers.']
  campaign_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for resource allocation and scheduling.']
  campaign_region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region(s) affected; uses ISO‑3 country codes.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign: safety recall, emissions recall, customer satisfaction program, or technical service bulletin action.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the campaign with applicable regulations.']
  customer_satisfaction_flag boolean [note: 'True if the campaign is a voluntary customer‑satisfaction program.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign is closed or no longer applicable (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign becomes effective for affected vehicles.']
  emissions_recall_flag boolean [note: 'True if the campaign addresses emissions compliance.']
  estimated_repair_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time, in minutes, required to complete the repair per vehicle.']
  nhtsa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign meets NHTSA requirements.']
  parts_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of parts required for the campaign.']
  parts_required varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers needed to perform the remedy.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']
  regulatory_reporting_date date [note: 'Date on which the campaign was reported to the regulator.']
  regulatory_reporting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the campaign\'s regulatory filing with NHTSA/UNECE.']
  remedy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the repair or corrective action required.']
  safety_recall_flag boolean [note: 'True if the campaign is a safety‑related recall.']
  technical_service_bulletin_flag boolean [note: 'True if the campaign originates from a TSB.']
  unece_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign meets UNECE regulations.']
  warranty_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign affects warranty coverage.']

  Note: 'Master record for a service campaign (recall or non-safety field action) issued by the OEM. Captures NHTSA recall number, campaign code, campaign type (safety recall, emissions recall, customer satisfaction program, technical service bulletin action), affected nameplate/model year range, affected VIN population count, remedy description, estimated repair time, parts required, campaign open date, campaign close date, regulatory reporting status, and NHTSA/UNECE compliance flags. Integrates with NHTSA recall database and SAP QM.'
}

Table aftersales.campaign_vin {
  campaign_vin_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the campaign VIN association record.']
  service_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_campaign. Business justification: Campaign VIN records need to be linked to the parent service campaign for proper campaign tracking.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Associates vehicles with recall or service campaigns, enabling campaign effectiveness reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the service campaign or recall.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the campaign purpose and remedy.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory compliance deadline for completing the remedy.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status for the vehicle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign VIN record was created.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer where remedy was performed.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the campaign is classified as critical safety.']
  labor_rate_usd_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Labor rate applied for the remedy in USD per hour.']
  labor_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Labor time recorded for the remedy in hours.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent service performed on the vehicle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes entered by the service technician.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle was notified about the campaign.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current notification status of the vehicle for the campaign.']
  odometer_reading_km varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at time of remedy in kilometers.']
  owner_response varchar [note: 'Owner\'s response to the campaign notification.']
  parts_consumed varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers consumed for the remedy.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Official recall number assigned by regulatory authority.']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Category of the recall or campaign.']
  remedy_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the remedy was completed.']
  remedy_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remedy execution.']
  scheduled_service_date date [note: 'Date scheduled for the service remedy.']
  service_center_location varchar [note: 'Location identifier of the service center where remedy was performed.']
  service_order_number varchar [note: 'Dealer service order number associated with the remedy.']
  total_labor_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost for the remedy in USD.']
  total_parts_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of parts consumed for the remedy in USD.']
  total_remedy_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost (parts + labor) for the remedy in USD.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  warranty_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates if the remedy is covered under warranty.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a specific VIN to an open service campaign or recall. Tracks VIN, campaign code, notification status (not notified, notified, scheduled, completed, parts-on-order, owner-refused), remedy completion date, dealer code where remedy was performed, parts consumed for remedy, and compliance deadline. Enables OEM and NHTSA to track recall completion rates at the individual vehicle level.'
}

Table aftersales.aftersales_service_appointment {
  aftersales_service_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service_appointment data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: OTA Update Appointment workflow ties a service appointment to the target connected vehicle to schedule firmware upgrades.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service appointment labor and parts costs are allocated to a cost center for service cost analysis.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle owner or service requester.']
  dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealership or authorized service center hosting the appointment.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealership or authorized service center hosting the appointment.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service advisor assigned to the appointment.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle owner or service requester.']
  service_advisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service advisor assigned to the appointment.']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: Appointment scheduling needs the ownership record to apply loyalty benefits and verify owner eligibility for scheduled services.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Ensures service appointment scheduling aligns with the correct vehicle record for service history and recall compliance.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service work was completed.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle actually entered service.']
  aftersales_service_appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appointment.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the service appointment, used in dealer and customer communications.']
  appointment_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the appointment was booked.']
  bay_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service bay where the vehicle will be serviced.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer checked in at the service center.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current confirmation state of the appointment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appointment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  customer_feedback_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (0‑10) provided by the customer after service.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned length of the service appointment in minutes.']
  estimated_gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected total charge before taxes and discounts.']
  estimated_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected total charge after taxes and discounts.']
  estimated_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected tax component of the service charge.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice generated for the service appointment.']
  is_first_time_customer boolean [note: 'True if this is the customer\'s first service appointment with the dealer.']
  is_repeat_service boolean [note: 'True if the vehicle has previously received the same service type.']
  labor_time_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual labor minutes recorded for the service.']
  no_show_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer failed to appear for the scheduled appointment.']
  parts_actual_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of parts actually used during service.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'True if the appointment is part of a manufacturer recall campaign.']
  required_parts_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specific parts must be ordered before the appointment.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the service is to start.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of the service (e.g., oil change, brake service, battery replacement).']
  service_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the service advisor or technician.']
  service_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the appointment for scheduling.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service (e.g., routine maintenance, warranty repair, recall, pre‑delivery inspection, customer‑pay).']
  total_actual_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final charge for the appointment after labor, parts, taxes, and discounts.']
  transportation_option varchar [note: 'Customer transportation provision during service.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the appointment record.']
  vehicle_mileage varchar [note: 'Odometer reading at the time of service appointment.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'True if the appointment is covered under a warranty agreement.']

  Note: 'Scheduled service appointment record for a vehicle at a dealership or authorized service center. Captures appointment date/time, customer contact, VIN, service type (maintenance, warranty repair, recall, PDI, customer pay), service advisor assigned, estimated duration, transportation option (loaner, shuttle, wait), appointment source (online, phone, DMS, mobile app), confirmation status, check-in time, and no-show flag. Sourced from CDK Global DMS scheduling module.'
}

Table aftersales.tsb {
  tsb_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the Technical Service Bulletin record.']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: Technical Service Bulletins are issued based on design specifications; the link provides traceability for compliance audits.']
  service_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_campaign. Business justification: Technical Service Bulletin (TSB) is issued as part of a service campaign; linking provides campaign context.']
  affected_model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year in the range affected by the bulletin.']
  affected_model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year in the range affected by the bulletin.']
  affected_vehicle_model varchar [note: 'Vehicle model name or code to which the bulletin applies.']
  affected_vin_end varchar [note: 'Ending VIN in the range covered by the bulletin.']
  affected_vin_start varchar [note: 'Starting VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) in the range covered by the bulletin.']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'Link to the full PDF or digital document of the bulletin.']
  author_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the bulletin (e.g., Powertrain, Body, Electronics).']
  author_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the OEM engineer who authored the bulletin.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bulletin record was first created in the data lake.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of bulletin distribution to the dealer network.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the bulletin recommendations become applicable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the bulletin is no longer applicable (nullable for open‑ended bulletins).']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated technician labor time required to complete the repair.']
  is_ota_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fix can be delivered via Over‑The‑Air update.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the bulletin was officially issued by the OEM.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard labor rate applied for cost estimation (currency assumed USD).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the bulletin was last reviewed for relevance or accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'OEM part number required for the repair.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the required part.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the bulletin satisfies a regulatory requirement (e.g., NHTSA, EPA).']
  repair_procedure varchar [note: 'Step‑by‑step instructions for service technicians to resolve the issue.']
  required_parts varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers and revisions needed for the repair.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent technical review.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Technical explanation of why the symptom occurs.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact severity of the issue addressed by the bulletin.']
  superseded_by_tsb_number varchar [note: 'TSB number that supersedes this bulletin, if any.']
  symptom_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the customer‑reported or observed symptom that triggers the bulletin.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the bulletin.']
  total_estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Combined estimated cost of parts and labor for the bulletin repair.']
  tsb_number varchar [note: 'OEM-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the bulletin (e.g., TSB-2023-001).']
  tsb_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bulletin.']
  tsb_type varchar [note: 'Category of the bulletin indicating its purpose.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bulletin record.']
  vehicle_system_affected varchar [note: 'Specific vehicle subsystem (e.g., ECU, transmission) impacted by the issue.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the bulletin content.']
  warranty_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair is covered under vehicle warranty.']

  Note: 'Technical Service Bulletin master record issued by OEM engineering to communicate repair procedures, diagnostic guidance, or part supersessions to the dealer service network. Captures TSB number, title, affected nameplate/model year/VIN range, symptom description, root cause, recommended repair procedure, required parts list, estimated labor time, issue date, supersession reference, and distribution status to dealer network. Integrates with PTC Windchill and CDK Global DMS.'
}

Table aftersales.aftersales_dtc_event {
  aftersales_dtc_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DTC event record.']
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Link to the service repair order associated with the DTC investigation.']
  ecu_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ECU module that reported the DTC.']
  ecu_specification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ECU module that reported the DTC.']
  fmea_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: DTC analysis uses FMEA data to identify failure modes (DTC‑FMEA Mapping Report).']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Recall root‑cause analysis links DTC events to the specific production order that built the vehicle for batch‑level defect tracking.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authorized service location handling the DTC.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Links diagnostic trouble code events to the VIN registry for emission compliance reporting and service history.']
  ambient_temperature_c varchar [note: 'Outside air temperature in degrees Celsius when the DTC was logged.']
  battery_voltage_v decimal(5,2) [note: 'Vehicle battery voltage measured at the event.']
  cleared_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DTC has been cleared (true) or remains active (false).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DTC event record was first inserted.']
  diagnostic_session_type varchar [note: 'Type of diagnostic session during which the DTC was captured.']
  dtc_category varchar [note: 'High‑level functional area the DTC belongs to.']
  dtc_code varchar [note: 'Standardized fault code captured from the vehicle ECU.']
  dtc_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the fault represented by the DTC.']
  dtc_recall_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the DTC is associated with an active manufacturer recall.']
  dtc_related_part_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the part linked to the DTC.']
  dtc_related_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number of the component replaced or inspected for the DTC.']
  dtc_resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the corrective action taken.']
  dtc_resolution_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the repair or fix applied.']
  dtc_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DTC (e.g., pending, confirmed).']
  emission_related_flag boolean [note: 'True if the DTC is related to emissions control systems.']
  engine_rpm varchar [note: 'Engine revolutions per minute recorded when the DTC occurred.']
  event_source_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system or device that generated the DTC event record.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the DTC event was recorded in the system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of source that produced the DTC event.']
  first_occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the DTC first appeared on the vehicle.']
  freeze_frame_data varchar [note: 'Snapshot of sensor values captured at the time the DTC was set, stored as a JSON string.']
  fuel_level_percent varchar [note: 'Fuel tank level as a percentage at the time of the event.']
  is_test_event boolean [note: 'True if the DTC record originates from a test or diagnostic simulation.']
  last_occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the DTC was most recently observed.']
  mileage_at_event varchar [note: 'Odometer reading (kilometres) when the DTC was captured.']
  occurrence_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the DTC has been recorded for this vehicle.']
  odometer_km varchar [note: 'Total distance traveled by the vehicle in kilometres at the time of the DTC.']
  service_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the vehicle was serviced for the DTC.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity of the fault, used for prioritization.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Software module that generated the DTC event.']
  source_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the source module at event time.']
  technician_action varchar [note: 'Summary of diagnostic steps or repairs performed by the technician.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the DTC event record.']
  vehicle_speed_kph varchar [note: 'Speed of the vehicle in kilometres per hour at the time of the DTC.']
  warranty_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair for this DTC is covered under warranty.']

  Note: 'Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) event record captured from OBD-II (On-Board Diagnostics) systems during a vehicle service visit or via connected vehicle telematics. Tracks VIN, DTC code (SAE J2012 standardized or OEM-proprietary), ECU module source, fault type (confirmed, pending, permanent), fault description, freeze frame data snapshot, first occurrence timestamp, last occurrence timestamp, occurrence count, MIL (Malfunction Indicator Lamp) status, cleared flag, repair order linkage, and technician diagnostic action taken. Enables fleet-wide DTC pattern analysis for TSB development, early warning quality detection, and warranty cost forecasting.'
}

Table aftersales.labor_time_standard {
  labor_time_standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each labor time standard record.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'Body style classification of the vehicle (e.g., hatchback, pickup). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coupe|sedan|hatchback|wagon|pickup|van|SUV — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_nhtsa_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory code from NHTSA that the labor operation must satisfy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the labor time standard becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the labor time standard is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the operation is mandatory for the vehicle model/year, false otherwise.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of labor (e.g., maintenance, repair).']
  labor_time_standard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the labor time standard.']
  labor_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the labor is covered under warranty, billed to the customer, or part of a recall.']
  last_revision_date date [note: 'Date when the labor time standard was last revised.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the labor operation.']
  oem_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number of the OEM component associated with the operation, if any.']
  operation_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the labor operation (e.g., "A123").']
  operation_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the labor operation performed.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the standard applies.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the revision of the standard.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Technician skill level required to perform the operation.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the labor time data (OEM guide, MOTOR, Alldata, etc.).']
  special_tool_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a special tool is needed (true) or not (false).']
  standard_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Flat‑rate labor time in hours for the operation.']
  tool_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the special tool required for the operation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  vehicle_model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year for which this labor time standard applies.']
  vehicle_model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year for which this labor time standard applies.']
  vehicle_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of vehicle (e.g., sedan, SUV) to which the standard is applicable.']

  Note: 'Reference master for flat-rate labor time standards (operation times) used to price warranty claims and customer-pay repair operations. Captures operation code, operation description, applicable nameplate/model year range, standard flat-rate hours, skill level required, special tool requirement flag, last revision date, and source (OEM labor time guide or MOTOR/Alldata industry standard). Used by CDK Global DMS to auto-populate labor time on repair order lines.'
}

Table aftersales.service_part {
  service_part_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the service part record.']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: REQUIRED: Service Part Origin Traceability needed for regulatory compliance (NHTSA/UNECE) links service parts to their inbound part records.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Required for parts traceability report linking service inventory to engineering part master specs, essential for compliance and quality analysis.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Service Part Master Data must reference the supplying vendor for procurement planning and compliance reporting.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Regulatory certification(s) applicable to the part (e.g., EPA, DOT).']
  core_charge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Refundable deposit required for parts that are returned for reuse.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating where the part was manufactured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the service part record was first created in the system.']
  dealer_net_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price offered to authorized dealers after standard discounts.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the part is retired from the service catalog (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the part becomes valid for use in service operations.']
  epa_hazardous_material_code varchar [note: 'EPA classification code for hazardous parts, if applicable.']
  hazardous_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification code for hazardous parts (e.g., UN number).']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is classified as hazardous material.']
  height_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical height of the part in millimetres.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current inventory availability status of the part.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the part was last consumed in a service transaction.']
  length_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical length of the part in millimetres.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the part within the after‑sales catalog.']
  list_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Manufacturer suggested retail price before any discounts.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer that produces the part.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the part type.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the part.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned part number used to uniquely identify the part across the enterprise.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for engineering changes to the part.']
  service_part_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the part, including fitment notes.']
  superseded_by_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number that replaces this part when it is superseded.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for inventory and transaction quantities (e.g., EA, SET).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the service part record.']
  warranty_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is covered under the standard warranty program.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the part is covered by warranty from the date of installation.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Net weight of the part in kilograms.']
  width_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical width of the part in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Aftersales service parts master record for parts stocked and consumed in dealer service and repair operations. Captures OEM part number, supersession chain (current and all prior part numbers), part description, part category (mechanical, electrical, body, consumable, fluid, accessory), unit of measure, standard list price, dealer net price, core charge amount, weight, hazmat flag, country of origin, minimum order quantity, and lifecycle status (active, superseded, discontinued, obsolete).
