  dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer associated with the blocked billing document, if applicable to dealer transactions.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer associated with the blocked billing document, if applicable to dealer transactions.']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer dispute record associated with this billing block, if the block is dispute-related.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that applied the billing block.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account associated with the blocked billing document.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_recall_campaign. Business justification: Billing blocks must reference active recall campaigns to prevent invoicing for recalled vehicles.']
  released_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that released the billing block.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Billing blocks are often placed on a particular vehicle for compliance or credit issues; FK allows block status reporting per VIN.']
  aging_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the billing block has been active since application, used for aging analysis and escalation triggers.']
  applied_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who applied the billing block, for business user reference and audit purposes.']
  applied_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing block was applied to the document, preventing invoice generation.']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the billing block was applied, including time-of-day for audit and tracking purposes.']
  block_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the billing block, used for tracking and communication purposes.']
  block_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing block. Active blocks prevent invoicing; released blocks have been cleared.']
  block_type varchar [note: 'Category of billing block applied. Indicates the reason category for preventing invoice generation.']
  blocked_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the sales order, delivery, or billing account that is blocked from invoicing.']
  blocked_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of document to which the billing block is applied (e.g., sales order, delivery, billing account).']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity to which the blocked billing document belongs.']
  compliance_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Specific compliance or regulatory reason for the billing hold, such as export control, sanctions screening, or regulatory investigation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing block record was first created in the system, for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  credit_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit exposure amount for the customer at the time the billing block was applied, used in credit limit evaluation.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved credit limit for the customer at the time of billing block application, used for credit hold decisions.']
  credit_limit_exceeded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing block was triggered due to the customer exceeding their approved credit limit.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for credit exposure and limit amounts.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Delivery document number associated with the billing block, if the block is applied at the delivery level.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'SAP distribution channel through which the blocked billing document was processed (e.g., retail, fleet, wholesale).']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP division or product line associated with the blocked billing document (e.g., passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, parts).']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing block was escalated for higher-level review or resolution.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing block has been escalated to management or a specialized resolution team due to age or complexity.']
  expected_resolution_date date [note: 'Target date by which the billing block is expected to be resolved and released, used for SLA tracking and customer communication.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year when the billing block was applied.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the billing block was applied, used for financial period reporting and analysis.']
  legal_hold_case_number varchar [note: 'Case or matter number for legal holds, linking the billing block to a specific legal proceeding or investigation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes or comments related to the billing block, capturing additional context, communications, or resolution actions.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the billing block for resolution workflow and escalation management.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the billing block. Maps to SAP SD billing block reason master data.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the reason the billing block was applied, providing context for resolution.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing block was released or cleared, allowing invoice generation to proceed.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the user when releasing the billing block, documenting resolution actions taken.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the billing block was released, including time-of-day for audit and SLA tracking.']
  released_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who released the billing block, for business user reference and audit purposes.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Sales order number associated with the blocked document, if applicable. Links the block to the originating sales transaction.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP sales organization code responsible for the blocked billing document.']
  sap_billing_block_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD system billing block code reference. Maps to SAP configuration table for billing block types and controls.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing block record was last modified, for audit trail and change tracking purposes.']

  Note: 'Record of a billing block applied to a sales order, delivery, or billing account that prevents invoice generation until the block is resolved. Captures block type (credit_limit_exceeded/pricing_incomplete/legal_hold/compliance_hold/dispute_hold/manual_hold), blocked document reference, block applied date, block applied by, block reason description, release date, released by, and SAP SD billing block code reference. Supports credit management and compliance-driven invoice holds.'
}

Table billing.payment_plan {
  payment_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_plan']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Accounts may have a default payment plan; adding the FK enables direct lookup.']
  superseded_payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_plan (superseded_payment_plan_id)']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'If true, the plan automatically renews at the end of its term.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Definition of the billing period used for invoicing.']
  billing_day_of_month varchar [note: 'Day of month on which recurring invoices are generated.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of debt collection activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment plan record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency used for the plan.']
  default_date date [note: 'Date when the plan entered default status.']
  defaulted_flag boolean [note: 'True if the plan is in default due to missed payments.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the plan, including purpose and key terms.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the total amount.']
  down_payment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Initial lump‑sum payment required at plan inception.']
  early_termination_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged if the plan is terminated before its effective end date.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan terminates; null for open‑ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan becomes binding.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which customers may enroll in the plan.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after due date before penalties apply.']
  installment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount due for each regular installment.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual percentage rate applied to the outstanding balance.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent payment was received.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed fee applied when a payment is received after the grace period.']
  next_payment_due_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled payment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions.']
  number_of_installments varchar [note: 'Total number of installments defined in the plan.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Remaining amount due under the plan.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment is initiated.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Regular interval at which payments are scheduled.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to settle payments.']
  penalty_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage applied to overdue balances as a penalty.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the payment plan in contracts and invoices.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment plan indicating the financing model.']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'Code representing a marketing promotion linked to the plan.']
  recurring_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount that recurs each payment period after down payment.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is eligible for renewal.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment plan.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated tax amount based on tax_rate and taxable amount.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is exempt from sales tax.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the plan (if not tax‑exempt).']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Overall amount to be paid over the life of the plan.']
  total_paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all payments received to date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payment plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_plan. Referenced by payment_plan_id.'
}

Table billing.billing_period {
  billing_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billing_period']
  previous_billing_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on billing_period (previous_billing_period_id)']
  billing_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the billing cycle associated with the period.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which billing is generated for this period.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date on which the period was closed, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the period record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code applicable to the period\'s financial amounts.']
  days_in_period varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days covered by the period.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the period.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter identifier for the period.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the period belongs.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period has been closed for posting.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the billing period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the billing period, e.g., "2023‑04".']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the billing period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period (e.g., regular monthly, quarterly, adjustment, forecast).']
  period_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the period definition, used when revisions occur.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the period record (e.g., SAP).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the period record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for billing_period. Referenced by billing_period_id.'
}

Table mobility.connected_vehicle {
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the connected vehicle record.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Sales Attribution Report links each connected vehicle to the dealer that sold it, enabling dealer‑level warranty and service responsibility.']
  driver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fleet driver assignment report requires linking each vehicle to its current driver employee for compliance and usage tracking.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Vehicle Asset Management report requires linking each connected vehicle to its equipment registry record for warranty, maintenance, and depreciation tracking.']
  fleet_mobility_fleet_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet to which the vehicle belongs, if applicable.']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Regulatory Homologation Report requires linking each vehicle to its homologation record for market approval tracking.']
  mobility_fleet_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet to which the vehicle belongs, if applicable.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: OTA campaign planning requires linking each connected vehicle to its product nameplate to apply correct software version and compliance reporting.']
  ota_compliance_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ota_compliance_approval. Business justification: OTA updates must be tied to regulatory OTA compliance approval to satisfy cybersecurity and safety audit.']
  owner_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Needed to identify the legal owner for billing, data‑privacy compliance, and service entitlement of each connected vehicle.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each vehicle contributes to a profit center for margin analysis; required for the Vehicle Profitability Report.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Warranty and service management need the exact SKU for each connected vehicle to determine coverage, parts, and service intervals.']
  software_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.software_version. Business justification: Storing the software version as a FK enables consistent version management and eliminates duplicated version strings on the vehicle record.']
  telematics_device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the telematics hardware installed in the vehicle.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Required for warranty, recall and OTA management linking each connected vehicle to the official VIN registry record.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the device activation.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was first activated.']
  battery_health_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated health of the battery relative to its original capacity.']
  battery_state_of_charge_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current state of charge of the vehicle\'s battery expressed as a percentage.']
  connectivity_tier varchar [note: 'Service tier defining data allowance and priority for the vehicle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the connected vehicle record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_plan varchar [note: 'Subscription plan governing cellular data usage.']
  data_usage_gb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative cellular data consumed by the vehicle in the current billing cycle.']
  data_usage_last_reset date [note: 'Date when the data usage counter was last reset.']
  deactivation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was deactivated or retired.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Model or family of the connected telematics device.']
  diagnostic_status varchar [note: 'Overall health status derived from the latest diagnostic data.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the telematics device.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the primary market of the vehicle.']
  last_diagnostic_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent vehicle diagnostic event.']
  last_error_code varchar [note: 'Most recent Diagnostic Trouble Code reported by the vehicle.']
  last_ota_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent Over‑The‑Air software update.']
  last_tpms_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent TPMS firmware or configuration update.']
  last_v2x_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent V2X software update.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer of the vehicle.']
  mileage_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total distance traveled by the vehicle, reported in kilometers.']
  mileage_last_update timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent mileage reading.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Marketing name of the vehicle model.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle (calendar year).']
  ota_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle supports Over-The-Air updates.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Primary propulsion technology of the vehicle.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle was enrolled in the connected mobility program.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vehicle\'s connectivity service registration.']
  sim_iccid varchar [note: 'Integrated Circuit Card Identifier of the SIM/eSIM used for connectivity.']
  sim_imsi varchar [note: 'International Mobile Subscriber Identity associated with the SIM.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the vehicle\'s on‑board software platform.']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription plan expires (null if open‑ended).']
  subscription_plan varchar [note: 'Service plan governing feature entitlements for the connected vehicle.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription plan became effective.']
  tpms_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle is equipped with Tire Pressure Monitoring System telemetry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the connected vehicle record.']
  v2x_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle can communicate Vehicle‑to‑Everything.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Broad category of the vehicle.']
  vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier assigned to each vehicle by the manufacturer.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle\'s warranty coverage ends.']
  warranty_status varchar [note: 'Current warranty coverage status of the vehicle.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all connected vehicles enrolled in mobility and telematics services. Owns the connected device identity per VIN, connectivity hardware profile (Geotab/Bosch IoT device), SIM/eSIM identifiers, connectivity tier, activation status, OTA capability flags, V2X capability flags, and TPMS sensor registration. This is the SSOT for connected vehicle device identity within the mobility domain, distinct from the vehicle master in the vehicle domain which owns VIN-level manufacturing identity. Links to telematics_device for hardware asset details.'
}

Table mobility.telematics_device {
  telematics_device_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each telematics control unit (TCU) record.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Device Installation Management process tracks which equipment asset each telematics device is mounted on for service contracts and calibration compliance.']
  installed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Device installation/maintenance logs need the installer employee to support warranty validation and service cost allocation.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Supports hardware warranty and recall process linking each telematics device to its manufacturing supplier for defect tracking.']
  battery_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current battery charge level expressed as a percentage.']
  calibration_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device has passed factory calibration (true) or not (false).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the telematics device record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_plan_expiration date [note: 'Date when the current data plan expires.']
  data_plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of cellular data plan assigned to the device.']
  device_make varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer that produced the telematics device.']
  device_model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the telematics device as defined by the manufacturer.']
  device_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the device.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the telematics device.']
  gps_latitude double [note: 'Most recent latitude coordinate reported by the device.']
  gps_longitude double [note: 'Most recent longitude coordinate reported by the device.']
  hardware_generation varchar [note: 'Generation identifier for the hardware platform of the device (e.g., Gen1, Gen2).']
  iccid varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the SIM card embedded in the telematics device.']
  imei varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier for the device\'s cellular modem.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the telematics device was installed in the vehicle.']
  last_firmware_update timestamp [note: 'When the device last received a firmware OTA update.']
  last_heartbeat_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent health‑check ping received from the device.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent scheduled maintenance performed on the device.']
  maintenance_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Planned interval in months between routine maintenance events.']
  odometer_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative distance traveled as reported by the device, in kilometers.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the device within the fleet.']
  ota_update_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports over‑the‑air firmware updates.']
  owner_type varchar [note: 'Entity type that owns the device.']
  provisioning_date date [note: 'Date the device was provisioned and linked to the fleet management system.']
  signal_strength_dbm varchar [note: 'Cellular signal strength measured in decibel‑milliwatts.']
  temperature_c double [note: 'Internal temperature of the telematics unit in degrees Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the telematics device record.']
  v2x_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if vehicle‑to‑everything communication is enabled on the device.']
  vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'VIN of the vehicle in which the device is installed.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the device\'s manufacturer warranty ends.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical or embedded telematics control unit (TCU) installed in a vehicle. Tracks device make, model, firmware version, IMEI, ICCID, hardware generation, installation date, calibration status, and operational health. Sourced from Geotab and Bosch IoT device registries. Distinct from connected_vehicle which is the vehicle-level enrollment record; telematics_device is the hardware asset record.'
}

Table mobility.service {
  service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mobility service offering.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Service management dashboards track which employee manages each mobility service for SLA compliance and accountability.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.nameplate. Business justification: Service eligibility matrix links services to specific vehicle nameplates for subscription eligibility and regulatory reporting.']
  service_tier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pricing tier applicable to the service.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Needed for Service Supplier Management report that tracks which external supplier provides each mobility service for billing, compliance, and warranty.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of billing for the service.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service record was first created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the service price.']
  data_privacy_level varchar [note: 'Classifies the privacy level of data collected by the service.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the service usage data is retained.']
  device_compatibility varchar [note: 'List of compatible telematics devices or platforms (e.g., Bosch, Geotab).']
  eligibility_rules varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which customers or vehicles may subscribe to the service.']
  end_of_service_date date [note: 'Date when the service is retired or no longer offered.']
  is_premium boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service is classified as a premium offering.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the service became available to customers.']
  max_simultaneous_devices varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicle devices that can be linked to a single subscription.']
  ota_update_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service includes over‑the‑air software updates.']
  predictive_maintenance_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service provides predictive maintenance alerts.']
  price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base price of the service before taxes or discounts.']
  provider varchar [note: 'Internal business unit or external partner delivering the service.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory approval was granted.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the service.']
  remote_diagnostics_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether remote vehicle health diagnostics are part of the service.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the service (e.g., remote diagnostics, OTA updates, V2X). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: remote_diagnostics|predictive_maintenance|ota_update|tpms_monitoring|v2x|fleet_telematics|autonomous_feature_package|usage_based_insurance — promote to reference product]']
  service_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the service in contracts and catalogs.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the service offering.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the mobility service.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service offering.']
  sla_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the service level agreement terms.']
  sla_hours varchar [note: 'Target response time in hours for service‑related incidents.']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Billing model for the service.']
  supported_vehicle_models varchar [note: 'List or pattern of vehicle models (by platform or VIN prefix) that can use the service.']
  tax_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is included in the listed price.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate as a percentage when tax is not included.']
  terms_url varchar [note: 'Link to the legal terms and conditions governing the service.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the service record.']
  usage_based_insurance_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service enables usage‑based insurance functionality.']
  usage_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed usage quantity (e.g., miles, hours) per billing period.']
  usage_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to measure consumption for usage‑based billing (e.g., data_volume, event_count).']
  usage_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the usage limit.']
  v2x_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service provides vehicle‑to‑everything communication features.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the service definition.']
  version_release_date date [note: 'Date when the current service version was released.']

  Note: 'Catalog of all connected mobility service offerings available for subscription or entitlement, including remote diagnostics, predictive maintenance alerts, OTA update service, TPMS monitoring, V2X communication, fleet telematics, autonomous driving feature packages, and usage-based insurance enablement. Defines service name, category, eligibility rules, supported vehicle platforms, pricing tier reference, and service lifecycle (launch date, EOP date). This is the SSOT for mobility service definitions.'
}

Table mobility.service_subscription {
  service_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each mobility service subscription record.']
  customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who owns the subscription.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Subscription Sales Attribution Report attributes each service subscription to the dealer that sold it for revenue tracking.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Financial Billing process allocates subscription fees to the specific equipment asset for cost accounting and depreciation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Subscription fees are booked to a revenue GL account; needed for the Subscription Billing and Revenue Recognition process.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who owns the subscription.']
  sales_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sales commission reports require linking each subscription to the sales representative employee who closed the deal.']
  service_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific connected mobility service offering.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Needed to tie subscription eligibility, billing and compliance directly to the vehicle master record for accurate invoicing and regulatory reporting.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the subscription is set to renew automatically at the end of the term.']
  billing_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base amount charged per billing cycle before taxes, fees, or discounts.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the subscription is billed.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription was cancelled by the customer or provider.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for terminating the subscription.']
  compliance_gdpr_consent boolean [note: 'True if the subscriber has provided consent for data processing under GDPR.']
  contract_terms varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the contractual obligations, service level agreements, and special conditions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the subscription record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the billing amount.']
  data_sharing_opt_in boolean [note: 'True if the subscriber agrees to share usage data with third‑party partners.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription terminates or expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  entitlement_tier varchar [note: 'Level of service features and data allowances granted to the subscriber.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the most recent update.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent subscription payment was received.']
  next_payment_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the upcoming subscription payment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the subscription.']
  overage_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary charge applied for usage beyond the allocated limit.']
  overage_fee_applied boolean [note: 'True if usage exceeded the limit and an overage fee was charged.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used for subscription billing.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent payment transaction.']
  privacy_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy accepted at subscription time.']
  promo_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount granted by the promotional code.']
  promo_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional discount expires.']
  promo_start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional discount becomes effective.']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'Code applied to the subscription for a discount or special offer.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next automatic renewal, if auto‑renewal is enabled.']
  service_subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the subscription.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system of record (e.g., Geotab, Bosch IoT).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription becomes effective.']
  subscription_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the subscription agreement, used in billing and customer communications.']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Category of the connected mobility service (e.g., telematics, infotainment, remote diagnostics).']
  trial_end_date date [note: 'Date when the trial period expires and regular billing begins.']
  trial_flag boolean [note: 'True if the subscription is currently in a free‑trial period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the subscription record.']
  usage_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed usage (e.g., data, minutes) per billing cycle.']
  usage_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for usage (e.g., gigabytes, minutes).']
  usage_used decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual usage recorded for the current billing cycle.']

  Note: 'Active and historical mobility service subscription agreements between a customer and a connected mobility service. Captures subscription start/end dates, billing cycle, entitlement tier, auto-renewal flag, cancellation reason, trial period flag, and subscription status. Links customer identity (from customer domain) to a mobility_service. This is the SSOT for mobility service entitlements and active agreements per vehicle/customer.'
}

Table mobility.telemetry_event {
  telemetry_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each telemetry event record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the driver associated with the vehicle at event time.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Telemetry Data Integration report joins raw telemetry events with equipment assets for reliability analysis and warranty claim validation.']
  individual_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the driver associated with the vehicle at event time.']
  trip_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the telemetry event to a specific vehicle trip or route.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Allows raw telemetry events to be associated with the official vehicle record for diagnostics, safety reporting and emissions compliance.']
  altitude double [note: 'Elevation above sea level of the vehicle at event time, in meters.']
  battery_soc_percent double [note: 'Current electric battery charge level for EV/HEV vehicles, expressed as a percentage.']
  battery_voltage_volt double [note: 'Instantaneous voltage of the vehicle\'s high‑voltage battery, expressed in volts.']
  charging_status boolean [note: 'True if the vehicle is currently connected to a charger and charging, otherwise false.']
  connectivity_status boolean [note: 'True if the telematics device had an active network connection at event time.']
  engine_rpm varchar [note: 'Engine speed measured in revolutions per minute at the time of the event.']
  engine_temperature_c double [note: 'Measured temperature of the engine coolant at event time, in degrees Celsius.']
  event_sequence varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing sequence number for events from the same vehicle, used for ordering.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that produced the telemetry record (e.g., Geotab, Bosch).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the telemetry event was generated by the vehicle sensor.']
  event_type_code varchar [note: 'Code that categorizes the type of telemetry event (e.g., speed, harsh_brake, idle).']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the telematics device firmware that generated the event.']
  fuel_level_percent double [note: 'Remaining fuel level expressed as a percentage of tank capacity.']
  gps_accuracy_m double [note: 'Estimated horizontal accuracy of the GPS position in meters.']
  heading_degrees double [note: 'Compass direction the vehicle is facing at event time, expressed in degrees from true north.']
  ignition_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle ignition is on (true) or off (false) at the moment of the event.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the vehicle at event time, expressed in decimal degrees.']
  latitude_accuracy double [note: 'Estimated vertical accuracy of the latitude measurement.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City name derived from the GPS coordinates at event time.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code derived from the GPS coordinates at event time.']
  location_state varchar [note: 'State or province name derived from the GPS coordinates at event time.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the vehicle at event time, expressed in decimal degrees.']
  longitude_accuracy double [note: 'Estimated vertical accuracy of the longitude measurement.']
  odometer_km double [note: 'Cumulative distance traveled by the vehicle, recorded in kilometers.']
  raw_payload varchar [note: 'Original JSON payload received from the telematics device before any transformation.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the telemetry record was first ingested into the silver layer.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the telemetry record in the lakehouse.']
  signal_quality varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the telemetry signal (e.g., good, moderate, poor).']
  speed_kph double [note: 'Instantaneous vehicle speed at event time, measured in kilometers per hour.']
  tire_pressure_front_left_psi double [note: 'Air pressure of the front‑left tire measured in pounds per square inch.']
  tire_pressure_front_right_psi double [note: 'Air pressure of the front‑right tire measured in pounds per square inch.']
  tire_pressure_rear_left_psi double [note: 'Air pressure of the rear‑left tire measured in pounds per square inch.']
  tire_pressure_rear_right_psi double [note: 'Air pressure of the rear‑right tire measured in pounds per square inch.']

  Note: 'High-frequency transactional record of raw and processed telematics events streamed from connected vehicles via Geotab or Bosch IoT. Each record captures event timestamp, VIN reference, GPS coordinates (latitude, longitude, altitude), vehicle speed, heading, ignition state, odometer reading, fuel level, battery state of charge (for EV/HEV), engine RPM, and event type code. Silver layer record represents cleansed and deduplicated stream from the bronze ingestion layer.'
}

Table mobility.trip {
  trip_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the trip record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the driver associated with the trip.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Asset Utilization Dashboard links trip records to equipment assets to calculate OEE and cost per vehicle.']
  individual_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the driver associated with the trip.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the predefined route, if the trip follows a known path.']
  telematics_device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the onboard telematics unit that reported the trip data.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vehicle that performed the trip.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vehicle that performed the trip.']
  average_speed_kph double [note: 'Mean speed of the vehicle over the trip.']
  battery_state_of_charge_end_percent double [note: 'Battery charge level at trip end (percentage).']
  battery_state_of_charge_start_percent double [note: 'Battery charge level at trip start (percentage).']
  charging_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was charged during the trip.']
  destination_latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the trip end location.']
  destination_longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the trip end location.']
  distance_km double [note: 'Total distance covered during the trip.']
  driver_behavior_score double [note: 'Aggregated safety score (0‑100) derived from driving events.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time from start to end of the trip.']
  emission_co2_kg double [note: 'Estimated carbon dioxide emissions for the trip.']
  end_odometer_km double [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the end of the trip.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the vehicle ignition was turned off and the trip ended.']
  energy_consumed_kwh double [note: 'Electrical energy drawn from the battery during the trip (applicable to EV/HEV).']
  fuel_consumed_liters double [note: 'Volume of gasoline/diesel used during the trip (applicable to ICE vehicles).']
  geo_fence_violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the vehicle crossed a defined geofence during the trip.']
  harsh_acceleration_count varchar [note: 'Number of harsh acceleration incidents detected during the trip.']
  harsh_braking_count varchar [note: 'Number of harsh braking incidents detected during the trip.']
  idle_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time the vehicle was stationary with engine on.']
  maintenance_alert_flag boolean [note: 'True if the trip triggered a predictive‑maintenance alert.']
  max_speed_kph double [note: 'Highest speed recorded during the trip.']
  mileage_since_last_service_km double [note: 'Distance driven since the vehicle\'s most recent service event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes entered by driver or operator about the trip.']
  origin_latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the trip start location.']
  origin_longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the trip start location.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the trip record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the trip record.']
  road_type varchar [note: 'Category of road surfaces traversed.']
  start_odometer_km double [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the beginning of the trip.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the vehicle ignition was turned on and the trip began.']
  toll_amount_usd double [note: 'Total toll fees incurred on the trip, expressed in US dollars.']
  toll_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the toll amount.']
  traffic_level varchar [note: 'Estimated traffic congestion experienced.']
  trip_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the trip (derived from start_timestamp).']
  trip_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable trip code used in operations and customer communications.']
  trip_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the trip.']
  trip_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trip purpose.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Observed weather during the trip.']

  Note: 'Aggregated trip record derived from telemetry event streams, representing a single continuous vehicle journey from ignition-on to ignition-off. Captures trip start/end timestamps, origin and destination coordinates, total distance driven, average speed, maximum speed, idle time, fuel consumed or energy consumed (kWh for EV), and driver behavior metrics (harsh braking count, harsh acceleration count, sharp cornering count, speeding duration, driver behavior score). Used for usage-based insurance (UBI), fleet safety management, driver coaching programs, and predictive maintenance triggers.'
}

Table mobility.mobility_dtc_event {
  mobility_dtc_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mobility_dtc_event data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Add direct link to connected_vehicle for DTC events to enable vehicle‑level diagnostics.']

  Note: 'Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) event record captured via OBD (On-Board Diagnostics) from connected vehicles. Each record stores the DTC code, SAE/OEM fault description, ECU source module, fault severity level, first occurrence timestamp, last occurrence timestamp, occurrence count, freeze frame data snapshot, and resolution status. Feeds remote diagnostics services and predictive maintenance alert generation. Sourced from Geotab and Bosch IoT OBD data streams.'
}

Table mobility.remote_diagnostic_session {
  remote_diagnostic_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the remote diagnostic session.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Remote diagnostic sessions are performed per vehicle; link to connected_vehicle for context.']
  driver_individual_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the driver or vehicle owner associated with the session.']
  individual_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the driver or vehicle owner associated with the session.']
  technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Remote diagnostic audit logs must record the technician employee performing the session for quality and liability tracking.']
  battery_state_of_charge_percent varchar [note: 'Battery charge level reported by the vehicle during the session, expressed as a percentage.']
  connectivity_status varchar [note: 'Current network connectivity state of the vehicle during the session.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_volume_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of telemetry and diagnostic data transferred during the session.']
  diagnostic_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the remote diagnostic session: full vehicle scan, targeted ECU, or custom set of modules.']
  error_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of error codes returned by the vehicle during diagnostics.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the session was escalated to after‑sales service for further action.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the vehicle ECU firmware queried in the session.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Communication medium used for the remote session.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form technician or system notes captured for the session.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the diagnostic session after processing.']
  recommended_action_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of action codes suggested by the diagnostic engine (e.g., repair steps, software patches).']
  remote_diagnostic_session_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the remote diagnostic session.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'External reference code for the diagnostic session, used in service logs and customer communications.']
  session_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the remote diagnostic session in seconds.']
  session_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the remote diagnostic session was started.']
  signal_strength_dbm varchar [note: 'Measured radio signal strength in decibel‑milliwatts at session start.']
  triggering_event_code varchar [note: 'Code of the event that initiated the session (e.g., DTC code).']
  triggering_event_type varchar [note: 'Origin of the session trigger – Diagnostic Trouble Code event, manual request, scheduled maintenance, or OTA update.']
  tsb_references varchar [note: 'Identifiers of TSBs surfaced during the session, if any.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the session record.']
  vehicle_odometer_km varchar [note: 'Odometer reading of the vehicle at the time of the session, in kilometers.']

  Note: 'Record of a remote diagnostic session initiated for a connected vehicle, either triggered automatically by a DTC event or manually by a service advisor or customer. Captures session initiation timestamp, triggering event reference, diagnostic scope (full scan vs targeted ECU), session outcome, recommended action codes, TSB (Technical Service Bulletin) references surfaced, and escalation flag to aftersales domain. Integrates with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Field Service for escalation workflows.'
}

Table mobility.predictive_maintenance_alert {
  predictive_maintenance_alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each predictive maintenance alert record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Link vehicle VIN to master connected_vehicle record; VIN column redundant.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Maintenance alerts trigger cost allocations to the plant\'s cost center; required for the Predictive Maintenance Cost Forecast report.']
  alert_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the alert (e.g., engine, brake, battery, software).']
  alert_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the alert for tracking and reference.']
  alert_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the alert.']
  component varchar [note: 'Name of the vehicle component or system predicted to fail (e.g., engine, brake, battery).']
  confidence_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Model confidence that the predicted failure will occur, expressed as a percentage (0‑100).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Textual description of the failure mode the model predicts (e.g., coolant leak, battery degradation).']
  generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the predictive maintenance alert was generated by the analytics engine.']
  mileage_at_alert varchar [note: 'Odometer reading of the vehicle at the time the alert was generated.']
  predicted_failure_end timestamp [note: 'Latest timestamp when the predicted failure is expected to occur.']
  predicted_failure_start timestamp [note: 'Earliest timestamp when the predicted failure is expected to occur.']
  recommended_service_action varchar [note: 'Prescribed maintenance or repair action to address the predicted issue.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert was marked resolved or closed.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the alert indicating potential impact.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating telematics or IoT platform that supplied the raw data (e.g., Geotab, Bosch IoT).']
  temperature_celsius decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature recorded by the vehicle sensor when the alert was generated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the alert record.']

  Note: 'Operational alert record generated when telematics and DTC data patterns indicate an impending vehicle component failure or maintenance need. Captures alert generation timestamp, VIN reference, affected component or system, predicted failure window, confidence level, alert severity, recommended service action, alert status (open/acknowledged/resolved/expired), and resolution timestamp. Distinct from a DTC event (which is a raw fault code) — this is a processed, actionable maintenance recommendation.'
}

Table mobility.ota_campaign {
  ota_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the OTA campaign.']
  ota_compliance_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ota_compliance_approval. Business justification: Each OTA campaign must reference its OTA compliance approval record for regulatory audit of firmware releases.']
  software_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.software_version. Business justification: OTA campaign targets a specific software version; use FK to software_version for version control.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the campaign after internal review.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of internal audit of the OTA campaign configuration.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the OTA campaign.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Operational state of the OTA campaign lifecycle.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Category of software being delivered (e.g., firmware, infotainment, ADAS, security patch, calibration).']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA‑256) used to verify integrity of the software package.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance checks against regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the OTA campaign record was created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date when the OTA rollout is expected to complete.']
  estimated_impact_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected proportion of the fleet that will successfully receive the update.']
  firmware_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the firmware package in megabytes.']
  max_concurrent_devices varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicles that may be updated simultaneously.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  ota_campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the OTA campaign purpose and scope.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory or compliance document linked to the campaign.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing changes, fixes, and new features in the update.']
  rollback_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a rollback to the previous software version is permitted.']
  rollout_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned deployment approach for the campaign.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the system is scheduled to finish the OTA rollout.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the system is scheduled to initiate the OTA rollout.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date when the OTA rollout begins.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Business market segment (e.g., US, EU, China) for the OTA campaign.']
  target_model_years varchar [note: 'Model year range (e.g., 2022‑2024) of vehicles eligible for the campaign.']
  target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Portion of the eligible vehicle fleet to receive the update (0‑100%).']
  target_region_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3 country codes where the campaign is applicable.']
  target_trim_codes varchar [note: 'List of vehicle trim identifiers that qualify for the update.']
  target_vehicle_criteria varchar [note: 'Logical expression or JSON defining vehicle population filters (model year, trim, region, etc.).']
  total_target_vehicles varchar [note: 'Count of distinct vehicles that meet the campaign criteria.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the OTA campaign record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the OTA campaign record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the OTA campaign record.']

  Note: 'Master record for an OTA (Over-the-Air) software or firmware update campaign targeting a population of connected vehicles. Captures campaign name, campaign type (ECU firmware, infotainment software, ADAS calibration, security patch), target vehicle population criteria (model year, trim, region), software version being deployed, rollout strategy (phased percentage, full fleet), campaign start/end dates, approval status, and regulatory compliance reference. Sourced from Geotab/Bosch IoT OTA management platform.'
}

Table mobility.mobility_ota_deployment {
  mobility_ota_deployment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each OTA deployment record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key for the vehicle entity within the data lake.']
  ota_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the OTA campaign to which this deployment belongs.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key for the vehicle entity within the data lake.']
  bandwidth_consumed_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total data volume transferred during the OTA download, measured in megabytes.']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Network technology used for the OTA download.']
  consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle owner has consented to receive the OTA update.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OTA deployment record was first created in the system.']
  data_package_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the OTA software package delivered to the vehicle.']
  deployment_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code assigned to the OTA deployment, used for tracking and reporting.']
  deployment_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA campaign was initiated for the vehicle.']
  download_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time taken to download the OTA package, calculated as download_end_timestamp minus download_start_timestamp.']
  download_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA package download completed.']
  download_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA package download began on the vehicle.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the OTA deployment failed, if applicable.']
  install_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time taken to install the OTA update, calculated as install_end_timestamp minus install_start_timestamp.']
  install_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA installation process completed.']
  install_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA installation process started on the vehicle.']
  mobility_ota_deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OTA deployment.']
  post_software_version varchar [note: 'Software version installed on the vehicle after successful OTA update.']
  pre_software_version varchar [note: 'Software version installed on the vehicle before the OTA update.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of retry attempts made after a failed OTA deployment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this OTA deployment record.']
  vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique 17‑character identifier of the vehicle receiving the OTA update.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking the execution of an OTA campaign update for a specific connected vehicle. Captures deployment initiation timestamp, vehicle consent status, download start/completion timestamps, installation start/completion timestamps, pre-update software version, post-update software version, deployment outcome (success/failed/rolled-back), failure reason code, retry count, and bandwidth consumed. Provides vehicle-level OTA traceability required under UNECE WP.29 R156.'
}

Table mobility.tpms_reading {
  tpms_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each TPMS telemetry record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: TPMS readings belong to a specific connected vehicle; VIN column redundant.']
  alert_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reading triggered a driver or service‑center alert.']
  battery_level_percent varchar [note: 'Remaining battery level of the TPMS sensor expressed as a percentage.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assessment of the TPMS reading after validation checks.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the sensor firmware at the time of the reading.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the TPMS record was loaded into the lakehouse.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the vehicle when the TPMS reading was recorded.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the vehicle when the TPMS reading was recorded.']
  odometer_km decimal(8,1) [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading in kilometres at the moment of the TPMS measurement.']
  pressure_status varchar [note: 'Derived status of the tire pressure based on business thresholds.']
  pressure_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the pressure_value (pounds per square inch or kilopascals).']
  pressure_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured tire pressure from the TPMS sensor.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the TPMS sensor measurement was captured on the vehicle.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Processing state of the TPMS record within the data pipeline.']
  sensor_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned unique identifier of the TPMS sensor device.']
  sensor_type varchar [note: 'Type of sensor that generated the event; fixed as TPMS for this product.']
  signal_strength varchar [note: 'Measured RF signal strength of the TPMS sensor transmission (0‑100).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the telematics platform that supplied the TPMS data (e.g., Geotab, Bosch).']
  speed_kph decimal(5,1) [note: 'Vehicle speed in kilometres per hour at the time of the TPMS reading.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for temperature_value (Celsius or Fahrenheit).']
  temperature_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured temperature of the tire at the time of the pressure reading.']
  wheel_position varchar [note: 'Location of the wheel on the vehicle (Front‑Left, Front‑Right, Rear‑Left, Rear‑Right, Spare).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of TPMS (Tire Pressure Monitoring System) sensor telemetry readings for each wheel position on a connected vehicle. Captures reading timestamp, VIN reference, wheel position (FL/FR/RL/RR/spare), measured tire pressure (PSI/kPa), tire temperature, sensor battery level, pressure status (normal/low/critical/flat), and alert flag. Feeds predictive maintenance alerts and customer-facing TPMS monitoring service. Sourced from vehicle telematics stream.'
}

Table mobility.geofence {
  geofence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the geofence.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Geofence speed limits and restrictions depend on jurisdictional regulations; linking enables jurisdiction‑specific enforcement.']
  mobility_fleet_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet account to which the geofence belongs.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current activation state of the geofence.']
  allowed_vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Vehicle categories permitted to enter the geofence.']
  area_sq_meters decimal(20,2) [note: 'Calculated surface area of the geofence.']
  associated_service varchar [note: 'Name of the mobility service (e.g., OTA, V2X) that uses this geofence.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest audit (passed, failed, or pending).']
  center_latitude double [note: 'Latitude of the geofence centre point (used for circular geofences).']
  center_longitude double [note: 'Longitude of the geofence centre point (used for circular geofences).']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the geofence is located.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the geofence definition.']
  country varchar [note: 'Country of the geofence location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the geofence record was first created.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days telemetry related to this geofence is retained.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the geofence expires or is de‑commissioned (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the geofence becomes effective.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems to reference this geofence.']
  geofence_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code used to reference the geofence in external systems.']
  geofence_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the geofence purpose and characteristics.']
  geofence_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the geofence.']
  geometry_coordinates varchar [note: 'Well‑Known Text (WKT) representation of the geofence geometry.']
  geometry_type varchar [note: 'Shape type that defines the geofence geometry.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this geofence is the default for its purpose.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit for the geofence.']
  max_speed_limit_kph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum vehicle speed allowed within the geofence.']
  min_speed_limit_kph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum vehicle speed required within the geofence.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information.']
  odometer_limit_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative odometer reading allowed while inside the geofence.']
  perimeter_meters decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total perimeter length of the geofence boundary.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose of the geofence, e.g., fleet zone, ODD, restricted area.']
  radius_meters decimal(20,2) [note: 'Radius of the geofence when geometry_type is circle.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code representing the region of the geofence.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the geofence record (e.g., Geotab, Bosch IoT).']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the geofence location.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the geofence record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the geofence definition for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier that created the geofence record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a geographic boundary (geofence) used in fleet management, mobility services, and autonomous driving feature activation zones. Captures geofence name, geometry type (polygon, circle, corridor), coordinate set, geofence purpose (fleet zone, ODD operational design domain for ADAS, restricted zone, dealer territory, charging station proximity), activation status, and associated service or fleet account. Used by fleet operators and autonomous feature entitlement management.'
}

Table mobility.geofence_event {
  geofence_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each geofence event record.']
  geofence_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the defined geofence area.']
  telematics_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the telematics hardware that reported the geofence event.']
  battery_level_percent varchar [note: 'Remaining battery charge of the vehicle (relevant for EV/HEV) at the event time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the geofence event record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_quality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event data passed validation checks (true = valid).']
  dwell_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of time the vehicle remained inside the geofence before exiting; populated for exit events.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to transmit the geofence event data.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vehicle crossed the geofence boundary.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of geofence interaction: entry, exit, or dwell within the boundary.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the vehicle at the geofence crossing point.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the vehicle at the geofence crossing point.']
  odometer_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the time of the event, expressed in kilometers.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the event within the data pipeline.']
  signal_strength_dbm varchar [note: 'Radio signal strength of the telematics device when the event was recorded.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating telematics platform that generated the event.']
  speed_kph decimal(6,2) [note: 'Speed of the vehicle at the moment of crossing, measured in kilometers per hour.']
  triggered_action varchar [note: 'Business action automatically initiated by the event, such as sending an alert, activating a service, or notifying fleet management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the geofence event record.']
  vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier of the vehicle involved in the geofence event.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a vehicle entering or exiting a defined geofence boundary. Captures event timestamp, VIN reference, geofence reference, event type (entry/exit/dwell), vehicle speed at crossing, dwell duration (for exit events), and triggered action (alert sent, service activated, fleet notification). Used for fleet compliance monitoring, ADAS ODD boundary enforcement, and usage-based service activation.'
}

Table mobility.mobility_fleet_account {
  mobility_fleet_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mobility_fleet_account data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fleet accounting requires mapping each fleet account to a cost center for expense tracking in the Fleet Cost Allocation report.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Fleet Account Management links fleet accounts to the responsible dealer for financing, support, and reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record for a fleet customer account enrolled in connected mobility and fleet telematics services. Captures fleet account name, fleet type (commercial, government, rental, corporate), total enrolled vehicle count, primary contact reference, billing account reference, contracted service tier, fleet management platform preference, and account status. This is the mobility domain\'s representation of fleet operators as a distinct customer segment — complementary to the customer domain\'s individual customer master.'
}

Table mobility.mobility_fleet_vehicle_assignment {
  mobility_fleet_vehicle_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mobility_fleet_vehicle_assignment data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Vehicle assignment should reference the connected_vehicle master record.']
  mobility_fleet_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.mobility_fleet_account. Business justification: Assignment records must capture which fleet account a vehicle is assigned to; the fleet account is the parent entity, so a FK from assignment to fleet account is appropriate.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a specific connected vehicle to a fleet account, capturing the assignment start date, assignment end date, vehicle role within the fleet (primary, backup, pool), assigned driver reference, cost center allocation, and assignment status. Manages the many-to-many lifecycle of vehicles within fleet accounts over time, supporting fleet utilization tracking and billing allocation.'
}

Table mobility.adas_feature_entitlement {
  adas_feature_entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the ADAS feature entitlement record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Link vehicle VIN to master connected_vehicle record; vehicle VIN is redundant after FK.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AD​AS feature revenue and profit are measured per profit center; needed for the ADAS Feature Profitability Dashboard.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the feature became active on the vehicle.']
  adas_feature_entitlement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the entitlement, conditions, or notes.']
  adas_feature_entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the entitlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the entitlement price. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|JPY|GBP|CNY|CAD — promote to reference product]']
  data_plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of data plan associated with the entitlement.']
  entitlement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the entitlement, used in contracts and billing.']
  entitlement_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged for the entitlement (if fee‑based).']
  entitlement_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the entitlement: factory‑installed, over‑the‑air activated, or subscription‑unlocked.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the entitlement expires or is scheduled to deactivate.']
  feature_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the ADAS feature in OEM catalogs.']
  feature_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ADAS or autonomous driving feature (e.g., Adaptive Cruise Control).']
  feature_version varchar [note: 'Version or release identifier of the ADAS feature.']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country or region code where the entitlement is valid (e.g., USA, CAN).']
  is_trial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entitlement is a trial (true) or a full entitlement (false).']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the status field last changed.']
  mileage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum vehicle mileage allowed while the entitlement remains valid.']
  odometer_at_activation varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the moment of activation.']
  ota_update_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates if the vehicle can receive OTA updates for this feature.']
  required_firmware_version varchar [note: 'Minimum vehicle firmware version required for the feature to operate.']
  subscription_plan varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subscription plan that provides the entitlement, if applicable.']
  trial_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Expiration timestamp for trial entitlements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the entitlement record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of feature activations or uses to date.']
  usage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the feature can be used or activated under the entitlement.']
  v2x_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether Vehicle‑to‑Everything communication is enabled for the feature.']

  Note: 'Master record defining which ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) and autonomous driving features are entitled and activated for a specific connected vehicle. Captures feature name (e.g., adaptive cruise control, lane keep assist, automated parking, Level 2+ autonomy package), entitlement source (factory-installed, OTA-activated, subscription-unlocked), activation date, expiry date, geographic restriction (ODD boundary reference), and current activation status. Supports feature-as-a-service monetization models.'
}

Table mobility.usage_record {
  usage_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the usage record.']
  billing_period_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing period to which this usage belongs.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Usage‑based billing entries are posted to a revenue GL account; essential for the Usage Billing Ledger and compliance reporting.']
  service_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the connected mobility service being consumed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the usage record was first captured in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the rating amount.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the usage line within the billing period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional information or comments about the usage.']
  rated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the usage has been rated for billing (true) or is pending (false).']
  rating_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount resulting from rating the usage, in the transaction currency.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the usage metric (kilometers, hours, megabytes, etc.).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the usage record.']
  usage_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the end of the usage interval.']
