  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to consolidate financial results across entities in the group.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center code associated with the intercompany group for internal cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the intercompany group record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, or notes about the intercompany group.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the intercompany group becomes effective for accounting purposes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the intercompany group ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from external systems (e.g., SAP, legacy ERP) linking to this group.']
  group_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the intercompany group in finance systems.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the intercompany group for reporting and analysis.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the nature of the intercompany grouping.']
  intercompany_accounting_rule varchar [note: 'Rule governing how intercompany transactions are accounted for within the group.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the intercompany group includes entities in multiple countries.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'True if the intercompany group is subject to corporate tax reporting.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the intercompany group was last reviewed for compliance or policy updates.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next review of the intercompany group.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or comments about the group.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center code linked to the intercompany group for profitability analysis.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region classification for the intercompany group.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for reporting the group\'s financials.']
  secondary_legal_entity_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of identifiers for additional legal entities participating in the group.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the intercompany group.']
  tax_regime varchar [note: 'Tax treatment applicable to the intercompany group.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the intercompany group record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for intercompany_group. Referenced by intercompany_group_id.'
}

Table finance.allocation_cycle {
  allocation_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for allocation_cycle']
  previous_allocation_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on allocation_cycle (previous_allocation_cycle_id)']
  actual_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount actually allocated in the cycle.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Primary driver used for cost allocation calculations.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs in this cycle.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default percentage applied when the allocation method is proportional.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that approved the allocation cycle.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation cycle was approved for use.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned monetary amount to be allocated during the cycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation cycle record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values; may reference a currency reference table. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|JPY|GBP|CNY|CHF|CAD|AUD|... — promote to reference product]']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the allocation cycle in financial systems.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the allocation cycle (e.g., "FY2025 Q1 Allocation").']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Category of the allocation cycle defining its periodicity.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or special conditions of the cycle.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the allocation cycle ends; null for open‑ended cycles.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the allocation cycle belongs.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cycle is the active one for ongoing allocations.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the period within the fiscal year (e.g., 1 for Q1).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the allocation cycle becomes effective.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the allocation cycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation cycle record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the allocation cycle definition for change management.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for allocation_cycle. Referenced by allocation_cycle_id.'
}

Table finance.intercompany_loan {
  intercompany_loan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for intercompany_loan']
  borrower_company_company_code_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subsidiary or entity receiving the loan.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subsidiary or entity providing the loan.']
  refinanced_intercompany_loan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on intercompany_loan (refinanced_intercompany_loan_id)']
  accounting_code varchar [note: 'Cost‑center or GL code used for accounting the loan.']
  accrued_interest decimal(18,2) [note: 'Interest that has accumulated but not yet been paid.']
  amortization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to amortize the principal over the loan term.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the loan was formally approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the finance officer who approved the loan.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'Nature of any security pledged for the loan.']
  collateral_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the pledged collateral at loan inception.']
  covenant_details varchar [note: 'Financial covenants or conditions attached to the loan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency used for the loan.']
  default_date date [note: 'Date on which the loan entered default status.']
  default_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the loan is in default (true) or not (false).']
  early_termination_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged if the loan is repaid before the scheduled maturity.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the loan agreement becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Maturity date when the loan is scheduled to be fully repaid; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  exchange_rate_at_inception decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency conversion rate applied when the loan was originated, if currencies differ.']
  guarantee_type varchar [note: 'Type of guarantee backing the loan.']
  interest_accrual_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate accrued interest.']
  interest_cap decimal(5,4) [note: 'Maximum interest rate applicable if the loan has a variable component.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate applied to the outstanding principal, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0750 for 7.5%).']
  interest_rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the interest rate is fixed for the term or varies with a benchmark.']
  internal_audit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of internal audit review for the loan.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if lender and borrower are in different legal jurisdictions.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent payment was received.']
  legal_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the legal contract governing the loan.']
  loan_agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the loan agreement by the finance department.']
  loan_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the loan relationship.']
  loan_purpose varchar [note: 'Business reason for the intercompany loan (e.g., working capital, asset acquisition).']
  loan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the loan.']
  loan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the intercompany loan indicating its structure.']
  next_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the upcoming scheduled payment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the loan.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard payment amount per repayment period.']
  principal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original amount of money lent, before interest and fees.']
  principal_outstanding decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining principal balance that has not yet been repaid.']
  repayment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often scheduled payments are due.']
  restructuring_date date [note: 'Date when the loan restructuring was executed.']
  restructuring_flag boolean [note: 'True if the loan terms have been restructured.']
  tax_implication varchar [note: 'Indicates whether interest or fees are subject to tax.']
  term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the loan in months.']
  total_outstanding decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of principal outstanding and accrued interest.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the loan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for intercompany_loan. Referenced by related_loan_id.'
}

Table finance.legal_entity {
  legal_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for legal_entity']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the entity\'s headquarters.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the incorporation address.']
  country_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the entity is legally incorporated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the legal entity record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for financial reporting.']
  data_privacy_classification varchar [note: 'Internal classification indicating the sensitivity level of the entity\'s data.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the entity\'s business activities.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the entity became legally active.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official communications.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity\'s legal structure.']
  fiscal_year_end_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) indicating the end of the entity\'s fiscal year.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code describing the entity\'s primary business activity.']
  is_public_company boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity is publicly listed (True) or privately held (False).']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The full registered legal name of the entity.']
  market_capitalization decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total market value of the entity\'s outstanding shares, expressed in reporting currency.']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total headcount employed by the entity.']
  parent_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent entity in the corporate hierarchy.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the entity.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the headquarters address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main point‑of‑contact for the entity.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number assigned by the jurisdiction of incorporation.']
  sox_control_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the individual responsible for SOX compliance controls for this entity.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the incorporation address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the entity.']
  status_change_date date [note: 'Date when the entity\'s status last changed.']
  tax_id varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the entity.']
  tax_residency_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3‑letter country code indicating the entity\'s tax residency.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the entity ceased to exist or was dissolved.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Business name under which the entity operates publicly (Doing Business As).']
  ultimate_parent_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the top‑most parent entity in the corporate structure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the legal entity record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the entity.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for legal_entity. Referenced by primary_legal_entity_id.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each employee record.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: HR tracks which dealership each employee works at for payroll, compliance, and regional performance reporting.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary plant or office where the employee works.']
  job_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_classification. Business justification: Standardize job classification reference; employee now references job_classification via employee.job_classification_id, removing redundant string code.']
  labor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_agreement. Business justification: Capture the specific collective bargaining agreement applicable to each employee for compliance and payroll calculations.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the direct manager or supervisor.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary plant or office where the employee works.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the employee\'s residential address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the employee\'s residential address (optional).']
  benefits_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment state for employee benefit programs.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any mandatory certifications (e.g., safety, quality).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the employee\'s residential address.']
  compliance_training_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the employee completed required compliance training.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the employee\'s country of residence.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the salary amount.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the employee, used for age verification and benefits eligibility.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department to which the employee belongs.']
  disability_accommodation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee has approved disability accommodations.']
  eeo_category varchar [note: 'Equal Employment Opportunity classification for reporting and compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protected_veteran|disability|gender|race|ethnicity|age|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary work email address for the employee.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact in case of an emergency.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee within the organization.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment relationship.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the employee.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the employee as used for official records.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee officially started employment with Automotive.']
  iatf_competency_status varchar [note: 'Employee\'s competency status required by IATF 16949 quality standards.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the employee\'s position.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the employee.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance evaluation.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued personal identifier (e.g., SSN, SIN) for the employee.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible for overtime compensation.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation band assigned to the employee.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating from the latest performance review.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the employee.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the employee\'s residential address.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee record.']
  retirement_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the employee\'s selected retirement savings plan.']
  salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual base salary for the employee, before bonuses or allowances.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the employee\'s residential address.']
  tax_withholding_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the employee\'s tax withholding classification.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended, if applicable.']
  training_completion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required training courses have been completed.']
  union_member_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a member of a labor union.']
  veteran_status varchar [note: 'Veteran classification for EEO reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record for every individual employed by Automotive, sourced from SAP SuccessFactors. Captures employee identity, personal details, employment type (full-time, part-time, contract, CKD-site), hire date, termination date, employment status, home plant or office assignment, job classification, pay grade, union membership flag, IATF competency status, and EEO category. Serves as the SSOT for workforce identity across all HR sub-domains.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the authorized headcount position.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department or functional area that owns the position.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for hiring into this position.']
  job_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_classification. Business justification: Standardize position classification by linking each position to its job classification; enables consistent reporting and removes need to store job_family and level redundantly.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the top‑level organization (e.g., corporate entity) to which the position belongs.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or facility where the position is located.']
  position_department_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department or functional area that owns the position.']
  position_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for hiring into this position.']
  position_filled_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee currently occupying the position, if any.']
  position_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the top‑level organization (e.g., corporate entity) to which the position belongs.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the position is eligible for performance‑based bonuses.']
  budget decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated for total compensation and related costs.']
  compensation_grade varchar [note: 'Internal grade used for compensation benchmarking.']
  compliance_iatf_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the role must meet IATF 16949 competency requirements.']
  compliance_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mandatory compliance training has been completed for the role.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created in the system.']
  criticality varchar [note: 'Business criticality rating indicating impact of vacancy on operations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the position is scheduled to be retired or become inactive (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the position becomes active in the organization.']
  flsa_status varchar [note: 'Fair Labor Standards Act classification indicating exempt or non‑exempt status.']
  is_filled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is currently occupied by an employee.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad classification grouping similar roles (e.g., Engineering, Manufacturing, Administration).']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the role within the job family (e.g., L1, L2, L3).']
  last_filled_date date [note: 'Date when the position was most recently occupied.']
  location varchar [note: 'Standard location code (e.g., plant‑area‑line) where the role is based.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if overtime compensation applies to this position.']
  position_category varchar [note: 'Business domain to which the position belongs.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the position within the organization.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the role, duties, and expectations.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the employment relationship for the position.']
  remote_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the position can be performed remotely.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of certifications mandatory for incumbents (e.g., OSHA, IATF).']
  required_skills varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of core skills needed to perform the role.']
  responsibilities varchar [note: 'Detailed list of primary responsibilities and accountabilities.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on safety, compliance, or operational impact.']
  salary_range_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum annual salary for the position.']
  salary_range_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum annual salary for the position.']
  shift_eligibility varchar [note: 'Shift patterns for which the position is eligible.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status, such as reason for vacancy or closure.']
  target_headcount varchar [note: 'Budgeted number of full‑time equivalents (FTE) allocated to this position.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the position as used in job postings and internal records.']
  travel_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role requires regular travel.']
  union_classification_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the union or collective bargaining classification applicable to the position.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the position record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the position record.']
  vacancy_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the position is currently vacant.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the position record.']

  Note: 'Authorized headcount position within the organizational structure, representing a budgeted role independent of the person filling it. Captures position title, job family, job level, plant or department assignment, shift eligibility, union classification code, FLSA status, target headcount, and whether the position is currently filled or vacant. Supports workforce planning and headcount reporting.'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the organizational unit.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each org unit is assigned to a cost center for budgeting; cost_center_id links the two.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Needed for associating each org unit/plant with its regulatory jurisdiction for environmental permit reporting and audit compliance.']
  labor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_agreement. Business justification: Org unit should reference the governing labor agreement entity; replace string code with FK.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the unit\'s manager or head.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent unit in the hierarchy.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the unit.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated to the unit (currency defined by currency_code).']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the unit is located.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance posture of the unit with applicable automotive regulations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the unit\'s location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|DEU|FRA|GBR|JPN|CHN|KOR|IND — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the organizational unit record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which monetary values for the unit are expressed.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the higher‑level division to which the unit belongs.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the unit ceased operation (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the unit became operational.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email for the unit.']
  full_time_equivalent decimal(10,2) [note: 'Aggregated FTE value for the unit, reflecting part‑time staffing.']
  headcount varchar [note: 'Number of employees assigned to the unit (full‑time equivalents counted as 1).']
  is_unionized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit\'s workforce is covered by a labor union.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal or external audit of the unit.']
  org_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used in ERP and reporting (e.g., "PLNT001").']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the unit\'s purpose or responsibilities.']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the organizational unit (e.g., "Engine Plant 1").']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the unit.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the unit within the enterprise hierarchy.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the unit.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant (if type=plant).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the unit\'s address.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region where the unit is located.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of safety certifications required for the unit.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the unit record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Organizational unit hierarchy node representing departments, cost centers, plants, divisions, and business units within Automotive. Captures org unit name, type (plant, department, division), parent org unit, cost center code, plant code, region, and effective dates. Enables hierarchical rollup of headcount, labor cost, and performance data across the enterprise.'
}

Table workforce.job_classification {
  job_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each job classification record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the job classification record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the job classification is retired or superseded (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the job classification becomes active for use in workforce planning.']
  flsa_exempt_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the job for overtime eligibility under the Fair Labor Standards Act.']
  iatf_competency_required varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the job classification must meet IATF 16949 competency standards.']
  is_managerial boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the job classification includes managerial responsibilities.']
  job_classification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job classification.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to reference the job classification across systems.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of duties, responsibilities, and scope for the job classification.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad functional family to which the job belongs (e.g., Production, Engineering).']
  job_level_band varchar [note: 'Standardized level band indicating seniority and responsibility (e.g., L1 = entry, L5 = senior).']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the job classification (e.g., "Assembly Line Technician").']
  pay_grade_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the salary range associated with the job classification.']
  pay_grade_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the salary range associated with the job classification.']
  requires_certification varchar [note: 'Indicates if the job classification requires a specific professional certification.']
  union_trade_classification varchar [note: 'Union‑defined trade category for the job (used for labor agreements).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the job classification record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who initially created the job classification record.']

  Note: 'Reference master for standardized job classifications used across Automotive\'s workforce. Captures job code, job title, job family, job level band, IATF competency requirements, union trade classification, FLSA exemption status, and applicable pay grade range. Used to standardize position definitions and link to compensation structures and competency requirements.'
}

Table workforce.employment_contract {
  employment_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the employment contract record.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the employee.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the direct manager or supervisor.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the employee.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job position held under the contract.']
  primary_employment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom the contract applies.']
  amendment_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the latest amendment takes effect.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the most recent amendment.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent contract amendment.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contract was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR or manager who approved the contract.']
  benefits_description varchar [note: 'Summary of benefits (health, retirement, etc.) provided under the contract.']
  collective_bargaining_agreement varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CBA governing the contract terms.']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'Structure of the compensation package.']
  compliance_training_completed boolean [note: 'Flag showing if the employee completed mandatory compliance training before contract start.']
  confidentiality_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a confidentiality clause is present in the contract.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract reference number assigned by HR for tracking and legal purposes.']
  contract_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status (e.g., probation, on‑hold).']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of employment contract indicating the nature of the employment relationship.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the contract document after amendments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  data_protection_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a data‑protection clause (e.g., GDPR) is included.']
  employment_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the employment contract terminates or expires; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  is_apprenticeship boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the contract is for an apprenticeship program.']
  is_expatriate boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the employee is an expatriate (e.g., CKD expatriate).']
  is_fixed_term boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the contract has a predefined end date.']
  is_permanent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the contract is for a permanent employee.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the contract record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification.']
  non_compete_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non‑compete clause is included.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required for notice of termination by either party.']
  probation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the probation period ends, if applicable.']
  probation_period_days varchar [note: 'Length of the probation period in days.']
  salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base monetary compensation agreed in the contract.']
  salary_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the salary amount.']
  salary_frequency varchar [note: 'How often salary is paid to the employee.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the employment contract becomes effective.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for contract termination (e.g., resignation, layoff).']
  union_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier for the applicable union agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  weekly_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the employee is contracted to work each week.']
  work_location varchar [note: 'Primary site or plant where the employee will perform duties.']
  work_shift varchar [note: 'Designated shift schedule for the employee.']

  Note: 'Formal employment contract record for each employee, capturing contract type (permanent, fixed-term, apprenticeship, CKD expatriate), contract start and end dates, probation period, notice period, governing collective bargaining agreement (CBA) reference, work location, contracted hours per week, and contract status. Tracks the legal employment relationship between Automotive and the employee.'
}

Table workforce.shift_schedule {
  shift_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shift schedule record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee assigned to the shift.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the shift occurs.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line associated with the shift.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or station within the line.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned break duration within the shift, expressed in minutes.']
  break_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a break is scheduled for the shift.']
  certification_required varchar [note: 'Specific certification needed for the shift (e.g., forklift, safety).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift schedule record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the shift schedule ends; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the shift schedule becomes effective.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the shift schedule.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee may accrue overtime during this shift.']
  overtime_rate_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the base pay rate for overtime hours.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the shift schedule, used for reference in planning and reporting.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the shift schedule.']
  schedule_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the schedule record.']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the schedule record for change tracking.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of work hours scheduled for the shift.']
  shift_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the shift pay rate.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the shift ends.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Pattern defining the time of day for the shift.']
  shift_pay_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base hourly pay rate applicable to the shift (currency defined by shift_currency).']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the shift starts.']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift schedule.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift (e.g., regular, overtime, holiday).']
  skill_required varchar [note: 'Skill or competency required for the shift (e.g., welding, assembly).']
  union_flag boolean [note: 'True if the shift is covered by a union labor agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shift schedule record.']

  Note: 'Planned shift schedule assignment for production and non-production employees at each plant. Captures employee assignment to a shift pattern (day, afternoon, night, rotating), effective date range, plant, production line, work center, scheduled hours, and overtime eligibility. Integrates with manufacturing.shift reference data and MES shop floor scheduling to ensure adequate staffing for SOP targets.'
}

Table workforce.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the time entry record.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organizational department responsible for the work.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who logged the time.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organizational department responsible for the work.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pay period to which this entry belongs.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing work order linked to the labor entry.']
  absence_type varchar [note: 'Category of absence if the entry represents time off.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the time entry.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the employee clocked in.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the employee clocked out.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True when the employee meets required competency records for the task.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Accounting cost center to which labor cost is allocated.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the time entry record.']
  is_overtime_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the overtime portion has been approved.']
  is_shift_differential boolean [note: 'True if shift‑differential premium applies to this entry.']
  job_role varchar [note: 'Specific role or title of the employee for this entry (e.g., assembler, technician).']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the work performed.']
  labor_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or level of the employee\'s labor classification (e.g., G1, G2).']
  labor_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly labor rate applicable to the employee for this entry.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional remarks or explanations.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours logged for the entry.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(3,2) [note: 'Factor applied to regular rate to calculate overtime pay (e.g., 1.5).']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Code of the internal project or initiative to which labor is charged.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the time entry record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the time entry record.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of regular (non‑overtime) hours worked in the entry.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the employee\'s safety certification.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the scheduled shift (e.g., A, B, C).']
  shift_differential_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked that qualify for a shift‑differential premium.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin that supplied the time entry data.']
  time_entry_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the entry creation request.']
  time_entry_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the time was recorded.']
  training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required training for the job role has been completed.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the union representing the employee, if applicable.']
  work_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the work was performed.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of actual hours worked, absence, and overtime for each employee per pay period. Captures clock-in/clock-out timestamps, regular hours, overtime hours, shift differential hours, absence type, approval status, and cost center allocation. Source of truth for payroll calculation and labor cost reporting. Integrates with SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking and SAP S/4HANA CO for labor cost posting.'
}

Table workforce.absence_record {
  absence_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the absence record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or HR representative who approved/rejected the request.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or HR representative who approved/rejected the request.']
  primary_absence_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee to whom the absence applies.']
  absence_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the absence is paid or unpaid.']
  absence_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number for the absence transaction.']
  absence_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the absence request.']
  absence_type varchar [note: 'Category of the absence (e.g., vacation, sick, FMLA, parental, union, bereavement, other). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vacation|sick|fmla|parental|union|bereavement|other — promote to reference product]']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the absence was approved or rejected.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the absence record was first created in the system.']
  duration_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the absence expressed in working days.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the absence expressed in hours.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the absence period.']
  medical_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the attached medical certificate document.']
  medical_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a medical certificate was provided for the absence.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments entered by the employee or approver.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the reason for the absence.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the absence request was submitted.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the employee to return to work after the absence.']
  shift_coverage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temporary coverage is required for the affected shifts.']
  shift_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the absence impacts scheduled production shifts.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the absence period.']
  union_leave_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the absence is covered under a union agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the absence record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing planned and unplanned employee absences including vacation, sick leave, FMLA, parental leave, union leave, and bereavement. Captures absence type, start date, end date, duration in hours/days, approval status, medical certification flag, and return-to-work date. Supports absenteeism tracking, IATF compliance, and production staffing gap analysis.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_result {
  payroll_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payroll result record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom this payroll result applies.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work location or plant where the employee is assigned.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Employee\'s bank account number for direct deposit.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number associated with the employee\'s direct deposit account.']
  base_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Regular salary component for the pay period.']
  benefit_deduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employee contributions to benefits (health, retirement, etc.).']
  bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Any discretionary or performance‑based bonus paid in the period.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center associated with the employee\'s labor cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll result record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department to which the employee belongs.']
  employee_ssn varchar [note: 'Government‑issued identifier used for tax reporting.']
  employee_type varchar [note: 'Employment relationship type of the worker.']
  employer_contribution decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employer‑paid amounts such as pension or insurance contributions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Quarter of the fiscal year for reporting purposes.']
  garnishment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Court‑ordered wage garnishments deducted from pay.']
  gross_pay decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total earnings before any deductions for the pay period.']
  is_garnishment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a garnishment was applied to this payroll.']
  is_union_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a union member.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Job classification or role code for the employee.']
  net_pay decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount paid to the employee after all deductions and contributions.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked in the pay period.']
  overtime_pay decimal(15,2) [note: 'Compensation for overtime hours worked during the pay period.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Hourly or salary rate applied to calculate base earnings.']
  pay_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pay rate (hourly, salary, commission).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver net pay to the employee.']
  payroll_group varchar [note: 'Frequency group defining the payroll cycle for the employee.']
  payroll_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the payroll transaction for reporting and audit.']
  payroll_result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll record.']
  payroll_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the payroll was processed and posted.']
  shift_differential decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional pay for working non‑standard shifts (e.g., night, weekend).']
  shift_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total regular shift hours worked in the pay period.']
  tax_deduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Statutory tax withholdings deducted from gross pay.']
  tax_withholding_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the employee\'s tax withholding status.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the payroll tax withholdings apply.']
  union_dues decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dues deducted for union membership, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payroll result record.']

  Note: 'Payroll processing result record for each employee per pay period, capturing gross pay, net pay, base salary, overtime pay, shift differentials, bonuses, deductions (tax, benefits, union dues, garnishments), employer contributions, and pay period dates. Sourced from SAP SuccessFactors Payroll and SAP S/4HANA FI payroll posting. Serves as the authoritative payroll ledger for workforce cost reporting.'
}

Table workforce.compensation_plan {
  compensation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the compensation plan record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the compensation plan.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the compensation plan.']
  job_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_classification. Business justification: Link compensation plan to job classification for consistent classification reference; remove redundant code column.']
  primary_compensation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual employee to whom the plan applies (if plan is employee‑specific).']
  base_salary_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum annual base salary amount defined by the plan.']
  base_salary_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum annual base salary amount defined by the plan.']
  bonus_multiplier decimal(5,3) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the bonus target based on performance rating.']
  bonus_plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of bonus structure (e.g., annual performance, quarterly incentive).']
  bonus_target_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target monetary amount for bonus eligibility.']
  classification varchar [note: 'High‑level classification used for reporting and analytics.']
  compensation_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks related to regulatory or union compliance.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the plan is applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compensation plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in the plan.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the compensation plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the compensation plan becomes effective.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Textual description of the criteria employees must meet to be eligible for the plan.']
  eligibility_end_date date [note: 'Date when eligibility for the plan ends, if applicable.']
  eligibility_start_date date [note: 'Date when an employee becomes eligible for the plan.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compensation review for the plan.']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (subsidiary, division) that owns the plan.']
  merit_increase_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard merit increase percentage applied annually under the plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the plan.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(5,3) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base hourly rate for overtime hours.']
  pay_equity_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the plan has been reviewed for pay equity compliance.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Internal pay‑grade identifier associated with the plan.']
  payroll_frequency varchar [note: 'How often payroll is processed for this plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code for the compensation plan, used in HR and payroll systems.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the compensation plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plan indicating the primary compensation component.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of formal compensation review (e.g., annual, biennial).']
  shift_differential_rate decimal(5,3) [note: 'Additional hourly rate applied for non‑standard shifts (e.g., night, weekend).']
  tax_withholding_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statutory tax withholding percentage applied to compensation.']
  union_cba_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan complies with applicable union collective bargaining agreements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compensation plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the plan for change management.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the compensation structure for a job classification or individual employee, including base salary range, merit increase eligibility, bonus plan type, shift differential rates, overtime multipliers, and effective date. Supports annual compensation review cycles, union CBA compliance, and pay equity analysis across Automotive\'s global manufacturing workforce.'
}

Table workforce.benefit_enrollment {

  employer_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount the employer contributes per pay period.']

  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the enrollment request.']

  is_auto_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee was auto‑enrolled.']

  payroll_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of payroll deductions for the benefit.']
  payroll_deduction_code varchar [note: 'Code used in payroll system to map the deduction.']
  plan_annual_maximum decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total benefit amount payable in a calendar year.']
  plan_change_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee may change the plan during the year.']
  plan_change_deadline date [note: 'Last date to request a plan change.']
  plan_cost_share_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the employee\'s cost‑share per period.']
  plan_cost_share_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the premium cost shared by the employee.']
  plan_enrollment_window_end date [note: 'End date of the open enrollment period for the plan.']
  plan_enrollment_window_start date [note: 'Start date of the open enrollment period for the plan.']
  plan_group_number varchar [note: 'Group number assigned to the plan for reporting.']
  plan_renewal_date date [note: 'Date the plan is scheduled for renewal.']
  plan_renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the plan renewal.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Tax treatment of the benefit (pre‑tax or post‑tax).']
  plan_vendor varchar [note: 'External provider of the benefit plan.']
  plan_wait_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after eligibility before benefits become payable.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the benefit coverage ends, if applicable.']
  total_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Combined employee and employer premium for the coverage period.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for waiving the benefit.']

  Note: 'Employee benefit enrollment record capturing active enrollments in health insurance, dental, vision, life insurance, disability, 401(k)/pension, employee stock purchase plan (ESPP), and wellness programs. Tracks plan type, coverage tier, enrollment date, effective date, employee contribution amount, employer contribution amount, and enrollment status. Sourced from SuccessFactors Benefits.'
}

Table workforce.talent_requisition {

  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department requesting the hire.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who owns the requisition.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department requesting the hire.']
  primary_talent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who owns the requisition.']
  tertiary_talent_filled_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee hired for the position.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the requisition\'s approval workflow.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the requisition was approved.']
  budgeted_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum salary budget allocated for the position.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date after which the requisition will no longer accept applications.']
  compensation_grade varchar [note: 'Internal compensation grade assigned to the role.']
  competency_requirements varchar [note: 'Key competencies and skills needed for successful performance.']
  compliance_iatf_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the position must meet IATF 16949 competency requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the requisition was initially created in the system.']
  filled_date date [note: 'Date the position was filled.']
  headcount_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of individual positions requested in this requisition.']
  headcount_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition creates new headcount or replaces an existing role.']
  interview_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the interview process.']
  is_internal_requisition boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the role is for an internal transfer or promotion.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to classify the job family or role.']
  language_requirements varchar [note: 'Languages and proficiency levels needed for the position.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments added by recruiters or managers.']
  position_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the job vacancy.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the requisition was posted to internal/external job boards.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the requisition.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of when the record was first captured for audit purposes.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record for audit purposes.']
  recruitment_budget decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for recruitment activities (advertising, agency fees, etc.).']
  relocation_assistance boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if relocation support is offered.']
  remote_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role can be performed remotely.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of mandatory professional certifications.']
  required_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum education qualification required for the role.']
  required_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum years of relevant experience required.']
  required_skills varchar [note: 'List of technical and soft skills required for the role.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the requisition.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the requisition.']
  salary_range_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the salary range offered.']
  salary_range_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the salary range offered.']
  sourcing_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which candidates are sourced.']
  target_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for the new employee to begin work.']
  travel_requirement varchar [note: 'Extent of travel expected for the role.']

  Note: 'Recruitment requisition record initiated when a position vacancy is approved for hiring. Captures requisition number, position, job classification, hiring manager, target start date, requisition status (open, in-progress, filled, cancelled), sourcing channel, headcount type (replacement, new headcount), and approval workflow status. Integrates with SuccessFactors Recruiting Management.'
}

Table workforce.job_application {
  job_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job application record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the hiring decision.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the job position the candidate applied for.']

  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score from any pre-employment assessments or tests.']
  background_check_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when background check was completed.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the candidate\'s background verification.']
  candidate_external_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external recruiting agencies or job boards.']
  candidate_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the candidate record.']

  current_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the candidate in the hiring funnel.']
  disability_accommodation_needed boolean [note: 'Indicates if candidate requires workplace accommodations.']
  disposition_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for final disposition of the application (e.g., not qualified, accepted).']
  education_level varchar [note: 'Highest education attainment of the candidate.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for candidate communication.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Nature of employment for the position.']
  interview_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated numeric score from interview evaluations.']
  last_stage_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the application last moved to a new stage.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the most recent update.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by recruiters or hiring managers.']
  offer_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when candidate accepted the job offer.']
  offer_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the offered salary.']
  offer_extended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a job offer has been extended to the candidate.']
  offer_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Salary amount offered to the candidate.']
  onboarding_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the new hire onboarding.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Contact telephone number of the candidate.']
  resume_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the stored resume document.']
  salary_expectation decimal(12,2) [note: 'Candidate\'s expected salary.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the candidate applied.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the application record.']
  visa_status varchar [note: 'Immigration status of the candidate relevant for employment eligibility.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total years of relevant professional experience reported by candidate.']

  Note: 'Candidate application record linked to a talent requisition. Captures applicant name, source channel (internal, external, referral, campus), application date, current stage in the hiring funnel (screening, interview, offer, hired, rejected), interview scores, background check status, offer details, and disposition reason. Supports time-to-fill and source effectiveness reporting.'
}
