  innovation_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Rating of the employee’s contribution to innovative ideas and improvements.']
  leadership_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Rating of the employee’s leadership competencies.']
  manager_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments entered by the manager during the review.']
  merit_increase_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage increase in base salary tied to the review outcome.']
  overall_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Aggregated overall performance rating on the organization’s rating scale.']
  promotion_recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommendation on whether the employee should be considered for promotion.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'The periodicity of the review (e.g., annual, mid-year, quarterly).']
  review_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review meeting took place or was recorded.']
  review_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the review period.']
  review_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the review was conducted.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier for the review, often used in HR reporting and audit trails.']
  review_period_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter of the review period.']
  review_period_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the review belongs.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the review period.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the performance review.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Methodology of the review: self-assessment, manager assessment, or 360-degree feedback.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the reviewer for reporting purposes.']
  teamwork_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Rating of the employee’s collaboration and teamwork abilities.']
  technical_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Rating of the employee’s technical or functional expertise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the performance review record.']

  Note: 'Annual or mid-year performance review record for an employee, capturing review cycle, overall rating, goal achievement score, competency ratings, manager comments, calibration status, and final approved rating. Supports merit increase decisions, succession planning, and IATF-required competency evaluation documentation. Sourced from SuccessFactors Performance & Goals.'
}

Table workforce.training_record {
  training_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the training record.']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the training course.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trainer or instructor.']
  primary_training_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the employee who took the training.']
  business_identifier varchar [note: 'External reference number for the training record (e.g., LMS transaction ID).']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the certification expires, if applicable.']
  certification_issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was issued.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Official certification number issued upon successful completion.']
  competency_category varchar [note: 'High-level competency area the training addresses.']
  competency_code varchar [note: 'Standard code for the competency (e.g., IATF competency code).']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date the employee completed the training.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the training with regulatory requirements.']
  course_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the training course.']
  course_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the training course.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Accredited credit hours awarded for the training.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the training.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the employee was enrolled in the training.']
  iatf_competency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the training satisfies an IATF competency requirement.']
  max_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum possible score for the course.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes related to the training record.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Result of the training assessment.']
  quantitative_result decimal(7,2) [note: 'Primary measured outcome of the training (e.g., score percentage).']
  recertification_due_date date [note: 'Due date for required recertification.']
  recertification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if periodic recertification is required.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training record was created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the training record.']
  safety_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the training provides a safety certification.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved by the employee.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the training data originated (e.g., SuccessFactors, LMS).']
  trainer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the trainer or instructor.']
  training_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the actual training event (e.g., when the session occurred).']
  training_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total hours of training delivered.']
  training_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where training was delivered.']
  training_provider varchar [note: 'Organization or vendor delivering the training.']
  training_record_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the training record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of employee training completions and enrollments, capturing course name, course type (IATF competency, safety certification, EV/ADAS technical, leadership, compliance), delivery method (classroom, e-learning, OJT), completion date, pass/fail status, score, certification expiry date, and training provider. Critical for IATF 16949 competency compliance and OSHA safety certification tracking.'
}

Table workforce.competency {
  competency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each competency record.']
  applicable_job_family varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of job families or roles for which the competency is relevant. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|manufacturing|quality|sales|service|management|supply_chain|R&D|finance|HR] — promote to reference product']
  competency_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of competencies (e.g., Technical Skills, Leadership Skills).']
  competency_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the competency across systems.']
  competency_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the competency, including scope and expectations.']
  competency_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the competency.']
  competency_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the competency.']
  competency_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the nature of the competency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the competency record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the competency becomes effective for use.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the competency is retired or no longer valid; null if indefinite.']
  iatf_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the competency is mandated by IATF 16949 standards.']
  is_core_competency boolean [note: 'True if the competency is considered core to the organization’s strategic capabilities.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the competency was last reviewed for relevance or revision.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role responsible for maintaining the competency definition.']
  proficiency_levels varchar [note: 'Standardized proficiency scale associated with the competency.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the competency data (e.g., SuccessFactors, HRIS).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the competency record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the competency definition.']
  weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weight assigned to the competency for scoring purposes in assessments.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the standardized competency framework used across Automotive\'s workforce. Captures competency name, competency type (technical, behavioral, leadership, safety, IATF-required), proficiency levels, applicable job families, and regulatory requirement flag. Provides the taxonomy for IATF 16949 competency records, performance reviews, and skills gap analysis.'
}

Table workforce.skills_inventory {
  skills_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the skill inventory record.']
  competency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.competency. Business justification: Skills inventory should reference the canonical competency master; remove duplicated descriptive columns.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who performed the assessment.']
  primary_skills_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee to whom the skill record belongs.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the skill proficiency was assessed.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the skill certification expires.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification state of the skill.']
  iatf_competency_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the skill is required per IATF 16949 competency standards.']
  is_core_skill boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the skill is a core competency.']
  is_critical_skill boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the skill is critical for operations.']
  last_assessed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent skill assessment.']
  last_training_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent training session for the skill.']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Assessed proficiency tier for the skill.']
  proficiency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric rating of skill proficiency on a 0-5 scale.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the skill record was created.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the skill record.']
  skill_assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assess the skill.']
  skill_certification_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the skill certification.']
  skill_certification_issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was issued.']
  skill_certification_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certification record.']
  skill_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the skill.']
  skill_last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent skill review.']
  skill_last_used_date date [note: 'Date when the skill was last applied on the shop floor.']
  skill_last_used_location varchar [note: 'Plant or location where the skill was last utilized.']
  skill_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes about the skill.']
  skill_owner_department varchar [note: 'Department responsible for the skill.']
  skill_review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between skill reviews.']
  skill_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the skill information.']
  skill_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the skill record.']
  skill_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when training was completed.']
  skill_training_completion_status varchar [note: 'Status of training completion.']
  skill_training_program_code varchar [note: 'Code of the training program.']
  skill_training_provider varchar [note: 'Entity providing the training for the skill.']
  skill_validity_end_date date [note: 'Date until which the skill is considered valid.']
  skill_validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the skill is considered valid.']
  skill_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the skill definition.']
  training_hours_completed varchar [note: 'Total hours of training completed for the skill.']

  Note: 'Employee skills profile record capturing assessed proficiency levels for each competency or technical skill. Tracks skill name, skill category (welding, robotics, EV battery assembly, ADAS calibration, quality inspection, forklift operation), assessed proficiency level, assessment date, assessor, and certification status. Enables skills gap analysis, cross-training planning, and JIT workforce deployment on the shop floor.'
}

Table workforce.succession_plan {
  succession_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the succession plan record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the person responsible for the plan.']
  primary_succession_incumbent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the current incumbent.']
  target_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the key position for which succession is planned.']
  tertiary_succession_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the person responsible for the plan.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the succession plan was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the succession plan.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the plan.']
  competency_gap_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score quantifying competency gaps between incumbent and successor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the succession plan record was created.']
  development_actions varchar [note: 'Planned development activities to prepare the successor.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the succession plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the succession plan expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the target position is a critical role for business continuity.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent assessment of the succession plan.']
  next_assessment_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next assessment of the succession plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the succession plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the succession plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the succession plan.']
  plan_review_date date [note: 'Date when the succession plan is scheduled for review.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the succession plan (e.g., leadership, technical).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority of the succession plan.']
  readiness_level varchar [note: 'Readiness assessment of the successor (e.g., ready now, 1-2 years, 3-5 years).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the succession plan.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the succession plan data (e.g., SuccessFactors).']
  succession_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  succession_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking to prioritize succession actions.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the succession plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number for change tracking of the plan.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the record.']

  Note: 'Succession planning record identifying key positions and their designated successors within Automotive\'s leadership and critical technical roles. Captures target position, incumbent employee, successor employee, readiness level (ready now, 1-2 years, 3-5 years), development actions, and plan review date. Supports talent pipeline management and business continuity for plant leadership and engineering roles.'
}

Table workforce.labor_agreement {
  labor_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the labor agreement record.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned to the labor agreement.']
  agreement_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the labor agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the labor agreement.']
  amendment_history varchar [note: 'Free-text log of amendments made to the agreement over time.']
  benefit_coverage_description varchar [note: 'Summary of health, retirement, and other benefits provided.']
  covered_department_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of department codes covered by the agreement.']
  covered_plant_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of plant codes where the agreement applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the agreement expires or ends.']
  grievance_procedure varchar [note: 'Process for handling employee grievances under the agreement.']
  is_collective_bargaining boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the agreement is a collective bargaining agreement.']
  is_unionized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement applies to unionized employees.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction governing the agreement.']
  labor_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments regarding the agreement.']
  overtime_rule_description varchar [note: 'Details of overtime eligibility and rates.']
  payroll_rule_configuration varchar [note: 'Configuration details for payroll processing derived from the agreement.']
  ratification_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement was ratified by both parties.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Status of compliance with labor regulations and standards.']
  seniority_rule_description varchar [note: 'Rules governing seniority for promotions, layoffs, etc.']
  union_local_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific local chapter of the union.']
  union_name varchar [note: 'Name of the labor union party to the agreement.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the agreement record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']
  wage_scale_description varchar [note: 'Description of wage scales and grades defined in the agreement.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Master record for collective bargaining agreements (CBAs) and union labor contracts governing Automotive\'s unionized workforce. Captures union name, agreement effective date, expiration date, covered plant(s), wage scale provisions, overtime rules, grievance procedures, seniority rules, and ratification status. Serves as the authoritative reference for union labor compliance and payroll rule configuration.'
}

Table workforce.grievance {
  grievance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each grievance record.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department or cost center where the employee works.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the union representative assigned to the grievance.']
  grievance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who submitted the grievance.']
  grievance_union_steward_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the union representative assigned to the grievance.']
  labor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_agreement. Business justification: Grievance should reference the labor agreement entity for proper contract linkage; replace string code with FK.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department or cost center where the employee works.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by auditors during compliance reviews.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the grievance handling complies with the applicable CBA provisions.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'True when the grievance must be handled confidentially under the collective bargaining agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the grievance record was first created in the data lake.']
  deadline_date date [note: 'Date by which the current step must be completed.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the settlement or corrective action becomes effective.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating how many times the grievance has been escalated.']
  filing_date timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the grievance was entered into the system.']
  grievance_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance concerns an individual employee, a group of employees, or the union as a whole.']
  grievance_description varchar [note: 'Full text description provided by the employee outlining the grievance.']
  grievance_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable grievance number assigned at filing, used for external reference and reporting.']
  grievance_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the grievance filing, indicating who initiated it.']
  grievance_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the grievance within the grievance procedure.']
  grievance_type varchar [note: 'Category of the grievance based on the underlying issue.']
  iatf_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True when the grievance process meets IATF 16949 competency requirements.']
  last_action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the latest workflow action taken on the grievance.']
  location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site associated with the grievance.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Resulting action taken after grievance resolution.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level used for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the grievance was formally resolved.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance has been settled, remains open, or was withdrawn.']
  settlement_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial amount paid or adjusted as part of the grievance settlement.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code (e.g., USD, EUR) for the settlement amount.']
  settlement_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative details describing the terms of the settlement.']
  step_in_process varchar [note: 'Specific stage of the grievance workflow the record is currently in.']
  supporting_documents_flag boolean [note: 'True if the grievance includes attached evidence or documents.']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the grievance should be resolved.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the grievance record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who entered the grievance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for formal employee or union grievances filed against Automotive under a collective bargaining agreement or company policy. Captures grievance number, filing date, grievant employee, union steward, grievance type, description, step in the grievance procedure, resolution status, resolution date, and outcome. Supports labor relations management and CBA compliance tracking.'
}

Table workforce.safety_incident {
  safety_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the safety incident record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for leading the incident investigation.']
  equipment_equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of equipment involved, if any.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of equipment involved, if any.']
  primary_safety_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee directly involved in the incident.']
  body_part_affected varchar [note: 'Anatomical body part injured or impacted, if applicable.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed corrective action to prevent recurrence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was first created in the system.']
  days_away_from_work varchar [note: 'Number of workdays the employee was unable to work due to the incident.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Category of equipment implicated in the incident.']
  incident_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level category grouping the incident for reporting.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the incident for tracking and reporting.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incident record.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the safety incident occurred at the plant.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incident (e.g., injury, near‑miss, property damage, environmental).']
  injury_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the injury or impact.']
  injury_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the injury as defined by OSHA guidelines.']
  investigation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation was concluded.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the formal investigation of the incident began.']
  lost_time_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the incident resulted in lost work time.']
  osha_300_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking to the OSHA 300 Log entry.']
  osha_recordable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident meets OSHA recordable criteria.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the incident took place.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned during incident assessment.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis result for the incident.']
  safety_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee had completed required safety training prior to the incident.']
  safety_training_date date [note: 'Date when the employee completed the most recent safety training.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the incident occurred.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the incident record.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the work center or production line involved in the incident.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing workplace safety incidents, near-misses, and OSHA recordable events at Automotive\'s manufacturing plants. Captures incident date/time, plant, work center, employee involved, incident type (injury, near-miss, property damage, environmental), body part affected, root cause, corrective action, OSHA recordable flag, days away from work, and OSHA 300 log reference. Supports OSHA compliance and IATF 16949 safety reporting.'
}

Table workforce.headcount_plan {
  headcount_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the headcount planning record.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit (e.g., division, department) the plan applies to.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant the headcount plan supports.']
  actual_headcount varchar [note: 'Recorded number of employees actually employed during the period.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the plan.']
  attrition_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assumed annual attrition percentage used for forecasting.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial budget allocated to support the planned headcount.']
  capacity_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned production capacity factor influencing headcount needs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the budget amount.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the headcount plan expires or is superseded.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year (e.g., FY2025) for budgeting alignment.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to generate the headcount forecast.']
  headcount_category varchar [note: 'Classification of headcount by employment type.']
  headcount_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  iatf_competency_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IATF 16949 competency certification is required for the roles covered.']
  job_family_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the job family or classification targeted by the plan.']
  labor_agreement_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the collective bargaining agreement governing labor terms.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the plan.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Vehicle model year associated with the production volume driving the headcount plan.']
  new_hire_plan varchar [note: 'Number of new hires projected for the period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the plan.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the planning period.']
  period_value varchar [note: 'Numeric value of the period (e.g., month number 1‑12, quarter 1‑4).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the headcount plan, used in reporting and approvals.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the headcount plan (e.g., "2025 Model Year Plant A Ramp-Up").']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan frequency or purpose.']
  planned_headcount varchar [note: 'Target number of employees planned for the period.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the plan is reviewed and updated.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of required safety certifications for the workforce segment.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the headcount plan becomes effective.']
  unionized_flag boolean [note: 'True if the workforce segment is covered by a union agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the plan.']
  variance_headcount varchar [note: 'Difference between planned and actual headcount (planned minus actual).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the plan for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the plan.']

  Note: 'Workforce planning record capturing planned headcount targets by org unit, job family, plant, and time period (monthly/quarterly/annual). Captures planned headcount, actual headcount, variance, attrition assumption, new hire plan, and approval status. Supports SOP (Start of Production) ramp-up planning, capacity planning for new model launches, and annual HR budgeting aligned with CapEx and production volume plans.'
}

Table workforce.disciplinary_action {
  disciplinary_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the disciplinary action record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manager who authorized or recorded the disciplinary action.']
  primary_disciplinary_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who is the subject of the disciplinary action.']
  acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has formally acknowledged receipt of the action.']
  action_date date [note: 'Date on which the disciplinary action was formally issued.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the disciplinary action, e.g., DA-000123.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Category of the disciplinary action taken against the employee.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any appeal filed against the disciplinary action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the disciplinary action record was first created in the system.']
  disciplinary_action_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the incident and the disciplinary action taken.']
  disciplinary_action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the disciplinary action.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the disciplinary action expires or is no longer enforceable (e.g., for temporary suspensions).']
  policy_violated_code varchar [note: 'Code of the internal policy, regulation, or collective‑bargaining agreement that was breached.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the disciplinary action based on policy impact.']
  union_rep_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee was represented by a union during the disciplinary process.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the disciplinary action record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of formal disciplinary actions taken against employees, including verbal warnings, written warnings, suspensions, and terminations. Captures action type, incident description, policy violated, action date, issuing manager, employee acknowledgment status, union representation flag, appeal status, and expiry date of the action. Supports progressive discipline management and labor relations compliance.'
}

Table workforce.employee_transfer {
  employee_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each employee transfer record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR or manager who approved the transfer.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR or manager who approved the transfer.']
  from_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source organizational unit (plant, department, cost center) from which the employee is moving.']
  from_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee\'s current position before the transfer.']
  primary_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee being transferred.']
  to_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination organizational unit to which the employee is moving.']
  to_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the new position the employee will occupy after the transfer.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the transfer approval workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the transfer was approved or rejected.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes entered by HR or the employee regarding the transfer.']
  compensation_change_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer results in a change to base salary or compensation package.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the transfer with IATF 16949 and other regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transfer record was initially created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the transfer becomes effective for payroll, benefits, and reporting.']
  employee_transfer_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the transfer record.']
  from_job_classification_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the employee\'s job classification in the source org unit.']
  new_pay_grade varchar [note: 'Pay grade assigned to the employee after the transfer, if applicable.']
  new_salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base salary amount effective after the transfer.']
  new_salary_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the new salary amount.']
  relocation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer requires employee relocation to a different geographic site.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of required safety certifications for the employee in the new role.']
  seniority_date date [note: 'Date used to calculate employee seniority after the transfer, often the original hire date or last promotion date.']
  temporary_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date for a temporary transfer, if applicable.']
  temporary_transfer_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transfer is for a limited period (e.g., project assignment).']
  to_job_classification_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the employee\'s job classification in the destination org unit.']
  training_completion_flag boolean [note: 'True if mandatory training for the new position has been completed.']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the transfer event, used for tracking and reference in HR processes.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the transfer is being performed (e.g., business need, employee request, succession planning).']
  transfer_source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Original identifier of the transfer record in the source system.']
  transfer_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source HR system (e.g., SuccessFactors, Workday) that originated the transfer record.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the transfer (e.g., promotion, demotion, lateral move, plant relocation, department change).']
  union_transfer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer is subject to a collective bargaining agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the transfer record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing internal employee movement events including plant transfers, department transfers, promotions, demotions, and lateral moves. Captures from/to org unit, from/to position, from/to job classification, effective date, transfer reason, relocation flag, and approval chain. Supports workforce mobility tracking, seniority management, and headcount reconciliation across plants.'
}

Table workforce.onboarding_task {
  onboarding_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each onboarding task record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR or functional owner responsible for completing the task.']
  primary_onboarding_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the newly hired or transferred employee the task belongs to.']
  actual_effort_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded effort spent on the task, expressed in hours.']
  badge_issued_flag boolean [note: 'True when the employee badge has been printed and assigned.']
  benefits_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the employee\'s benefits enrollment process.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date the task was marked completed.']
  completion_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed minutes from task start to completion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding task record was first created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the task should be finished.']
  effort_estimate_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated effort required to complete the task, expressed in hours.']
  iatf_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task includes mandatory IATF 16949 competency training.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the owner or employee.']
  onboarding_task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the onboarding task.']
  plant_access_granted_flag boolean [note: 'True when the employee has been granted physical access to the manufacturing plant.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority used to sequence work across new hires.']
  task_category varchar [note: 'Category that groups the onboarding activity for reporting and compliance.']
  task_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier used by HR to reference the onboarding task.']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what the task entails.']
  task_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the onboarding activity (e.g., "IT System Setup").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the onboarding task record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking the completion of onboarding activities for newly hired or transferred employees. Captures task name, task category (IT setup, safety orientation, IATF training, badge issuance, plant access, benefits enrollment), assigned owner, due date, completion date, and status. Ensures new hires are production-ready and compliant with IATF 16949 competency requirements before shop floor assignment.'
}

Table workforce.workforce_certification {
  workforce_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each certification record.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Internal reference to the scanned or digital verification document.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who holds the certification.']
  certification_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or number assigned by the issuing body.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'Level or grade of the certification (e.g., Level 1, Advanced).']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the certification (e.g., Forklift Operator, IATF Internal Auditor).']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of the certification indicating its primary focus.']
  competency_area varchar [note: 'Business or regulatory domain the certification applies to (e.g., IATF, OSHA, EPA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certification expires or must be renewed.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'True if the certification is currently valid; false if expired or revoked.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the certification is required by law or internal policy for the employee\'s role.']
  is_voluntary boolean [note: 'True if the certification is optional but encouraged.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was originally granted.']
  issuing_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the authority or body that issued the certification.']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the certification was last validated by HR or compliance.']
  next_renewal_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date by which the certification must be renewed to remain valid.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the certification.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether renewal is mandatory, optional, or not applicable.']
  required_job_code varchar [note: 'Job classification code that mandates this certification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']
  validity_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between issue_date and expiry_date.']
  verification_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored verification document in the document management system.']
  workforce_certification_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the certification.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking mandatory and voluntary certifications held by employees, including safety certifications (forklift, crane, lockout/tagout), quality certifications (IATF internal auditor, SPC, APQP), technical certifications (EV high-voltage safety, ADAS calibration), and regulatory certifications (OSHA 10/30, EPA). Captures certification type, issuing body, issue date, expiry date, renewal status, and verification document reference.'
}

Table workforce.labor_cost_allocation {
  labor_cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each labor cost allocation line.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department to which the employee belongs.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department to which the employee belongs.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant where the labor was performed.']
  primary_labor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee whose labor is being allocated.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing production order linked to the labor effort.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific location or work cell where labor was performed.']
  activity_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific shop‑floor activity or operation.']
  allocated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost (hours × rate) allocated to the cost object.']
  allocated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total labor hours assigned to the cost object.']
  allocation_batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the allocation batch or header record to which this line belongs.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the labor cost allocation was posted.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Business rule used to allocate the labor cost (e.g., direct, absorption, activity‑based).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the allocation\'s approval process.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the allocation was approved.']
  cost_allocation_type varchar [note: 'Business purpose of the allocation (e.g., production, maintenance, R&D).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier to which the labor cost is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the allocation line was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the allocated cost.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the allocation is no longer valid; null for open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the allocation becomes effective; null if immediate.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter or period label for reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year associated with the allocation.']
  iatf_competency_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee meets the IATF competency requirement for the activity.']
  is_manual_allocation boolean [note: 'True when the allocation line was entered manually rather than by system run.']
  job_classification_code varchar [note: 'Code linking to the employee\'s job classification (e.g., skill level, trade).']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Classification of labor as direct production or indirect support.']
  labor_cost_allocation_status varchar [note: 'Processing state of the allocation record.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Hourly compensation rate for the employee at the time of allocation.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the allocation line within the batch.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional remarks or business justification for the allocation.']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'True if the allocated hours include overtime premium.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the payroll period in which the labor was earned.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the payroll period in which the labor was earned.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Code of the work shift during which the labor was performed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating source system (e.g., SAP CO, SuccessFactors).']
  union_cba_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the union contract governing the employee\'s labor terms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the allocation line.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version used for record concurrency control.']
  wbs_element_code varchar [note: 'Project or work‑breakdown‑structure element that receives the labor cost.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the allocation of employee labor costs to cost centers, production orders, projects, and plant activities. Captures employee, pay period, hours allocated, cost center, WBS element or production order reference, labor rate, total allocated cost, and allocation method. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA CO-PA for product costing and manufacturing overhead absorption.'
}

Table workforce.department {

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the department record was first created in the system.']

  department_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the department.']
  department_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the department office.']

  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the department\'s purpose and responsibilities.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the department became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the department is scheduled to be retired or restructured (null if open‑ended).']

  is_unionized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the department’s workforce is covered by a labor union.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the primary physical location or plant where the department operates.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the department.']
  operating_hours_per_week varchar [note: 'Typical number of work hours per week for the department.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift schedule used by the department.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the department.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the department record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for department. Referenced by department_id.'
}

Table workforce.benefit_plan {
  benefit_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for benefit_plan']
  superseded_benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on benefit_plan (superseded_benefit_plan_id)']
  contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount contributed by the employer toward the employee\'s premium.']
  contribution_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the employer contribution is applied.']
  coverage_details varchar [note: 'Summary of benefits, limits, and covered services provided by the plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the benefit plan record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan becomes binding for eligible employees.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan ends or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  eligibility_age_max varchar [note: 'Oldest employee age allowed to enroll.']
  eligibility_age_min varchar [note: 'Youngest employee age allowed to enroll.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules defining which employees qualify for the plan.']
  eligibility_employment_status varchar [note: 'Employment status required for eligibility.']
  eligibility_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location(s) where employees are eligible (e.g., country code).']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Last date employees may enroll in the benefit plan.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'First date employees may enroll in the benefit plan.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if employee participation in the plan is mandatory.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the plan was last reviewed for compliance or updates.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next formal review of the plan.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of the plan (e.g., core, supplemental, voluntary).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the benefit plan (e.g., HR catalog number).']
  plan_coinsurance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of costs the employee shares after deductible is met.']
  plan_coverage_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount the plan will pay for covered services.']
  plan_deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount the employee must pay before the plan begins to cover expenses.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the benefit plan as used in HR communications.']
  plan_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the benefit plan.']
  plan_out_of_pocket_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total amount an employee will pay in a benefit year.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the benefit plan indicating the type of coverage offered.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the plan (e.g., v2024.1).']
  premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount the employee (or employer) pays for the plan per payment cycle.']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the premium amount (e.g., USD).']
  provider_email varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the benefit provider.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor or insurer offering the benefit.']
  provider_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone contact for the benefit provider.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit plan.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tax rate applied to the premium when taxability_flag is true.']
  taxability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan premiums are subject to payroll tax.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the benefit plan was terminated or discontinued.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the benefit plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for benefit_plan. Referenced by plan_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for pay_period. Referenced by pay_period_id.'
